-4782315901562449638:-337303635474561203.name: Something From Nothing pt 2
-4782315901562449638:-337303635474561203.desc: This multiblock works just as a normal drilling rig, but with the power of Technology it won't reduce the underground field size so the output will always be the same.

-6215508413557630456:-330443813993036947.name: Industrial Water Purification
-6215508413557630456:-330443813993036947.desc:
For a while now, you have been able to get away with cutting and engraving your silicon circuit wafers with nothing but the bare metal of your machines. However, this can cause significant imperfections in the final wafer, so now that you are producing even more advanced circuitry such imperfections are no longer tolerable.

With the ability to make LuV components, you can now make the Water Purification Plant. This multiblock allows you to make purified water, which will be helpful in making wafers of increasing purity and perfection. For your current Naquadah-Doped Wafers, this is not yet necessary, but using purified water may significantly speed up the process, so it's definitely worth checking out.

The Water Purification Plant does not run recipes itself. Instead, it provides the core logistics for your water purification line to work. Around it you can build water purification units that link to the Water Purification Plant. The plant then provides power and maintenance to the different units, and synchronizes their recipe cycles.

-8754088215918060056:-544274892336709208.name: Capturing Light on a Circuit Board
-8754088215918060056:-544274892336709208.desc: Using various carefully picked materials, you've managed to create a circuit board that will be able to contain the flows of photons in these new circuit parts.

-7733034115414850723:-520252506323270845.name: Indestructible Vessel
-7733034115414850723:-520252506323270845.desc: In light of your thaumaturgical research, you have come to the conclusion that Greatwood is, in fact, a great material for constructing a resilient boat. Utilizing this material to its fullest potential has allowed you to construct a boat that is slightly faster and will never break on collision.

-5569503959352868746:-357380327371092079.name: Prismatic Naquadah Composite Slurry
-5569503959352868746:-357380327371092079.desc:
Why stop at Prismatic Naquadah? Given it's malleable nature you suspect that it may be advantageous to use it's unique strength to form new, much more durable, foil types.

Combing the Prismatic Naquadah with your choice of binding powder in a Mixer and you'll create exactly the type of substance you were looking for; A Prismatic Naquadah Composite Slurry to mold into foil.

-8480210258690868044:-321987492323863621.name: Netherite Scrap Seed
-8480210258690868044:-321987492323863621.desc:
Finally, we can start putting some of that Netherite Scrap to use!

Combine the Hot Neterite Scrap and some Rich Nether Waste in an Autoclave for a 10% chance at getting a Netherite Scrap Seed. 

Notably, as mentioned before, the Netherite Scrap gets recycled during this recipe so it only really costs Rich Nether Waste to create. Given the low probability, you want the recipe to be running as often as possible.

-7880854746900516763:-319039424791732188.name: The Redstone Sniffer
-7880854746900516763:-319039424791732188.desc:
Ever wonder where your redstone transmitters are? Or wish they were labeled? Maybe your teammate placed one down, didn't tell you, and now you're stuck wondering what private frequency they placed?

Now, you have a way to do all of that.

Introducing the Redstone Sniffer!

For regular wireless redstone, the sniffer will list all currently used frequencies, tell you whether that frequency is private, and gives you an option to label it.

For advanced wireless covers, the sniffer will additionally tell you where that cover is currently located, including in other dimensions, and offers you the option to also highlight or teleport to it.

-4667288543375339252:-341432512930025917.name: AE Tier 2 Fluid Storage
-4667288543375339252:-341432512930025917.desc:
This Processor allows you to craft 256k, 1024k, 4096 and 16384k Fluid Storage Components.

These Components need to be paired with Advanced Fluid Storage Housings to turn them into Fluid Storage Cells.

-8306054735867726258:-337201432602854931.name: Cetane Boosted Diesel
-8306054735867726258:-337201432602854931.desc:
The other option is Cetane-Boosted Diesel. 

You will need to mix either Diesel or Bio Diesel with Tetranitromethane. Check out the related quests in [quest]Tier 3 - HV[/quest] or [quest]Working with Oil[/quest] tabs for more information.

-7880209593223277990:-334761337273606093.name: Heliofusion Exoticizer
-7880209593223277990:-334761337273606093.desc:
Using large quantities of graviton shards creates regions of space so extreme that exotic forms of matter can exist with far greater stability than usual. Unfortunately, this region is so extreme that it is essentially spatially disconnected from the rest of the Forge, unable to utilize existing upgrades to the structure and its other modules.

[note]The fourth and final module of the Godforge, Heliofusion Exoticizer allows for the production of Quark Gluon Plasma and Magmatter. Initially only Quark Gluon Plasma is possible, with Magmatter requiring a much later upgrade. Consult the Godforge Information section of the UI to read how to produce these materials.[/note]

[note]Usage of this module requires the QGPIU upgrade.[/note]

-8516599160743502345:-331553468313526073.name: Raw Carbon Fiber
-8516599160743502345:-331553468313526073.desc:
Alternatively, you can use Raw Carbon Fiber for your Reinforced Circuit Boards.

[note]You can use Epoxid to get a better yield on it![/note]

-8494011482519438197:-326724761055970867.name: Quantum Entangled Singularities
-8494011482519438197:-326724761055970867.desc:
To make an entangled pair for usage in the quantum ring, put the Singularity and Enderpearl Dust on the ground, then use an explosion (Tiny TNT works well) to create two Quantum Entangled Singularities.

[note]QESs are each a unique pair and will only work with one another.[/note]

-8361079222456284577:-326366012090146791.name: A steam-powered whirl!
-8361079222456284577:-326366012090146791.desc:
How does one centrifuge rare materials in the Steam Age? The answer is by building and using the Steam Separator, of course! By using steam to create a spinning force, this Multiblock can separate different materials quickly and easily.

If you think separation is taking forever, the Steam Separator will speed things up!

This Multiblock can process recipes up to LV Tier, and does accept an Output Hatch for recipes. But if you are looking to use recipes which require Fluid Inputs, you might have to build an LV Centrifuge in the future.

-4618074744092982901:-314762439768118042.name: Waterproof Tech
-4618074744092982901:-314762439768118042.desc:
With the ability to bend spacetime at your will, you can prevent fluids and electronics from interacting.

Fluid containing patterns can be processed.

[note]Otherwise it works like crafting input bus.[/note]

-5009088933424523272:-310008041856484761.name: Incense Crucible
-5009088933424523272:-310008041856484761.desc:
The forefather of the 1.12.2 Incense Altar.

The bonuses it gives are mostly similar, but the way it works is completely different.
Each incense is meant to be tiered one after another. Currently you can only use Wood Ash and Byrrus (Red).

White smoke will come out when there is an incense in the crucible, and the crucible will light up when it finds a player to increase the incense value of.
Once the player has an incense value greater than 0, their sacrificial knife will get super-charged, allowing the player to completely sacrifice all of their hearts but one.

Putting Wood Ash in the crucible will increase the incense value from 0 to 400.
Byrrus increases it from 400 to 800. It cannot get you from 0 to 800 alone.

The LP you get from self-sacrifice will get a boost depending on your Incense Value.

LP = 200LP * HeartSacrificed * IncenseModifier

IncenseModifier = IncenseValue * 0.001

-4765124701548164912:-308969747267182528.name: Can I Build A Mothership For My Father?
-4765124701548164912:-308969747267182528.desc: The Mothership is a Space Station which you can move to a different celestial body (even stars!). It is the only way to get to the outermost system, the Amun-Ra system. That system has six planets and moons you can access. The most important one is Seth, where you can find Raw Tengam Ore.

-7243693864692384265:-306779392685242601.name: Fountain Of Conjuration
-7243693864692384265:-306779392685242601.desc:
The Fountain of Conjuration, as I'm sincerely hoping you can guess at this point, functions exactly like the Mana Fountain and the Fountain of Alchemy, except it handles exclusively recipes that are typically done with a Conjuration Catalyst underneath a Mana Pool.

If you need any further details about it's functionality, check out the Mana Fountain quest or the Lexica Botania.

[note]Reminder that this can also wall-share with any type of Mana Fountain you may currently have.[/note]

-6897090588793529674:-264044301390674153.name: Finally, Universium!
-6897090588793529674:-264044301390674153.desc:
You've finally solved it. Who knew that leaving dead stars long enough would cause your artificial solar system to implode and lead to a Big Bang?

If only you could tap into that much mass right before it started expanding again, you could literally hold the entire universe in the palm of your hand.

This is your third and final material from the EOH.

[note]It's mainly used for UXV crafts, but your first few should go in the Eternity loop. The quest for it is located in the Endgame Goals tab.

Congrats! You can now craft Magnetohydrodynamically Constrained Star Matter (MHDCSM).[/note]

-8752523978928826755:-262206917218675700.name: Steel Barrel
-8752523978928826755:-262206917218675700.desc: The Steel barrel also has 72 slots.

-6794591825239688845:-279511653343715244.name: Wireless Multi-Amp Hatches
-6794591825239688845:-279511653343715244.desc:
With Shirabon now acquired, you can create 4A, 16A and 64A wireless energy hatches. These are particularly useful for machines which can use multi-amp, but do not need extreme amounts of power warranting a laser hatch.

[note]These used to be needed in large quantities, being the best energy hatch to use on a Processing Array. May they rest in peace.[/note]

-6186734719426077723:-269155610232400988.name: The Legs On The Shuttle Rocket Go Step, Step, Step...
-6186734719426077723:-269155610232400988.desc: The Shuttle Legs are very useful, as they allow the Shuttle Rocket to land without any aid. I highly recommend you build them! You don't have a choice anyway.

-8693302957682373366:-256773967714075855.name: SMD Inductors
-8693302957682373366:-256773967714075855.desc: Now that you have Neodymium you can make the last of the SMDs, the SMD inductor. It can replace Small Coils in your circuit crafting.

-5708915228904413980:-253111511254320501.name: THE RAINMAKER
-5708915228904413980:-253111511254320501.desc:
The Rainmaker is a Forestry block capable of stopping/producing rain & creating thunderstorms.

The Rainmaker has no GUI, but can be right clicked with 3 different capsules:

Iodine Capsule: Produces Rain

Phosphoric Iodine Capsule: Produces a Thunderstorm

Dissipation Charge: Stops Rain

The Rainmaker requires around ~30 seconds after activation for the rain and thunderstorm to start.

The dissipation charge will stop the rain after a few seconds until the next activation.

-9206706300448087195:-250940857986290206.name: Big Beautiful Brewery
-9206706300448087195:-250940857986290206.desc: Start your own local brewery with the BBB! You can use it to make tons of potions, obscure alcoholic drinks, or, more likely, industrial quantities of lubricant.

-7616949155129872515:-244447483332891223.name: Integrated Ore Factory - All in One!
-7616949155129872515:-244447483332891223.desc: The Integrated Ore Factory allows you to process ores through multiple processes at once.

-8084303837458271711:-239621719673619688.name: Marie Curium?
-8084303837458271711:-239621719673619688.desc:
It's like cured meat... no, not at all, many sacrifices were made on the path to understanding radioactivity, may they be remembered forever. This goes to you, Marie and Pierre Curie.

This is the only non-plasma produced in a fusion reaction in this entire chain. That's not the important part, though: the important part is that [warn]this is one of the bottlenecks of the chain.[/warn] For every reactor running the final recipe, you will need around 0.144 reactors of this, so you won't make it to a compact at the end with just one mk1 reactor. Expect to follow the count of the second-to-last recipe, the Advanced Nitinol one.

-6712615400254826381:-237713023857680156.name: Upgrades
-6712615400254826381:-237713023857680156.desc:
You want to automate recipes involving fluids, but the crafting module only accept items? You want to see how machines are handling recipes in the request table to see if something gets stuck? You will need upgrades.

You can apply upgrades to pipes and modules, but you can't just access the upgrade menu by right-clicking the chassis with a wrench.

Introducing the Logistics Pipe Controller. This item is like a wrench for LP, except it will open the upgrades menu. It can also allow you to view statistics related to the pipe you are accessing.

-8014877735925203463:-236524594541341799.name: Stopping the Infinity Bleed
-8014877735925203463:-236524594541341799.desc: Already struggling to keep up with infinity demand? Not to worry, the component assembly line may just be what you need. You probably have one already. Upgrade it to UEV to allow creating UEV components at a significantly reduced cost.

-8483351188359370115:-202939962477921695.name: Energized Wireless Dynamo Hatch
-8483351188359370115:-202939962477921695.desc:
The Energized Wireless Dynamo Hatch acts just as you may expect; it will inject EU directly from an energy-producing multiblock into your wireless EU network. No need for those pesky laser conduits and LSC middlemen!

[warn]The dynamo transfer rate is the same as the laser source it's crafted with: 65kA UMV. Also has a very large internal storage.[/warn]

[note]Great for Dyson & Large Naquadah Reactor.[/note]

-4981735154459347867:-227148253266809255.name: XL Turbo Steam Turbine
-4981735154459347867:-227148253266809255.desc:
The first of the XL Turbines. Because this is the first, this quest will include an explanation for the mechanics shared across all XL Turbines.

Every XL Turbine can hold 12 turbine items inside, in the 12 Rotor Assemblies, but their total speed is 16x higher than the regular multiblock equivalent. Make sure all the turbine items inside are of the same material, and also that you can provide enough input to match the flow rate that the multi needs! If you need even more speed, you can enable Loose Mode with a screwdriver on the controller, which can greatly increase turbines' flowrate while decreasing the efficiency.

These huge turbines also support TecTech hatches, so you can use multi-amp dynamos with a much higher output amount than normal, or even laser outputs later on to sustain the hundreds of millions of EU/t that the strongest turbines can produce.

Each of the 5 turbines takes in the same fuels as their smaller counterparts, with some exceptions that will be mentioned in the respective quest. This XL Steam Turbine only consumes Steam, as expected.

-8541989652632873807:-225455486441067032.name: Basic Flight Potion
-8541989652632873807:-225455486441067032.desc:
Tired of jetpacks? Want normal flight with no strings attached? Look no further!

Using your brand new MV Assembler and a Chicken Trophy, you can craft a basic Potion of Flight from Blood Magic, giving you 7 minutes of Creative flight with 8 uses before you have to make a new one.

[note]Chicken Trophies are a rare drop from killing chickens.[/note]

[note]Once you unlock Blood Magic, you can craft your own Flight Potion Flasks with a duration of up to 19 minutes, and refill them with any Filling Agent in an Alchemic Chemistry set.[/note]

-6407498567809638090:-224818826906846751.name: My Eyes Hurt! #2
-6407498567809638090:-224818826906846751.desc: The optical assembly is the second optical circuit. It is a UHV-tier circuit.

-8191827436027574183:-215787065986611368.name: Do You Really Need Them All?
-8191827436027574183:-215787065986611368.desc:
There are many more backpacks: Coiners, thaumaturges, trackmanns...

[note]Hint: Scroll to the Bottom[/note]

-5429883527448864555:-212101005470234843.name: Cribs
-5429883527448864555:-212101005470234843.desc:
As your researches for Applied Greg continued, you found a way to combine ME interface's pattern processing ability with input buses, crafting input buses! Now, you can directly place patterns into input bus. However, input buses lacked sufficient item space and computational power to process items coming from your ME network. Thus, you added additional storage and circuits.

You can even place non-consumable items like lenses, molds inside!! However, consumable items won't do anything.

But, still you lack enough technology to process fluids, they are just not good for electronics.

Crafting input bus is fully isolated from other buses/hatches, so you may have to change your patterns.

[warn]Crafting input proxy[/warn] will let you wallshare multiblocks without actually wallsharing them. UIV-Tier

[warn]Crafting input buffer[/warn] will be waterproof. UEV-Tier

-5925519573260698196:-211820084670317969.name: Raw Copper Ore Exchange
-5925519573260698196:-211820084670317969.desc: You can exchange your stack of Raw Copper Ore for IC2 Copper Ore.

-7628239345660310137:-209027638045761132.name: Dense Netherite Plate
-7628239345660310137:-209027638045761132.desc:
Once you've managed to aquire 36 of these Intensely Bonded Netherite Nanoparticles you can finally create your very first Netherite material: The Dense Netherite Plate.

You will need one of these for the Mk1 Fusion Reactor, which is required to create the rest of the Netherite materials.

-5865034814944347891:-177269577133835655.name: Magnetohydrodynamically Constrained Star Matter
-5865034814944347891:-177269577133835655.desc:
Or MHDCSM for short.

This is your primary material for UXV components. It's tricky to make and extremely expensive, as all endgame materials should be.

Instead of solidifying it directly, you need to coat Eternity parts with it.

Have fun!

-7131856560517868006:-176749391011464621.name: Handle Resources Like a Bo$$ #1
-7131856560517868006:-176749391011464621.desc:
A feat of human engineering at your disposal!

This machine allows you to filter by machine recipe type and allows you to effortlessly set up automations. To use it simply put a machine of any type into the GUI to create a filter, e.g. a Macerator will allow the machine to only accept items that can be macerated. The same machine can be used to create an inverse filter (a button in the GUI) that will allow you to exclude all items that can be processed by that machine.

-8171783490200761150:-175926805187924662.name: Fluid Heater
-8171783490200761150:-175926805187924662.desc:
You will need a Fluid Heater for some chemical processes, for the best fries, but also right now to make liquid sodium for NaK coolant. To proceed you will need either an MV or an HV Fluid Heater. Make one of them.

The HV one can be used later to better process bauxite after you have been to the moon.

[note]If you used sodium sulfate to create liquid sodium instead, you can hold 2 60k NaK coolant cells in your hand to skip over this quest.[/note]

-4916213553338882423:-199754652631778572.name: A Better Furnace!
-4916213553338882423:-199754652631778572.desc:
Build a Blast Furnace!

Enjoy faster smelting for your metals!

[warn]Will not process all items that a regular furnace will. Check NEI for more detail.[/warn]

-5843632203454110643:-197994902626524839.name: Shuttle Nose Cone
-5843632203454110643:-197994902626524839.desc: This Rocket Cone doesn't have the spiky thing like the others have. Is this good or bad? I don't know, I'm only here to tell you what to do. So go build one Shuttle Nose Cone! Otherwise I will revoke your license to progress!

-5638703973738633044:-195265666198213335.name: Need More Information?
-5638703973738633044:-195265666198213335.desc:
A brand-new tool for you, amigo.

This little buddy not only has a larger card slot than the normal one, but also can show more information about your network storage.

Clicking the component icon can switch between ITEM, FLUID, and ESSENTIA details.

[warn]NOTE:
|  unit  |      range   |
|    B   |    0~2^10   |
|   KB   | 2^10~2^20 |
|   MB   | 2^20~2^30 |
|   GB   | 2^30~2^40 |
|   TB   | 2^40~2^50 |
|   PB   | 2^50~2^60 |
|   EB   | 2^60~2^70 |
|   ZB   | 2^70~2^80 |
|   YB   | 2^80~2^90 |
|   BB   | over 2^90  |[/warn]

-6860812645522504692:-193802212352620586.name: Trigger: Nano Boots of the Traveller Skip

-5193610363268664517:-189322022016330749.name: Trigger: Nightvision Goggle Skip
-5193610363268664517:-189322022016330749.desc: Skip is here for those who get the goggles in a lootbag

-7132625864114193932:-184445211139058685.name: It's everytime these Transistors
-7132625864114193932:-184445211139058685.desc:
Hello fellow achievement hunter.

Looking for the OC Transistor to get one out of all these achievements?
Lucky you, because now you can craft it and it's really simple.

Just put one of your first GT Transistors in your 2x2 crafting grid and be happy with its result. Don't forget to change it back the same way.

-8399516922550336751:-182192496315444162.name: Filtering by Type
-8399516922550336751:-182192496315444162.desc:
There are situations where there are a large amount of the same type of items, but the quantity of each aren't large enough to get them dedicated storage...

The Type Filter ItemSink Module can assign an item type to inventory space it is connected to. All items of the same type will be assigned to that inventory space.

-7951601137615098449:-180588778704540409.name: Guzzling Massive Amounts of Gas
-7951601137615098449:-180588778704540409.desc:
It can run 4 different recipes simultaneously, with 4 parallels for each recipe.

A worthy upgrade for some SpaceTime.

-7880130124221230423:-147194354347138680.name: A Better Coolant for Your Superconductors
-7880130124221230423:-147194354347138680.desc:
The subzero temperatures of SpaceTime make it an ideal substitute in all of your superconductor crafts.

You won't need billions of Liquid Helium anymore!

[note]The earlier tiers of superconductors are also faster to craft with this.[/note]

-5282439462436342828:-171043867742376461.name: IV Field Generator
-5282439462436342828:-171043867742376461.desc: Oh look, the latest and greatest field generator! I wonder what you'll end up using these for... maybe a Tree Growth Simulator? Maybe a new Matter Manipulator?

-4646567102207181371:-166390956998254514.name: Pseudostable Black Hole Containment Field
-4646567102207181371:-166390956998254514.desc:
As the materials around you achieve transcendental properties, you have found that traditional modes of compression like pistons or gases will not be enough to compact them as densely as you require.

The solution? Using the sheer force of gravity. By using extreme amounts of explosive force on your fourth-dimensionally engineered tesseracts, you can tear tiny rifts in the fabric of space, enough to create a seed which can birth a black hole. If you can build a machine which can harness it, it will be able to compress even the strongest materials to the most dense states possible.

Taming a black hole is no easy task - even with all of your precautions, it defies your efforts to stabilize it and attempts to collapse itself constantly. Meaningfully extending the lifespan of the black hole will be extremely difficult. By bending spacetime through carefully aligned glass, you can reverse the flow of hawking radiation back into the black hole and keep it completely stable - problematically, however, the amount of spacetime required to maintain this process increases at an exponential rate.

As the black hole decays, it becomes even more powerful - but be wary, as it will eventually become unstable and you will no longer be able to recover your items from the edge of the event horizon. To safely close the black hole and restart this process, you have engineered a collapser out of the same fourth-dimensional materials.

[note]The Pseudostable Black Hole Containment Field is the most powerful compressor in the game, capable of creating superdense plates out of any material, as well as being able to perform more advanced neutronium compressor recipes that will save incredible amounts of resources.[/note]

[note]You must use programmed circuits in the input buses to determine if the machine will act as a Compressor or Neutronium Compressor![/note]

-8146663606087231605:-165717985114755506.name: Raw Gold Ore Exchange
-8146663606087231605:-165717985114755506.desc: You can exchange your stack of Raw Gold Ore for vanilla Gold Ore.

-5744442785667642528:-158262221269727355.name: Fits The Mold
-5744442785667642528:-158262221269727355.desc:
Making machines for every mold sounds tiring... fortunately, the Fluid Shaper multiblock can handle any number of molds by using Solidifier Hatches. This multiblock is also extendable, gaining more parallels if you add additional segments. Be sure to expand it if it's too slow!

[note]The Fluid Shaper also speeds up as it is running, making it more efficient to do large batches.[/note]

-8999379980467781725:-154417426012279355.name: Smoke Your Normal Warp Away
-8999379980467781725:-154417426012279355.desc:
Having too much normal warp? Sanitizing Soap is too expensive for you?

Well, luckily for you, you can remove your Warp using a Silverwood Smoking Pipe and some Relaxing Tobacco. This is a fairly cheap and sustainable option for removing your Normal warp (assuming you have a good option to get a lot of Vis).

It also has a chance to reduce your temporary warp.

[note]The chance to reduce your normal warp per Relaxing Tobacco is 10%, so you'll be needing a bunch of it.[/note]

-7159553166987312386:-153210756413638206.name: UMV Energy Hatch
-7159553166987312386:-153210756413638206.desc:
The penultimate energy hatch.

Hopefully you know what to upgrade first.

[note]Have you checked out wireless energy hatches yet?[/note]

-7003067506139187002:-137411703072559639.name: Fluid Storage
-7003067506139187002:-137411703072559639.desc:
Logistics Pipes can handle fluids.

There are multiple pipes for fluids in logistics pipes. Logistics Fluid Basic Pipe will be the one you will be mainly using.

You can attach it to a tank, assign a fluid to it, and it will assign the buffer tank for that fluid. Basically a fluid version of the ItemSink.

-7518403751933312267:-133057608522299710.name: Nefarious Production
-7518403751933312267:-133057608522299710.desc:
Now that you're producing Nether Semifluid, you can run it through a Distillation Tower to start extracting the nefarious products. You will need the Heavy Hellish Mud later in the line, and you'll need the Nefarious Gas for the next step.

[note]Don't neglect the byproducts! There are a lot of useful bonus resources to be collected.[/note]

-8730662353812275470:-115737557605290495.name: Pls Provide
-8730662353812275470:-115737557605290495.desc:
Just like items which have itemsink modules for storage and provider modules for request, fluids have basically the equivalent pipes.

The Logistics Fluid Provider Pipe will do the job. It will provide the fluid to other pipes and machines connected to them that need the fluid.

-6965903878335952711:-125268385768424863.name: Distill the Atmosphere
-6965903878335952711:-125268385768424863.desc:
Using the Vacuum Freezer you can create Liquid Air. Liquid Air can be centrifuged into additional gases you won't get when centrifuging normal compressed air. If you can gather enough Liquid Air you can instead distill it into a larger quantity and variety of rare gases.

Make these special hatches to hold and store the amounts required for this recipe.

-6509950608256890330:-122435090433322037.name: Tier 13 (UXV)
-6509950608256890330:-122435090433322037.desc:
Welcome to the final tier of NH (for now).

We hope you enjoy your stay, though be prepared for a lot of waiting.

Now that you have a reliable way to make MHDCSM, it would be wise to upgrade your EOHs to T9 for the Universium boost.

Have fun crafting the Stargate!

[warn]This tier is far from complete. Expect changes.[/warn]

-5479647572889222662:-114053641563607653.name: Crafting on Demand
-5479647572889222662:-114053641563607653.desc:
We can now request items, but it sucks when the systems lack the component while we CAN craft those components.

The system needs to know how to craft the items it is requesting.

To get started, get a crafting module. Both automation of machine-crafted recipes and crafting table recipes need this module, in one way or another.

-4677630017092864794:-111041124028060433.name: Ultimate Voltages Require Ultimate Lubricant™!
-4677630017092864794:-111041124028060433.desc: For your UIV components to work their interdimensional magic, you're going to need some dimensional border easing lubricant to not cause a universal collapse.

-7374234948579230118:-108897514059882442.name: Another Type of Steel
-7374234948579230118:-108897514059882442.desc:
Proto-Halkonite Steel is a super stable material, completely indestructible once formed.

You first have to make Proto-Halkonite base, which then you pour into components made out of infinity.

After getting Creon and Mellion, you can make Proto-Halkonite materials cheaper.

-7756273166616703872:-107958158276386588.name: Relocator
-7756273166616703872:-107958158276386588.desc:
The relocator allows for teleportation to previously marked locations, even between dimensions.

[note]Uses quite a lot of power so a GraviChestPlate or other energy sources are recommended.[/note]

-7863286137106609104:-107454240301395148.name: Knowing your Throughput!
-7863286137106609104:-107454240301395148.desc:
[quest]ME Throughput Monitor[/quest]

The ME Throughput Monitor will show the speed of items entering your network.

Clicking with an item will add it to the monitor.

Use a Wrench to lock the monitor

When the monitor is locked, use Shift+Right Click to change the measured time frame. 

Modes: 

Per Tick
Per Second
Per Minute
Per Hour

-7223099091008597338:-920334513683216560.name: Hot Netherite Scrap
-7223099091008597338:-920334513683216560.desc:
Heating up the Netherite Scrap is a rather quick and cheap process; simply throwing it in the Chemical Bath with a little lava ought to do the trick! 

Bear in mind though, you will be re-heating this scrap many times over so it's definitely recommended to have a substantial supply of lava to be able to pull from.

-5600778106165283022:-855071566655370213.name: Prismatic Acid
-5600778106165283022:-855071566655370213.desc:
Almost there I promise!

Now that you have obtained the Prismatic Gas, you must cool it down in a Vacuum Freezer with the assistance of Liquid Nitrogen. This will give you Prismatic Acid.

[warn]Aside from continuing down this line, you can also use Prismatic Acid in the processing of Gaia blocks later.[/warn]

-9040111725259888874:-798473871130869738.name: Chassis
-9040111725259888874:-798473871130869738.desc:
Basic Pipes can interact with adjacent blocks, but they also don't do much on their own.

Logistics Pipes has modules that provide the pipes with more complex functions, but the Basic Logistics Pipes can't make use of them.

To make use of the modules, there are special pipes called Chassis.

There are multiple tiers of Chassis in Logistics Pipes. For now, a Mk 1, which can hold 1 module, will work.

You can insert modules directly in the chassis by right-clicking said chassis with the module. But if you need to take modules out of a chassis or configure a module in one, you need to right-click the chassis with a wrench to access its UI.

-7228129145658002387:-795101217016493444.name: Collision Course
-7228129145658002387:-795101217016493444.desc:
Finally, to actually get some ICs, use your photons in a Target Chamber.

Note that the EU/t value listed in TC recipes is only a minimum, as recipe time is entirely determined by the Amount of photons you give the multi.

More specifically, you can get the recipe time by dividing recipe Amount by your input beam Amount, then multiplying that by 5s.

-8284474244884064236:-730852476632676945.name: Netherite Materials
-8284474244884064236:-730852476632676945.desc:
Now that you've completed the Netherite line and successfully set up your Mk1 Fusion Reactor, you finally have access to Boron Plasma, which is necessary for creating all of the different Netherite materials. 

These will be important for the rest of the game and are used often in a variety of items and materials, so it's important to make upgrades to your line both in terms of power and recipe usage as you continue to progress through the tiers.

[note]UHV power and the QFT are the biggest milestones in terms of recipe upgrades for the line.[/note]

[warn]You must use a High-Current Industrial (Plasma) Arc Furnace, a regular one isn't powerful enough.[/warn]

-6653872471765810391:-703325969313020818.name: Calcium Bottlenecking
-6653872471765810391:-703325969313020818.desc:
Craft yourself some bone-strengthening Calcium in plasma form.

This step is almost twice as fast as the Boron one, so you only need half the reactors, plus one, or the tier above the reactors on the Boron side. It would normally be a lot slower, but the next step needs only a small amount of Calcium.

Keep in mind that, at the end of each of the three fusion chains, the plasma amount gets multiplied, which means that most of your bottlenecks will be near the end of the entire setup, if you're going for Celestial Tungsten Plasma.

You will need 2.13 reactors with this recipe to feed 1 reactor with the next step.

-8303416969468780104:256342629506565719.name: Splicer
-8303416969468780104:256342629506565719.desc:
The Splicer is much like the Inoculator in function, however it has the benefit of not requiring any liquid. 

The Splicer will take a Genome Template from the Sequencer and will 'Splice' in the DNA, allowing you to change a bee's genetics.

The primary drawback of using this compared to the Inoculator is the increased expense of the block, and the significant power cost in comparison.

-7288053330851601378:255232111816426182.name: T2 Maceration Stack
-7288053330851601378:255232111816426182.desc:
Upgrade your Maceration Stack by replacing all the casings with Maceration Stack Casings and then placing the Upgrade Chip into the slot in the controller.

The Tier 2 Maceration stack has 4x more parallels!

248.name: The Choice: Ordo
248.desc: In exchange for 6 clusters, I will give you this node. May it serve you well. Oh, don't place it too close to another node, or it might start to drain vis...

249.name: The Choice: Aqua
249.desc: In exchange for 6 clusters, I will give you this node. May it serve you well. Oh, don't place it too close to another node, or it might start to drain vis...

250.name: The Choice: Aer
250.desc: In exchange for 6 clusters, I will give you this node. May it serve you well. Oh, don't place it too close to another node, or it might start to drain vis...

251.name: Not Just For Decoration
251.desc: Sure, they look pretty, but they serve a much more important purpose: Channeling vast amounts of vis and essentia. Your first uses will be an infusion setup and an alchemical furnace.

208.name: Protect Your Base: Tier 4
208.desc: The small parts for the powerful turrets.

209.name: Make Love, Not War
209.desc: For those who don't want to kill things. Just teleport them away, however this takes a large amount of power.

210.name: Why Grenades...
210.desc: ...when you can use rockets? Yes, rockets! They search and destroy, and go off with a huge boom.

211.name: Tier 4 Fence and Walls
211.desc: Another upgrade for your walls. One more to go!

212.name: Pulverize the Planet
212.desc:
Literally. You can grind the rock of most planets, to get a chance for...stuff. It depends on the planet. Does consume quite a bit of power, but can really help you out.

For Tungstate, you can centrifuge previously macerated moon dust, macerate end stone with an HV+ macerator and then centrifuge it, or centrifuge endergoo (found all over the end, does not give back buckets).

If you want to make your rocket, you need to get at least a little before you can get to a planet with the ore. However, keep in mind that you'll need an EBF with TPV Coils to smelt Tungstensteel Dust into the ingot!

[note]Do one of the tasks.[/note]

213.name: Tier 5 (IV)
213.desc:
IV-Level. You will need Tungstensteel for machines. Suggested planets and moons to visit: Mars, Phobos, Deimos.

Make sure to get a set of TPV coils for your EBF.

You should've learned "how to GregTech" by now. If not, go clean up the machine craters in your base...

Many multiblock machines unlock at this tier. Make sure you have an Alloy Blast Smelter ready to go and check the Multiblock Madness tab.

214.name: A Reward
214.desc:
You have really come a long way to get here! However, getting to the moon is just the start of your journey, as you'll have access to even more powerful and awesome stuff. You have proven yourself worthy, and mastered the challenges to get here. Congratulations!

Pick one of the reward quests below.

215.name: On Your Way to Asteroids and Other Rocket Tier 3 DIMs
215.desc: There's some good stuff in the asteroids, so you should stop by. Especially for the naquadah.

217.name: Reward choice: Personal Dimension
217.desc:
Tired of mobs in your base? Want to build somewhere... different? Probably in a personal dimension? Then this is your chance. But choose wisely; You can only pick one option.

When you first activate the portal, you can configure the world to your liking: it can be a void world, or any superflat world as long as it only consists of blocks such as grass, stone, cobblestone, bedrock, etc.
Be careful! Once you configure the world, you can only change the cosmetic settings such as sky color by shift-right-clicking the portal.
Editing world generation settings after the world is created is only possible if a server administrator unlocks it for you via the /pspace allow-worldgen-change DIMID command.

[note]PS: The Last Millenium is also a void dimension you could use.[/note]

If you're used to the buggy garden/void dimensions from UtilityWorlds, this is now a new mod that should resolve most of the previously known issues with blood magic, etc.

218.name: Reward Choice: Loot
218.desc:
Don't need a personal dimension, eh? Well, then you'll probably be happy with some extra loot. Choose wisely; you can only pick one.

[note]PS: The loot is lame, don't pick this one.[/note]

219.name: Almost Unbreakable
219.desc: Probably not required any more, but you've made so many walls already. So why not get the best ones?

220.name: Protect Your Base: Tier 5
220.desc: Tough materials, for tough weapons. Railguns, and lasers, and... stuff. I assume you want a lot of those soon.

221.name: Turret Base Tier 5
221.desc: The last one, I promise. Used for the most powerful turrets available. You wonder if they might help you take out some more... "Dangerous" enemies?

222.name: PewPew!
222.desc: Requires no ammo, but still does a ton of damage. All you need to do is feed it power, a lot of power.

223.name: Railguns, What Else
223.desc: Massive power, and a wonderful sound when they fire. I recommend to use upgrades to increase range and damage.

224.name: Witchy (Wo)man
224.desc:
If you've made the spinning wheel and done the research, you can make these.

The vis discount is very useful both to craft more efficiently and to ease your wand progression. Maybe you should look around for more discounts?

225.name: Searching for Answers
225.desc:
Last night you had a dream. A dimension, so strange and different, almost... magical? Like the saplings you found. You decided to go there and look for answers.

First, place water in a 2x2 grid, with 12 flowers surrounding it. Then toss in a portal crystal to open it up. And no, a diamond is not enough.

You should be able to use any vanilla flower.

226.name: See the Unseen
226.desc: Everything is made of 6 primal aspects. Those aspects can be combined to form compound aspects, but in the end, there are only 6, kinda like the infused shards you found. You noticed that those shards start to vibrate when you bring them next to something that is made of the respective aspect. Maybe you can create a device to actually "scan" items, and reveal their compounds? This could be very useful later on.

227.name: A Table to Work On
227.desc:
Now you need something on which to craft magical items. It probably needs to be a little magical itself. Let's see what the book says.

I should probably take this time to read all the stuff in the Thaumonomicon I don't know about while I'm at it, instead of posting on Discord and looking stupid.

228.name: Tier 2 Sigils
228.desc: With Reinforced Slates, you can make new sigils.

229.name: Infused Stones
229.desc:
This whole place here is filled with magic. No matter where you look at, it's just everywhere. You wonder what happens to regular stone if it's exposed to large amounts of magic for such a long time. Maybe it's time to go mining?

There are 3 veins. There's amber and cinnabar in each. They aren't super useful, but make sure to keep hold of some at least.

Even when you find these infused stones, they won't drop the infused shards you desperately need. Rather, you have to process the raw ores themselves. The easiest way to do that is to throw these raw ores in your Forge Hammer.

Later when you have a Sifter, you can sift purified ores for more shards. An autoclave can also turn shard dust into a shard. Maybe there's a magical way to do the same thing?

230.name: Your First Wand
230.desc:
You're sure that this place will give you all the answers you're looking for! To continue, you need something to handle the magic energy called "vis". Guess it's time to craft your first wand.

If you haven't started on defeating the TF bosses yet, you'll need to do so to proceed. All wands require some kind of TF boss drop, with better wands needing later boss's drops. Once you've defeated them once, you can buy a spawn egg (only usable in the TF) or use the trophy+salis mundus to make more of the drop.

You should look for wand recipes in NEI. Not all of them are correct in the Thaumonomicon.

231.name: Tome of Knowledge
231.desc: All the things you've discovered so far, you need to write them down. But you're lazy, and you have a wand, so why not try a magical way to "write down" your research? You wonder if you could enchant a book with your wand, so that it will keep track of your progress... You should try that on a bookshelf!

232.name: Improving Your Wand
232.desc: Your wand does a great job in storing and releasing vis as you please. However, those iron caps are... weak. If you don't pay enough attention to it, it might hurt the node. They also make crafting harder. Maybe you should try to improve it with caps made out of a more valuable material. Like... gold, perhaps?

233.name: Getting Shards
233.desc: However you obtained them, you've collected a variety of Infused Shards. Now on how to use them...

234.name: A "Greater" Wand
234.desc: While doing all your research and mining of these infused stones, you had an idea how to use the wood of a Greatwood tree to make a new wand. Actually, it turned out to be pretty simple, but you lacked one important thing back then: vis! The vis makes sure the wood doesn't lose its magical conductivity while forming the rod.

235.name: Better Screws
235.desc: Unfortunately, your aluminium screws don't work with the greatwood rod. Somehow, the wood seems to repel the material. Luckily, you already have a blast furnace, so you can get a better material than aluminium.

236.name: A More Powerful "Scale"
236.desc: The naga scales are weak, they can only hold a very limited amount of vis. You need a better, more powerful component to store vis in your wand. You've heard rumors about an evil wizard in the Twilight Forest. Maybe he has... something... that can be used?

237.name: "Stores 50 Vis" Wand
237.desc:
With gold caps you avoid the downsides of iron. So best to upgrade to a Gold Banded Greatwood Wand!

You could also consider making your Greatwood Wand with enchanted cloth caps instead. That gives you an extra 5% discount!

With this Greatwood Wand you are more powerful than ever!

[note]You only need one of the tasks to complete the quest.[/note]

238.name: Fuuuuuu...(sion)!
238.desc: Your arcane table does a great job with infusing primal vis into items while crafting. However, you feel that there must be more. What if you could infuse items with all kinds of magic aspects? The possibilities are endless...

239.name: Yet Another Brick in the Wall...
239.desc:
A magical one this time. You're on your way to infusion which needs 8 of these. Another 4 will be needed for the alchemical furnace.

Yes, you need an MV PLE, deal with it.

240.name: Essentia Cooking
240.desc:
In order to get essentia, you need to dissolve items. Obviously, you only want to dissolve those items you don't really need, and only those with essentia you need to craft things.

Make sure you keep your furnace as empty as possible. If it fills up with essentia, it can cause a large TPS drop.

241.name: Not For Soup
241.desc:
At least not for edible soup, but for essentia slurry. Get yourself a cauldron and give it a solid whack with your wand. Place some netherrack 2 blocks under the crucible and set it on fire with some flint and steel. Lastly you need to fill it with water. This one works with pipes, so you can automate that.

Now you can do crucible recipes!

Just don't put in too much or, well, Bad Things will happen.

242.name: Balanced Shards sdrahS decnalaB
242.desc: By combining all six primal aspects in a crucible, you are able to make balanced shards. They can be ground up into "Salis Mundus", an absolutely crucial thing to have, trust me!

243.name: Crystal Clusters
243.desc: You, as a Thaumaturge, like shiny things. And what could be more shiny than a cluster made of those shards? You totally should get one of each.

244.name: The Choice: Ignis
244.desc: In exchange for 6 clusters, I will give you this node. May it serve you well. Oh, don't place it too close to another node, or it might start to drain vis...

245.name: The Choice...
245.desc: By now, you should've collected a few (or a lot) of locations for aura nodes to get vis from. You might also have noticed that you're probably low on a certain aspect, like ordo or ignis. Well, good news. In exchange for some crystal clusters, I will give you one of my old nodes. They're all pale, but they'll still help you out. You may only choose one, so think twice before you make your decision.

246.name: The Choice: Terra
246.desc: In exchange for 6 clusters, I will give you this node. May it serve you well. Oh, don't place it too close to another node, or it might start to drain vis...

247.name: The Choice: Perditio
247.desc: In exchange for 6 clusters, I will give you this node. May it serve you well. Oh, don't place it too close to another node, or it might start to drain vis...

0.name: Your First Night
0.desc:
Hello, friend, and welcome to GTNH!

Don't get used to things being nice and comfy, because in about ten minutes the sun will set, and unlike Vanilla Minecraft, there's no light whatsoever at night but plenty of Infernal mobs coming to get you. So your first task is to find food and shelter for the night.

But by the end of this quest tab you will have a bed to make things easier.

You don't need to keep this book. Even if you don't have the item, you can press Tilde (~) to view it at any time...unless you decided to change the keybind.

If you need any extra help or just want to hang out, feel free to visit our Discord server at [url]https://discord.gg/gtnh[/url]. And yes, the links are clickable! On the Discord, select your tech tier in #role-select - it will help people to tailor their advice to your situation.

Many GTNH topics are explained in our Wiki at [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Main_Page[/url]. It does not have the same extent and structure as the Questbook, but some things are much better with pictures and tables! The wiki also has tips on extra topics like what additional mods might work with the pack, how GTNH can be installed for modern Java versions or how to set up a server.

Additionally, there is a spreadsheet at [url]https://www.gtnewhorizons.com/spreadsheet[/url] for the info you would want a spreadsheet for.

If you play through a launcher that relies on [warn]CurseForge[/warn], be aware that it doesn't always work properly. It doesn't play well with all the custom versions of mods that this pack has. So if you have any problems, first switch to another launcher like MultiMC, Prism, or Technic. The wiki has instructions there as well. The latest version of the pack is available on the Discord channel from #announcements or with the !download command.

Hopefully you didn't get munched by a monster while reading this, good luck!

1.name: Where's the Flint?
1.desc:
As you might have noticed when collecting gravel, no Flint dropped for you.

Well, there is a... recipe for that. Look up Flint in Not Enough Items (NEI) and craft some!

The search field of NEI is at the bottom of the screen when you open your inventory. The results are on the right side.

2.name: Sticks 'n Stones
2.desc:
This book has a built-in reading light, lucky you! You can read about quests while you're waiting for the night to be over, but for now, you may want to get the basics done. If it isn't dark already, go out and collect some gravel and wood.

Keep in mind that you need food, so look for Pam's Harvestcraft gardens. Save some of the drops from them for seeds. Alternatively, right-click the gardens with an empty hand to get the garden item itself. If you place it somewhere else, it will spread into more of the same garden up to a limit within that area.

Make sure to read the info-quests linked to the first quest, they have important info!

3.name: Storage For Days
3.desc:
Now that you've gotten your tools and are about to go collect a bunch of stuff, it's time to make some storage for it.
Take some of your wood, and craft a chest. You will need 4 wood, 4 planks, and 1 flint.

4.name: Main Quests and Secondary Quests
4.desc:
Now that you've made it this far, you might notice how the two new quests look different.

The quest with the jagged edges is a main quest - it is linked to other main quests by a thick line. You must complete main quests to unlock the next tier and to progress in general. If you can't progress, go back to previous tiers and look for uncompleted main quests.

The other quest is an optional quest, and is not needed to unlock new tiers. However, optional quests often introduce you to useful items or processes.

What are the coins for? You can use them to purchase things in the "Coins, Coins, Coins" questbook tab - you have to scroll to the bottom once it unlocks!

In case you started in a bad biome, here's enough coins to purchase some saplings.

5.name: Tools
5.desc:
Now that you've got a shelter and a crafting table, you should make some tools, unless you'd like to continue punching trees with your bare hands.

You should probably make the axe first so you can get wood for the rest more easily.

6.name: You Reap...
6.desc:
You won't always get food rewards from the quests, so it might be a good idea to take care of that problem.

As you've crafted a hoe already, go and find some water nearby to make a field. With this modpack you can only make farmland within range of water. Let's trade some sticks for a carrot, so you can start your own farm.

To get other wheat, use a hoe on grass away from water. You'll get Witchery seeds too - save those for later.

Start planting a Cotton field because it's a key early-game resource.

You can also get seeds from villages, and grow the seeds from the Pam's garden food.

7.name: ...What You Sow
7.desc:
If, by chance, you did read the previous quest and planted the carrots, then you're fine.

Meanwhile, you should turn some more fruits and vegetables into seeds, and increase the size of your farm to get a steady supply of food.

8.name: Get That Stone
8.desc:
Look at you, with your shiny wooden pickaxe!

Time to mine some cobblestone. Yay! You can even improve your dirt hut!

9.name: Fire, Fire, Grandpa!
9.desc: Time to burn some stuff, isn't it? You can't make charcoal in it, but you sure will find some usage for it soon™.

10.name: Rest in Pieces
10.desc: It's GLOPPing time! Go kill some pigs to gather meat.

11.name: Monster Hunter
11.desc:
It's about time to craft something more useful: A sword!

Now that you have a sword, you will get credit for monster kills in the Kill All the Things tab. Watch out, monsters in this world will chew you up and spit you out!

You can find additional weapon quests on the ...Without Dying tab.

12.name: Fluffy and Red
12.desc: Epic quest: Kill some sheep! (And try to get some wool, for a bed, obviously...)

13.name: SO...TIRED...MUST...SLEEP...
13.desc:
Sleepy time, and the end of the tutorial.

Congratulations! You've built your first hideout, and you should have a steady supply of food by now.

See you again in Tier 0!

[note](Any color bed will complete this quest)[/note]

14.name: What's That...?
14.desc:
You've seen a lot of trees, but there are a few that look really special. You are sure that these trees are much more than just wood to build your home with or used to get charcoal. You're sure that these will be Very Important soon™.

The best place to find them is in a Magical Forest, but if you can't find one, you can try a Garden or Cherry Wood Grove. Or just randomly everywhere.

If you have trouble finding them, you can go to the Coins tab and buy them.

15.name: Ready, Set, Go!
15.desc: You probably should have a home by now. A steady supply of food is a fundamental requirement at all times. Take this journal to keep track of the things you've eaten. You will have to travel far in this tier, better be prepared!

16.name: Better Tank... Iron Tank
16.desc:
Storing more than 16 buckets of fluid in one tank? Sure! Just make an iron tank or upgrade the Buildcraft tank.

Diamond tanks carry up to 64 buckets and obsidian ones are blast proof. There's even more past that, but you should probably use GT tanks or large cells instead.

17.name: Sand: The Gathering
17.desc: In a strange vision you saw a big structure which would allow you to make charcoal. Finally some torches! According to your calculations, you will need a lot of sand for that.

18.name: Basic Processing
18.desc: Most things are unavailable at the moment, so let's begin with something simple. Take some of your cobblestone and smelt it into regular stone. Since this modpack is evil, you'll need to use wood for that. How much fun is that?!

19.name: Macerator v0.1 Alpha
19.desc:
This fancy little device works (almost...) like a macerator. Some people say it's even better, since it does not require power, yay! You only need to craft one for this quest, but you probably should make some more if you can...

[note]The mortar lets you make flint much, much more efficiently - you only need one gravel instead of three. Many tools and stations you'll make in the future will also open up better crafting recipes for things you can already make as well. If you need something but are hard up on resources, use NEI to check the recipes for that item - you may have options you didn't before.[/note]

20.name: Gravel: The Gathering
20.desc: You think it would be a good idea to collect more gravel in order to provide yourself with flint for the journey ahead. Try searching near water.

21.name: Clay: The Gathering
21.desc: While bored, you thought that it might be a good idea to take up pottery. You should go and find some clay, the riverbanks should have some.

22.name: Finite Water!?
22.desc:
Finite water is a problem, that's for sure. Last night, you had an idea. If you can drink cacti, you should also be able to make water out of 8 cacti and use that for most recipes that require water. How cool is that? So what about another trade? 10 wood for... 3 cacti so you can set up a farm. Deal?

To automate the farm, place a string next to the cactus and a sand on top of the string. Now when the cactus grows, it will automatically break! You can stack this horizontally and vertically to make a very efficient cactus farm. Use water to redirect the cactus to a convenient pickup location or to above a hopper.

First player to make a base running off of only cactus water gets a cape!

23.name: Something to Carry Liquids
23.desc:
You tried to move water with your bare hands, which didn't work at all. Luckily, you remember how to make a bucket out of clay dust.

Put it in a mortar to get small clay dust which can be crafted to clay dust.

24.name: Getting (Char)coal
24.desc:
Now you can make the bricks for your first multiblock structure. 104 bricks to be exact. Good that you just collected a lot of resources! What a coincidence...

The bricks you'll need are somewhat special. Make sure to craft the correct ones. With that done, let's build a coke oven to get some charcoal or coal coke! It may be useful to note that the oven doesn't automatically output its fluid contents. Faucets and pumps can help to change that.

26.name: Forming Press v0.1
26.desc:
As you may have noticed, you cannot turn clay into bricks directly. Well, to be fair, it wasn't a good recipe anyway. You'll probably end up with something bread-shaped.

In order to make bricks, you need a form. Get yourself a knife by putting flint above a stick on the crafting grid. With a knife, you can cut a brick sized piece out and craft a blank pattern. It's as easy as that.

27.name: Finally, Some Powerful Fuel
27.desc: Now that you have a coke oven, you can start producing charcoal. The more, the better. Creosote is produced at the same time, and the Coke Oven will stop when it's full of either one, which means you can't just leave the Creosote there. Refer to the Creosote quest from before to learn what to do with it.

28.name: Copper Sky, Iron Curtain
28.desc: With your newly acquired fuel source, you are now ready to process more advanced materials than stone or clay; Iron or Copper, for example. Just in case you don't know, they spawn in rather large veins. Use the following quests to locate the most important veins.

29.name: Another Fuel Source
29.desc:
Lignite and regular coal are the fuel sources you'll definitely need. The latter can be turned into coal coke, which is a superior fuel source to smelt your first large batch of ore.

Lignite you can toss in a boiler once you have one.

30.name: Copper
30.desc: Copper ingots can be created by smelting copper, chalcopyrite, or malachite ore.

31.name: Tin
31.desc:
Tin ingots can be created by smelting Tin, Cassiterite, or Cassiterite Sand ore. Tin and Cassiterite ores are only found at high altitudes, but Cassiterite Sand ore is plentiful near the surface.

If you get lucky and find a high altitude Tin/Cassiterite vein, save one of the Cassiterite ore to find a Mica vein later on using the ore finder wand.

32.name: Book Parts
32.desc: After you found a way to move water around, you had another brilliant idea: Paper! Paper is no longer made with sugarcane, you'll need wood pulp instead. You'll need a lot of wood to craft wood pulp, so better grab your axe and go chop some trees.

33.name: Tinker-Time
33.desc:
While using your wooden tools is fun in terms of killing pigs and cows, they are not long-lasting and can't be repaired. Plus Infernals laugh at your attempts to kill them. You discovered how to craft tools that can be repaired and upgraded, but for that you definitely need a more advanced crafting technique.

[note]If you don't have access to oak wood for creating your Part Builder, you can shapelessly craft yours into the oak variant for purposes of completing this quest[/note]

34.name: Zinc Gravel
34.desc:
You can find it in chunks of gravel on the surface.

Use as little as possible and save the rest for when you get a macerator in Steam age, you'll get twice as much.

If you can't find any, you can buy them in the Coins tab.

If you get enough and fully process it, you can also get Gallium for Gallium Arsenide in LV age.

35.name: Making Bronze
35.desc: Now that you've found copper and tin, you should make yourself some bronze. Use your macer... mortar to grind up some copper and tin ingots, and mix them together!

36.name: Important Tools
36.desc:
In order to craft even basic machines, you'll need new tools. Most of them can be replaced by machines later. For now, you should become familiar with each of their recipes, as you will need a lot of these tools, starting today.

Your new tools can also be used to make Railcraft water tanks, a very useful multiblock considering the finite water feature.

There are also electric versions of the tools, you can upgrade later if you want. Also, there are many versions of the tools, they generally follow the same rules as tinkers parts in terms of how good they are, similarly to turbines. You can look at the spreadsheet for more info on GT tool stats.

For this quest, you can use whatever materials you want. The gem version of the saw is crafted differently to metal versions. Higher tier tools need steel or tungstensteel rods instead of sticks.

[note]If you want to know the recipe for a tool using your specific material, be sure to take advantage of the magnifying glass icon at the top of the tool list so you can search for it. (Try out Iron for these quests!)[/note]

37.name: Getting Iron
37.desc:
Iron can be found in multiple veins, and there are many types of ores that will smelt down into iron ingots such as magnetite, brown / yellow limonite, banded iron, roasted iron, and granitic / basaltic mineral sand.

Iron-Rich Veins:
Magnetite veins (Y 60-180)
Chalcopyrite veins (Y 5-60) 
Gold veins (Y 30-60)
Limonite veins (Y 10-40)

[note]You can use NEI to see information for all of the ore veins in the game![/note]

38.name: Making Better Tools
38.desc:
With zinc and copper, you can create a material with a higher melting temperature - Brass - which can be used to cast better tools.

For that, you need a smeltery. See the Multiblock Goals tab for details. You can also grab some extra blocks from some villages, but they don't count towards the quest.

Alternatively, there are also one-time-use clay casts available.

You can then use your fancy bronze to make better tools with a higher mining level.

Remember, you need to use the pickaxe a bit to gather mining XP if you want the full mining level on your bronze pickaxe. Certain tool upgrades can give that directly if you have an upgrade slot available.

39.name: Better Storage
39.desc: While standard chests are good for your basic items and goods, they are not ideal for storing a large amount of ore. You should build yourself some barrels in order to keep things organized.

40.name: You Shall Proceed
40.desc:
As you've gathered and crafted all materials and tools you need for your basic steam machinery, you may now proceed to the next tier. Keep in mind that you'll need mountains of resources in order to craft even the simplest things, so you probably should stock up on all ores in this tier. But that's up to you.

As a special reward, you get a pair of Holo Glasses to look inside your inventories. Place it into the mask slot, and use the keybind to turn it on.

[note]Tip: If you double-click the NEI search field the resulting highlighted search also applies to what you can see with your Holo Glasses![/note]

41.name: Don't Burn Your Fingers!
41.desc:
You have decided it's time to make some tasty pizza, but your fingers got burned badly. Reading an ancient cookbook you discovered a pair of magical items that would allow you to carry anything hot, even lava in a bucket.

The gloves have to be placed in the Baubles ring slots. Order does not matter.

For the protection effect, the hot item has to be in your active slot. I wonder if there is some way to carry more than a few buckets at a time?

42.name: Cow Tipper
42.desc: Before you had to kill some pigs and sheep to complete quests. Now it's time to kill some cows and hopefully get some leather. If you are lucky and get a cow trophy you may never need to kill another cow in the future.

43.name: Cotton, Cotton and More Cotton
43.desc: You've found out that cotton is very useful, it can be used to make string, wool, and some other useful items too. Make a small cotton plantation to get tons of it.

44.name: Welcome to Tier 0.5, Steam!
44.desc:
Welcome, and well done so far! This tier is all about your first machines, getting more stuff from your ores and making bronze ingots in a more efficient manner.

For water, check out the Water Tank multiblock in the "Water Dilemma" quest. (Multiblock Goals tab)

To connect up your boilers, be sure to provide water from the bottom, they'll accept only water from this side, so no risk of melting your pipe. Wooden pipes are fine for this. Make sure you never run out of water - [warn]adding water to a dry hot boiler will cause it to explode![/warn]

Steam will come out from the sides. Remember, steam is hot! It will destroy wooden pipes. And it can burn you if you walk on bronze pipes full of steam. Cover them with wooden planks or paper. Or just don't walk on/next to them.

And no, you can't automate it. Make an electric one if you want to do that.

45.name: Multiblock Tanks... Railcraft Tanks
45.desc:
576 buckets of fluid, that should be enough to store all the creosote you have accumulated thus far. If that isn't enough you can make this tank bigger. The smallest tanks are 3x4x3 while the biggest are 9x8x9.

Fluid will auto-output without any pumps if you place a valve on its bottom side at the center. The output rate is 1000 L/t so twice as fast as a GT HV Pump Cover. You can color the tank with Buildcraft paintbrushes and the GT spray can.

If you still use Railcraft tanks in later tiers, you boost the output rate by using pumps.

46.name: More Advanced Alloys
46.desc:
Your smeltery does a great job mixing metals into alloys, but it's not very efficient, and limited in its complexity. You need something new, so why not put your steam to some use? The Alloy Smelter can make bronze for you directly, using ingots, dusts, or even nuggets. You can make a nugget mold as well to turn ingots into nuggets if you have steel.

It might also come in handy when processing raw rubber. You should keep an eye on recipes to see when machines offer better options to make items...

[note]NOTE: Like all steam machines, the output port is a steam outlet when recipes complete. It must not be blocked. Also, steam is hot, remember? Change the output side by whacking the machine with a wrench. The 3x3 grid will show you which side the output will move to when you whack it.[/note]

You can empty the machine by putting a hopper beneath it.

[warn]WARNING: The Alloy Smelter recipes take a lot of steam.[/warn] Do them one by one until you understand how fast you use steam, and how much you will need buffered. Large pipes are a decent "poor man's" buffer, and the boiler can hold quite a bit of steam, as well as the machine itself. Once you get a little further along, make a larger steam buffer with an iron or steel railcraft tank.

47.name: Upgrade: High Speed Alloys
47.desc:
Like most machines, there is a more advanced version of the alloy smelter. It uses the same total amount of steam as its predecessor but does the job twice as fast.

Keep in mind that it uses quite a lot of steam, so you better have enough of it in boilers, pipes, or tanks.

48.name: A Very Important Alloy
48.desc:
The most important alloy (until you reach a higher tier) is undoubtedly red alloy. Fortunately, you have an alloy smelter, so grab some redstone and combine it with copper to get yourself a handful of that.

Don't confuse your bronze with your copper or else you'll be scratching your head.

49.name: The Hell is That?
49.desc:
In order to get more machines, you'll need pistons. But not those amateurish ones, you need special ones, and I shall guide you in the making of them.

PS: Infernals can drop them, though you won't get credit for the quest that way.

50.name: Macerator v1.0!
50.desc:
Finally you are able to make a device that will replace your mortar. However, the "no-durability" feature of this device comes with a cost: You need 2 diamonds for the grinding heads.

The macerator will double your ore output. Put ores through it instead of smelting directly.

NOTE: Remember, don't block the output port and don't stand behind it.

51.name: Extracting Stuff
51.desc: As you'll need rubber later to make cables from wires, you should get yourself a steam extractor. You can also use this machine to get more dyes from flowers, or process different fruits into juice.

52.name: A Clear View
52.desc:
Are you still living in your dirt/cobble hut? How about letting in some sunlight? Grind up some sand and flint and craft some glass dust. Put it in your smeltery and pour it into a casting basin to make clear glass.

[note]Hint: In order to use it for recipes, you have to chisel it.[/note]

[note]PS: The macerator no longer gives tiny piles, just uncraft a normal pile to get tiny ones. You'll appreciate this once you have a mixer in LV.[/note]

53.name: Hammer time v2
53.desc: Using 6 ingots to craft a hammer seems pretty wasteful, as this tool has durability. How nice would it be to have a machine that does the same but without having durability and using three ingots to make two plates? Guess what, there is one! And you should totally get one.

54.name: Compressing Stuff
54.desc: This rather simple device does a very important job: compression. Its main function is to press 9 ingots into a block, but it's also useful for a variety of recipes, for example making firm tofu.

55.name: Upgrade: A Better Compressor
55.desc: An updated version of the steam compressor. Uses the same total amount of steam as its predecessor but does the job twice as fast.

56.name: Upgrade: Forge Hammer
56.desc: An updated version of the steam forge hammer. Uses the same total amount of steam as its predecessor but does the job twice as fast.

57.name: Upgrade: Extractor
57.desc:
An updated version of the steam extractor. Uses the same total amount of steam as its predecessor but does the job twice as fast.

You probably want to craft this, as the extractor does also extract your nerves...

58.name: Upgrade: Better Macerator
58.desc: An updated version of the steam macerator. Uses the same total amount of steam as its predecessor but does the job twice as fast.

59.name: Steam Alternatives
59.desc:
There is more than one way to get steam for your machines. This completely optional questline shows you some alternative methods for producing steam.

For the ones that need glass, you'll need to progress a few more quests to see how to do so, though you can also make it now if you know how to use NEI to find the recipe.

60.name: Upgrade: Coal Boiler
60.desc: This is basically just an upgrade to your current coal boiler, however it produces more than twice as much steam. Something you should consider building if you're planning to get some of the upgraded machines.

61.name: Another Use for Java, I Mean Lava
61.desc:
Now that you have Steel, you can craft a sturdy enough Boiler to handle Lava. Steam is only outputted in the back, and you require Water input on the side and Lava input on the top. But as an added benefit, this Boiler will reward you with Obsidian!

Did you know? Over 3 billion machines run on Lava!

62.name: The Power of the Sun
62.desc:
The sun has quite a bit of power, so why not use it to produce Steam?

Fill it with Water on the bottom side to produce Steam. Unlike the other Boilers, this only outputs Steam on the output port side.

You need Silver from small ores to craft Solar Boilers. Later in LV you can get much more in the Twilight Forest.

Hint: The Simple Solar Boiler calcifies and becomes less efficient over time, dropping down to a minimum of 40L/s. Later on you can consider using Distilled Water to avoid calcification.

63.name: Using Steam to Cook Things
63.desc: A rather slow process, but it's more efficient than using a regular furnace.

64.name: Upgrade: Better Furnace
64.desc: An updated version of the steam furnace. Uses the same total amount of steam as its predecessor but does the job twice as fast.

65.name: Blastoff!
65.desc: It's about time to make some steel. Look in the Multiblock Goals quest tab and build a Bricked Blast Furnace. Return here once you've made at least 8 steel ingots.

66.name: Time For Some Logic
66.desc: Good news! You are now able to advance to the next tier! Bad news: You still have to craft a lot of things before you can begin. Fear not mortal, I shall guide you!

67.name: Wired, Weird?!
67.desc: Two of the very basic materials for machines are wires and cables. For cables you can either use a rubber sheet in the crafting table or use rubber bars in the alloy smelter. Not very efficient, but there will be a better way later.

68.name: Your First Electronic Circuit
68.desc: Steam machines are quite stupid, they just do one thing over and over again. As you advance into the electrical age, you've decided that you want the machines to have more features, like automatic output in order to create some automation. For that, you need electronic circuits.

69.name: Wire-Wrap
69.desc: Placing wires without insulation seems to be a bad idea. Now that you have an extractor to extract raw rubber out of sticky resin and an alloy smelter to further process it using sulfur, you should work on getting some insulating rubber bars.

70.name: Steam-a-licious!
70.desc:
You've almost made it. One last crafting step is required in order to advance to Tier 1. Keep going!

This Steam Turbine generates electricity from Steam. The power source you've been using is the same, but from this point on you will generate EU, and bring it to your machines via cables, instead of bringing the Steam directly to them. Luckily, electric machines don't need an exhaust port, and they are considerably more efficient than Bronze machines.

At a later point, you will most likely switch from Steam to another fuel, though.

71.name: Basic Crafting: Motors
71.desc: Motors are little devices that can turn electrical power into rotational energy. Very useful for all kind of machines, and not that difficult to craft. The pattern is always the same, only the materials change with tiers. For now you can use redstone to magnetize your iron rod. Later on you will build an Electromagnetic Polarizer.

72.name: Basic Crafting: Rotors
72.desc: Rotors are also crafted using an identical pattern with different materials. A bit annoying to craft at the moment, but you don't need a lot of them anyway.

73.name: Basic Crafting: Machine Hulls
73.desc:
All machines need a hull to keep their private parts together. Good news is: They are crafted in the same way for every tier, only the materials change.

As noted on the tooltip, you can also use a machine hull to pass EU through, at 1A. This can be useful for a 1A diode on Thermos servers, splitting cables from 4A->1A or in a cleanroom later. You can also pass items or fluids through it.

74.name: You Are Now Prepared, Hopefully!
74.desc:
No matter where you got this smeltery from, you have one now. Time for some casting!

Buy the zinc gravel in the Coins Coins coins tab if you can't find it.

Note: The book says a 3x3 base, but you can make a smeltery with a smaller base. Taller/wider smelteries can do more items at one time, conserving fuel.

[note]The smeltery does not double ores in GTNH. Later machines will help you there.[/note]

75.name: The Water Dilemma
75.desc:
Finite water is a problem. But fear not, there are solutions! I shall guide you to a steady supply of water. The Railcraft water siding tanks provides enough water to last the early game.

The humidity of a biome is very important and will increase the base fill rate. If it is raining, the tank will fill much quicker. One block of the water siding tank must see sky for the maximum output. The best part is that you don't need a pump to extract water. Tanks and pipes placed next to it will be autofilled by the water siding tank.

76.name: Time to Get Some Steel
76.desc:
You need steel? Uhh... fine. Let's get busy...

The Bricked Blast Furnace (BBF) can share walls with other ones, just like most GT multiblocks. How will this affect the air quality though...

If you are short on Stone Dust, you can get more by macerating cobblestone.

If you spawn near a biome with Hardened Clay you can macerate it to get the clay dust you need. You can also use brick blocks from Villages to get brick dust in the macerator.

Crafting the Worktable from Forestry can make it easier as you can use all the buckets for crafting in one click.

77.name: EBF-Time
77.desc:
In order to get aluminium ingots, you need an Electric Blast Furnace. It's another very important step towards victory, so let's get started.

Getting the Mica for the heating coils might be a little tricky, but you can use a substitute for the first tier of coils.
You need 2 LV hatches with 4A of 32V total, or 3 LV hatches with MOOOORE power if the multiblock is not fully repaired.
In order to not scrape by for Nickel, check out the quest Source of Nickel in the Novice Thaumaturgy tab.

A few recipes produce CO2/SO2 as a fluid. This fluid can be collected by placing a fluid output hatch on the top layer of the EBF. You could also enable fluid voiding in the EBF controller block GUI. For recipes with such a C02/S02 output you must do one or the other or the EBF will not start.
The amount of fluid recovered depends on the tier of the muffler hatch.

Bart's Platinum Series recipes output fluids on the BOTTOM and are NOT affected by the muffler hatch tier, so if you want to do Platinum Stuff later, remember that.

Before the launch, don't forget to fix maintenance issues and put a programmed circuit if necessary.

If you want a really fancy EBF, you can add more input fluid hatches and store different fluids in each one.

The EBF also has a heat adjustment of 100K for each power tier above or below MV. You can only make LV energy hatches right now, but installing two LV energy hatches will overclock the EBF to MV power, eliminating the 100K penalty and setting the initial heat to 1801K.

As you upgrade your EBF over the course of the game, this heat adjustment will increase. Two MV energy hatches (overclocking to HV) will add +100K, two HV energy hatches (overclocking to EV) will give +200K, and so on. Coils only set the base value. This adjustment does not apply to the Volcanus.

If the pollution gets really bad, check in GT++ Multiblocks for a pollution scrubber, or upgrade your Muffler.

78.name: More Heat - Kanthal Coil Upgrade
78.desc:
