10 can be set per device, so get creative! ...Unless you want to go to other Dims, which it can't do. But hey, faster travel around your base!

332.name: Fire Burn and...
332.desc:
In order to get started with your basic witchery needs, you need a Witches Oven.

The Witches Oven is used to get the most basic ingredients for your recipes - Fumes.

The oven is able to capture the by-products produced in the cooking process. Activating the oven to show the interface screen, you will see an additional slot to the right of the fuel slot. Clay jars must be placed in this slot. If there are clay jars present when something is cooked, there is a chance a by-product will be captured in one of the jars. These by-products are important ingredients in other recipes. If there are no clay jars in the oven when something is cooked, no by-products will be produced.

Upgrading an Oven

An oven can be upgraded by placing either a Fume Funnel or a Filtered Fume Funnel to the left and right of it. Each Fume Funnel will increase the cooking speed by an additional 10 percent (over a furnace) and increase the chance of a by-product being produced by an additional 25 percent for a Fume Funnel or 30 percent for a Filtered Fume Funnel.

A fully upgraded oven will therefore cook at 130 percent speed (compared to a furnace) and have a 90 percent chance of producing a by-product.

333.name: Offerings
333.desc:
For all your offering needs. And of course, it looks fancy!

An altar is created by placing six altar blocks in a 2x3 pattern or by placing nine altar blocks in a 3x3 pattern. You will know it's correct when a red cloth appears over the top of the altar.

Right-clicking on it will bring up a screen showing how much power the altar has collected, how much it can collect and its current recharge rate. It is important to check that there is sufficient available power to perform a given rite or use a distillery, or certain kettle or cauldron recipes.

The maximum amount of power available is determined by how many natural items are in the vicinity of the altar: dirt, grass, trees, plants, etc. all contribute; stone and sand do not. The important thing is to have a diverse mix of different plants, trees and crops (plants from other mods are supported to an extent as well).

Certain objects (or foci) placed on the altar boost its focusing capabilities. This can increase the rate at which the altar replenishes focus, the focus capacity, and the focus range. Only one of each type of item (skull, chalice, etc.) has an effect. There may also be other less savory foci that dark magic practitioners could make use of. 

 [note]P.S. The 3x3 Altar doesn't provide any practical benefits over its 3x2 counterpart, however enjoy the increased ability to center it within your builds.[/note]

334.name: ...Cauldron Bubble
334.desc:
The second thing you'll need is a cauldron. It is used to craft most of the basic items, like mutandis. It requires anointing paste, which you will need an MV mixer to make. It is made by placing a regular Cauldron in the world, and then using Anointing Paste on it.

To brew a potion, cast a ritual or create an item, the cauldron must first be filled with water. This can be achieved by using three buckets of water on it, pumping water into it using pipes, or by using a Dispenser containing a Brew of Endless Water.

Once a cauldron is full of water, it must boil. Lighting a fire beneath the cauldron is the only way for this to happen. Using a netherrack block will guarantee the fire does not go out, although if it does go out, it may be set to boil again without any consequences (unlike brewing in a Kettle!).

When the water is boiling, ingredients may be added to the Cauldron (one after another), to create the desired effect. The book, Witches' Brews gives a complete overview of all ingredients and what they can do when added to a brew.

335.name: Don't Touch the Needle!
335.desc: While having not many recipes by itself, the few it has are rather important for your further witchery progression.

336.name: Probably Usable For Soup
336.desc:
But its main purpose is to brew potions and infusions.

A kettle must be set up in the correct way before it can be used.

Firstly, a fire must be set under the kettle (netherrack is best for this, but wood will do just as well).

Next the kettle must be filled. You can use pipes to automate this.

Thirdly, each of the six ingredients for the brew or infusion must be thrown into the kettle, one at a time (the liquid will change color according to the recipe being used).

Finally, a glass bottle must be used on the kettle to bottle the resulting brew, this must be done multiple times depending on the recipe.

It is important to keep the fire going constantly while the recipe is being prepared, otherwise it will be ruined. Swirls coming from the kettle is a sign all is well, black smoke (and brown liquid) indicates the brew is ruined. When a brew is ruined, the kettle must be emptied with a glass bottle, or just filled with more water, and the whole process repeated.

When a brew is complete, the liquid will be the color of the resulting brew and will splutter (in addition to white swirls).

Some recipes will require magical energy from a nearby Altar before they are bottled. If the brew is bottled when insufficient energy is available, the brew will be ruined. Black swirls coming from the Kettle is a good sign there is not enough power. A kettle must be within 14 (to 16) blocks of an Altar in order to obtain power from it (it can obtain power only from a single altar).

337.name: Don't Shake it Too Much
337.desc: Unlike nitroglycerin, this stuff doesn't explode. But you still shouldn't shake it too much...

338.name: Spirit Coin Transfer
338.desc:
The Potion of Spirit can only be made in the spirit world. Wondering how you can get your stuff like the Kettle, Spinning Wheel or Cauldron to the spirit world? You can transfer a few coins to the spirit world and buy some stuff.

[warn]Don't claim the coins until you've gone to the other side.[/warn]

339.name: Circle Magic
339.desc: For circle magic, you need... a circle, obviously. There are 4 types of chalk you can use, and you'll need all of them to proceed.

340.name: Portable Power Source
340.desc: By infusing a stone with fiery magic you can create an "Attuned Stone". These stones can be used to link non-magical devices to the magical currents present...

341.name: Flying OINKment
341.desc: You tried, it doesn't fly. And it also doesn't make any OINK sounds, sadly. But there might be some use for it...

342.name: Nimbus 1999
342.desc: Not as good as its successor, the 2000'ish model, but still "somehow" useful...

343.name: Bloodlust: Ghast Tear
343.desc: If you can't pin down a ghast but still want to make refined evil.

344.name: Bloodlust: Witches Hand
344.desc: In case you don't like the infernal witches...

345.name: Seeping Shoes
345.desc: Who needs an antidote? Now with free fertilizer!

346.name: Poppets
346.desc:
Get a poppet and make the type you like with it to protect you against that thing. We recommend the death protection one.

Consider making the poppet shelf so you don't need to carry a bunch of them around with you. It needs to be chunkloaded to work.

347.name: Bloodlust: Wool of Bat
347.desc: In case you just can't get it to drop.

348.name: Bloodlust: "Cain" Sword
348.desc: Serves 2 purposes: Style. And... ah, yeah... Something useful for vampires.

349.name: Be a Vampire; Dress Like a Fancy Person
349.desc: Sometimes it can be troublesome to get fancy clothing. However, if you prove yourself worthy, you get a set for free.

350.name: Naquadria
350.desc: A naquadria bee. It's a bit tricky to obtain. And before you ask, yes you will still need the naquadria production line to refine it.

351.name: Manganese
351.desc: A manganese bee. Not very useful but one step closer to some more interesting bees.

352.name: Tungsten
352.desc: Let's breed a tungsten bee. It is particularly interesting because you can make pure tungsten from its combs and not just tungstate or scheelite.

353.name: Iridium
353.desc:
An iridium bee. You can actually get small amounts of pure osmium and iridium from its combs.

[note]With these combs and the uranium ones you can now make climate control units for modules in your industrial apiary![/note]

354.name: Enderium
354.desc: An enderium bee. In particularly interesting for the blinding speed trait!

355.name: Neutronium
355.desc:
You made it all the way to the Neutronium Bee! Wow!

The Neutronium Combs can not just lighten the burden on your smelting setup, you can also use them to finally make the Advanced Mutatron.

356.name: Arid
356.desc: The Arid Bee species. Useless you say?

358.name: Naquadah
358.desc: A naquadah bee. It doesn't let you avoid the naquadah line, but if you have sufficient technology you can make a Mutatron now!

359.name: Decaying
359.desc: The Decaying Bee is not any more useful than the previous three. But now it should be clear, we are on our way to the Lutetium Bee!

508.name: Mutatron
508.desc:
Mutations can be boring, they can be annoying, and most times, they are frustrating. With this machine, you will get a 100 percent pure bred mutation. If the combination of princess and drone can result in multiple mutations, the outcome is random. But who cares?

[note]You can now upgrade your leftover Bees Advanced bags to Bees Expert in the last quest tab.

If you missed grabbing an effect from a species, this is a way to get it again.

You'll need naquadah bees to make this, and the other Gendustry machines.[/note]

509.name: Genetic Sampler
509.desc:
With this machine, you can extract a random trait from any bee, sapling, butterfly or flower. The bee/sapling/butterfly/flower will be destroyed in this process. Each process requires an empty gene sample and some labware.

The resulting sample is random, so make sure to recycle the extra gene samples you don't want.

510.name: Diamondware
510.desc: Also known as labware. Be prepared. In order to do serious bee-work, you need stacks of diamonds. I'm not joking, stacks! Better start working on that diamond bee!

511.name: Genetic Imprinter
511.desc:
With this powerful machine, you can inject gene samples, which allows you to alter any aspect of your bee, tree or butterfly. The gene sample (or pattern) will not be destroyed.

The machine has 30% chance to downgrade pristine into ignoble and 80% chance to kill ignoble bees.

One labware is consumed per operation.

360.name: Desolate
360.desc: The Desolate species. Maybe you can see where this is going?

361.name: Lutetium
361.desc: Lutetium is extremely useful if you are in ZPM. You will need quite a bit and bees can help you with that.

362.name: Americium
362.desc: A remarkable bee. If you thought americium can only be obtained from fusion you would be wrong.

363.name: Gassy Bees
363.desc:
There is whole swarm of gas producing bees. Let's breed just one of them here. Check out NEI to see all the options you can aim for.

[note]The Xenon Bee might be the most useful one. You can use it for Void Miners and potentially smelting time reductions.[/note]

364.name: Naga
364.desc: You don't like fighting bosses or you play on peaceful for whatever reason? Well you can still get Naga drops with this bee!

365.name: Insurgence
365.desc:
Is your infusion very slow? And, alas, to resolve that you need a world accelerator. That probably causes a lot of lag.

Well no longer! Try out the new Infusion Interceptor. Read more about it on the Thaumic Insurgence page in your Thaumonomicon.

366.name: Lich
366.desc: Lich bones without a boss fight.

368.name: Ur-Ghast
368.desc:
Need a lot of magical wood? Well, this bee produces carminite.

[note]This bee requires a Thaumcraft Thaumium block below the apiary to be bred. To get the correct one, chisel a GT Thaumium block[/note]

369.name: Snow Queen
369.desc: I don't know why you would need a lot of Snow Queen blood. But this is the way to do it.

370.name: Steel
370.desc: It's not much steel, but it doesn't cause pollution.

371.name: Stainless Steel
371.desc: Not particularly strong to be honest.

372.name: End Dust
372.desc: A bit of a misnamed bee. What it really gives you is Ender Goo and End Powder.

373.name: An Industrial Mobfarm
373.desc:
Extreme Entity Crusher is your newest and probably best spawner/grinder, with this you are not only able to get drops without any TPS issues but also with style and extreme efficiency.

Of course that depends on the quality of your sword; damage, looting, and durability are the only stats that will translate towards the efficacy of the machine. (weapon is optional however, there are spikes in it after all!)

Will ocassionally spawn infernal mobs if possible and will use x8 power with every infernal spawn so you need to take that into account!

If you are actively using the Blood Magic "Well of Suffering" ritual to power your altar, this multi may be used in combination with it to 'generate' and 'kill' mobs automatically for life essence, so you don't need a setup involving an actual spawner!

Notes:
Will generate 120L of liquid XP with every cycle.
In case of any problems with multiblock animation just use soldering iron on the machine to disable it.
Read the tooltip! There are a few unique quality of life settings that you may find of use.

[warn]Enchanting the spikes does nothing to effect the machine.[/warn]

374.name: Item Void Cell
374.desc:
[warn]Caution! Use with utmost care! This cell does exactly what it says. It deletes everything you put on there. So better make sure you have precise control over what can actually go there.[/warn]

[note]Will only void items, not fluids[/note]

375.name: Power, So Much Power!
375.desc:
If only I could harness it... Well, hello there my friend. I need your help with a project of mine. I want to create an immense power source, but I have a few problems that I need to solve first. Will you help me? You'll be well rewarded.

[note]The quests to get items will trigger the next part once you hold the item in question.[/note]

376.name: I'm Hungry
376.desc: What... I work a lot! So get me some food please.

377.name: A Casing
377.desc: I need something for the outer shell. Again, a crystal-based material would be best, because I like it shiny. I tested all crystals I know of and none of them met the requirements. I heard you have access to outer space? Can you try to find a suitable material while you're out there?

378.name: It Needs a Power Plug...
378.desc: Given the fact that I will be able to craft this device, it needs some kind of "plug" to get the power out. I need something to conduct immense amounts of power. I thought about superconductors, but they don't fit with the crystal casing material I have in mind. I need 2 different crystals, one for positive and one for negative charge.

379.name: I need a stabilizer
379.desc: Opening the wormhole is one thing, but I need to stabilize it, so it doesn't harm anything. I guess I need something made of crystal. Why? Because I like it fancy. Find me a crystal-based, transparent material with immense power, would you?

380.name: A Problem that Matters
380.desc: I need to open a wormhole. But for that, I need an immense gravitational field. One like those created upon the death of a massive star, a black hole. But I can't really "kill" a sun for this. I need to compress matter somehow... But... How? I think I may need a specialized machine for this, one that does not use explosions, as that won't get dense enough.

381.name: Fancy!
381.desc: Yes. Yes! That's it! Bring me more of this stuff! I probably need a lot of it...

382.name: Red and Blue
382.desc: Really? A red and blue crystal? How awesome is that! I think I will need 8 of each color, just to be sure.

383.name: Shiny!
383.desc: What in the world is this? This is amazing! I need more of these! They will be an amazing containment material for the wormhole!

384.name: Now that Matters!
384.desc: Of course! The matter condenser! How come I didn't think of that?

385.name: A Few More Things...
385.desc:
Ok, I think I have everything I need to assemble the device. Can you assist me with some additional materials? I need to update some of my machines, as these exotic components you got me damaged some of them already. Don't worry, I have a list here.

Some of my circuits burned to a crisp while I was trying to measure the conductivity of these soul and life shards. I need better ones.

Good lord, these mysterious crystals have eaten away my diamond grinding head! How could that happen? Can you get me a better one?

Do you see that pile of Rhodium and Palladium over there? No? Neither do I. Because the machine was VAPORIZED! Good that you gave me 2 singularities, the first one... Well... Just get me a better casing, would you?

It seems, that the stabilizers need... stabilizing... But I'm out of field generators. Can you help me with this?

387.name: Industrial 3D Copying Machine
387.desc: Need large amounts of chiseled blocks? This is the machine for you.

388.name: Draconic Evolution
388.desc:
You have tinkered long and hard to try to combine magic and technology. It looks like you finally found a way! Combining your powers as a thaumaturge and blood mage with your UHV technology you think you have found a way to distill the power of a dragon into a small core. This machine can do it, you are sure. If only you know how precisely to assembler such a core.

[note]Use a hologram projector to see how it is built. You can also upgrade your draconic cores but for that you need the higher tier schematics and you need to upgrade the multiblock.[/note]

389.name: A volcano for your base.
389.desc: The ultimate machine for cobblestone production. Or maybe to turn redstone into obsidian on a large scale for a renewable production of Silicon, Magnesium, Iron, and Oxygen.

390.name: Indium
390.desc:
This bee multiplies all sources of Indium by 3x.
The Indium bee is available in LuV, or in IV with the help of magic.

391.name: Osmium
391.desc: Quite the interesting bee. Maybe integrate it into your osmium processing?

392.name: Co-Processing x16
392.desc: For an even more compact crafting CPU.

393.name: Co-Processing x4
393.desc: Your crafting CPU is massive and still can't keep up? Don't worry, we have a custom solution for that!

394.name: Graphene
394.desc: Graphene is a material that is just one atom layer thick. It has very favorable electric properties. To create though you will have to make some Graphene you can use a powerful adhesive to pull a single layer of material away from Graphite.

395.name: Dragon Blood
395.desc: The Dragon Blood Bee is a lot of effort to obtain but quite handy for hypogen in late game.

396.name: Goals
396.desc:
GregTech New Horizons is a long and intricate mod pack with massive amounts of custom content and over three thousand quests to guide you through it.

We recommend you set yourself small goals from tier to tier and see how far your journey takes you.

The ultimate late game goal is to craft a Stargate or two, but the recipe is outrageous, and very few players have ever done so.

If you need a big goal for your run, we suggest to aim for something less crazy.
Like going to the moon if it's your first time playing.
Or making and automating an Assembly Line if you have never done so.
Or crafting the Tier 8 Rocket which is the last regular rocket and is reached in UHV.

397.name: Even more challenges needed?
397.desc:
You have played through most of the pack a few times already? Maybe you even made a Stargate? Or are you just looking for something a bit different? Don't worry, we have a few options for you:

- Play in Hardcore. Either Minecraft Hardcore, or BetterQuesting Hardcore. The latter is a bit more forgiving. Maybe this seems impossible, but people have indeed reached UEV+ in Minecraft Hardcore. Better be prepared to restart a few times. This is obviously not for everybody.

- Never make a rocket. The so called no-rocket run is somewhat supported by now, you will be able to do almost everything in the pack. Use bees, crops, and blood magic meteors as your main resource production. Be warned though that such a challenge is grindy and slow. For instance, you might need to collect large amounts of andradite ore or via the aluminium bauxite slag route to get your first titanium. Or have to start breeding bees early without much technology to help.

- Skyblock. Yes, really. It is also known as Garden of Grind and it is absolutely brutal. There is no actually supported skyblock version of the pack but some players have figured out the minimal amount of changes needed to make it work. Ask around or search on the official discord channel for all the details. But don't expect any fun down this road.

- Speedrunning. A few players have designed some basic rules and have started to record speedruns to early game goals like the Coke Oven. Ask around or search on the official discord channel for all the details. Maybe you just want to watch how they do it.

398.name: Kill Baba Yaga
398.desc: Baba Yaga is a witch (or one of a trio of sisters of the same name), who appears as a deformed and/or ferocious-looking elderly woman. She flies around in a mortar and wields a pestle. She dwells deep in the forest, in a hut usually described as standing on chicken legs, with a fence decorated with human skulls. Baba Yaga may help or hinder those that encounter or seek her out.

399.name: Kill Horned Huntsman
399.desc: A Horned Huntsman is a member of the Wild Hunt, a powerful entity created by the Horned God. If one is encountered, either prepare for a hard fight, or flee.

400.name: Kill Ender Dragon
400.desc:
The dragon must die. Long enough has she destroyed those precious endstone blocks!

[note]PS: Hold shift and whack it with a vanilla sword.[/note]

401.name: Kill Shade of Leonard
401.desc: Leonard is the demonic being who is rumored to have taught a cadre of German witches their trade, and often presided over their sabbats. A skilled coven with the witches cauldron can summon a Shade of the Demon according to the method described in the Witches' Brews book.

402.name: Kill Lord of Tormentum
402.desc: A Lord of Torment is a mighty demonic being that is able to create prisons to torment its victims. Those foolish enough to summon one of these creatures must prepare for an arduous fight.

403.name: Kill Mog and Gulg
403.desc: Mog and Gulg are the god-like beings that hobgoblins worship. They may send their avatars to pay witches a visit who exploit their worship (with a statue of hobgoblin patron). Rumor has it that using a nether star may make them take immediate notice.

404.name: Kill Demon
404.desc: Demons are powerful, evil beings who dwell in the infernal dimensions. To date, none have been seen roaming the Nether, where their minions: ghasts, blazes, and their ilk seem to dominate. To meet a demon without a way to protect oneself is almost certain death. They are known to hit like a proverbial truck, and require a significant amount of damage to kill. Some demons have even been seen shooting fireballs! They are, of course, also immune to fire.

405.name: The Power of Infinity
405.desc: You've mastered everything possible. And now it's time to give your equipment the final boost. The infinity egg is just the right addition to your altar. It's quite expensive, but well worth the cost.

407.name: Tier 1 Slates
407.desc:
The basic slates. Basically just stone with blood infused in it.

You can either pump the blood out, use buckets, or fill cells to get the blood for the cutting machine.

408.name: See All the Things
408.desc:
It's quite difficult to see how much blood there is in your altar, or your personal blood network. This little device will solve that problem for you. Just a little prick in the finger, and everything will be revealed.

You can also make the sigil of sight when you have the alchemy set too.

409.name: Basic Rune
409.desc:
The Blood Rune is the basic rune that makes up blood altars, expect to make a lot of them.

You need 8 runes for your tier 2 altar. You can make new stuff with your upgraded altar.

410.name: Alchemy With Blood
410.desc:
Not like "blood alchemy". You probably will need more than one later, but for now one is sufficient.

Place an orb in the slot to start crafting, using LP from your network. You'll need higher tier orbs to craft more complicated items.

If the LP in your network falls to 0 while you're working with this alchemy, you will get a permanent Nausea debuff until it is increased again.

411.name: Potion Catalysts
411.desc: In order to manipulate potions you need catalysts. You then combine the ingredient you used to get the potion effect in with either a lengthening or power catalyst to increase the duration or the strength of the potion effect.

412.name: Simple Alchemical Things
412.desc: Alchemy can be weird, and tedious. There are a lot of reagents and even more possible combinations. For now, and in order to get started, you need a couple of basic things.

413.name: Refilling Your Potions
413.desc: If empty, you don't have to throw the potion away. Just refill it.

414.name: Special Potion Flasks
414.desc: Regular glass phials are unable to hold these special potions you want. You need to imbue them with some blood from your altar.

415.name: Potion: Flight Mode
415.desc: Just to give you an idea what is possible. Let's do a sample potion. Shall we? Get yourself a potion flask, a feather and one of your weak binding agents you made. Combine those in the chemistry set, and you'll get something that you'll probably want to keep for a while.

416.name: Simple, But Powerful Spells
416.desc: The simple spell system is rather easy to set up, and can be done early on in your blood magic career.

418.name: Spell Foci
418.desc: There are nine basic elements and each has a focus, an item that represents them. You place this focus in your altar next to the spell table in order for your magic to flow through it and the paradigm.

419.name: Some Examples
419.desc:
There are a LOT of spell combinations to discover, so just use trial and error!

[note]Or the internet.[/note]

422.name: Tier 2 slates
422.desc: You need MOOORE. Imbue some slates with even more blood.

425.name: Elemental Chalk
425.desc: Necessary for rituals. You can technically use these to make the rituals, but just make the diviner.

426.name: Sacrificial Runes
426.desc:
Poking your finger might work for simple slates and runes, but getting enough blood for advanced stuff is tedious. With sacrificial runes you are able to increase the blood gain from mobs or yourself.

These increase the blood gain by 12% additively for each rune.

427.name: Tier 3 Runes
427.desc: The first rune increases the I/O from the altar's buffer by a multiplicative 50% rate, and the second increases your altar's capacity by 3500L. This also increases the size of the I/O buffer, which is always 10% of the altar.

428.name: Rituals Made Easy
428.desc: Now that you have access to elemental chalk, you can get yourself the basic ritual diviner. A convenient way to place simple but yet complex magical rituals. Shift-right-click to cycle between rituals.

429.name: Powerful Armor
429.desc: A long way to go, and rather expensive, but a really powerful armor and well worth the effort.

430.name: Getting Soul Shards
430.desc: Enable your sword by shift-right-clicking and slay some enemies! Collect as many shards as possible. You will need a ton of them...

431.name: Bound Armor
431.desc: Time to assemble your armor. You need to build the pattern with blocks as you would craft the armor in a crafting table. The soul forge must connect to the structure on at least 2 sides. Then, right-click the forge block with an empty hand to craft the armor piece.

432.name: A Bloodmage's Sword
432.desc:
Diamonds and emeralds are limited in their ability to store LP. With this sword, you are able to get the crystallized souls from your enemies. Time to go hunting.

[warn]While active, all bound tools, including this sword, will actively draw LP from your network. This can have deadly consequences if you run out of LP, so be sure you have a reliable source.[/warn]

433.name: Rituals
433.desc:
Time for some rituals. The most basic one you'll probably get first is the ritual of binding.

There are various rituals, some useful, some not. The other ones you're likely to care about are: Serenade of the Nether (if you don't like everburn urns), Ballad of Alchemy (for automating alchemy), Well of Suffering (for automating blood collection), Mark of the Falling Tower (summon a meteor filled with ore), and the endgame Convocation of the Damned.

434.name: Bound Tools: Pickaxe
434.desc:
Mining never was easier. Just right-click with this tool and all the stone is gone! Like all bound tools, this uses a massive amount of LP.

[note]Watch out for falling into lava![/note]

435.name: Bound Tools: Shovel
435.desc:
Ever wanted to clear out a large piece of terrain with dirt, gravel and sand? Why not let demons do it? This shovel, imbued with the soul of a demon will unleash the power by your will. But be warned: The demon you're about to summon with this is thirsty, and it costs a lot of LP to make use of it, so make sure your LP pool is full!

[note]Will not dig beneath you.[/note]

436.name: Bound Tools: Axe
436.desc: Upon right-clicking while activated, this axe will clear out any wood within a large area. Be warned: This uses a huge amount of LP. You might suffer from sudden death...

437.name: A true blood-mage
437.desc: Bloodmagic is fine, so is Thaumcraft. Why not combine both worlds?

438.name: Complex Spell System
438.desc: Unlike the simple spells with foci and paradigm, the complex spell system is incredibly...complex. There are way too many combinations to cover them here, so it's advised to look for the knowledge in other places. However, with the items from this quest, you are able to craft a simple mining spell.

440.name: Oak Trees Are Not Apple Trees
440.desc:
Oak trees that give apples? Who the hell added this to minecraft?
You found a way of combining an oak sapling and an apple to get an apple tree sapling. Try to plant one to get a permanent source of food.

Once you complete this quest, check out the rest of the Quest Tabs. A new one, "Coins, Coins, Coins", has unlocked. You'll want to look through all the quest tabs to make sure something new hasn't unlocked somewhere else occasionally. There are lots of tabs, so be sure to scroll down!

441.name: Basic > Steam Lootbag Trade
441.desc:
Trade three Basic lootbags to get a better one: Steam.

After you've completed the Steam tab and head into LV you will unlock recipes to uptier your lootbags in the assembler, but for now here is a quest to do so! (Repeatable)

442.name: "Baked" Potatoes
442.desc: Eating raw potatoes isn't that healthy, as you might have noticed. Some potatoes are green and poisonous already. After you get a furnace you can make delicious baked potatoes as another food alternative. Put a baked potato on a stick to make it even more delicious.

444.name: Soft Mallet Adventure
444.desc:
You now have some melon slices or pumpkins but what can you do with them?

You can just eat them or... use a mallet to get some seeds. OK, let's craft one. Perhaps you can use this mallet for some other things too.

446.name: Shields UP
446.desc: With a sword in one hand, what can you do with the other? Right, you need a shield. Let's make one.

448.name: Shield Update
448.desc: Your wooden shield is very weak. So you should make a better one. Looks like you need some leather, so go and find some cows.

450.name: Your First 'Long Range' Weapon
450.desc:
You can't make a vanilla bow or Tinkers weapons yet, but you can make a spear!

Some monsters will become much easier to kill with this long-range weapon.

451.name: Trigger: LoC
451.desc: Triggered by having a "Lamp of Cinders" in your inventory.

453.name: Trigger: Shard of Creation
453.desc: Triggered by making a "Shard of Creation".

454.name: Trigger: Life and Soul Shard
454.desc: Triggered by making a "Life Shard" and a "Soul Shard".

456.name: Trigger: Life
456.desc: Triggers the Life Canister Quest after a Wither death.

457.name: Energy Infuser
457.desc:
The Energy Infuser is the tech answer to the Thaumic Restorer for repairing items and also acts a charger.

When fed with energy it is able to charge any item that is fed into its Input Bus. Fully charged items will be placed in the Output Bus. The items will be fed with the amount of energy provided by the used Energy Hatch.

Apart from its charging capabilities the Energy Infuser can repair items when provided with UU-Matter and energy. However, GregTech tools can not be repaired. When repairing the machine consumes 1L UU-Matter and 1000 EU per repaired durability point.

458.name: Quantum Anomaly
458.desc:
All your theoretical knowledge tells you this can't possibly exist. But there it is.

Maybe you can find some use for it?

459.name: Advanced Glue
459.desc: Combine that with Acetone or Methyl Acetate to obtain Advanced Glue.

460.name: Polyvinyl Acetate
460.desc: You know how polymerization works by now. Use Air or Oxygen and optionally Titaniumtetrachloride to obtain Polyvinyl Acetate.

461.name: Vinyl Acetate
461.desc:
Graphene is required for IV motors. This organic compound is just a stepping stone.

Already automated Graphene? Hold 128 dust to complete this questline.

462.name: Wyvern Core
462.desc: Wyverns are rare dragons that can walk on two legs. More powerful for sure. Researching your Draconic Core, you found a way to improve it. The Wyvern Core.

463.name: Runes Tier 2
463.desc:
The speed rune is the only tier 2 rune. The speed rune does what its name implies, provides an additive 25% increase to the processing speed of the blood altar.

HOWEVER, this makes it much more difficult to actually craft anything, so don't use them unless you're sure you can craft the item.

760.name: Clay Electrolyzing
760.desc:
If you looked through NEI you may have found out that electrolyzing clay dust will give you Alumina, Silicon Dioxide, Sodium, and Lithium dust. And water, but you're probably just voiding that already, right? There are plenty of stained clay blocks found in canyon, mesa, lush desert biomes or several to a couple dozen on mountains that can be pulverized to clay dust.

There are more ways to get alumina now that you're in MV, enough that it's basically worthless by the time you get to EV.

761.name: Powderbarrels
761.desc: Powderbarrels are pretty bad in the implosion compressor. They are good for prospecting though. Craft one stack.

762.name: TNT
762.desc:
TNT is much more powerful than the powderbarrel but much harder to craft. There are LV and HV ways to craft. At LV you need heavy or light fuel to make some toluene which can be solidified to gelled toluene. Alternatively you can use sugar and plastic dust in a chemical reactor to make toluene more efficiently. Mix it with sulfuric acid to make TNT.

The HV petrol way requires an oil cracking unit to make all kinds of cracked fuel or cracked naphtha and a distillation tower to extract toluene from that.
Though you can also go the wood tar route in mid-MV.

763.name: iTNT
763.desc: Industrial TNT is the HV variant of TNT. It explodes with more power, and you can't pick it up. Not by hand anyway

720.name: Weeds II
720.desc:
Weeds, weeds, and more weeds. You get so many of them while cross breeding.

Need some biogas cells for your power production in exchange? Or maybe some glowstone to light up your fields?

721.name: Unknown Seeds No More
721.desc:
You have a lot of unknown seeds already. Use your scanner to scan all the seeds.

Seeds can be planted again to breed better and more resilient crops.

Later you can use your portable scanner to see the crop stats while still in the ground.

Growability depends on having dirt beneath your crop (4 max), biome (swamp/jungle types are all pretty much the same), air quality (increases up to Y124 and don't place a lot above/around your plants), hydration, and fertilizer. When the crop tier and plant stats get high enough, they will actually die without enough Growability. This is never a concern if you use a good biome though.

As long as you water and fertilize them, you can pretty much grow crops anywhere.

If the Growth stat gets too high, greater than 24 or so, the plant will spread weeds onto neighbors.

Some crops, such as Redwheat or oreberries, require certain light levels to crossbreed or grow.

722.name: Speed Up Your Plant Growth
722.desc: To speed up the growth process a bit you can use a fertilizer. You will note, however, that bone meal no longer works. A bit of ash, apatite or calcite mixed with some water in a chemical reactor will give you the fertilizer you need. Apply by hand.

723.name: Potatoes, Carrots, Pumpkins and Melons
723.desc:
Your seeds have three different values: Growth, gain, and resistance (GGR).

The only way to change a crop's GGR without 3rd party tools (like NBTedit!) is through cross breeding. When two plants cross-breed, the resulting plant will have a GGR score that is between both "parent" plants' GGR. Sometimes it will also be a little bit higher or lower. Thus, to obtain high GGR scores you must go through numerous generations of crossbreeding.

The highest score for GGR is 31, while the lowest is 0.

[note]BTW, if you want to skip all this BS, the nbtedit command is /nbtedit me, click root in the upper left, then scroll down to inventory, and find the seedbag (empty your inventory first to make it easier) and click on the icons until you find the page with the stats. Once you've found the GGR stats, select one and click edit in the upper left, make sure to save.[/note]

[warn]Remember that you can permanently brick your character! So make a backup first if you don't know what you're doing, or want to edit your actual character.[/warn]

Of course, you can't do any of this on a server.

724.name: Weeds
724.desc: Weeds will show up from time to time on empty or double crop sticks. They will spread from there and take over all your precious crops, so harvest the weed with your weed trowel before you start desiring to reload the last backup. Later you can make some Weed-EX to prevent them from appearing (until it runs out).

725.name: Flower Power
725.desc:
Growing dyes on crops sticks? Try to plant some flowers. If you cross breed Rose and Dandelion flowers maybe you'll get a new flower and new dyes with different colors.

You will need to plant 4 vanilla flowers to get them on a cropstick. They also don't plant unless they are in bright light.

To get "Rose Seeds" place poppies. Honestly there are several flowers you can use, though.

[note]If you actually want these for dyes, go make the rainbow cactus instead.[/note]

726.name: Chemical Rubber Production
726.desc: A much more efficient way to make insulated cables is by making molten rubber in a chemical reactor, which can be applied to a cable in the assembling machine. When you put both machines next to each other you can output the molten rubber directly to the assembling machine by clicking the fluid auto-output button on the chemical reactor. Make sure to set the correct side of the reactor as output side by wrenching it. Also remember to use the right circuit in the assembling machine.

727.name: Better Than Barrels
727.desc:
While barrels offer a slot for a single item, drawers can have up to 4 slots each, holding 16 stacks per slot.

However, they do come with some downsides. You won't be able to make them point up or down, and you can only access the contents from the front. The automation can use any side.

728.name: Compact Drawers
728.desc: They are very compact and add some fanciness to your glorious dirt base.

729.name: Framing Drawers
729.desc: Now you can really make some swaggy and even fancier drawers by using this table, all you have to do is place some decorative blocks and it will take their texture, the TOP left is the outer square, bottom left is for the front face, and top right is for the borders. This table works with almost all decorative blocks.

730.name: Bio Fuels
730.desc:
You've been using steam, creosote, and possibly oil plus their byproducts to generate power. It's time to start making bio diesel. The first step is to make plant balls out of organic materials. You can use wheat, sugarcane, apples, saplings or, well, practically anything. Indigo blossoms are 4:1 and unlike the rest of the IC2 crops with the same ratio, not really useful for anything else. Realistically, you'll probably just use saplings though, since you'll want/need a tree farm anyway.

Be sure you have a Forestry multifarm, Steve's Carts Farm, IC2 Crop Sticks with a harvester, or whatever, because you need plenty of bio materials for constant bio diesel production.

731.name: Plantballs
731.desc:
Next you should compress plantballs out of your wheat.

The MV compressor is twice as fast as the LV. You might think this means using it is a good idea. Well, it depends. Remember it also uses 4x the power. This means you spend 2x the EU in total to get the same amount of EU out. If you overclock too much in your fuel production setup, you end up with an EU deficit! Overclocking once is usually well worth it though.

Eventually you want to use multiblocks for better speeds and efficiency!

732.name: Plant Mass
732.desc: Macerate the plantballs down to plant mass for better processing. The HV macerator has an extra slot so you can get more, but it's not worth it power-wise, same for EV and IV.

733.name: Bio Chaff
733.desc: Plant mass needs to be centrifuged into bio chaff, the base material to make biomass.

734.name: Way One: IC2 Fluid Canner
734.desc:
The first way you get biomass out of bio chaff is to use the IC2 Fluid Canner. One bucket of water and one bio chaff gives one bucket of biomass.

[warn]This option is not really recommended. You should focus on GT machines, not IC2. It also has some buggy interaction with normal cells, you might need to use universal cells.[/warn]

735.name: Way Two: Brewery
735.desc: If you are lucky and find some brewing stands or have started using Thaumcraft to make one, you can craft a brewery. You get 1000mb of biomass out of 1000mb water and 1 bio chaff. Use Distilled water to get a better ratio and a much faster recipe.

736.name: Fermented Biomass
736.desc: Fermenting your biomass allows you to get more products out of it like methanol, ethanol, ammonia, etc.

740.name: Extrude All the Things
740.desc:
Now that you've hit MV tier, it's time to get a better ratio when making rods, pipes, wires, plates and many more parts. Most extruder recipes use more than 32 EU/t so you need an MV extruder. After crafting the machine you need extruder shapes for the different types of parts you will want to make.

There are a few more shapes than the ones listed here. Make sure to have a look.

[note]Most of the Tinkers Gregworks tool parts are made in the extruder. They also have long extrusion times to balance out their usefulness.[/note]

741.name: Tool Forge
741.desc:
After getting obzinite, it's time to craft a tool forge for making bigger (and better) Tinkers tools.

You do not have to use iron to create the forge; you can also use other materials like tin or copper, among others.

742.name: One Block Mining is so Old School
742.desc:
By making a hammer you are now able to mine a shaft of 3x3 blocks. You can use iron, steel, or obzinite poured from the Smeltery, it doesn't need to be steel. Higher tier metals require the MV extruder. You'll need a hammer head, two large plates and a large rod for your hammer. Be careful in the Nether or you could be boiled in lava very quickly.

The quest should accept the tool whatever materials you used.

743.name: Cutting a Tree log by log is so Old School
743.desc:
By making a lumber axe, you are now able to cut down an entire tree in one hit. Don't forget your backpack when you're cutting a sacred oak down.

Protip: With wooden equivalents of casts, make a lumber axe completely out of netherrack - lower mining speed means less XP needed to level it up. Use the modifier slots to add Reinforced. Once you've leveled it up enough, upgrade the parts for better ones, such as steel, obzinite, or others. The higher-level metals will require an MV Extruder. Keep in mind that the auto-smelt modifier doesn't work, so don't bother with it.

The quest will accept a lumber axe from any material.

744.name: Mining Dirt Block by Block is so Old School
744.desc:
By making an excavator you are now able to mine dirt in a 3x3 area. You can use iron, steel, or obzinite poured from the Smeltery, it doesn't need to be steel.

Higher tier metals require the MV extruder. Don't destroy your whole garden.

The quest should accept the tool whatever materials you used.

745.name: Glue
745.desc:
To make Good Circuit Boards, you need either glue or refined glue.

You can obtain refined glue either by centrifuging sticky resin or distilling glue. Note that it is GT glue, not Tinkers' Construct glue.

Alternatively, you can extract glue from slime trees that you can obtain from slime islands in the sky.

746.name: Solar Grade Ingots
746.desc: To turn your solar grade dust into solar grade ingots you need to use an EBF with kanthal coils and then cool the resulting hot ingots in a chemical bath.

747.name: Diodes
747.desc:
Good circuits need diodes. There are two main ways to craft them. You can crush the Gallium Arsenide crystals to dust and combine that with fine copper wires and molten glass. Once you have solar grade silicon you can use it to make silicon wafers in your EBF to make even more. Note that the wafers are much more efficient on gallium and arsenic, so even a reduced amount is still worth it.

Make sure you check all the NEI recipes for advanced items instead of just using the first one you find. You can use annealed copper wires and polyethylene to increase your output even more.

748.name: Empty Circuit Board
748.desc:
Good circuit boards require wooden pulp and refined glue to make phenolic circuit boards. By adding gold wire you can craft a good circuit board.

There are more efficient ways to make it if you look in NEI.

749.name: Are You Prepared For HV?
749.desc:
Stainless steel will be the tier material for HV. Use an MV mixer to produce the dust.

Manganese can be acquired as ore byproducts from spessartine, pyrolusite and tantalite ore. You can also pull them apart to get even more. Alternatively you can take apart Red Garnet dusts. If you get desperate, you can buy some ore from the Coins tab.

Chrome can be electrolyzed out of ruby dust. You can get ruby dust from redstone if the ruby in redstone veins isn't enough.

750.name: Molten Plastic
750.desc: Molten polyethylene can be made in a chemical reactor using ethylene, compressed air cells or oxygen - using oxygen gives a 50% boost. Use a mold for making plates in a fluid solidifier to make plastic sheets.

751.name: Mixing at MV level
751.desc: MV Mixer is needed to progress toward HV. It also unlocks Diesel, more efficient fuel.

752.name: Silicon Plate
752.desc: Advanced circuits require transistors, which are made out of high grade silicon plates, fine tin wire and molten polyethylene.

753.name: Stainless Steel EBF processing
753.desc:
Stainless Steel ingots are made in the Electric Blast Furnace at a temperature of 1700K.

It takes 85 seconds at 480 EU/t or 60 seconds at 480 EU/t by adding Oxygen gas. Always add a gas if you can, you're saving tons of power by doing so, and you're gonna need a TON of SS. The Cracker, Large Steam Turbine, and Distillery Tower all need huge amounts of SS. A single DT uses several stacks, and you're gonna want more than just one.

754.name: MV Energy Hatch
754.desc:
Some materials like stainless steel require 480 EU/t. Your LV hatches are not good enough. Craft a minimum of two MV hatches to upgrade your electric blast furnace to HV.

[note]BTW, if a Multiblock's recipe is 1EU over 1A for that tier, you must use a second energy hatch for it to use 2A or more.[/note]

755.name: Better Than Duct Tape
755.desc:
Using a soldering iron tool to remove the "burned out circuits" message is cheaper than duct tape.

The soldering iron requires one soldering material item and 10,000 EU. Valid soldering materials are fine wires, rods, and ingots made from either tin, lead, or soldering alloy. The soldering iron will consume the first such material it finds in the player's inventory. Since you'll likely have more than enough, Fine Wires of Soldering Alloy are probably best.

Other uses for the soldering iron:

- It will adjust the redstone strength from weak to strong emitted by GT machines.
- While holding it all pipes, wires, and cables will look very small, so that you can see everything past them, and also be sure of the exact connections.
- Shift right-clicking on a some machines with it will allow you to toggle multi-stack input, which can be very useful for automating certain assembler recipes.

In order to "complete" this quest you'll need to charge the soldering iron after you craft it.

For the other problems other tools are necessary. Keep in mind that you can put all needed tools in a toolbox to fix all problems in one click.

Possible problems                Tool to fix
"Pipe is loose."                    Wrench
"Screws are loose."           Screwdriver
"Something is stuck."            Soft Mallet
"Platings are dented."           Hammer
"Circuitry burned out."    Soldering Iron
"That doesn't belong there."   Crowbar

756.name: Less Power Loss
756.desc: When you reach MV tier you can make your own duct tape in case you have to move your multiblock machines around. Duct tape will fix all maintenance problems in any multiblock machine.

757.name: Ruby Electrolyzing
757.desc:
Ruby is a good source for chrome and a little alumina. Once you get low on Ruby, you can centrifuge Redstone to get more.

Another good source of alumina and oxygen is red granite dusts. You can get those from red stonelillies. Check out the Fishing Farming Cooking tab for more details about IC2 crops.

758.name: MV Battery Hulls
758.desc:
Better machines need better batteries. Let's make some MV battery hulls for new batteries.

It's up to you what kind of battery to make, but realistically you'll probably make Sodium or Lithium.

759.name: Molten Polyethylene
759.desc:
Polyethylene is made out of ethylene and oxygen in a chemical reactor. Molten Polyethylene cells hold 144L, perfect for 1 ingot or plate.
If you want precise amounts, you can use a Volumetric Flask once you electrolyze some Boron from Borax.

You may have started getting damaged from holding the cells. If you have, put your glove baubles back on. All Molten cells cause damage and store 144L. Mostly, Molten cells come from metal-type materials, or stuff that was melted. Excuse me, Fluid Extracted, whatever that's supposed to mean.

Notice some leftovers? Try using the plunger, or if you have the materials, you can use 12 ethylene cells and 84 oxygen cells to make exactly 125 cells of polyethylene.

512.name: Genetic Transposer
512.desc:
DNA samples of species and/or traits are pretty valuable. You always should keep at least one of each gene sample you get your hand on in a chest somewhere safe. By using this machine, you are able to copy any gene sample, for the cost of an empty serum vial and some labware.

[note]You don't actually need to keep all of them if you don't intend to use them of course.[/note]

513.name: Advanced Mutatron
513.desc:
A more advanced version of the mutatron. It uses more energy to mutate a drone with a princess, and you can select which mutation you want to have. This machine is a must-have for high-tier bees.

[note]You'll need neutronium bees to make this.[/note]

514.name: Blank Gene Samples
514.desc:
To get the various traits from a bee, you need a lot of power, labware and an empty gene sample. You don't need a lot of those, as you can just throw them in a furnace to remove remaining DNA of traits you don't need or want.

In the meantime you can start working with the Genetics mod, since we all know Gendustry is too easy... Genetics is used for bees, trees, and Botany flowers so it gets its own quest tab. Gendustry is really good for bees, but will require top tier equipment.

515.name: Genetic Template
515.desc: Templates are used to combine multiple gene samples into one large batch. It can be used to apply multiple traits at once, but you can also replicate a bee from liquid DNA and protein, if you manage to fill all 13 traits in one template. These templates can also be copied, just like the small samples.

516.name: A Rather Cruel Disposal
516.desc: You'll probably end up with a lot of excess drones while doing bees. (Or saplings if you do trees). Instead of throwing them away, you could also turn them into... something useful.

517.name: Arnie's Blender
517.desc: They say breakfast is the most important meal of the day. This machine turns raw meat into liquid protein, which is used to create bees from scratch. Which has absolutely nothing to do with breakfast.

518.name: And There Shall be Light
518.desc:
Out of princesses to inject traits to? Too lazy to find hives and get more bees? Not a problem. With this nifty little device, you can use a ton of power, liquid DNA and protein to create any bee you wish to have. The only drawback is: All bees will be ignoble, which means they will die after a random amount of cycles after 90.

Of course, there's an upgrade for the industrial apiary to deal with that

519.name: Only the Best: Temperature
519.desc:
On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available.

[note]None is for testing jubilant state, while 3 is what you'll have on most of the time. You can hit all temp requirements with 3 if you work in a normal temp area. You can go up to 5 if you use the acclimatiser and want nether bees working in an icy area for some reason.[/note]

520.name: Only the Best: Humidity
520.desc:
On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available.

[note]None is for testing jubilant state, while 2 is what you'll have on most of the time. 3+ don't do anything as the range doesn't go that high.[/note]

521.name: Only the Best: Fertility
521.desc:
On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available. Bees bees everywhere!

[note]F4 is great while breeding bees, but F1 is what you want to use when producing stuff.[/note]

522.name: Highend species: Draconic
522.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available.

523.name: Highend species: Doctoral
523.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available.

524.name: Only the Best: Speed Blinding
524.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available. Buzz buzz buzz, working hard and really buzzing!

525.name: Highend species: Silicon
525.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available.

526.name: Only the Best: Rain
526.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available. Beekeeping in the rain! Make sure to keep your GT machines covered!

527.name: Only the Best: Day and Night
527.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available. Bee working day and night, all night and all day long!

528.name: Only the Best: Cave
528.desc: On your way to awesome bees, you have to collect only the best traits available. Bees with Cave can work in housing that is covered - by more housing!

529.name: You're Gonna Hate This #5
529.desc:
The fifth item you'll need a lot of. For the microwave, the scanner, the precision laser engraver, etc.

The Emitter and Sensor aren't used as much as the other items, fortunately.

530.name: You're Gonna Hate This #6
530.desc:
The sixth item you'll need a lot of. For the scanner and the seismic prospector.

[note]BTW, there's technically one more, the Field Generator, but you need higher tier circuits to make it, and it isn't used as often until later tiers.[/note]

531.name: Scanning Bees, Crops and Moooooore!
531.desc:
Automate your bee, sapling, or crop scanning. For crops, this is the only real way to find the crop's stats until you can craft the Portable Scanner, which needs HV circuits that you can make in the next tier.

Later on, this machine gets very useful for scanning high tier machine parts for the assembly line research data or to fill data orbs with data for UU-Matter production. Data sticks with raw prospecting data need a scanner too.

532.name: Exploding Fluids For Power
532.desc:
The Combustion Generator produces EU from several fluids and spends fuel only when necessary. Some fluids are more popular than others, but the Oil processing line has a different combustion fluid in every tier from LV to EV, and all of them are burned in these generators.

To start, LV is the tier of Light Fuel. It is worth much more than the Oil itself for generating power, so you should distill and desulfurize the Oil to get clean Light Fuel and use it for power. It's a great way to run your base, and can be upgraded further in the next tiers. Check the "Working with Oil" tab for more information, once you've made a Pump and found some Oil.

If you're dealing with Oil processing, this is the type of generator you'll use the most.

533.name: Hydrogen, Methane or Some Farts... (Natural gas)
533.desc:
Some centrifugal processes leave gases behind like Hydrogen or Methane. Centrifuge some Brown and Yellow Limonite to get Hydrogen or dump your unused food in a Centrifuge to get Methane gas. Wood will even give 60 mb per log, and many other crops will also generate Methane this way.

The main powergen line that uses this generator is Benzene, but that needs MV tier.

If you're dealing with fully renewable powergen, this is the type of generator you'll use the most.

534.name: Pollution What....?
534.desc: If you are running your bricked blast furnace for a long time you will sometimes get headaches, nausea and other weird effects. Yes this is pollution... from making so much steel. If you can collect some rubber sheets, I will trade you a complete hazmat suit.

535.name: Redstone, Early Game Processing
535.desc:
Are you wondering how to process raw redstone ore without a macerator?

Use the hammer in the crafting table to get some crushed redstone ore from your raw redstone ore. After that, use the hammer in the crafting table again to get some impure redstone dust.

Lastly, fill a cauldron with water and drop the whole stack of dust into it, either manually or by pressing Ctrl+Q. The cauldron can handle the entire stack with the same amount of water. You will get pure redstone dust for your redstone needs.

You could also take the hammer with you on your mining trip and break the redstone ore with it directly to get crushed redstone ore, thus skipping the raw redstone ore.

536.name: Rubber Sheets
536.desc:
Let's make a mold for the rubber sheets. They are mainly used for conveyors and cables.

At this stage, making cables by hand with rubber sheets is faster and cheaper in power than the Alloy Smelter recipe for the cables.

With the sheets, you should be able to craft Piston Boots - a massive upgrade to your mobility. Check out the questline Getting Around to find them.

537.name: Time to Kill (Witch)
537.desc: Witches bring stitches because they are always infernal and very dangerous. Be careful since they throw potions at you.

538.name: Basic Ore Washer
538.desc:
Macerating ores usually doubles the output. If you want MOOOOOOORE, craft an ore washer and wash out some rare materials.

Note that these both use water, and are painfully slow.

Put crushed ore in them.

[note]You can also consider using the steam multiblock instead if you want.[/note]

539.name: Round and Round and Round...
539.desc:
After purification of your ore dust, you can centrifuge it to get even more byproducts out of the dust. On top of that, you can process many items into their more useful byproducts with this machine, such as Sticky Resin and Brick Dust.

It's always useful to check NEI for Centrifuge and Electrolyzer recipes for any item/fluid you may need. It might just be that you can get that from stuff you already have in your base!

540.name: A Truly Sharp Saw Blade
540.desc: Combining cobalt brass and diamond dust makes a really sharp saw blade for your cutting machine.

541.name: A New Alloy - Cobalt Brass
541.desc:
If you want to cut diamond blocks into plates, it's time to get a new alloy, Cobalt Brass.

First you need to make brass out of zinc (only found in small ores or TiCo Zinc Gavel Ore in the Overworld, in Nether Sphalerite, or as a centrifuge byproduct from tin or tetrahedrite) and copper. Mix it with tin and cobalt dust (you can get it as a byproduct of a couple ores, go searching in the nether, or go to the Twilight Forest for Cobaltite) to get the cobalt brass dust.

Now that you've come this far, we think that you're able to look up the ores that you need by yourself with the help of NEI.

542.name: Electrically Separating Dusts
542.desc:
Using the power of electricity you can get even more out of your dusts. Somehow. With no catalysts, electrolytes, or anything. Look, don't ask me how it works. It just works.

Aside from that, this is a very important machine to process some items and fluids into other, more useful items and fluids. A basic example of this is electrolyzing Water, which should be completely free to you, to get both Hydrogen and Oxygen, two very important fluids for your progression.

543.name: C9H8O4...NaCl...H2O...
543.desc:
Want to make some cetane-boosted diesel, plastic or some chemical dyes? Then it's time to craft a chemical reactor.

You can use the battery slot and the other extra slot in the GUI to store items.

544.name: Compact? Or is it?
544.desc:
It's a compressor but that doesn't mean it's compact...

It is highly recommended to get one of these electric compressors, as the efficiency in terms of coal/steam used to compress something is a lot better when using EU.

[note]You can also consider using the steam multiblock instead if you want.[/note]

545.name: Suction Device... Nice
545.desc:
I hope you're not thinking about what I'm thinking you're thinking about...

It's highly recommended to get one of these, as the efficiency in terms of coal/steam used to extracting something is a lot better when using EU.

546.name: Hello Baron, How's Your Black Gold Today?
546.desc:
During your travels you may have seen some huge oil spouts. Now's the time to make a pump and go collect this black gold into tanks. Oil can be burned directly in a combustion generator (onsite to power the pump, perhaps?) or be refined for better fuels and crafting materials.

Bring a generator or battery buffer (with full batteries obviously) with you to power the pump. Thanks to the advancement of technology the pump can auto output on top. Stacking multiple pumps makes them work faster. In that case, oil has to be removed from the bottom of the stack using a pump cover or other means like a conduit.

Taller spouts have more oil. But for the LV pump, it's better to use the short spouts if possible since it doesn't have enough range to fully collect the larger ones in the horizontal range. Save the taller spouts for MV or HV pumps.

Mining pipes are needed for pumping the oil, same as the auto miner. When it hits the bottom (or something in the way), if there's no fluid left to collect, it will start retracting the pipes. Same thing if you disable it with a soft mallet. You can also change the range with a screwdriver if there's a reason to do that, like collecting lava in a limited area faster.

These are GT machines, so remember to protect them (and their power sources!) from rain on all sides. And set a waypoint with JourneyMaps so you don't forget where you placed it!

[note]Once you run out of surface oil, you'll want to turn to:
1) Oilsands ore, though this is better suited for making heavy oil
2) Making oil from logs in a pyrolyze oven
4) Bacterial Vat (EV+)
5) Crops
6) Bees
7) Underground (UG) oil. Dimensions generally have a couple fluids that can be accessed with a multiblock version of the pump, available in MV+[/note]

[note]Heavy oil cannot be used until MV for anything except lubricant and burning in semifluid generator since it requires MV distillery.[/note]

547.name: Your Oil is Too Crude? Refine it!
547.desc:
Burning oil in the combustion generator is a very inefficient way of producing power. Every bucket/cell of oil only gives you 20,000 EU. Time to refine it.

You'll need to use a circuit to pick which of the products you want. Later on you can make a Distillation Tower to get all of them, but that's far away for now.

You can also distill many other fluids! There are two that are useful, but take a long time to distill, so you should set them up in advance:
Lubricant (from Creosote) and Distilled Water (from Water).

548.name: Light Sulfuric Fuel
548.desc: You can distill your oil into light sulfuric fuel, that has a burn value of 40,000 EU.

549.name: Light Fuel
549.desc:
By adding Hydrogen to Sulfuric Light Fuel in a Chemical Reactor you can produce Light Fuel with the very high burn value of 305,000 EU per cell. It is also the main component of Diesel, an excellent fuel that becomes available in MV

The remaining Hydrogen Sulfide can be used to produce Sulfuric Acid for, well, tons of stuff. You can never have enough of it! You can also crack Light Fuel in a Chemical Reactor and then distill it to get Ethylene, for Polyethylene. But Refinery Gas is better for that.

550.name: H for Hydrogen... the Rest of the Alphabet is Too Complicated...
550.desc:
Centrifuge some brown or yellow limonite dust, or use an Electrolyzer with water to get some hydrogen.

There are many other ways of obtaining hydrogen which you might want to look into. Some are only available much later.

Examples are: chemical processing or electrolysis of methane obtained from organic sources, electrolysis of water or salt water, mixing sodium with water, or eventually siphoning hydrogen directly from a gas giant.

551.name: Heavy Sulfuric Fuel
551.desc: You can distill your oil into heavy sulfuric fuel, that has a burn value of 80,000 EU.

552.name: Heavy Fuel
552.desc:
By adding Hydrogen to sulfuric Heavy Fuel in a Chemical Reactor you can produce Heavy Fuel, with a burn value of 360,000 EU per cell! Aside from this, it's also required for Diesel, in MV, which is a better fuel, and it can also be distilled into three very useful fluids: Benzene, Toluene and Phenol.

If you want to use Heavy Fuel as a power source, you will need Semi-Fluid generators, unlike Light Fuel and Diesel which use Combustion generators.

It's honestly not especially efficient to make Heavy Fuel out of regular Oil. You should try to get Heavy Oil to make it, like from Oilsands Ore in MV once you can. Then use it to make Diesel.

553.name: Diesel Fuel
553.desc: If you get an MV mixer, you can mix your light and heavy fuel together to give diesel. At 480,000 per cell, this turns 1,885,000 EU into 2,880,000, not accounting for how much you spent to get it, so it's definitely worth it.

554.name: Hey DJ, Mix it!
554.desc:
The mixer is a very useful device. You can mix all kinds of fluids and items. Useful to prevent dust from falling to the ground when crafting - most mixer recipes give a slight boost over hand crafting!

You get +3 slots in the EV+ versions, this one has 6.

You can use the battery slot and other extra slots in the GUI to store anything you like!

[note]You can also consider using the steam multiblock instead if you want.[/note]

555.name: N Like Nitrogen... Hide, the Alphabet Strikes Again!
555.desc:
You can get some nitrogen by centrifuging... air. This process takes a long time, especially at LV tier.

You get that air from compressed air cells, which are gotten from running cells through a compressor. Each one contains 2000L, unlike a normal cell that contains 1000L.

556.name: Glyceryl Trini...... What?
556.desc:
Kids, today we will make some glyceryl trinitrate (aka Nitroglycerin) with some glycerol.
    - Chemistry 101

557.name: NITRO! (Cetane-Boosted Diesel)
557.desc:
1,000,000 EU per cell, way more dense than before. Yes, yes, this is very good! Make some cetane-boosted diesel fuel and power your Combustion Engine with it.

[note]BTW HOG is a different path, so you'll need to completely redo your setup if you want that in EV.[/note]

558.name: How Can I Carry All These Items?
558.desc:
After you made your first steel, you are able to craft a small backpack. It has 36 slots, which is a bit more than a small chest. Later you can make it even larger.

You can have at most four of these backpacks in your inventory.

You can put one on your back and set it to automatically pick up stuff. In your controls, check that the command "Open Backpacks" has a key and doesn't have conflicts. The key only works after the backpack is added to the back slot, which can be done by using Shift+<Open Backpacks Key>.

You can rename your backpack by pressing Shift+RMB when holding it in your hand.

559.name: Low-Pressure Boiler Tank
559.desc:
The low pressure boiler tank is the easiest way to make steam with an RC boiler. The firebox needs this tank on top to produce steam. The bigger the boiler the slower it heats up, but it will produce more steam when heated up.

Extract steam by attaching a pipe to the boiler tank.

[note]This variant is very useful for inconsistent operation. It has lower efficiency but heats up significantly faster than the high pressure variant meaning the fuel will be used more efficiently at the beginning of the operation, the drawback is that this variant is 50% as efficient as the high pressure one is at max temperature. If instead you want to have a constant high-throughput steam generation use high pressure variant instead.[/note]

[warn]Actual fuel usage depends on the machine size and other factors such as machine temperature, if you want to know the actual number you will need to test it on your own setup.[/warn]

560.name: Railcraft Steam Power
560.desc:
As an alternative way to make steam you can use the railcraft boilers. They require at least one firebox.

More than one size is possible: (1x1 which the quest gets you to make) 2x2 and 3x3.

Provide water and coal to the firebox. Make sure you don't run out of water or...BOOM.

Each boiler needs 1, 2 or 3 layers of tanks on top, equal to the size (1x1x1, 2x2x2, or 3x3x3) - see the quests linked below.

561.name: Liquid-Fuelled Firebox
561.desc:
Creosote oil into steam! After you make your first steel you are able to craft a liquid fueled firebox.

Diesel, ethanol, creosote and Biodiesel all work well here. This will also require a boiler tank - see the quests linked below.

562.name: High-Pressure Boiler Tank
562.desc:
After you make steel you are able to craft a high pressure boiler tank. The upgraded version increases the temperature and produces more steam than the low pressure version. It will take longer to warm up, so make sure to keep it well fed!

Extract steam by attaching a pipe to the boiler tank.

[note]This is better than the low pressure variant only if you keep it working for very long time, if you intend to turn it on and off all the time this will waste a lot of resources! If you keep it at top temperature it will use ~2x more fuel than low pressure and will give ~4x more steam per sec making it 100% more efficient! If you can't sustain the fuel drain or want a more flexible power source instead use the low pressure variant.[/note]

[warn]Actual fuel usage depends on the machine size and other factors such as machine temperature, if you want to know the actual number you will need to test it on your own setup.[/warn]

563.name: Time to Kill (Wither Skeleton)
563.desc:
Gimme yo Skuuuuull!!! <-- The thought of every non-noob player upon seeing one.

BTW, in case you didn't know, you can summon the Wither by placing 4 soul sand blocks in a T shape, with 3 wither skulls on top. Don't do so in your base.

576.name: Working Table 2.0
576.desc:
A manual crafting table which remembers the last nine used recipes. Left-click one of the recipes shown in the right-hand side to automatically fill in the crafting grid. Right-click to lock a memorized recipe and prevent a newer recipe from bumping it off.

Resources need to go into the internal inventory. The crafting matrix is only a visual representation.

The Forestry worktable is super-useful, and can reduce the tedium of many common jobs because you can click-drag across a row of items, and it will use tools in its own inventory to continue crafting.

Specifically:
Making <Item X> with <Bucket of Y> - Put all the ingredients into the table, and if the recipe is selected, shift-click the result to craft them all at once (until you run out)
Making fresh water for Pam's - Same as above, but no second item
Making clay bricks - The wooden form is automatically reused
Making anything that uses a tool - Also automatically reused, while consuming durability

[warn]Warning: Stored recipes will be lost if the worktable is broken! To avoid this, move it using a dolly.[/warn]

[note]For more forestry quests you need to go to the dedicated tab.[/note]

577.name: Forestry Core Block
577.desc: This sturdy casing is the basis for almost all machines in Forestry, essentially being the Forestry equivalent of a machine casing.

578.name: The Carpenter
578.desc:
The carpenter is similar to an automatic crafting table. It has an item input slot and a storage slot for the recipes. Some recipes need an additional fluid like water, creosote, honey or molten redstone. With the carpenter you can make farmblocks, woven backpacks, bee and tree analyzers and many more.

The carpenter needs power to work. You can use a GT cable or a battery box to connect to the machine or make some forestry, railcraft or buildcraft RF engines.

[note]PS: Yes it's a pain in the ass to automate, just make one for each thing you want to make.[/note]

579.name: Molten Redstone?
579.desc:
You heard that the carpenter needs some molten redstone for a few recipes. Converting redstone dust into a fluid requires a fluid extractor. Let's build one.

If you want to get that molten redstone into a cell, you'll need a fluid canner instead of a GT tank. Why? Because Reasons.

Fortunately, most fluids work properly, but redstone, cracked fluids, and certain other ones do not. Or just use a universal cell.

580.name: Thermionic Fabricator
580.desc:
The thermionic fabricator is used to craft electron tubes and stained glass. It requires redstone flux (RF) energy to run.

It is your first machine made in the carpenter. Once warmed up, it needs to maintain a certain heat level to operate. The amount of power supplied will determine how much heat will be generated. If you do not supply enough power to maintain the current temperature it will slowly drop. It will continuously draw power, so disconnect it if you don't need it.

581.name: A Series of Tubes
581.desc:
Before you can craft your first farm block you need apatite tubes. Make some apatite rods first with a lathe, then use a forge hammer to join them into long apatite rods. You need golden bolts and a red alloy plate.

Put some glass or sand into your thermionic fabricator and make your first tubes.

[note]You can also use the circuit assembler for a better output. Why did we have you make the TF again?[/note]

582.name: The Multifarm
582.desc:
The smallest farm you can build requires 36 farm blocks (3x4x3 - the largest is 5x4x5 or 100 farm blocks). Place the underlying blocks corresponding to the farm type you built. In this case, Stone Bricks. You can place these blocks beside or under the multi structure, but not the layer above. It will search 6 blocks above these for dirt/farmland/sand/soul sand/humus depending on farm type, with the appropriate crops, saplings, etc. on top of that.

You can use different modes to collect different crops. Arboretum for trees, infernal for nether wart and so on. You can even mix and match modes on the same farm - cutting trees down for wood in part, and collecting fruit from trees without destroying it in another, for example. So long as they're on different sides, at least.

You may need to experiment a bit to see what works, but [url]https://ftbwiki.org/MultiFarm#Managed_Farms[/url] has a decent list, though it doesn't specify cotton for managed crop farms for example. It might be a GTNH change, I dunno. A number of crops have an added IC2 crop version as well, so you may prefer those for the higher yield if you level them up. Orchard mode works with Pam's, and Witchery, just to save you some time. Pam's nether crops however don't seem to work at all though.

583.name: Farm Logic
583.desc:
This chip allows you to select the farm type (when combined with the right electron tubes), and not use the default of growing trees. Unless that's what you want, in which case you don't need it.

For each circuit tier, you can configure 1/4 more of your farm.

584.name: Changing Your Foresty Circuit Configuration
584.desc:
The (forestry) soldering iron is a tool used for attaching electron tubes to circuit boards in order to manipulate how an electrical engine works or to program multifarms, as well as upgrading the squeezer or centrifuge. Honestly, you'll probably only use it for the multifarm though.

Once electron tubes have been attached to a circuit board they cannot be removed, only replaced. The circuit board is automatically crafted as soon as the basic recipe requirements are met, so it's best practice to first place your electron tubes and then add the circuit board.

The soldering iron can also be used to remove the circuit board from electrical engines, multifarms, squeezers and centrifuges by opening the machine's GUI and left-clicking with the soldering iron on the circuit board slot.

585.name: Heating Coils
585.desc:
Your thermionic fabricator needs heating coils to function. So combine some magnetized (requires polarizer) steel rods with some copper wire and make some.

[note]Why is this a separate quest again?[/note]

586.name: Welcome to Forestry
586.desc: Want to make some automated farms or work with tree or bee breeding? Want the Forestry Worktable for easier recipe access? Then this is the right chapter for you. You'll need a bookshelf to unlock it, better rev up that compressor.

587.name: Magnetize Your Rods
587.desc: Magnetic steel rods will need a Polarizer to craft. You'll need them later on for MV Motors and MV hatches.

588.name: Finding Apatite
588.desc: It's now time to search for some ores. Apatite is the most important material in Forestry. You can make fertilizer, farm blocks and other things with it. The vein is found between Y 40-60.

589.name: Multifarm: Hatch
589.desc:
You can supply and extract items from the farm by using farm hatches that are connected to pipe systems.

Supplying items
The farm hatch is clever enough to know which item goes where. If an item isn't appropriate for the farm's configuration, the hatch will simply reject it.

Extracting items
Any item that is harvested by the Multifarm is automatically ejected by the farm hatches into inventories or pipes. Pipes can be used to extract from other sides. You may need more than one, or a better conveyor if it isn't extracting fast enough for you.

590.name: Multifarm: Valve
590.desc:
