Most farms require water for hydration purposes. Although you can do it manually by supplying it through liquid containers (such as cans), you can also automate the process by using farm valves.

By connecting fluid pipes into the farm valve and supplying water, you can automatically fill the farm's water tank.

If it hasn't rained for a while, the required water will go up, up to 1500%. Use an Everfull Urn, or Reservoir (RIO) for serious water needs.

591.name: Multifarm: Control
591.desc: You can control which fields of your farm work by applying a redstone signal to the control block. You can either shut down the whole farm by applying a redstone signal from top or below or you can disable a specific side by applying a redstone signal on the matching side of the control block.

592.name: Multifarm: Gearbox
592.desc:
To power your farm and have it automatically harvest crops, you will need at least one farm gearbox to supply power. You can use either RF or EU. If you use EU, make sure to use a cable to allow conversion. Farm operations can be hastened with additional gearboxes.

Testing has shown that splitting the amps to 4 gearboxes (4A ULV) works better than sending 1A LV to 4 gearboxes.

A max sized IC2 Crop farm (5x4x5) using all space possible with max stat seeds (31/31/0) on a fast crop needs only 6A ULV to keep up.

Similarly, a max sized small farm (3x4x3) using all space possible with max stat seeds (31/31/0) on a high tier, moderate speed crop needs only 4A ULV.

Sending more power to the same gearbox doesn't work. If it can get to max RF capacity but your farm can't keep up, sending more power won't help, so add more gearboxes.

In addition, you lose 2 EU from the energy emitting device for every Amp of ULV (which is only 8 to begin with), so it's not nearly as efficient as using LV or higher. Just keep in mind that more A to more gearboxes is better than to the same one.

Of course, if you mix your slower crops with your faster ones, you aren't likely to need to min-max to this extent.

593.name: Enhanced Farm Logic
593.desc: This circuit allows you to configure 2/4 areas of your farm.

594.name: Refined Farm Logic
594.desc: This circuit allows you to configure 3/4 areas of your farm.

595.name: Intricate Farm Logic
595.desc: This circuit allows you to configure 4/4 areas of your farm. Honestly, you should probably just skip the others.

596.name: Upgrade of the Upgrade of the...
596.desc:
It's upgrade time. Unlock redstone and obsidian level for your different tools.

Remember, a fresh tool head starts at one level below. So you'll need to use it enough to level it up before you can mine at the proper level. Go strip-mine a mountain or something.

597.name: Tetrahedrite, Stibnite and Copper
597.desc:
Maybe you're wondering why you need tetrahedrite or stibnite. Well, antimony can be centrifuged out of it which is very useful for batteries. Batteries store power, so you can have highs and lows in your power production while still outputting stable power.

The veins can be found between Y level 80-120.

598.name: Antimony
598.desc: The most efficient way to get antimony for batteries is to run stibnite through a BBF or EBF, burning off the impurities (sulfur), and giving you more antimony. You can also centrifuge it if you want to keep the sulfur. You can also centrifuge tetrahedrite dust if you're desperate.

599.name: Thermal Centrifuge
599.desc:
A machine to get different byproducts out of your crushed or purified ore dust. It's useful for getting a bit of Gallium out of crushed Zinc Ore for your Diodes needed in good circuits.

When you have IC2 nuclear reactors and want to recycle your depleted fuel rods, it's the only way to do so.

If you can't produce the 2A of LV power for this machine right now, wait until you get to MV and use transformers to power an LV version.

In LV, you can obtain the same ore byproducts via different methods. The Thermal Centrifuge only becomes really important in HV when you can combine it with the HV macerator.

[warn]Sending MV power to an LV device, even if it uses more than 1A will cause an explosion, so don't do that.[/warn]

600.name: Hunger No More
600.desc: The hunger is constantly following you, consuming you, never letting you rest. The world around you is full of nourishing fruits, vegetables and crafted meals. Use them to curb your insatiable hunger.

601.name: Building Made Easy
601.desc:
Placing blocks by hand can be tedious. Wouldn't it be nice to be able to place blocks like "Another 9 here"? Well, good news. Get yourself some of the various building blocks, and I shall reward you with something nice.

You could also just craft it (or the better one) with unstable ingots yourself, too.

You can upgrade to the various other BBWs too.

602.name: Spatial Distortions
602.desc: You detected a weird disturbance in the air around an enderman. I suggest you try to steal some of their pearls and research them.

603.name: Dimensional Chest
603.desc: You've heard rumors, that the endermen had built laboratories in the End. To invent new machines and weapons in order to attack the Overworld. One of their inventions is a chest, which is somehow linked to all instances of itself. Go and try to find one of those.

604.name: RF Batteries
604.desc:
Basic Capacitor Banks are RF batteries which can store the RF power you generate. You can put many blocks together to enlarge your total battery capacity - they will merge into one battery.

You can also change the mode of each face using your wire cutters or yeta wrench instead of the GUI. One option is a nice display of input and output RF, but it has to be taller than it is wide.

These are great for interfacing with RF machines. GT wires will convert to RF, but they send a full amp packet no matter what. EnderIO machines properly accept it, but others like Genetics do not and waste most of the packet, so consider using them with any RF machine if it looks like you need it.

605.name: Slay the Eyes!
605.desc: Find and kill Ender-Eyes, that guard the Towers.

606.name: Ender Guardians
606.desc: Well if you've decided to grind then do it... Kill some of the guardians.

607.name: Slay the Dragon. (Again)
607.desc: If you're bored, you could resurrect the dragon. Or, you can find a chaos dragon, if you dare. Anyway, kill one of those, and you'll be well rewarded.

608.name: A Lead About Lead That Leads to Lead
608.desc: The next step of making batteries is to collect some lead. Look at the lead quest in the novice thaumaturgy tab for more info.

609.name: Battery Alloy
609.desc:
Mixing antimony and lead in the alloy smelter will give you battery alloy. Combine it with tin cable and voila, there's your small battery hull.

Small = LV, Medium = MV, Large = HV. It gets weirder after that.

Note that if you make the battery alloy in the mixer you need to use dusts, but if you use the alloy smelter you can use either.

Check out the quest tab Storing and Transforming EU for all details about EU power storage.

610.name: Lead, Galena, Silver and Cryolite
610.desc:
You can't find Lead in the Overworld or the Nether. So it's time to explore the mysterious Twilight Forest a bit and find a Cryolite vein at Y 5-40.

You can get Lead, Silver, Cryolite for Aluminium production and Galena, which is more lead, in the vein.

There is some Silver in small ores in the OW and Nether, but this is a better source.

611.name: Single Use Battery: Mercury
611.desc: Mercury and acid batteries are single-use batteries. They are crafted fully charged but cannot be re-charged. Once their stored energy is depleted, they can be placed in an extractor to be converted back into small battery hulls.

612.name: Single Use Battery: Acid
612.desc: Mercury and acid Batteries are single-use batteries. They are crafted fully charged but cannot be re-charged. Once their stored energy is depleted, they can be placed in an extractor to be converted back into small battery hulls.

613.name: Reusable Battery: Sodium
613.desc:
Sodium, cadmium, and lithium batteries are rechargeable batteries. They're crafted without stored energy, but can be charged in any gregtech machine. If unneeded, they can be placed in an extractor and converted back into small battery hulls, but their contents are lost.

If placed in a machine, it will fill up if there's excess power, and drain if the machine can't get enough. Handy for high-EU machines you don't want to choke.

[note]Sodium is the one that stores the least EU of the 3 rechargeable batteries, but is definitely the easiest to get.[/note]

615.name: Reusable Battery: Lithium
615.desc:
...and the Lithium one.

[note]Lithium is the one that stores the most EU of the 3 rechargeable batteries.[/note]

764.name: Dynamite
764.desc:
Dynamite is the most basic explosive you can use in the implosion compressor.
Mix some paper, string and glyceryl trinitrate in a chemical reactor to get dynamite.

765.name: Time to Drill for Oil!
765.desc:
Pumps are nice but you're going to reach a point where you'll want MOOOOOOOOOOOOORE oil. This multiblock will help with that but you'll need to search for oil using a seismic prospector. You can find raw, light, medium, and heavy oil. There's also a chance to find natural gas.

Higher tiers of power will drill faster. You need to upgrade the energy hatch corresponding to the power tier.

Later you can upgrade the oil drilling rig so it extracts from more than one chunk at a time.

You need mining pipes in the controller. If you need more than 64, you can use an input bus.

These are GT machines, so remember to protect them (and their power sources!) from rain on any side. And set a waypoint with JourneyMaps so you don't forget where you placed it!

Space dims can also have fluids beneath bedrock. For info, check the Tips and Tricks tab.

766.name: Let's Get Crackin!
766.desc:
Cracking oil products like light and heavy fuel with the oil cracker is faster and more efficient than using a chemical reactor, producing 25 to 50 percent more output, depending on which cracking method it's doing (steam does 50%, hydro does 25% but consumes 20% less hydrogen). Cracked products will result in additional by-products in the distillation tower. It is a smart idea to get this multiblock since you are going to need TONS of industrial TNT from Toluene for advanced tier rockets.

The 16 coils form 2 vertical rings of 8 on either side of the controller. The casings between the rings can be replaced with a hatch for steam/hydrogen input. Energy hatch and maintenance hatch can replace any of the casings. The input product hatch and output product hatch must replace one of the casings outside of the coils.

Hint: Only GT/Railcraft steam can be used, not ic2 steam. You can use a fluid heater to make it instead of a boiler.

[note]The circuit goes in the controller.[/note]

[note]PS: I listed all the cracker recipes on the spreadsheet for you to look at in a convenient format. Now you can easily compare which oil path to take![/note]

767.name: Analyzing the Soil
767.desc:
Tired of looking for ores by hand? Want to find underground oil that will last a long time? A seismic prospector will tell you the ores in the surrounding chunks in a (sorta) handy book you can read, and also the underground fluids beneath you. It doesn't need to be powered.

To determine which kind of fluid there is in the chunk you need to scan the soil by right-clicking an Advanced Seismic Prospector with 2 Powderbarrels, 4 Glyceryl Trinitate cells, 8 iTNT or 16 TNT. This one doesn't explode the user too on use. Features! There are four types of oil or natural gas for the overworld. See the quest in Tips and Tricks once you've made an oil drill.

1) Right-click explosives.
2) Use a single Data Stick to extract the data by right-clicking on it after the animation has finished. Each will hold ONE result.
3) Next you need to put the data stick in a GT scanner to have it analyzed.
4) Place the analyzed data stick in the bottom right slot of a printer and add 3 paper in the top left slot and make sure you have at least 144L squid ink in it.
5) Take the printed pages and combine it with a piece of leather in an assembling machine filled with at least 20 mb of refined glue.

[note]TADA![/note] Now you have a book with detailed info about the chunks you scanned. Visual Prospecting adds the information to your map the moment you make the book.

Data Sticks are not easy (or possible) to make right now, so do not lose them. You can put it in a crafting table to erase it when done.

Ore locations are given as the center of the chunk. Ores that are marginal in that chunk may not be found in the center but on an edge. Look at where ores are located and determine the center of the vein. At higher tiers, it scans more chunks for ores. If you just want to know about fluids, use the lower tier version so the book isn't as long.

Each underground fluid field is 8x8 chunks. This starts at 0,0 and continues for 8 chunks east and south, containing 64 chunks. Not all fields have anything in them though. Within the field the amount can vary +/- 25 percent. The amount reported in the book is the minimum and maximum per field across all chunks in the field. The prospector reports on 3x3 fields (as in, 9 fields of 8x8 chunks) at all tiers with the center field where the seismic prospector is located. Fluid extraction rate depends on many factors, check the wiki for details. In general, anything above 300 is really good. The total amount in the chunk is complicated.

616.name: Mercury
616.desc: Mercury can be centrifuged out of redstone. If you find some cinnabar ore in a redstone vein or one of the infused stone veins you can use that too.

617.name: Fluid Canner
617.desc:
The fluid canner used to be the main way to fill cells, single-use batteries, and coolant cells. It was needed because direct interaction with cells was limited.

Now everything is much easier and cells almost always work as expected, so you likely won't need this, even for molten redstone.

However, if you want an electric hoe or treetap, you'll need to make IC2 batteries in a fluid canner.

Another interesting thing about this is it also works as a fluid uncanner. And maybe later you could fill a Lighter with it.

618.name: Sulfuric Acid
618.desc:
Combining sulfur trioxide and water in the chemical reactor will give you sulfuric acid for your single use acid batteries. You'll need to make sulfur dioxide first.

On the other hand if you've made some light fuel you'll have some spare hydrogen sulfide you can also turn into sulfuric acid (after a couple steps), and if you've automated it, you'll be drowning in it honestly. Once you can make an LCR, you can skip directly from HS to SA in one step. Keep in mind that you'll need all that acid for making stuff instead of for single use batteries by then.

619.name: Battery Buffer
619.desc:
Batteries work pretty much the same in a battery buffer as in a regular machine's battery slot. Battery buffers are used to buffer power to stabilize output.

They come in different sizes: 1, 4, 9(!) and 16 slots. They can draw 2A and output 1A per battery inserted - watch your wire amperage! Cables come in 1x, 2x, 4x, 8x, 12x, and 16x, with tin transferring 1A per size. Make sure not to mess up, or your cable will melt, becoming a fire, and possibly an explosion.

Remember that there's loss when outputting EU from any GT machine, so putting 4 4-slot ones in a row loses 8 power compared to 2 from 1 16-slot one. And this is for every amp too.

[note]You can import batteries into one, but ONLY empty acid batteries can be exported from one. No, you can't get around this.[/note]

620.name: IC2 Battery
620.desc: A few recipes require IC2 batteries. Fill your small battery hull with some molten redstone and you'll get an RE-Battery.

621.name: How to Get Sodium
621.desc:
A second source for sodium is glauconite ore or glauconite sand. This type of ore vein can be found at Y level 20-50.

Soapstone and talc can be used for lubricant.

622.name: How to Get Sodium and Lithium
622.desc:
Salt ore mixes can be found in the Overworld between Y 50-70.

Salt isn't only used to get sodium, it's an important ingredient in many pam's harvestcraft food recipes.

Lepidolite and spodumene are good sources of lithium for your batteries. Later on you can get a lot more in different ways.

623.name: Cadmium
623.desc: You can get Cadmium from Sphalerite (centrifuge or thermal centrifuge). Later on you can use GT++ Ore processing chains to get Cadmium from Rare Earths. I don't care how you get it, just get some Cadmium.

624.name: Basic Canner
624.desc:
The canning machine is used to fill batteries, fill cells with stuff (usually dust, not used that often), or to fill tin cans with any kind of food (HV+).

At LV-HV tier you can make batteries with sodium, cadmium, or lithium.

625.name: Moving Your Tanks, Chests, and Barrels
625.desc:
Want to move your chests and barrels without spilling all the things (buildcraft™)? No problem! Make a dolly and you can carry your full chests, barrels or storage drawers anywhere you want. But be warned, you will get a slowness debuff so hopefully you don't have to travel very far. You can also move non-portable buildcraft and iron tanks without losing fluids, and worktables without losing stored recipes.

Shift-right-click an empty dolly to collapse it. It won't carry anything until you unfold it, but it will stack and can go in your backpack.

PS: Later on you can fly or make a traveller's belt to get around the debuff.

626.name: Portable Mob Spawners...? Cool!
626.desc: Do you want to move a spawner around and build a cool mob farm? That will be no problem if you get the diamond dolly. You can move 6 spawners before the durability runs out, which is probably more than you'll need right now.

627.name: Grain Bait
627.desc: Crafting some nice grain bait for your animal trap.

628.name: Fruit Bait
628.desc: Crafting some tasty fruit bait for your animal trap.

629.name: Veggie Bait
629.desc: Crafting some veggie bait for your animal trap.

630.name: Fish Bait
630.desc: Crafting some fish bait for your fish trap.

631.name: Animal Trapping
631.desc: Tired of hunting for cows, zombies or sheep? You can build a trap and hope some animal or monster drops will be caught by the netting inside.

632.name: A Fisherman's Friend
632.desc: Have you ever tried to catch a fish with your bare hands? It's time to craft a fishing rod and get some fish the easy way. Cast your fishing rod in some water and wait until the bobber dips below the surface. Then reel back in your line and hopefully you caught yourself something nice.

633.name: Fish Trapping
633.desc: Fishing can be very boring. Why not to automate it with a fish trap?

634.name: Wheat Time
634.desc: It's time to plant some wheat to make some bread or to prepare grain bait for your animal traps.

635.name: Floury Flurry
635.desc:
The first step in making bread is to macerate your wheat into flour with a mortar or a macerator.

Flour can be used in many food recipes for different Pam's HarvestCraft foods.

636.name: First Handmade Bread
636.desc: Baking your dough in a Furnace makes some tasty bread. Your first bread!

637.name: Do'h!
637.desc:
Water and flour makes a dough or two... Dough can be used in many different food recipes from Pam's HarvestCraft.

Don't use the terrible NEI recipe, instead drop a stack of flour into a cauldron full of water. Celiacs beware!

638.name: Tasty Toast
638.desc: Smelt your bread again and you get some toast! It gives you more nutrition points, and unlocks more culinary potential!

639.name: Sulfuric Naphtha
639.desc: Another distillate you can make out of oil is sulfuric naphtha with a burn value of 40,000 EU per cell in the gas turbine.

640.name: LPG
640.desc: Centrifuging Butane or Propane gives you LPG with a burn value of 320,000 EU.

641.name: Naphtha
641.desc:
You can process Sulfuric Naphtha to fluid Naphtha which has a burn value of 220,000 EU in the Gas Turbine. Naphtha can be used to get Ethylene for Polyethylene, but it shares this use with Refinery Gas and Light Fuel, so decide for yourself which one you want to use.

If you choose to make High-Octane Gasoline in EV and onwards, it will need a lot of Naphtha. You can also make Polycaprolactam (Nylon), which doesn't have any useful purpose yet, but it might get some in the near future.

642.name: Sulfuric Gas
642.desc: Another distillate for your gas turbine you can make out of oil is sulfuric gas with a burn value of 25,000 EU per cell.

643.name: Refinery Gas
643.desc:
Refine your gas in a chemical reactor with Hydrogen gas to give it a better burn value of 160,000 EU. It used to be the least useful fraction of oil, but it's now possible to distill it into large amounts of Ethylene if you steam-crack the Refinery Gas beforehand.

This early, you will have to use a Chemical Reactor to crack it, but later on there's a dedicated multiblock for this purpose, the Oil Cracker. For best Ethylene results, you should severely steam-crack it, and you will get lots of Methane as well.

644.name: Methane
644.desc:
Distilling some "refinery gases" gives you some methane gas. There are many more (and probably better) ways to get methane.

You will need this in later tiers, for PTFE as an example.

645.name: Glider Wings
645.desc: If for some reason you want to be able to jump down a cliff without getting the message "Player X fell from a high place", a great idea would be to find a way to slow your fall. The store was out of feather falling potions, so make a hang glider instead.

646.name: Electrotine Battery
646.desc: The electrotine battery is exclusively used in project red machines and the jetpack. Electrotine is made by mixing redstone and electrum dust in a mixer, or mined from the Nether.

647.name: Electrotine Jetpack
647.desc:
Your glider gets you to the ground safely but it does not have any method to get up. After you researched electrotine you discovered that it is powerful enough for your first jetpack.

A diamond chestplate surrounded with 4 electrotine batteries is powerful enough to bring you up into the air.

It does not have a hover mode so you better take your glider with you.

Hint: This jetpack can't fly higher than Y-128.

648.name: Refill Your Jetpack
648.desc:
GREAT.... you now have an empty jetpack. You need 25 electrotine dust to completely fill it. Let's craft an electrotine generator and a charging bench to use the power of electrotine.

You will very quickly discover that, while it is fast and cheap, it's empty in the blink of an eye. Despite this, it is useful in combination with the hang glider to get into the air and to get away from hairy situations.

649.name: Coal Steam Jetpack
649.desc:
Your coal jetpack needs coal and water to produce steam. After a short warm-up period you can go and take it for a ride. Keep in mind you will need your glider to make a soft landing.

Put your jetpack on your back and hit Shift and the adventure backpack's action-key to start it.

Max flying height for this jetpack is Y-185.

Vertical velocity decreases linearly with height after overcoming optimal height - 135 for jetpack and 100 for copter.

650.name: Copterpack, More 'Hover' than 'Jet'
650.desc:
The copterpack is the first jetpack with a hover mode. It uses several different types of fuels to power it.

Buckets of creosote, lava, oil, diesel, cetane, ethanol, and fuel will work.

Equip your jetpack and hit Shift and the adventure backpack's action-key. You can also switch between hover and normal mode.

Max flying height is limited to Y-200.

Vertical velocity decreases linearly with height after overcoming optimal height - 135 for jetpack and 100 for copter.

[note]Dropped items will be pushed away from you, just like a helicopter pushes things away, so be careful in the End, or by cliffsides.[/note]

651.name: Electric Jetpack
651.desc:
Your first electrical jetpack, It can be charged in any LV machine. You need to hit the IC2 mode-switch key to toggle between normal mode and hover mode while holding down the jump key. When descending, remember to shut off hover mode or else you'll drain your jetpack.

The only limitation is that you can't fly higher than Y-185.

For charging on the go, you can craft a charging crystal from ic2 charging components. You have to take the backpack out of the armor slot and put it in your hotbar and activate the charger by sneak-clicking.

Later on GT++ charge packs are belts that can charge everything in your inventory including armor. Check NEI for the recipe.

Finally, the GT++ wireless charger will charge everything in your inventory, including the jetpack, from your base at long ranges. A quest is unlocked once you finish your nanosuit. Just remember when you are off world or in the Nether that your jetpack isn't charging!

652.name: Fueled Jetpack
652.desc:
This jetpack uses IC2 biogas and can fly up to Y-245. You can switch between hover and normal mode using the IC2 mode-switch key.

Your jetpack can be (re-)fueled in the IC2 fluid canner with 30 buckets/cells worth of biogas.

653.name: Advanced Electric Jetpack
653.desc:
The advanced electric jetpack is an upgraded version of the electric jetpack.
While worn in the chest armor slot and charged with EU, it allows the player to fly. It provides no armor protection, but will charge tools being used in the same manner as the advanced lappack from its 3,000,000 EU internal storage.

The advanced electric jetpack is limited to a maximum flying height of Y-256.

[note]To turn it on, hit the fly key listed under gravisuite. You can then use space to fly up. To activate hover, hit the IC2 mode key while holding space. Will also recharge tools.[/note]

654.name: Advanced Nano Chestplate
654.desc:
Combining the advanced electric jetpack and nanosuit yields you the advanced nano chestplate. Now you have the protection of the chestplate combined with the freedom of movement of the jetpack.

[note]Works like the AEJ.[/note]

655.name: Gravi Suit
655.desc:
The GraviChestPlate is the ultimate quantum armor combined with a high powered jetpack. Some notable features include:

- 30M EU Capacity
- Spacesuit Capability
- Hazmat Protection
- Gravitation Engines (Creative Flight)
- Levitation Mode
- Quantum Shield (Invincibility, requires full Quantum Armor)

[note]KEYBINDS
HUD: Gravi HUD Key
Gravitation Engines: Gravi Fly Key
Levitation Mode: Spacebar + IC2 Mode Switch Key
Quantum Shield: Sneak + IC2 Mode Switch Key[/note]

656.name: Iron Shields
656.desc: You've been using your hide shield long enough. After you make your first tools, grab a bit of iron ore and make a nice iron shield.

657.name: Stone spear
657.desc:
Upgrade your wooden spear with a stone arrowhead and iron screws to a stone spear.
This spear has better durability than the wooden one.

658.name: Iron Spear
658.desc:
Upgrade your wooden spear with an iron arrowhead and two steel screws to an iron spear.

The iron spear has better durability than the stone variant.

659.name: Diamond Spear
659.desc:
At MV Tier you can upgrade your iron spear to a diamond one. Get a diamond arrowhead, two stainless steel screws and a wooden spear.

This spear has superior durability.

660.name: Diamond Shield
660.desc: Your iron shield is old and worn out. With a shield made from diamond plates you can maintain block for up to 5 seconds.

661.name: Bow, Arrows and Quiver
661.desc:
Wow, your first handmade ehm machine made bow. Now it's time to make a quiver and maybe some arrows.

To place arrows in the quiver, put the quiver and the stack of arrows in a crafting grid. Make sure the Gameplay key Special doesn't conflict with other keybinds. By default, it is Z. When you have the bow selected, use Z to swap arrow slots in the quiver.

While it seems like these might be worthless, these arrows do have special uses. You really want to share a cookie with friends, don't you?

Let's go hunt a bit.

662.name: Basic Arc Furnace
662.desc:
The arc furnace is an alternative to macerating stuff back into dusts and for making wrought iron, annealed copper, and glass (in MV). You get ingots, and sometimes different materials back from recycling than with a macerator. You also have all 4 slots at LV. The recipes use oxygen and 3A of power.

Just ignore the plasma one.

And no, you can't just send 1A of MV. That will blow it up.

663.name: Faster Steel Production
663.desc:
A faster alternative to steel production is to use the arc furnace and smelt iron ingots (or dust) into wrought iron ingots. Then macerate them to dust and smelt them very fast in the EBF to steel.

Of course, you'll need enough oxygen to do so.

664.name: 16 Slot LV Battery Buffer
664.desc:
Upgrade your battery buffer to hold 16 batteries for primary storage.

You can use different buffers - probably with fewer batteries - for your EBF, Arc Furnace, Thermal Centrifuge, and so on.

665.name: Kitchenware
665.desc:
To cook some healthy food you need a few tools crafted first.
Saucepan,
pot,
rolling pin,
mortar,
mixing bowl,
soft mallet,
skillet,
knife...

666.name: Row, Row, Row Your Boat
666.desc:
You live close to the water and don't have a boat? Man, get it together.

You need wooden planks, slabs and a knife to craft one. (This is also quest id 666, be careful, something is going on.)

[note]We here at QuestBook, Inc. do not guarantee the usefulness of boats. The standard worldgen does not include oceans, so their usefulness is heavily curtailed. There are lots of rivers, but horses might be more useful if not on a server.[/note]

667.name: Trainspotting
667.desc:
The wooden tracks can be made without any machine, though always check NEI for assembler recipes for discounts. Functionally equivalent to normal rails, but with a lower maximum speed (for safety reasons).

[note]Hint: You can use your wooden rails for Steve's Carts farms.[/note]

668.name: Rubber Boat
668.desc: The rubber dinghy requires only a few rubber plates. Like the vanilla wooden boat, it will break and dismantle whenever it hits a surface, however instead of returning sticks and planks, it will return a damaged rubber dinghy, which can be re-crafted with two pieces of rubber back into a usable rubber dinghy!

669.name: Inflatable Boot
669.desc: I meant to say boat... The inflatable boat is the first boat that doesn't break on impact with a solid block.

670.name: Carbon Fiber Boat
670.desc:
Unlike all other boats, this amazing carbon fiber canoe is COMPLETELY INDESTRUCTIBLE!* This means that you can smash it into as many beaches, lily pads and alligators as you like, the carbon fiber canoe won't take a scratch!

Warning: We are not held responsible for any damage caused by riding the carbon fiber canoe into a lava lake or the Void.

*Therefore, warranty is NOT included.

671.name: Electric Boat
671.desc: The electric boat works mostly like a vanilla boat, chugging through the water at a decent pace. It will hopefully drop itself once you crash, which is slightly nicer than the usual sticks. That's all well and good, but what if we utilize the electric engine? Wearing an energy storage device in your chestplate slot, like a batpack or energypack, will cause the boat to drain energy from it and propel you forward at supersonic speed! Doing this will consume about 100 EU per block traveled, so even a decent batpack won't last long!

672.name: Boat Shape?
672.desc:
Really? Yes for the more advanced boats you need a new shape form.

You need to make an Extruder first to progress further.

673.name: LV Extruder
673.desc: The extruder is not very useful in LV. But it will extrude your next tier of boat, make rubber sheets more efficiently, get you extra fluid cells, or make some Tinkers Construct tool parts.

674.name: Wooden Rail Variants
674.desc:
The wooden junction, wye and switch tracks need an assembling machine to craft. They allow you to join and switch to tracks going to different locations.

NEI doesn't show the right recipe on the first page. Go through the recipes a bit to find the right one.

675.name: Switch Lever
675.desc:
The switch lever is the manual version of the switch motor, requiring a player to operate it instead of a redstone signal.

It is used in various signaling systems, mainly to change the way switch tracks and wye tracks work.

676.name: Crowbar
676.desc:
The crowbar comes in a Railcraft (and thaumcraft versions of RC) and a gregtech variant. It is used to manipulate machines from railcraft, remove plates and any other covers (pumps, conveyor modules, etc.) from gregtech pipes and machines as well as breaking certain objects faster, such as tracks. This tool is also used to access certain inventories from Railcraft.

Hold a crowbar and shift-right-click to attach carts. Click the starting cart then continue to click to add more carts to the link. Carts will act slower the more connections are made.

The crowbar can be used as a weapon, though it costs twice as much durability on attack (just like any other non weapon tool...).

677.name: Minecart
677.desc:
After you craft your first cart the journey can begin...

Well, not really. You will need some kind of tracks first.

[note]We here at QuestBook, Inc. do not guarantee the usefulness of minecarts or trains in any way. Especially since you can mine out a vein in a single session once you have a hammer and backpacks.[/note]

[warn]WARNING: Unprotected carts can be knocked off their rails by lightning. Cover the area with blocks to prevent carts from disappearing.[/warn]

[warn]Also, make sure to keep your cart in one chunk, or use chunkloaders covering the whole area the cart works within. Loading/unloading chunks may cause carts to disappear or duplicate.[/warn]

[note]You should only make these if you want to have fun with them, as anything they can do is better done by other methods, including the Long Distance Fluid Pipeline. Excessive minecart and train use on servers may cause lag and you may be asked to switch later anyway.[/note]

683.name: Wet Dirt Armor?
683.desc: If you don't have any armor, "mud armor" is better than nothing. Mud can be found in some wet biomes, or crafted from dirt and water.

684.name: Wooden Armor
684.desc: If you can't find mud you can make very poor armor out of wood. Cutting down a few trees to make a set should do. You will need a soft mallet too, so save a few logs for that.

685.name: Armor 2.0
685.desc: Iron armor is much better than the leather armor. Especially when you have to go outside during the night.

686.name: Leather Armor
686.desc:
Killing some cows will give you enough leather to craft a complete set of leather armor.

Hint: You are able to dye this armor.

687.name: Bronze armor
687.desc: Bronze armor has the same abilities as iron armor. Same durability, same protection and same enchantment capability.

688.name: "Nice to Have" Armor
688.desc: The gold armor is a "nice to have" armor. The durability is very low but you look expensive when you wear it.

689.name: "Ooo, Shiny" Armor
689.desc: Diamond armor is the best armor in Vanilla Minecraft, but over here there are much better armors available.

690.name: Fancy Armor
690.desc: Not really better but fancier. The chain mail armor is made out of steel rings.

691.name: Armor 2.5
691.desc: The steel armor has more durability than the iron/bronze or chainmail armor.

692.name: Composite Vest
692.desc: Combining the strength of advanced alloy plates with the steel chestplate makes your vest very hard. Now you have double the durability and increased armor points by 1.5.

693.name: Fallen From the Sky
693.desc:
You may have noticed big meteors everywhere, and maybe wondered if they're useful somehow? Well, they are, but not just yet. I'll give you a reward for collecting some now.

Inside the meteor you can find a skystone chest containing some processor press plates. Save them for later, you can't do AE2 without them. Though you can also just make them yourself in EV. Plus the chest is nice, and can be placed underneath blocks and still opened.

If all surface meteors have been raided, put some skystone in your Ore Finder to find underground ones, or wait until you can make a Meteorite Compass to find them.

694.name: One Anvil...
694.desc:
With your brand-new Compressor you are able to make some metal blocks for your new anvil. You're required to craft at least one for the Forge Hammer.

With the anvil you can repair, rename and enchant armor and tools.

695.name: Automation, Tier 1
695.desc:
Do you want to automate your smeltery because you're planning to make stacks and stacks of glass blocks?

No problem, craft a large bronze pipe and place it under the faucet. Make sure to connect the pipe to the faucet using a wrench. Now the molten fluid will flow inside the pipe. Under or next to the pipe you have to place the casting basins or tables.

A hopper and a chest underneath will auto-output products from the casting blocks.

NOTE: Faucets will output precisely 144L each activation if you toggle it off immediately. That's going to have some uses later on...

696.name: Automation, Tier 2
696.desc: You want a better automation than using pipes? Sure, craft a comparator and link it with redstone dust placed on the ground. The redstone needs to be adjacent to the faucet to activate the crafting process.

697.name: 2x Steel
697.desc:
Steel production is so slow. If you got enough bricks and clay, build a second BBF. Build it attached to the first one, so they can share a wall and you will save some bricks, clay and concrete.

[warn]Note that this will generate twice as much pollution in total - consider moving them further away.[/warn]

698.name: 4x Steel
698.desc:
Not even two BBFs are fast enough...Can you figure out how to save the most bricks making 4?

[note]PS: If you can't, this pack may not be for you.[/note]

[warn]Note that this will generate massive amounts of pollution - have you considered the Nether?[/warn]

699.name: Why One Coke Oven When You Can Have Ten?
699.desc:
Your bricked blast furnace uses coal like hell and your steam boilers do too? Well it's time to make more coke ovens and automate them a bit. Wooden fluid pipes and tin item pipes will help you reach your goal. You can use either faucets with redstone clocks or LV pumps to empty the creosote. For the charcoal, hoppers are easy to get once you have an assembler.

Unlike GregTech multiblocks, coke ovens must have 1 space separating each oven.

Search NEI for a more efficient way to make coke oven bricks.

Remember that tin item pipes require a steel wrench to dismantle them.

The whole coke-oven process generates a lot of pollution, so consider putting it further away.

700.name: How Dark Can You Go?
700.desc: Seriously, how dark can one be? You're sure the face in that crystal was nothing but imagination...

701.name: Ascended
701.desc: You feel empowered, light, almost like a ghost.

702.name: Tier 5 slates
702.desc: 1023 Buckets of blood in one slate! How much more I can store, oh I wish I would know. Then I add one and check if it works. 1024 buckets of blood in one slate! ... ...

703.name: Powerful Shards
703.desc: The demons summoned are rather strong, but their souls contain immense power!

705.name: Accelerated Plant Growth
705.desc: As you might've discovered by now, plants grow very slowly. So why don't you try to speed that up a little? A watering can will come in handy.

706.name: Ethereal Chalk
706.desc: Top tier chalk, for fancy wall paintings?

709.name: Elemental Chalk II
709.desc: Enhanced elemental chalk, to write "darker" runes than you're used to...

710.name: Tier 4 Runes
710.desc:
Runes of Superior Capacity increase the Altar's size by 14% multiplicatively.

Runes of the Orb increase the soul network size by 4% each. You'll need them later on.

711.name: Tier 5 Runes
711.desc: The final frontier: There are not many runes available. The acceleration rune reduces the tick time until the next buffer I/O interaction. It starts at 20, and is reduced by 1 for each rune, with the minimum being 1.

712.name: Advanced Rituals
712.desc: More powerful rituals require dusk runes. With your new dusk chalk, you can enhance your ritual diviner to place those. Allowing you to perform a lot more rituals.

713.name: High-end Rituals
713.desc: The last diviner so far, used for the most powerful rituals.

714.name: Convocation of the Damned
714.desc:
Time to open a portal to the Demonic Realm. What could possibly go wrong? (It's recommended to do this ritual in a space dimension, as the demon city spreads up to 1000x1000 blocks)

[note]You should also be starting the ritual from the ground rather than in the air for the best results![/note]

Requirements:
- 15M LP (Archmage Blood Orb and >= 13 Runes of the Orb)
- 8 Crystal Belljars (one filled with each of: magicales, potentia, incendium, terrae, tenebrae, sanctus, aether, and aquasalus)
- 1 Sacrifice (Bottle a lesser demon/elemental in a soul vial)
- 1 Master Ritual Stone, 128 Ritual Stones, and 1 Blood Altar
- 1 Awakened Activation Crystal

Steps to the Ritual:
1. Use a Ritual Diviner to place all the Ritual Stones.
2. Place the Belljars atop the 8 pillars surrounding the center.
3. Link the Belljars to the Master Ritual Stone.
4. Place the Blood Altar on top of the Master Ritual Stone.
5. Start the ritual with the Awakened Activation Crystal.
6. The Belljars are drained of their reagents.
7. Kill the lesser demon/elemental with a Dagger of Sacrifice.
8. The Demon Portal is opened.

For more details and images, visit [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Convocation_of_the_Damned[/url]

[warn]The OW is now blacklisted for the portal. Too many people did not follow the recommendation and servers suffered for it. It also does not work well in the Personal Dimension.[/warn]

715.name: Imbued Spell Upgrades
715.desc: With these new shards you're able to craft the most powerful spell components. Maybe it's time to upgrade your mining spell?

716.name: Process Some Certus Quartz
716.desc: For the emitter and the sensor you'll need to get some certus quartz.

717.name: Good Circuits
717.desc:
Good circuits are an improvement on basic circuits. They're an MV-tier circuit, which is a component of all MV gregtech machines.

To open MV, you still need to make an EBF and do the Aluminium quest if you haven't already, since everything needs aluminium.

This quest also opens the Project Red quests - one of the early automation options.

718.name: Moron's Manual to Fecund Farms
718.desc:
If you ever wanted to know how crop breeding works, you are in the right place! Get some Crops - the sticks, that is - and some seeds from farming with a hoe or mattock. You can also place some plants and berries directly on crop sticks. Good plants to start with are flowers, wheat, cactus and sugarcane.

Make a plot of farmland with your tool and place water in the middle. For now, make 3x1 plots, separated by a row of dirt or cobble. You want to be able to see the center of each column of 3x1 plots all at once, like this:
FFFdddFFF
dddFFFddd
FFFdddFFF
dddFFFddd
FFFdWdFFF
dddFFFddd
FFFdddFFF
dddFFFddd
FFFdddFFF
Now place crop sticks in each end of the 3x1 plot. Then place the seeds on the sticks and wait a bit.

When they are almost fully grown you can put double crop sticks in the center spots by using crop sticks once more on already placed crop sticks. This center area can now grow new crops, even new species.

With some luck you might get a new unknown seed which can be scanned later.

Weeds need to be removed with a trowel or spade otherwise your field will become overgrown by them.

Using 3x1 plots is easy and not much work. Check them every 5-10 minutes to remove weeds and gather new seeds. If you forget, you only lose one or two seeds.

If you find a really nice seed, you can do 3x3 or 5x5 plots with the nice seed in the middle and others next to it for crossing. But this requires more babysitting since weeds could destroy the whole field.

Note that the more crops next to the double crop stick the faster new plants will show up, up to 4 obviously. This is also the way to get new versions of the same plant, you can't do it with just one parent like Agricraft.

Don't leave new crops in these intensive fields. Pull them out and hopefully get a seedbag. Put low stat seeds in production fields that don't require babysitting. Keep the high stat seeds for more crossing.

Crossing happens when two or more almost-mature crops are next to a double cropstick. There is a high chance of getting the same species as the parents. The rest of the time will be a mutation. For more details, check out the IC2 cropnalyzer quest.

719.name: First Breeding
719.desc:
New plants have grown on your crop sticks. You can harvest the crop by right-clicking it.

Left-clicking will destroy the plant and drop a seedbag. This is not guaranteed however, but the chances rise as the maturity does. Collect 8 seeds of any kind to complete this quest.

To unlock the next quest, you will need the LV scanner.

If you haven't already, consider building some Filing Cabinets. They make storing and moving large quantities of seeds a breeze. Plus they will properly sort the seeds based on name. To find a specific seed, type its name in the NEI search bar, and double-click the bar so it highlights yellow. Now you can look through the Filing Cabinet and find the exact seedbag you want.

Or use double chests and toss the ones you don't want.

1016.name: Integrated Processor
1016.desc:
This is the start of the first 4-circuit circuit line. This one doesn't really have a consistent naming scheme unlike the later ones, but it still works the same way, aside from the fact that you get your first EV and IV circuits from it, all the rest only have 1 new tier of circuit. Because of this you'll need to make 2 new CAs to finish it.

The first one is the Integrated Processor, the final type of MV circuits. You also don't need to craft any other circuits to make it, so it's the cheapest. This repeats each circuit tier.

[note]This and all circuits past it need a cleanroom.[/note]

1017.name: Processor Assembly
1017.desc: This is the second step to making EV and IV circuits.

1018.name: Central Processing Unit
1018.desc: Integrated Processors need central processing units. You can get these by cutting a central processing unit wafer.

1019.name: Small Coil
1019.desc:
The Processor Assembly circuit needs some small coils. You can make these a couple different ways.

[note]If you're an overachiever and created SMD Inductors already you may hold 24 in your hand to skip this quest.[/note]

982.name: Sulfuric Acid
982.desc: Want some sulfuric acid? Buy some!

983.name: Engineering Processors
983.desc: Want some engineering processors? Buy some!

984.name: Magical Saplings
984.desc: Silverwood saplings are always rare to find. Want some? Buy now, and I'll throw in some Greatwood ones too!

985.name: Time to Kill (Ghast)
985.desc: They shoot you with explosive fireballs but they are weak when you hit them or knock the fireballs back with a weapon. Better upgrade your armor with fire and blast protection.

986.name: Is This the End? No it's the END Dimension
986.desc:
You investigated the pearls, and managed to reactivate them to become eyes. This has shown you that the source of the disturbance seems to be coming out of the portal frames inside the strongholds, deep underground. They seem strangely drawn towards its mysterious depths. Perhaps they can be used to reactivate it?

Hint: Throwing Ender Eyes in the air in the Overworld will guide you to the nearest stronghold. From time to time Ender Eyes are destroyed in this process, so make extras. Try to do so in a place without obstructions so you can see where it went.

987.name: Upgraded RF Batteries
987.desc:
Capacitor Banks are upgraded RF batteries which can store the RF power you generate.

BTW, you can also use capacitors to upgrade your EnderIO machines. They'll use more power, but also work faster.

988.name: Bigger, Better, Chargier?
988.desc: The Vibrant Capacitor Bank is the largest non-creative RF Battery from EIO. It can store 25 million RF in a single block.

989.name: Vibrant Alloy
989.desc: Vibrant alloy is a high tier EnderIO alloy made out of ender eye, energetic alloy and chrome dust.

990.name: Energetic Alloy
990.desc:
Energetic alloy is a new alloy from EnderIO made out of gold, conductive iron and black steel dust. Double-layer capacitors need it, as does the HV energy hatch.

[note]The direct mixer recipe has 7 ingredients, so you need an EV mixer or a multiblock to use it. You have to use the other one for now.[/note]

991.name: Dark Steel Armor Basic Upgrades
991.desc: In order to upgrade one armor item with basic upgrades, you need a vibrant crystal and 10 XP levels. Some upgrades can be applied directly, but more advanced ones will require empowering the armor first.

992.name: Go Find Some Tropical Bees
992.desc: Next you'll need to find some tropical bees. The hives can be found in Jungle biomes. Be careful as these bees are very aggressive and can poison you. Collect the silky combs and extract some silk wisp from those in order to make some Apiarist's clothes. Try to get pristine bees. If you're having trouble finding them, you can use Nature's Compass to search for a specific biome.

993.name: Silky Combs and Propoli
993.desc:
Collect silky combs and extract some silk wisp out of it in order to make some Apiarist's clothes.

This can take a very long time, so parallelize!

Use a GregTech or Forestry Centrifuge to get Silky Propoli. For higher tier (and useful) combs you need a GT centrifuge.

994.name: The Centrifuge
994.desc:
The Forestry Centrifuge is able to centrifuge up to 9 items out of bee combs. It uses RF energy.

Latter bee combs need the GT one though, so keep that in mind.

995.name: Woven Silk
995.desc:
Centrifuge your silky propoli to get some silk wisp at a chance of 60 percent. Put 9 silk wisp in the Forestry carpenter or GregTech assembler to get woven silk.

This fine fabric can be used for crafting Apiarist's Clothes or to upgrade a Forestry Backpacks to 45 slots.

996.name: Apiarist's Cloth
996.desc: If you have collected 24 woven silk you can make a complete Apiarist's suit to be protected from bee stings and other bad bee effects. You can also use the suit parts to upgrade your EnderIO Dark Steel Armor to have the same effect.

997.name: Dark Steel Armor Advanced Upgrades
997.desc:
Your Dark Steel armor can be upgraded in various ways by combining it with various other items. You will need an anvil and a lot of XP.

Make sure when making the potions to only do three at a time, so the NBT data matches.

998.name: Dark Steel Tool and Weapon Basic Upgrades
998.desc: In order to upgrade a Dark Steel weapon or tool, you need a vibrant crystal, 10 XP levels, and your trusty anvil.

999.name: Dark Steel Tool and Weapon Advanced Upgrades
999.desc: Your weapons and tools can be upgraded even more by adding capacitors and various other items.

1000.name: RF Solar Panel
1000.desc: It's possible to power RF machines with a solar panel. It outputs 10 RF/t. Perfect for low use RF machines.

1001.name: RF Solar Panel Tier 2
1001.desc: The upgraded RF solar panel is much better. It outputs 40 RF/t.

1002.name: Enchanted Earth
1002.desc: Enchanted Earth makes saplings and seeds planted on it grow faster. When stacked on top of each other it has a chance to add ticks to the block above accumulating all the way to the top. This will randomly speed up growth a bit.

1003.name: Power of the Sun at MV Level
1003.desc: The next solar panel you can make is the MV one. It gives 128 EU/t. You'll need to make some sunnarium first though.

1004.name: Power of the Sun 1x1 MV
1004.desc: Put those 128 EU panels on a block to make them placeable in the world.

1005.name: Indium
1005.desc:
At EV you're able to make Indium. Indium cannot be found in Ore form [note](except in super small amounts on Ross)[/note].

First you need to make Indium concentrate out of purified galena and sphalerite ore, then mix in some aluminium to get Indium. Indium is used in large amounts later on, automate this ASAP.

You also get lead, zinc, silver, sulfur, and water back from the lead-zinc solution. So it's not like you don't get the materials from the ores at all, just less of them.

1006.name: Power of the Sun at HV Level
1006.desc: The next solar panel you can make is the HV one. It generates 512 EU/t. First you need to produce enriched sunnarium by mixing irradiated uranium and sunnarium. The Avaritia 9x9 dire crafting table is required to make this and later panels.

1007.name: Power of the Sun 1x1 HV
1007.desc: Put those 512 EU panels on a block to make them placeable in the world.

1008.name: Books
1008.desc:
Books can be found easily enough in villages or in the roguelike dungeons surface brick house. I'm sure they have a recipe too.

Now glue on the other hand...or should I say horse? Try and get a horse inside a smeltery and feed it hay to keep it alive.

Or you can wait until after you go to the Nether and get some sulfur. Either way.

1009.name: Fish Trapping 2.0
1009.desc:
Fishing can be very boring. Why not automate it with a fish trap?

This is fish trap 2.0.

1010.name: Fresh Water and Milk
1010.desc:
Many cooking recipes need milk and water. Crafting it in the crafting table can be very tedious.

It is much easier using a Forestry worktable. Move all your buckets into the worktable, and shift-click to craft them all at once.

1011.name: Find Bauxite
1011.desc:
The Bauxite vein contains two ores that get you Titanium, Bauxite and Ilmenite. Bauxite also contains Aluminium, as does (obviously) the Aluminium ore. You want tons of Titanium, so collect a bunch.

When you're mining you'll surely pick up some Meteoric Iron from small ores, enough to make your rocket. But if it's not enough, or you want more, you can also mine the meteors that fall out of the sky.

1012.name: Rutile
1012.desc:
You've gotten some Rutile as a byproduct by now, right? Or EBF'd some Ilmenite?

Rutile, Chlorine and Carbon makes Titaniumtetrachloride, and some CO. You can recycle this for Carbon if you want to. Best use a Large Chemical Reactor in that case to get the full recipe.

Combining TiCl4 with Magnesium in an HV EBF will give you a Hot Titanium ingot, and Magnesiumchloride. You can recycle this as well to get all the Magnesium and Chlorine back using some Sodium.

1013.name: SMD Circuit Components
1013.desc:
Surface-mount devices are more advanced circuit parts. They're needed for circuits starting next tier and are cheaper to craft than basic capacitors, transistors, diodes and resistors. Once you get a reliable polyethylene setup, you can save a lot of resources by using SMD components.

Be sure to check how much gallium you have available. It might not be worth it to use SMD diodes and transistors until you have a steady supply with an HV macerator or bees. Also look into IC2 crops to 4x your zinc, sphalerite, and bauxite.

1014.name: Workstations, Your First EV Circuit
1014.desc:
Your first EV circuit is the Workstation.

You can make Blue Alloy with Silver and Electrotine in an Alloy Smelter. If you don't have Electrotine, you can make it from Redstone and Electrum in a Mixer. Have fun!

1015.name: Empty Plastic Board
1015.desc:
Plastic circuit boards require a plastic plate with Sulfuric Acid to make an Empty Board. By adding copper foil and Iron(III) Chloride or Sodium Persulfate you can craft a plastic circuit board. Later on you can make them more efficiently.

[note]PCBs are used in the circuits in HV tier.[/note]

768.name: You Smell Like a Distillery!
768.desc:
This bad boy will tremendously boost the byproducts of your cracked fuel or pyrolyse oven. This way you can make industrial TNT, polyethylene, polytetrafluoroethylene and other plastics more efficiently. You'll also need this for the platline.

You'll need to make EV circuits in a cleanroom to build this machine.

For hatches, you can choose your own tier. 1 MV energy does most of the useful recipes. 2 MV energy or 1 HV energy hatch will cover almost all the rest.

You will need 1 input hatch, and at least 5 output hatches. An output bus will capture any solid outputs.

[note]PS: I listed all the DT recipes on the spreadsheet for you to look at in a convenient format. Now you can easily compare which oil path to take![/note]

769.name: Playing With Dust
769.desc: This machine will help you turn dust into crystals which you will need for high tier recipes.

771.name: Dust to Crystals
771.desc: Use the autoclave to crystallize your dust. You can also make Raw Carbon Fiber out of Carbon Dust, and do other things.

772.name: Precision is Required
772.desc:
You'll use this a lot to make high tier circuits, and for other recipes.

[note]You'll soon find that everything you use the PLE on needs a cleanroom, so make sure you start on that.[/note]

773.name: Raw Carbon Mesh
773.desc: This way of making carbon plates requires autoclaving carbon dust. There are 4 different fluids you can use, check NEI to see which gives how much.

774.name: Bio Organic Mesh
774.desc:
If you want to save the environment you can autoclave 16 plantballs to get raw bio fiber with the following liquids:

Biomass        33% chance
Methanol       50% chance
Fuel            90% chance
Nitro-diesel 100% chance

However, making carbon plates this way will require an electric blast furnace.

775.name: Carbon Parts
775.desc:
These are the first parts you will need for your beloved nano armor.

[note]To actually complete the nano armor you will need EV circuits and nano crystals (see Storing and Transforming EU).[/note]

776.name: Seeing Things Brighter
776.desc:
The nano helmet is a bit special. It will provide you with night vision as long as you have energy!

[note]If you got these from a lootbag, hold these in your hand after completing the "Carbon Parts" quest to skip this quest.[/note]

777.name: Firestone
777.desc:
Don't burn yourself.

[note]It's also possible to get Firestone from the Sulfur Bee, though it has a notably smaller comb output.[/note]

778.name: Carbon Plates
778.desc: Carbon plates are used for a bunch of stuff, including eventually the nanosuit. Go check out the armor chapter.

779.name: Carbon Skin
779.desc:
You can stop worrying about some mobs for now. This will help you survive a bit and will give you some of that SWAG. Your journey doesn't stop here traveller, as quantum armor awaits you!

[note]PS: The nano suit also acts as hazmat gear.[/note]

780.name: Organic Way
780.desc: Bio carbon plates are made out of raw carbon mesh. Put it in the electric blast furnace to make carbon plates.

781.name: Still Not Enough Charcoal?
781.desc:
This oven is the best way to get charcoal and creosote (x50 faster than Railcraft's coke oven) and other byproducts. You will need tons of bronze, iron and steel to build it, but it's worth it. Finally, it will be possible to dismantle the charcoal pit.

With Cupronickel coils the oven is a bit slower. Each coil tier speeds up the charcoal production.

782.name: Monocrystalline Silicon Boules
782.desc: To make wafers, you need to make monocrystalline silicon boules first. The process takes 450 seconds and requires solar grade silicon dust and a small gallium arsenide crystal in the electric blast furnace. You can make 16 wafers out of every boule.

783.name: We Need Big Toys
783.desc:
All your little turbines take up too much space? Are you running out of water? It's time to solve these problems. This Large Steam Turbine can help you produce a tremendous amount of energy. It will also distill water for you, so you can use the same water again inside your Large Boiler. Don't forget to make a rotor for the turbine and put inside the controller, otherwise this multiblock won't do anything.

Each turbine has a specific optimal flow, the Steam flow that gives the highest fuel efficiency, the value shown on the turbine's tooltip. This is the flow you want to match, and you can use a Fluid Regulator as a cover to make it exactly what you want, but you can go beyond this flow to get an even higher EU/t value, at a loss of fuel efficiency. You can make turbines in 4 different sizes, with larger turbines producing more EU/t, but with varying amounts of fuel efficiency. Generally, Large is the best due to having the highest fuel efficiency and a good optimal flow rate

When using turbines, be careful of flow rates. To find the optimal flow rate, you divide the optimal energy flow rate listed on the turbine, by the energy value for the plasma. You can also use the Large Turbine Calculator (there's a link on the main spreadsheet, which is linked to in discord), or scan the multiblock's controller while feeding it a minimal amount of plasma to see what the optimal flow is. Make sure to use a Fluid Regulator set to this flow rate for highest fuel efficiency, or go beyond it to get more EU/t at a cost to efficiency, with Steam Turbines being the worst at retaining efficiency beyond the optimal flow.

If you go beyond the optimal flow, you will get a higher EU/t value, but less fuel efficiency, and the maximum EU/t value depends on the turbine type and its Overflow Efficiency tier. For this type of turbine, tier 1 reaches the max at 150% of optimal flow to produce 112% of the EU/t, tier 2 reaches the max at 200% of optimal flow to produce 133% of the EU/t and tier 3 reaches the max at 250% of optimal flow to produce 156% of the EU/t. The closer you get to this maximum, the more fuel efficiency you're throwing away. Don't exceed your dynamo hatch's maximum EU/t output, though, or you will have problems!

Use NEI with the appropriate long rod to find assembler recipes that will work for you. Or the spreadsheet. Long magnalium for small turbines, long titanium for normal turbines, etc.

Pre-moon, the best rotors are Shadow Metal or Vibrant Alloy.

Post-moon, the best HV rotors are Shadow Metal or Oriharukon (this is IV-tier). Luckily, turbines are now fully functional with multi-amp dynamos, so give those a try if you need extra amps!

For additional choices, see the spreadsheet and Large Turbine Calculator.

784.name: It's Time to Get More Steam
784.desc:
As soon as you have the ability to store energy in large quantities, it would be necessary to have a way to generate energy in large quantities. You need a large bronze boiler. But in order to make this boiler, you will need a mountain of bronze. This boiler produces 800L of steam per tick. This is enough to run 10 basic steam turbines.

The large boiler can use either water or distilled water. Water is consumed at a rate of 1L of water per 150L of steam.

To make a large boiler you must make a 3x3x5 structure with Bronze Pipe Casings in the center of the middle 3 levels. The lowest level must contain at least 1 or 2 input hatches, 1 or 2 input buses, 3 or 4 fireboxes, 1 muffler, and one maintenance hatch. The lowest level can not contain any plated bricks. All hatches, buses, and mufflers must have their back side touching a firebox. The output hatch can be placed on any of the upper 4 levels.

Warning: Different from other boilers, Large Boilers must contain water at all times while active. If at any point the Boiler runs out of water while it is active, it will explode.

785.name: Transistor
785.desc:
For the next tier we need advanced circuits. To make advanced circuits, you need transistors.

[note]If you already made SMD Transistors you can hold 16 of them to skip this quest![/note]

786.name: Steam is Never Enough
786.desc: Now you can make Advanced Circuits, which means it's time to build a Large Steel Boiler. Each one of these produces 1000 EU/t in Steam, which means you will need several Steam Turbines to convert it all to power! Keep in mind that the MV ones are less efficient, so calculate your losses when building your setup.

787.name: Moooooore Steam
787.desc:
The Titanium Large Boiler is a distant cousin to the Bronze and Steel Boilers, and it has a significant difference. Instead of accepting just about any burnable fuel, it will only accept specific ones and reject all others. You can see which ones are accepted by looking at the appropriate NEI tab, but the Solid Super Fuels are guaranteed to work.

This is a big downside, but the upside is that these boilers output respectable power even in these tiers. Without any other inputs, this boiler produces Superheated Steam at a flow rate matching 4000 EU/t, on turbines running at 100% fuel efficiency. If this becomes 150%, then the steam output will produce 6000 EU/t, almost the same as a boosted Large Combustion Engine!

To get all the power from this, though, you will need an HP Steam Turbine, followed by a regular Steam Turbine, or more than one of each if the flow rate is too big for the turbine material you've chosen. Make sure to send the Distilled Water back to the boilers!

788.name: Even More Steam? Are You Sure?
788.desc:
The Large Tungstensteel Boiler is the biggest and baddest of the four. Like the Titanium one, it is a specialized boiler that only accepts specific fuels, mainly the Solid Super Fuels, but it will produce Superheated Steam at a rate equivalent to 16000 EU/t, at 100% fuel efficiency. If you have good Steam Turbines, especially Oriharukon ones, you will get much more power than that, around 24000 EU/t or more.

Depending on how many of these you build, you might need a huge amount of turbines. To solve that problem, consider going a bit above the optimal flow on each turbine, or even switching to Loose mode, although this is only a good choice if you can match the optimal flow in that mode.

789.name: Energy Crystals
789.desc: Make energium out of ruby and redstone dusts in the MV mixer. Then use the autoclave to make Energy Crystals. Energy crystals can store 1 million EU and work as an HV battery.

790.name: Nano Crystal
790.desc: Nano crystals are used for making the nano armor.

791.name: MV Precision Laser Engraver
791.desc: Advanced circuits need an MV precision laser engraver. Let's craft one.

792.name: Silicon Wafer
792.desc:
To create some silicon wafers, put the monocrystalline silicon boule in a cutting machine. It would be best to make the MV version because you'll need it soon for ILCs, RAM, and other chips.

You can also use wafers to make diodes more efficiently if you have polyethylene as well.

793.name: Good Integrated Circuits
793.desc: By the way, did you ever try looking at the oredict for circuits? You can tell what tier they are from that. They go: Primitive (ULV), Basic (LV), Good (MV), Advanced (HV), etc. Now make your Good Integrated Circuits.

794.name: Integrated Circuit
794.desc: In order to make HV machines, you need HV circuits. The first one you can make is the Advanced Circuit from IC2. You'll need to make LV circuits to make MV circuits to make the HV circuits. Yay for recursive crafting! Start with the LV Integrated Logic Circuit. And no, you can't use the IC2 Electronic Circuits.

795.name: Basic Circuit Assembler
795.desc:
Advanced (HV) circuits require an LV circuit assembling machine. Basic (LV) and Good (MV) ones can also be made much more efficiently with this machine.

However, all circuit assemblers need higher tier circuits than the other machines of their tier. This means you have to use MV circuits instead of the LV ones you've been using until now. I know you want it, so make it already!

796.name: Sensor Lens
796.desc: The night vision goggles need special sensor lenses to work.

797.name: Advanced Heat Exchanger
797.desc: The night vision goggles need advanced heat exchangers to work.

798.name: Iron Capped Greatwood Wand
798.desc:
Making a gold capped greatwood wand requires 30 vis, but your old wand's limit is 25. So first create an iron or copper capped greatwood wand reload it with 50 vis and then later on in the next quest create a gold capped greatwood wand.

You can always press the NEI 'U' command on the caps, the core, or the boss ingredient to find the correct GT:NH recipe for the wands.

799.name: Don't Try to Dive With It
799.desc: Combining a scuba helmet with a nano helmet in an assembling machine will give you a nano scuba helmet. You'd better not try to dive with it because you can't. It is a part of the new quantum suit helmet.

800.name: Plated Leggings - Better Not Try to Wear It
800.desc: Combining stainless steel platings with some nano leggings in the assembler gives you nano plated leggings. Better not try to wear them because you can't. They are part of the new quantum suit leggings.

801.name: Don't Try to Walk With It
801.desc: Combining some rubber boots with nano boots in the Assembler gives you Nano Rubber Boots. Better not try to put them on because you can't. They are part of the new quantum suit boots.

802.name: Iridium Plated
802.desc: Plating all your armor parts with iridium alloy casings gives them a better durability. Before you can wear these quantum armor parts you need to activate them with some quantum crystals.

803.name: Quantum Armor
803.desc: The quantum armor is a high tier and very durable armor with a lot of features like night vision, fall damage reduction and running very fast.

805.name: Lapotron Dust
805.desc: Lapotron crystals require you to mix your energium dust with some lapis dust. You should check out the Storing and Transforming EU tab for the lapotron crystal and the Lapotronic Supercapacitor Multiblock.

806.name: Lapotron Crystal
806.desc: With your new HV mixer you can make lapotron dust. Place it in an autoclave to make some raw lapotron crystals. With an EV assembler and advanced circuits you can make lapotron crystals which store up to 10 million EU.

807.name: Quantum Crystal
807.desc: For your quantum armor you need some quantum crystals. Place a lapotron crystal in your precision laser to get a quantum crystal. You'll still need to be in IV to make it though.

808.name: Mixing at HV Level
808.desc: Mixing better alloys needs a better Mixer.

809.name: Dire Crafting Table
809.desc: The Avaritia 9x9 dire crafting table...is no longer used to make rockets :(. But you can still make it, if you want solars, kinetics, or whatnot. First of all you need 81 crafting tables to make a double compressed crafting table, a few HV components, wafers and a crystal matrix. And yes, you do need that many nether stars.

810.name: Rocket Tier 1
810.desc:
After hours of hard work and a few stacks of TNT your first rocket is finally ready. But before you can go to the Moon you need to actually craft it.

If you've crafted the rocket with a chest, there's a crafting table recipe that will turn it into the normal rocket that completes this quest. Check NEI.

[note]In case you forgot to complete any, holding the rocket in your hand will unlock all pre-requisite quests in addition to this one.[/note]

811.name: Tier 1 Control Computer
811.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

812.name: Tier 1 Heavy Duty Plates
812.desc: Rockets require heavy duty plates for the main hull. Let's craft 37 of these plates out of compressed aluminium plates, compressed bronze plates and compressed steel plates.

813.name: Rocket Nose Cone
813.desc: Every rocket needs a nose cone.

814.name: Rocket Fins
814.desc: Every rocket needs a few rocket fins.

815.name: Rocket Engine
815.desc: Every rocket needs a rocket engine. Better craft two, because the Moon lander also needs one.

816.name: Moon Lander
816.desc: The Moon lander is required for a soft landing. You will need an additional frequency module for hearing things on planets, and a second seat for a Moon buggy you may build later.

817.name: Buggy Seat
817.desc: The buggy seat is part of the Moon lander.

818.name: Frequency Module
818.desc: The frequency Module will help you hear sounds properly while in space (it's also used with the telemetry unit so that your buddies can keep track of you from your base, if you are playing with friends). You need it in your inventory and in the Moon lander.

819.name: Small Fuel Canister
819.desc: Every rocket needs fuel storage canisters. For the tier 1 rocket you need to craft two of them.

820.name: Rocket Launch Pad
820.desc:
Every rocket needs a launch pad. Build a 3x3 launch pad and place your rocket on top of it.

Storms and other events that affect entities can knock your rocket off (but not lightning warp anymore), so make sure to not leave it alone.

[warn]Before liftoff, make sure you clear the airspace above you![/warn]

Be sure to check out [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Moon_Travel_Checklist[/url] for a Departure Checklist!

[note]Also note that they emit pollution, lots of it. So either put it far from your base, or use a scrubber while launching. You probably won't have to worry until T4 or so though.[/note]

821.name: Gas 'Er Up
821.desc:
To pump fuel into your rocket you need a fuel loader. Place it next to any side of the launch pad. The green side is for energy, while the yellow is for fuel. Pipe it in if you don't want to lose your cells.

You can also power it with batteries, if you're worried about it draining your power, or don't want to set up the infrastructure.

[note]Rockets keep their fuel in them on landing now, so you don't need to take this with you anymore unless you want to go to multiple places in one trip. Or you didn't fill it all the way or something.[/note]

822.name: Oxygen Gear
822.desc: Oxygen gear is used to attach oxygen tanks to the oxygen mask. This is absolutely vital for any astronaut.

823.name: Oxygen Mask
823.desc: The oxygen mask is part of the system used to breathe on planets without oxygen, the others being oxygen gear and oxygen tanks.

824.name: Oxygen Tanks
824.desc:
The Light oxygen tank is an oxygen storage tank that can hold up to 1000 units of air. It is used to provide a portable source of oxygen while in areas that do not have an oxygen source of their own.

Usage:
Up to two oxygen tanks can be worn at a time. To wear any type of oxygen tank, open your inventory and click on the Galacticraft tab at the top of the menu. Here you will see the available spots for your various types of equipment. Simply place your tank(s) in one of the slots with a gray picture of an oxygen tank. Other types of oxygen tanks include the medium oxygen tank, which holds twice the amount of oxygen as a light tank, and the heavy oxygen tank, which holds three times the amount of a light tank.

825.name: Copper Ore
825.desc:
Can be found between Y 5-60 either as "Copper Ore" or "Chalcopyrite". Most of the time you'll find it together with "Iron" and "Pyrite".

To see where an ore can be found, click the raw ore in NEI, and it will show you more details. Works with small ores too.

826.name: Iron Ore
826.desc:
With iron tools, you are able to mine most of the Overworld ores. You've probably already found iron or magnetite, given how common it is. So bring me some ores and make some iron ingots out of them.

To see where an ore can be found, click the raw ore in NEI, and it will show you more details. Works with small ores too.

827.name: Rocket Fuel
827.desc:
For every flight the rocket needs some fuel. So refine some fuel for your rocket.
This quest chain will guide you through the progression of rocket fuels.

For your T1 & T2 rockets you have 2 options. You can either use Cetane-Boosted Diesel or make RP-1 rocket fuel.

For the moon, a full rocket leaving from the Overworld should have enough fuel left to make it back, but you might want to take extra just in case.

[note]Rocket Fuel usage is not based on the destination nor the type of fuel used. Rather, it is based on the tier of the rocket, with higher tiers both storing more fuel, and consuming less. Also, the gravity of the planet affects how much fuel is used, with lower gravity locations using less fuel to launch.[/note]

828.name: Oxygen Collector
828.desc: The oxygen collector is a block that gathers oxygen from leaves and farm plants to use in an oxygen bubble distributor, oxygen sealer, oxygen compressor, and other oxygen-reliant machines. If you plan to build a Moon base, which needs a continuous oxygen supply, you will need to build one.

829.name: Oxygen Compressor
829.desc: The oxygen compressor is used to fill empty or depleted oxygen tanks. Connect an oxygen pipe to the blue ring input side and a power source to the gray square input side. Place an oxygen tank in the bottom right slot and it will take oxygen from the input and place it into the tank.

830.name: Parachute
830.desc:
A parachute is used to prevent death upon re-entry into the Overworld's atmosphere. The parachute is an essential part of your journey to space. You should never journey into space without it. There will even be a warning if you try to launch without one on you.

Use the assembler to save materials.

831.name: Advanced Wafers
831.desc: The advanced wafer is used for more advanced machines such as the advanced solar panel or the Avaritia dire crafting table. It is also used in the creation of a space station.

832.name: Wafers
832.desc: The basic wafer is used for creating basic machines such as the basic solar panel, the fuel loader, the energy storage module and the compressor.

833.name: Purge Your Warp
833.desc:
The warp you have accumulated over time is starting to become a problem. Making bath salts to clean off the warp is only a temporary solution. To truly get rid of it, you need something more powerful, a pure tear. If you collect a few things for me, I will give you one. Think wisely about using it, since it's a very long journey to unlock it through research.

PS: You need an LuV-powered EBF for the osmiridium.

834.name: Circuit Boards
834.desc: For every circuit you need a circuit board. Craft a coated circuit board and use copper wire to finish it.

835.name: Resistor
835.desc:
Every basic circuit needs two resistors to work.

There are better versions you can make later.

836.name: Gallium Arsenide Crystal
836.desc:
The diode requires gallium arsenide, and you may be wondering how to get gallium or arsenic this early in the game.

Sphalerite and zinc are the primary sources of gallium at this tier.

You can find sphalerite in sulfur veins at Y 5-20, and the small zinc ores in the Overworld or the Nether at Y 80-210. You won't find much on the Overworld because of obvious reasons. To get additional zinc crushed ore drops, use a Tinker's Construct tool with the Luck (Fortune) enchant on it, probably the best use for all that lapis you got while looking for calcite. Or use a miner, if you don't mind how slow it is.

- Sphalerite can be electrolyzed for a small chance of a gallium dust. Better put in a lot or you might get unlucky.
- Zinc crushed ore can be washed and then thermal centrifuged or forge hammered and then centrifuged. But you don't always get the crushed ore as a drop, the impure dust doesn't give gallium.

You can get arsenic from realgar small ores in the Nether at Y 15-85. Centrifuge the dusts in a centrifuge.

Other sources are Tinker's Construct cobalt ore in the nether and cobaltite ore in the Twilight Forest. You can use your EBF for that. You also need to level up your pickaxe to ardite or make an Ironwood Pickaxe or LV Miner to harvest cobalt ore.

Another way to get gallium and arsenic is with XP buckets in a crafting grid if you're really desperate.

With your EBF you can combine these two dusts to make a Gallium Arsenide Crystal.

837.name: ILC and RAM
837.desc:
Advanced circuits need integrated logic circuits and random access memory chips.
You can get those by cutting the ILC and RAM wafers.

838.name: Advanced Circuits
838.desc:
