Finally, the HV circuit! To make it, you need good integrated circuits, RAM, an integrated logic circuit, and transistors.

By the way, circuits get cheaper to make as you go along, but not till HV.

839.name: Handle Tiny Piles Like a K1ng
839.desc:
Don't you hate having to manually compress all those tiny piles? Well, let me show you how to automate that. A combination of type filter, chest buffer (or super buffer in HV) and packager frees you from that annoying task. The chest buffer can hold up to 9 stacks of tiny piles and the super buffer can hold 256 stacks of tiny dusts, which is more than enough. You can set its mode with a screwdriver on the output side. Set it to 9 to export 9 tiny piles at a time and let the packager compress these into regular dusts.

Super buffers can be lag sources, so be careful to keep them as empty as possible. If you're using a multiblock packager, be sure to change the buffer from Stocking mode to Transfer Size mode.

840.name: Butane and Propane
840.desc:
You can crack and distill a lot of different fluids out of your already distilled products, such as Butane, Butene, Butadiene, Benzene, Ethane, Ethylene, Helium, Hydrogen, Methane, Octane, Propane, Propene, Toluene, and even more Light Fuel, Heavy Fuel, or Naphtha. You can then crack them down even further too sometimes. Many of these are only used for power. Go check out the spreadsheet for more details than this quest can encompass.

For now, get some Butane and Propane.

841.name: Tetranitromethane
841.desc: Bio diesel and diesel can be boosted using tetranitromethane. Ethenone and nitric acid are required to make tetranitromethane in a chemical reactor.

842.name: Methane Gas
842.desc: Methane gas has a burn value of 104,000 EU per cell and can be burned in the gas turbine.

843.name: Methanol
843.desc: Methanol has a burn value of 84,000 EU per cell and can be used to make bio diesel and other fluids. It's also used to make certain rocket fuels.

844.name: Ethanol
844.desc: Ethanol has a burn value of 192,000 EU per cell can be used to make bio diesel and other fluids.

845.name: Bio Diesel Fuel
845.desc: With ethanol or methanol and seed oil or fish oil you can make bio diesel. Its burn value is 320,000 EU per cell, less than regular diesel, but more renewable. Diesel or bio diesel are needed for cetane-boosted diesel.

846.name: O like Oxygen
846.desc:
Oxygen can be made in lots of different ways. Most commonly you'll either electrolyze it out of cassiterite sand, sugar from sugar canes or sugar beets, water, or various other oxygen-rich dusts, or centrifuge it from brown or yellow limonite dust.

Later on, water electrolyzing or air centrifuging is probably going to be your main source.

847.name: Ethylene
847.desc:
You can make ethylene several different ways

- Ethanol and sulfuric acid in a Chemical Reactor
- Ethanol in a Chemical Dehydrator
- Distilling various gases (i.e. wood gas) in a Distillery
- Obtain it from Oil in the Working with Oil tab

[note]You will need much more than 12 cells later on so keep that in mind.[/note]

848.name: Seed Oil
848.desc: Pam's peanuts are a good seed oil source, and can be sped up with sprinklers. Or you can use IC2 crops to get Rapeseed. Another option is Forestry tree nuts such as Hazelnuts, Butternuts, or the King of nuts, coconuts. Or you can be lame and get them from regular seeds like wheat seeds.

849.name: Sodium Hydroxide
849.desc:
Sodium hydroxide is needed for bio diesel. You can make it out of sodium and water, or out of salt water.

Sodium hydroxide is also used in advanced aluminium production.

850.name: Ethenone
850.desc:
Hopefully you saved some sulfuric acid. If not, make some more. Together with acetic acid you can make ethenone.

Save the ethenone for HV, you'll need it to make tetranitromethane for cetane boosted diesel.

851.name: Nitric Acid
851.desc: Nitric acid can be made out of water, oxygen and nitrogen dioxide.

852.name: Acetic Acid
852.desc: Acetic acid can be made out of Oxygen and Ethylene. Alternatively, it can be made out of Biomass, Wood Vinegar or Vinegar. It is an important part of making Ethenone on your way to Cetane-Boosted Diesel.

853.name: Nitrogen Dioxide
853.desc: To make nitrogen dioxide you need to combine oxygen gas and nitric oxide in a chemical reactor.

855.name: Nitric Oxide
855.desc: To make nitric oxide you need to combine oxygen and ammonia in a chemical reactor.

856.name: 1,1-Dimethylhydrazine
856.desc: Use your Formaldehyde to make 1,1-Dimethylhydrazine. You need to combine it with some Hydrazine and Hydrogen along with a Formaldehyde Catalyst in a Chemical Plant.

857.name: Formaldehyde
857.desc: Combining Oxygen and Methanol along with a Formaldehyde Catalyst in a Chemical Plant produces some Formaldehyde.

858.name: Chef Training
858.desc:
I need some ingredients to help me train since they don't grow well where I am, could you provide some? I'll give you the results in return.

[note]You only need to turn in one set each time you complete the quest.[/note]

860.name: Titanium, Finally
860.desc: You just need to cool down those ingots in your Vacuum Freezer and you'll get your first Titanium.

861.name: Tungstensteel
861.desc: Tungstensteel is needed in all machines for the next tier. Can you get enough to advance?

862.name: Your First Bow
862.desc:
Since vanilla bows are only craftable in the assembling machine and mobs don't drop them very often you should make a Tinkers bow.

Wood would be the best material for now. Craft the bow limbs out of wood and the bow string out of strings. The arrow that has the most durability is bone, so craft the stick out of bone. It is much lighter but less durable than a regular arrow. Use a flint tip to make it repairable with flint.

You can repair your bow the same way as all the other Tinkers tools. The arrows can be regained by repairing them or picking them up from the floor. The arrows should be in your inventory if you want to pick up the ones you fired.

863.name: Bronze Javelin
863.desc: The bronze javelin is a bit expensive and has only 7 throws, yet it is a good mid-range weapon. With one bronze ingot you can replenish your stack of javelins.

864.name: Your First Flint Throwing Knife
864.desc:
Is the bone bow too expensive for your taste? Then make a flint throwing knife instead. You need flint and a stick to get 12 knives. The durability and the attack damage are very low but it still helps with killing mobs from a safe distance.

The knives should be in your inventory if you want to pick up the ones you fired.

865.name: Monster Trap
865.desc:
If you're lucky enough to spawn near a sand- or rainforest biome, you can find some quicksand.

Collect a stack of it, dig a small ditch, and fill it with the quicksand to protect your "dirt base" home. Just don't fall in!

866.name: The Transcontinental Railroad
866.desc: All the different mining veins that have been found seem to be really far apart. One way to shorten the travel time is to create a railroad from your base to the different mines. Sadly this requires a large amount of both iron and creosote. Let's not forget to place the last symbolic rivet on one of your new train lines.

867.name: Lapis, Lazurite, Sodalite, Calcite
867.desc:
In order to make your bricked blast furnace you need to find a lapis vein containing calcite.

You can find the vein in the overworld at Y 20-50.

868.name: Gypsum
868.desc: To make bricks for the blast furnace you need concrete and gypsum. Gypsum can be found in a vein together with basaltic and granitic mineral sand, and fullers earth. Y 50-60 and only in the overworld.

869.name: Sifting Machine
869.desc:
Do you need flawless or exquisite diamonds, emeralds, rubies or other gems? Or, interested in getting more flint, coal, or whatnot out of your ore? Well then it's time for you to make a Sifting Machine.

You can find small Zinc ores (look in NEI) or you can search the Nether for Sphalerite.

The Sifter only takes Purified ores, but you can get these easily by throwing Crushed ores into a Cauldron with water.

[note]Note that you need Polyethylene to make this machine![/note]

870.name: Smelt All the Things...
870.desc:
Smelting up to nine items at once sound nice? With a bit of steel you can make a railcraft Steam Oven. 2x2x2

Hint: All furnace recipes work just fine, but you cannot use it to make charcoal from wood.

871.name: Molds, Molds, Molds
871.desc:
After you can make steel and have a tinkers smelter you can make molds. Molds can be used in the alloy smelter and the fluid solidifier. You need them to make vacuum tubes, ingots, gears and more.

[note]PS: The 'shapes' are for the Extruder.[/note]

1020.name: Iron (III) Chloride
1020.desc:
Iron (III) Chloride can be made by first making hydrochloric acid out of hydrogen and chlorine. Then combine this with some iron dust to make Iron(III) Chloride.
You can etch your board with it.

1021.name: Sodium Persulfate
1021.desc: Sodium Persulfate can be made in the electrolyzer out of sodium bisulfate dust. Sodium bisulfate is made out of sulfuric acid and salt in the chemical reactor. You can etch your board with it.

1022.name: Magnetic Steel Rods
1022.desc: Want some magnetic steel rods for motors? Buy some!

1023.name: Aluminium Rods
1023.desc: Want some aluminium rods? Buy some!

872.name: Maple Syrup?
872.desc: Spent hours and hours trying to find some maple trees? Why not use your forest ranger coins to buy a sapling?

873.name: Seed Bags
873.desc: Finding some special "Pam's HarvestCraft" seeds can be very difficult. With your farmer coins you can buy some seedbags.

874.name: Jetpack v.0.1 Alpha
874.desc: Do you like flying? Ok then, make a slimy bouncepad and enjoy the view.

875.name: Slime Island Journey
875.desc:
Rubber trees are not the only source for raw rubber. Go and find a slime island high in the sky. Slime balls and slime leaves give raw rubber too.
Make some slime tree farms beside your rubber farm.

Liquid slime is not allowed to be placed down, as it acts as a spawner, and can cause lag.

876.name: Bring Me My Vat of Polyethylene!
876.desc:
Tired of using small cells to process chemicals? Build a Large Chemical Reactor and you can just pipe in and out fluids. Or use the glorious large fluid cells to deliver and remove fluids from the hatches.

You can decide if the reactor should void any extra products when the outputs are full or not by toggling the void mode in the GUI!

There are also recipes specific to only the LCR, sometimes even allowing you to skip a step, so keep your eyes open for that.

Additionally, as a multiblock, LCR also counts as a cleanroom for any cleanroom recipes.

You will also need:
1 Maintenance Hatch
1-2 Energy Hatches
0+ Input Bus (if a recipe has an item input. For full automation, you should probably use 1 for each material)
0-1 Output Bus (if a recipe has an item output)
2+ Input Hatch (use 1 for every fluid)
1+ Output Hatch (same as above)

You can use a screwdriver on an output hatch to cycle through its modes. Use shift-right-click to go directly to specific fluid mode, then lock the fluid with a cell for easier automation. This works on more than just the LCR.

877.name: Gold-Banded Greatwood Scepter
877.desc:
A scepter is crafted from a wand core, a primal charm, three wand caps, a twilight forest monster mob drop, and some screws. Any sort of core or cap may be used, but the caps must match each other. A scepter cannot hold a focus, but holds 150 percent as much vis as a wand with the same core, and gives an additional 10 percent discount for costs, beyond any discounts from its caps or the user's worn equipment.

This is ideal for all your crafting needs and to progress further!

[note]There are multiple ways to get enough vis capacity to craft the scepter. If you are not yet able to make it, maybe first work on Fusion and make yourself some boots with better discounts.[/note]

878.name: Magic Staff Core
878.desc: A staff requires an extra step for crafting: Two (matching) wand cores must be combined with a primal charm, and crystal clusters to produce a staff core. (While the recipes are all similar, each type of staff core requires a separate research topic to be learned.)

879.name: Primal Charm
879.desc:
With your new 50 vis wand you are able to make a Scepter which holds up to 75 vis.
First you need some primal charms which cost 50 vis to craft.

881.name: Pigmen
881.desc: You feel lonely. Do you want some bacon?

882.name: Blaze
882.desc: You feel lonely. Let me warm you up a bit.

884.name: Enderman
884.desc: You feel lonely. Don't look me in the eyes!

885.name: Cave Spider
885.desc: You feel lonely. Let me sing itsy-bitsy spider for you.

886.name: Ghast
886.desc: You feel lonely. At least you're not a big cry-baby?

887.name: A Wizard's Staff Has a Knob on the End
887.desc: Join the core with two wand caps, a twilight forest mob drop, and screws just as for making a wand. A staff cannot be used for crafting, but holds 2.5x as much vis as a corresponding wand. It can even serve as a backup weapon for melee. While it cannot receive weapon enchantments, its damage is decent, and it does not take damage with use (no durability bar).

888.name: Silverfish
888.desc: You feel lonely. Let me be as annoying as possible.

890.name: The GT Tank, ULV Version
890.desc:
This is the first GT-type tank. It stores 32 buckets or 32,000L, and can be used to I/O cells. The ULV Tank from GT++ needs steel plates, iron plates, tin plates, a clay pipe, and a water bucket.

GT tanks do not auto-output. If you want to move fluids out of the tank, use a GT pump, conduit, or something similar.

891.name: GT Tanks, the LV Version
891.desc: The next version of GT tanks stores 64,000L in a tank. It actually requires a pump this time, so it's a little expensive. Consider staying with your ULV ones if you only need basic I/O.

892.name: GT MV Tank...Already?
892.desc: These store 128,000L. The dark steel plates and LV pump make it a little annoying to make, since it needs an EBF.

893.name: You're Gonna Hate This in MV Too #2
893.desc: The second item you'll need a lot of in MV tier. When you want to get fluid out of tanks you'll want one, though you don't necessarily need an MV one.

894.name: GT Tanks, HV Tank
894.desc: This one stores 256,000L. The ULV circuits make it annoying to craft, so make them only if you need them. This is the last of the Voltage Tier ones, after this is the Super Tanks, which you can actually make right now.

895.name: You're Gonna Hate This in MV Too #1
895.desc: The first item you'll need a lot of in MV too. If you want to pull items out of chests or barrels you'll need one. Way faster than the LV one, so it's actually useful.

896.name: You're Gonna Hate This in MV Too #3
896.desc: An electric piston for your MV machines. The third item you're going to craft a lot of in the future.

897.name: You're Gonna Hate This in MV Too #4
897.desc: The fourth item you'll need a lot of in MV, which is still a pain to craft.

898.name: Simple Ore Washer
898.desc:
Too lazy to make an ore washer, yet bored with your non-automatable cauldron? Then the simple washer is the machine for you! It works much faster than a normal ore washer. Of course, you don't get any byproducts, but who cares!

There are simple washers for every tier up to UV, but the speed doesn't increase much past the HV version.

900.name: Don't Look it in the Eyes!
900.desc: Bad things may happen if this creature is allowed to stay alive. The ores in its cave being valuable have nothing to do with why you need to kill it. Seriously.

901.name: I Don't Even...
901.desc: OK so there is this maze. And there are... minotaurs. But what in the world is a minoshroom?

902.name: A Big Ugly Snake
902.desc:
Required for your first wand, and in general, an ugly creature.

[note]To progress in TF, you need to follow the achievements. They frequently bug out, so drop and pickup everything that dropped from the bosses. Yes, right after you picked it up the first time. It's dumb, we know.[/note]

[note]Also, BQ3 has a tendency to bug out detecting boss kills if you don't melee them, so if you want to complete these quests, use the spawn eggs from the coins tab and melee them when they get low on health if you missed them the first time.[/note]

903.name: Adventure Time!
903.desc: Stop him before he makes his wish at Prismos'!

904.name: Who Ya Gonna Call?
904.desc: Phantom Busters! Or wait... Ah whatever. They drop good armor by the way.

905.name: The Big Fluffy Crybaby
905.desc: It flies, it makes funny sounds, and... IT'S SO FLUFFY! Well, kill it.

906.name: Let Her Go
906.desc:
It seems the cold doesn't bother her. Committed crimes: None. But... you know... you have to progress somehow...

[note]PS: Keep going.[/note]

907.name: Yeti. Alpha, Yeti.
907.desc:
Wanted: Alpha Yeti, Dead.
Committed crimes: Violently throwing ice at people, for no reason.

908.name: Secret: A Nice....
908.desc:
Hey, you! I want that lamp. I have something for you in return. You don't need it anyways, do you?

[note]If you want the TF achievement, get that first.[/note]

909.name: Time to Kill (Creeper)
909.desc:
Who hasn't experienced this...? You're just minding your own business, mining a few diamonds, and suddenly, you wake up in your bed, all your items gone.

Time to strike back!

910.name: Time to Kill (Pigman)
910.desc: "Zombie Pigman"? What the hell is going on with you zombies?! Maybe you should encase your animals better at night...

911.name: Time to Kill (Spiders)
911.desc: Eeeek! Wall climbing monsters, gross! Slay them. Quickly! Watch out for the witch spiders, they can give as good as they get.

912.name: Time to Kill (Skeleton)
912.desc:
Arrows are nice, if you shoot them. If one hits you, well, bad things happen.

Hint: Arrows are an excellent source of telum.

913.name: Time to Kill (Blaze)
913.desc: You should be careful with this task. Fire protection is highly recommended.

914.name: Time to Kill (Zombie)
914.desc: Nobody knows who they are, or where they came from. They're just annoying at night, and too stupid to make a crafting table.

915.name: Time to Kill (Enderman)
915.desc:
Uh, fancy teleport pearls. You need them. All of them. Now!

[note]PS: Top is regular enderman, bottom is special mob's version. Complete one set. Blame SM for their method of creating new endermen types for this. HEE ones like angry don't count.[/note]

916.name: Time to Kill (Wither)
916.desc:
After collecting a couple of those wither skeleton heads, you feel like you could use some more action. So why not kill a wither? You'll need a ton of nether stars anyway...

Kill it twice for something nice.

917.name: Secret: The Trade For Life
917.desc: You have been busy my friend. I have something for you. Give me your hearts, and I will magically enhance them so you can actually use them.

918.name: Skeleton: Evolved
918.desc: In order to advance to a higher tier and reach Mars, Deimos and Phobos, you need to make yourself a better rocket. There are rumors that an Evolved Skeleton hiding out on the Moon has the schematics of such a device.

919.name: Creeper: Evolved
919.desc: Not that regular creepers are nasty enough, no. There is an evolved creeper boss! He is your key (Well he HAS a key...) to reach the next tier of planets (Asteroid Belt, Ceres, Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto).

920.name: Blaze: Evolved
920.desc: In order to proceed to Tier 4 Planets, the blaze boss needs to be killed. Find him in one of the dungeons on Ceres.

921.name: Ghast: Evolved
921.desc: Fluffy, fluffier, ghasts! And also big crybabies. This one hides in a dungeon on IO, and holds the key for Tier 5 planets.

922.name: Crystal Boss: Ayyy... Wait, What?
922.desc: Seriously, a crystal boss? Anyway, it's your key to Tier 6 planets, and he lurks around on Enceladus.

923.name: Slime: Evolved
923.desc: Totally looks like a thaumic slime to me. Don't you think so? Find this dude on Proteus.

924.name: Wolf: Evolved
924.desc: The final frontier... A... wolf that is not even an Iron Maiden fan?... What did you expect, a super boss? Heh. No. The budget was too tight...

925.name: Killing: Evolved
925.desc: I guess you're good at killing things, huh? And if you got this far, maybe making things too? If you want to really prove yourself though, head on over to the Endgame Goals tab.

926.name: Blood Altar Tier 3
926.desc:
To make your altar more powerful you need to upgrade it. Add another ring of Blood Runes with a two-high pillar of blood-soaked thaumium blocks at each corner. Top each of the pillars with a block of glowstone.

View the structure preview in NEI or visit [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Blood_Altar[/url] for images on how it should look.

927.name: Blood Altar Tier 4
927.desc: If you want to make tier 4 slates you need a Tier 4 altar. This can be made like the tier 3 altar with pillars out of void-blood-soaked stones four blocks high and a blood brick on top of the pillars.

928.name: Blood Altar Tier 5
928.desc: If you want to make tier 5 slates you need a Tier 5 altar. This can be made with 4 beacons. Beacons have thaumcraft recipes and can be found in the thaumcraft book on the GTNH page. The quest is most of the way through adept thaumaturgy.

929.name: Blood Altar Tier 6
929.desc: The highest tier of the blood altar is tier 6. You need pillars of blood-soaked ichorium 7 blocks high and on top the crystal cluster blocks made out of demon soul shards.

930.name: Auto Mining
930.desc:
Automatic mining in LV tier? Wow! This nifty machine will collect ores for you, which can be helpful especially in the Nether, as it won't anger pigmen.

Place up to two stacks of mining pipes in the right slots. Each pipe can go 1 block, and allow the miner to dig 1 block further. If you're above Y64, you may need to pipe more in.

This machine only needs 8 EU/t to work, so LV batteries in a batbuff work OK if you don't want to make another generator.

The miner searches for ores in a 17x17 block work area (adjustable with a screwdriver), and comes with a level 1 fortune bonus! Great for gathering additional small ores!

Don't forget to cover it up! These are GT machines, so remember to protect them (and their power sources!) from rain on all sides. And set a waypoint with JourneyMap so you don't forget where you placed it if you place it far away! You'll need to chunkload the miner if you want it to keep working when you're not there.

931.name: Auto Mining MV
931.desc: The MV miner will find ores for you faster than the LV miner, and in a larger range. It also has a larger fortune bonus, great for gathering small ores in the Nether. Plus it's faster, but I'm sure you figured that.

932.name: Auto Mining HV
932.desc: The HV miner is faster, has longer range, and has more fortune than the previous two. If you want a better one, look in the multiblock tab. It's recommend to take one with you to the Moon to gather meteoric iron.

933.name: Power of the Sun
933.desc:
Now it's time to invest in some renewable energy. Let's start with solar panels. The basic one only gives 1 EU/t. Most GT machines can use them as covers, and receive power, but only so long as it can see the sun (the top). Be sure to put a glass layer above them in case it rains while you swap panels. You'll still want to keep rain off the sides of the machine hull too.

[note]The Aluminium Iron plate needs an MV Forming Press.[/note]

934.name: Power of the Sun ULV
934.desc: The next solar panel you can make is the ULV one. It generates 8 EU/t.

935.name: Power of the Sun 1x1
935.desc: Put those single EU panels on a block to make them placeable in the world. Use an MV assembler to combine the panel with a ULV hull.

936.name: Power of the Sun at LV Level
936.desc: The next solar panel you can make is the LV one. It generates 32 EU/t.

937.name: Power of the Sun 1x1 LV
937.desc: Put those 32 EU panels on a block to make them placeable in the world. Use an MV assembler to combine the panel with an LV hull, a battery, and a robot arm. Now this panel will clean itself!

938.name: Automated Sticky Resin Farmer
938.desc:
If you're tired of crafting tons of tree taps, how about a machine that uses power but never breaks? Let's craft an electric tree tap!

(If you're looking for better ways to get Sticky Resin, you might want to consider the Stickreed crop from IC2 crops. To get it, plant Sugarcanes on Crops on an X formation, with spaces between them, and then put 2 Crops on each empty slot. For more information, check the crop quests.

939.name: Automated Farmer
939.desc: Your hoe must be getting worn out by now. Why not craft an electric one which never breaks?

940.name: Cleanroom
940.desc:
If you want to make more advanced (and cheaper!) circuits, you will need a Cleanroom. The Cleanroom is an interesting multiblock, because you will be placing other machines inside of the room!

The cleanroom is a hollow multiblock between 3x4x3 and 15x15x15 (WxHxL) blocks. The base does not need to be a square; a rectangular shape is fine. The top layer is built out of Filter Machine Casings, except for the edges, which are Plascrete Blocks. The controller needs to be in the center of the top layer. If the width or length of the room are even, then it can be any one of the two (or four) middle blocks. The rest of the structure is built out of Plascrete Blocks.

Up to 30% of those Plascrete Blocks can be replaced by other valid blocks or materials:

A Reinforced Door lets you get in and out of the cleanroom. Make sure to keep the doors closed, or the cleanroom will rapidly lose efficiency. Travel Anchors and Elevators also work, you can even embed them in the floor if you want to.

In order to automate power and item transport through the walls, you can use Cable Diodes or Machine Hulls. Just be really careful when upgrading your machines to a higher voltage. Be sure to remove the machine hulls and diodes and replace them with the new power tier, or else you will be very sad. You don't need to do this for ones you only use to pass items through.

If you want to see inside, you can use any EV+ tier glass (for example, Reinforced Glass or Managlass). If the Cleanroom does not form, mind the 30% limit: often the problem is that you added too much glass.

Finally, the Cleanroom itself will need a Maintenance and Energy Hatch to run. The power usage of this multiblock is a bit unusual. It starts out at 40 EU/t and then gradually goes down. But even when it uses 40 EU/t - which is usually MV - a single LV energy hatch suffices, as long as you give it enough power. Just keep in mind that this makes the Cleanroom an exception, not the rule.

Let's build a 5x5x5 room for now. You can expand it later as needed. Give it power and let it run until it reaches 100% efficiency. Below 100% efficiency, machines inside the cleanroom have a chance to void outputs, so you want to keep the cleanroom running at all times!

Any maintenance issue reduces the maximum efficiency by 10%. Monitor your cleanroom with a Hand Scanner, Industrial Information Panel, or using a Needs Maintenance Cover and a Redstone Powered Light or Howler Alarm.

[note]Multiblocks such as the Large Chemical Reactor or Hyper-Intensity Laser Engraver don't need to be in a cleanroom. However, you will still need the cleanroom later on for other machines, like Bio Labs.[/note]

[note]Other helpful hints: you can use an AE Wireless Connector or a Transvector Interface for item transport to avoid machine hulls, and use a higher tier energy hatch to reach 100% efficiency faster.[/note]

[warn]Keep in mind that you can not use anything in the cleanroom that causes pollution, or the cleanroom will stop working.[/warn]

941.name: A Very Useful Scanner
941.desc:
The portable scanner is a tool capable of showing advanced information about almost every block in the game. In order to function, the tool needs to be charged first. It can hold up to 400k EU and accept 128 EU/t or less. This is MV tier.

It can scan crops by right-clicking it to fully scan it, unlike a cropnalyzer which needs a seed bag in your inventory, and takes multiple goes. Crops that were scanned in the world will drop a scanned seed bag.

It can scan machines from GregTech, giving information about current progress, the stored energy, efficiency, problems and CPU-load - be sure to check the full chat window for additional details.

For any block in the game it scans, it gives information about its ID, Metadata, position in the world and hardness value. It also gives information about pollution level in the chunk the scanned block belongs to.

In addition it shows when the cleanroom has reached its 100 percent efficiency level.

942.name: It's a Dirty Job, and You're Going to Do It
942.desc: The Plunger is used to clear liquids from pipes. It can also be used on a machine to remove up to 1000mb of fluid from its internal buffer. So you don't have to wrench your machine and re-place it. It can be crafted from a rod of the desired material and rubber sheets.

943.name: Performing Chemical Experiments at HV Level
943.desc:
There are a number of chemical recipes that are unlocked with an HV Chemical Reactor; you will finally be able to make Ender Eyes, Ammonia, Gelled Toluene, and plenty of other useful components. 

Alternatively, if you're up for it, you can head over to the Mass Processing tab and build yourself a Large Chemical Reactor (LCR) instead! In addition to having a more efficient overclock, the LCR can input multiple liquids so you won't need to mess with those pesky cells any longer. 

[note]If you choose to go the LCR route, be sure to take a look at the PTFE questline just ahead of this one, starting with "Teflon Don".[/note]

944.name: Super Tank I
944.desc:
This tank works the same as the smaller GT tanks, but holds way more. Specifically, 4,000,000L. Only make this for things you need tons of, LV circuits are expensive after all.

Now you can store the same amount of fluids a steel 5x5 Railcraft tank contains in a single block.

[note]A lot of nice functionality has been added to Super and Quantum tanks. You can now pick them up with a wrench without voiding the fluids! Super tanks (but not Quantum) will give you heavy debuffs though, so better prepare some milk.

If you actually want to void the fluids you can crouch while using the wrench.

They now also have optional automatic fluid output, fluid locking, overflow void mode, or full void mode. And all that with a nice and simple GUI. Quite the IMPACTful change I would say.[/note]

945.name: Chad - A Better Way to Make Paper
945.desc: Making paper out of wood pulp and water can be tedious and difficult. Why not use a mortar to grind the sugar cane into chad and press it with two stone slabs to paper? Later on you can use the chemical bath for an even better ratio.

946.name: Bamboo Fence
946.desc:
Maybe you're lucky and started in a bamboo forest. Bamboo is a very useful tree since it grows really fast and when you break the bottom-most block, the whole thing comes apart.

You can use them as sticks, or you can place the bamboo sticks to grow and make a three-block-tall wall to protect your base. You can also use Berry Bushes for that, assuming you don't want to spend materials on fences.

947.name: Punji Sticks
947.desc: An early game protection moat can be made out of punji sticks. It hurts all mobs/players and additionally gives a slowness II potion effect. Make a trench around your base and put the punji sticks inside.

948.name: Better Than Iron
948.desc:
But not as good as steel. Mostly, this is used in machine hulls, or in tools if you don't want to use steel up for that.

To make wrought iron, you need a tinkers smelter to melt some iron ingots down and cast nuggets; or use an ingot and a saw to get your nuggets. Now put these in a furnace to get wrought iron nuggets. With the compressor, you can compress the nuggets into ingots.

Hint: Crushed or purified iron ore (not a mixed version like pyrite, look for Iron Ore in Chalcopyrite veins) can be smelted to iron nuggets directly. Or you can use an alloy smelter to turn iron ingots into nuggets faster than a smeltery. You can also use a saw to cut up iron ingots into nuggets.

Later on, you can use an arc furnace to make wrought iron ingots directly.

949.name: Garden Bags
949.desc:
Finding some special "Pam's HarvestCraft" gardens can be very difficult. With your farmer coins you can buy some garden bags.

[note]This includes the nether version BTW.[/note]

950.name: XP Berries
950.desc: And a few XP berries for cash.

951.name: Fluid Solidifier
951.desc:
Use molds to make plates, sticks, bolts and many more item parts in the fluid solidifier.

Molds are != to Extruder shapes. You're gonna need 2 sets. If they're too expensive, just make the most important ones for now.

952.name: Base Too Big? I've Got a Fix For You!
952.desc:
Keeping a base spread apart for pollution or explosion prevention can really wear you out, so why not make some concrete to get around faster?

Harvest some Marble and centrifuge it into Calcite, and mix up some concrete in your Mixer. Next output it into a Fluid Solidifier with a block mold to make concrete blocks.

You'll move faster on top of these blocks. They can even be turned into bricks to prevent unwanted spawns! Hint: 10 buckets of wet mix converts to exactly 160 blocks. These blocks can also be Chiseled into different varieties.

953.name: Dark Steel Pipes
953.desc:
Dark steel pipes are much better than the bronze, steel, or potin pipes you might have been using. Make sure your wrench can handle mining level 3 or you can't deconstruct them.

[note]If you don't need these, just recycle them to get the materials back. Not everything needs ginormous pipes or high heat tolerance.[/note]

954.name: The 'Water Problems' Are Solved
954.desc: After entering the HV age you've found a way of making a water reservoir which solves the finite water problem. Craft it with some fused quartz glass, HV pumps, and cauldrons.

956.name: Bronze Fluid Pipes
956.desc: Want some bronze fluid pipes? Buy some!

957.name: Magnetic Iron Rods
957.desc: Want some magnetic iron rods for motors? Buy some!

958.name: Tin Rotors
958.desc: Want some tin rotors? Buy some!

959.name: Iron Rods
959.desc: Want some iron rods? Buy some!

960.name: Brass Rods
960.desc: Want some brass rods? Buy some!

961.name: Certus Quartz
961.desc: Want some certus quartz? Buy some!

962.name: Vroooooom...
962.desc:
Tired of standing around to collect all the drops and XP from your diamond spikes?

Make yourself a vacuum hopper to suck up all the drops and XP. Feed the XP into a tank to store it.

963.name: Basic Circuit Boards
963.desc: Want some basic circuit boards? Buy some!

964.name: Good Circuit Boards
964.desc: Want some good circuit boards? Buy some!

965.name: Vacuum Tubes
965.desc: Want some vacuum tubes? Buy some!

970.name: RAM
970.desc: Want some RAM chips? Buy some!

971.name: Integrated Logic Circuits
971.desc: Want some integrated logic circuit chips? Buy some!

1272.name: Wheaty Juice
1272.desc: The first step to brew beer or other alcoholic fluids is wheaty juice.

1273.name: Brewing, the Industrial Way
1273.desc: If you want brews or alcohol, I recommend you to build one of these. You might have noticed that you cannot build a brewing stand without Thaumcraft. But fear not, you can still find them in secret rooms on the first floor of some roguelike dungeons or ur-ghast towers. Better get searching...

1274.name: Ferment Your Brew!
1274.desc: What's better than lame brews? Alcoholic brews! Get one of these to make beer, rum or even vodka.

1275.name: Fake Jagermeister
1275.desc: Jägermeister is a common drink in Germany, why don't you grab a bottle? This one seems to be a cheap fake tho...

1232.name: You're Gonna Hate This 5 and 6 in HV
1232.desc: Some machines need emitters and sensors, like the analyzer or the circuit assembler.

1233.name: Ender Eyes
1233.desc: Ender eyes are available at HV in the chemical reactor. Combine some ender pearls with blaze powder.

1234.name: Empty Epoxid Board
1234.desc:
Epoxid circuit boards require an epoxid plate, gold foil and sulfuric acid to make an empty board. By adding electrum foil and Iron(III) Chloride or Sodium Persulfate you can craft an Advanced Circuit Board.

[note]If you used IC2 Scrap Boxes to obtain the empty boards instead of using epoxy, hold 64 Advanced Circuit Boards in your hand to unlock this quest.[/note]

1235.name: LV Microprocessor
1235.desc:
The Microprocessor is the highest tier version of the LV circuit, getting you 2 per recipe.

In addition to what's listed, you'll need 2 Resistors, Transistors, and Capacitors, SMD or non-SMD.

This advanced circuit type needs a cleanroom.

1236.name: HV Cutting Machine
1236.desc:
NOR, NAND and other high tier chips require an HV cutting machine.

[note]You'll soon find that everything you cut needs a cleanroom, so make sure you start on that.[/note]

1237.name: Phosphorus Doped Monocrystalline Silicon
1237.desc:
To make more advanced wafers, you need to make phosphorus doped monocrystalline silicon first. The process takes a while, so make sure you have stable power.

You can also get more wafers, and thus more chips out of higher tier boules. You still need to make the lower tier ones sometimes though.

1238.name: Phosphorus Doped Wafer
1238.desc: To create some phosphorus doped silicon wafers, cut them in a cutting machine. An HV version of the cutting machine is needed for future wafers and chips.

1239.name: NOR and NAND Chips
1239.desc: NAND is used for data sticks, data orbs, and UV circuits. NOR chips are needed for data orbs and LuV circuits. You can get these by cutting NAND and NOR wafers. Just worry about the data sticks for now.

1240.name: Data Sticks
1240.desc: Data sticks are very important for seismic prospecting and for the assembly line in the LuV age. Go and craft a few data sticks.

1241.name: Radon Loop
1241.desc:
Radon is needed for EV emitters and sensors.

If you can get some Plutonium 239 and Uranium 238, you can use the LCR recipe and just keep cycling the Plutonium around to get more.

1242.name: EV Chemical Reactor
1242.desc:
An EV chemical reactor is required to get some indium or make your radon production much faster. If you already made a large chemical reactor use that instead.

[note]Complete either task.[/note]

1243.name: Reactor Fuels
1243.desc: For impure radioactive ores for your nuclear reactor, you can look for pitchblende and uraninite. You can find them on Mars and Phobos at Y 30-60.

1244.name: Other Ores
1244.desc: You can find several other ores on these planets (some of which actually don't have a use, honestly), but the most useful right now are probably the ones on the right. There's also Draconium, Jade, Mirabilite, Tanzanite, Vinteum, and Vyroxes.

1245.name: You're Gonna Hate This 1-4 in EV
1245.desc: Pumps, pistons, conveyor modules and robot arms are the most needed machine parts in EV. Go and craft one of each.

1246.name: You're Gonna Hate This 5 and 6 in EV
1246.desc: Some machines need emitters and sensors, like the analyzer or the circuit assembler.

1247.name: Quantum Eye
1247.desc: Quantum eyes are required in EV emitters and sensors. Just infuse your ender eye with radon to get one.

1248.name: Nano Processor
1248.desc: This is the Nano Processor circuit line. Similar to the previous circuit line, these are four circuits. The nano circuits go all the way to the LuV-tier Nano Mainframe.

1249.name: NanoCPU Wafer and Chip
1249.desc: The NanoCPU Wafer is made out of a CPU Wafer with raw carbon fiber and molten glowstone in an EV chemical reactor. Cut it for nano CPU chips.

1250.name: Nano Assembly
1250.desc: The Nano Assembly is an EV circuit.

1251.name: Nano Supercomputer
1251.desc: The Nano Supercomputer is an IV circuit.

1252.name: EV Chemical Bath
1252.desc: An HV chemical bath or better is needed for the quantum eye. If you make an EV one you can also process Ilmenite better.

1253.name: 16k Component
1253.desc: Second largest vanilla storage component, used to make 16k ME storage cells, or can be upgraded to make 64k ME storage components. Can be recovered from a crafted 16k ME storage cell by fully emptying the storage cell, and shift-clicking it in your hand.

1254.name: 16k Crafting Storage
1254.desc: Component of the crafting CPU which provides 16,384 bytes of storage for crafting.

1255.name: 64k Item Storage Cell
1255.desc:
Highest tier storage cell, which can contain 65,536 bytes of storage. 65,536 bytes of storage can hold 8,128 stacks of a single item, or 4,160 stacks, while holding 63 different items. The 64k storage cell uses 512 bytes of data to store a single type.

When placed inside a drive or chest will consume 2.0 ae/t.

1256.name: 64k Component
1256.desc: Largest vanilla storage component, used to make 64k ME storage cells. Can be recovered from a crafted 64k ME storage cell by fully emptying the storage cell, and shift-clicking it in your hand.

1257.name: 64k Crafting Storage
1257.desc: Component of the crafting CPU which provides 65,536 bytes of storage for crafting.

1260.name: Crafting Monitor
1260.desc: Component of the crafting CPU which displays the top level job and its current progress so you can monitor at a glance how your crafting jobs are progressing.

1261.name: Nano Mainframe
1261.desc:
The Nano Mainframe is the first LuV Circuit, and the end of this circuit line produced in EV. To craft the Tier 2 Rocket and reach Mars, Phobos and Deimos, you need to reach this circuit and make 8 of it.

After that, you will be able to progress into IV much more easily, but you will need two more circuits to make the IV Circuit Assembling Machine.

1263.name: Blue Steel
1263.desc: Sunnarium batteries are made out of blue steel: a mixture of steel, black steel, brass and rose gold.

1264.name: EV Sunnarium Battery
1264.desc: Small Sunnarium batteries are EV tier. Some might think they're cheaper than Lapotrons, but it depends on Sunnarium. And they hold less energy.

1265.name: EV GT++ Batteries
1265.desc: Want cheaper EV batteries? Mash your old ones together, and somehow it works!

1266.name: Low Voltage Transformer
1266.desc:
It is possible to run MV machines while still in LV - build a transformer and use 4 power sources 32 EU/t to power one 128 EU/t machine.

It transforms power up or down, depending on the mode, which you can switch with a soft mallet. Step-up means 4A LV in, 1A MV out, while step-down means the reverse.

Make sure you understand the following if you prefer your base not being a crater:
A step-down mode is the default one. In this mode, the large dot is the input. In a step-up mode, it's the output. Do not mix it up. LV machines that receive MV power will explode.

If the transformer is connected to power, or was in the past, then do not change the mode with stuff connected. It's better to break and place it again to get rid of the stored power rather than rotate it with the wrench and blow things up. Make sure to also disconnect the nearby wires with a wire cutter.

As a reminder, machines don't blow up if they are given more amps than they can consume. They blow up from higher voltage tier.

1267.name: Medium Voltage Transformer
1267.desc: These work the same as the LV ones, but 1 tier higher.

1268.name: LV High-Amp Transformer
1268.desc: If you need a transformer with more than 4A you need a high amp transformer. You can input 4A MV and output 16A LV. Useful for running lots of LV machines. Its mode can be changed with a soft mallet, just like before.

1269.name: Low Voltage Power Transformer
1269.desc: If you need even more amps then craft a power transformer. This one is 16A -> 64A. The soft mallet is still how you change modes.

1270.name: Time for a Spade
1270.desc:
This item is a combined Weeding Trowel, Hoe and Shovel. If you're the type that accidentally does things, only use this when necessary, as it's easy to mess up and use it to harvest/destroy your crops when you wanted to get rid of a weed.

You can use it to get seeds from crops when they're immature using right-click at very low rates. Using [warn]left-click[/warn] when immature will result in nothing.

If it's mature, use left-click to have a higher chance of a seed dropping. Using [warn]right-click[/warn] when mature will result in lower seed drops than punching it normally.

Keep in mind that the higher the tier/stats of the crop, the lower the chances. By a lot.

[note]The formula is 1+tanh((-1)(X+5((-Re/2)+Gr+Ga)/21)/12); X=0 to 16, where Re, Gr, Ga are respectively Resistance, Growth and Gain stat of the crop, and X is tier. Don't ask me whether this is for L/R-click, ask bart or look at the code.[/note]

1271.name: HV Extruder
1271.desc: The HV Extruder is needed to extrude tough metals like Tungstensteel and HSS.

1024.name: Bronze Rotors
1024.desc: Want some bronze rotors? Buy some!

1025.name: Steel Fluid Pipes
1025.desc: Want some steel fluid pipes? Buy some!

1026.name: Electrum Rods
1026.desc: Want some electrum rods? Buy some!

1027.name: Flawless Emeralds
1027.desc: Want some flawless emeralds? Buy some!

1028.name: Ender Pearl
1028.desc: Want some ender pearls? Buy some!

1029.name: Magnetic Neodymium Rods
1029.desc: Want some magnetic neodymium rods for motors? Buy some!

1030.name: Stainless Steel Rods
1030.desc: Want some stainless steel rods? Buy some!

1031.name: Steel rotors
1031.desc: Want some steel rotors? Buy some!

1032.name: Stainless Steel Fluid Pipes
1032.desc: Want some stainless steel fluid pipes? Buy some!

1033.name: Chrome Rods
1033.desc: Want some chrome rods? Buy some!

1034.name: Ender Eyes
1034.desc: Want some ender eyes? Buy some!

1035.name: Be Sure to Get Your Armpits
1035.desc:
Want to use all that delicious XP to raise your levels? Move the tank with XP above you and attach this convenient shower head to take the XP from the tank.

This thing can be laggy, so be careful about letting too many XP orbs out of the tank. Apply a redstone signal to turn it off.

1036.name: Plastic Circuit Boards
1036.desc: Want some plastic circuit boards? Buy some!

1037.name: Central Processing Units
1037.desc: Want some central processing unit chips? Buy some!

1038.name: SMD Resistors
1038.desc: Want some SMD resistors? Buy some!

1039.name: SMD Diodes
1039.desc: Want some SMD diodes? Buy some!

1040.name: SMD Transistors
1040.desc: Want some SMD transistors? Buy some!

1041.name: SMD Capacitor
1041.desc: Want some SMD capacitors? Buy some!

1042.name: Logic Processors
1042.desc: Want some logic processors? Buy some!

1044.name: Iron (III) Chloride
1044.desc: Want some iron (III) chloride? Buy some!

1045.name: Sodium Persulfate
1045.desc: Want some sodium persulfate? Buy some!

1046.name: Pyrochlore Ore
1046.desc: Can't find Pyrochlore ore because of old worldgen? Buy some Ores.

1047.name: Forest Hive
1047.desc: Want some Forest Bees? Buy some!

1048.name: Deep Hive
1048.desc: Want some Attuned Bees? Buy some!

1049.name: Ender Hive
1049.desc: Want some Ender Bees? Buy some!

1050.name: Bee Houses
1050.desc: Want some bee houses? Buy some!

1052.name: Impregnated Frames
1052.desc: Want some impregnated frames? Buy some!

1053.name: Infernal Hive
1053.desc: Want some Infernal Bees? Buy some!

1054.name: Oblivion Hive
1054.desc: Want some Oblivion Bees? Buy some!

1055.name: Did You Want to Know Your Warp Level?
1055.desc:
After a few forbidden researches your warp level has increased a bit. Do you want to know how much warp you have?

Craft some litmus paper to print out the warp level.

1056.name: Meadow Hive
1056.desc: Want some Meadows Bees? Buy some!

1057.name: Modest Hive
1057.desc: Want some Modest Bees? Buy some!

1058.name: Tropical Hive
1058.desc: Want some Tropical Bees? Buy some!

1059.name: Wintry Hive
1059.desc: Want some Wintry Bees? Buy some!

1060.name: Marshy Hive
1060.desc: Want some Marshy Bees? Buy some!

1061.name: Water Hive
1061.desc: Want some Water Bees? Buy some!

1062.name: Rocky Hive
1062.desc: Want some Rocky Bees? Buy some!

1063.name: Nether Hive
1063.desc: Want some Embittered Bees? Buy some!

1064.name: Curious Hive
1064.desc: Want some Mystical Bees? Buy some!

1065.name: Unusual Hive
1065.desc: Want some Unusual Bees? Buy some!

1066.name: Resonating Hive
1066.desc: Want some Sorcerous Bees? Buy some!

1067.name: Space Station
1067.desc:
You can build a space station if you want. You can add up to four spin thrusters to your station to rotate it.

Days on space stations last longer than on the overworld, so you can generate more solar power.

You can build space stations here:
1. Moon - T1
2. Mars - T2
3. Jupiter - T3
4. Venus - T4
5. Saturn - T5
6. Uranus - T5
7. Neptune - T6

Each Retrieval Task in this quest corresponds (in order) to the resources you will need in order to build a space station at one of the planets listed above. The "T#" afterwards refers to the tier of rocket you will need to get there.

[note]You will need to complete at least one task to complete this quest![/note]

[warn]It is likely that you will loose your rocket when flying back to earth or another space station. Make sure you have an alternative way to return (some form of teleportation) or use another planet/moon as proxy (if you get an error while doing so, you spawn on the planet/moon and can launch your rocket again).[/warn]

1068.name: Are You Prepared?
1068.desc:
Are you prepared for the Moon?

If you die on the moon you will be returned to earth without your stuff. A Soulbind enchantment will keep an item with you when you die.

1069.name: Vanilla Saplings
1069.desc: You spawned in a desert and need Oak or Birch saplings? No problem! For five forest ranger coins you can select which one you want to buy.

1070.name: Magic Feather
1070.desc:
In order to understand where you are, and where you want to be, you'll need a map. This is a magical place, so why not a magical map? The main part is a Magic Map Focus.

First, go find some Raven feathers. You can get them by killing Ravens. They like to hang out near the obsidian pillars.

Then you need some Torchberries (found underground) and some Glowstone dust.

1071.name: Magic Map
1071.desc:
For the magic map you need the magic map focus and a paper stack, made from a stack of paper.

It will fill in the area near you, and reveal boss locations. You need to kill these to get access to the rest of the Twilight Forest. Start with the Naga.

You can see what to do next in the TF achievement tab. There's also quests for the boss kills on the kill all the things tab. You can only get one map every 24 hours, so be careful with it.

Only use the map in the Twilight Forest, using the map in any other dimension will lag the server!

1072.name: Moon Arrival
1072.desc:
I have a small present for you: a Talisman of Remedium which is very useful to persevere during the dark nights with all these monsters.

First you have to prove you can survive on the moon by yourself. After mining some moon turf, dirt, and rock, you can have it.

Make sure you check back in the HV questline for necessary ores you should gather while here.

1073.name: Naga
1073.desc:
Summon a Naga.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1074.name: Lich
1074.desc:
Summon a Lich.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1075.name: Minoshroom
1075.desc:
Summon a Minoshroom.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1076.name: Hydra
1076.desc:
Summon a Hydra.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1077.name: Knight Phantom
1077.desc:
Summon a Knight Phantom.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1078.name: Ur-Ghast
1078.desc:
Summon an Ur-Ghast.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1079.name: Yeti
1079.desc:
Summon a Yeti.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1080.name: Snow Queen
1080.desc:
Summon a Snow Queen.

[note]Only works in the Twilight Forest dim.[/note]

1081.name: MV Bending
1081.desc:
This Plate Bending Machine allows you to directly make double, triple or more compressed plates, which might be useful for your first rocket in HV Tier.

It also allows you to make dense plates at all.

1082.name: MV Battery Buffers
1082.desc:
You'll probably want MV battery buffers as well.

[note]You only need to complete one task.[/note]

1083.name: MV Batteries
1083.desc:
Better machines need some better batteries. Lithium is the best material to use when making batteries.

[note]You only need to complete one task.[/note]

1084.name: Lubricant
1084.desc:
Using lubricant instead of water or distilled water in your cutting machines will increase their speed.

You can make lubricant out of oil, seed oil, fish oil or creosote in a distillery (or distillation tower later on). If you're able to craft a brewery you can use talc or soapstone to get much more lubricant each cycle.

Lube cells are required to make low tier energy hatches, so stockpile a few cells and use the rest to make your cutting machine faster.

1085.name: More Compact Storage
1085.desc:
Now that you are starting to find more and more iron, you feel your copper chests are too small to hold all the stuff.

[note]Do you have copper chests that are too small? You can also upgrade with a fitting upgrade item.[/note]

1086.name: Juice
1086.desc: Most of your berries can be extracted to juice form. This is a chance to get more food variants and the nutritional value is higher than eating the fruit directly.

1087.name: Potato Cakes
1087.desc: Potatoes, onions, butter and a skillet make very tasty potato cakes.

1088.name: Fried Eggs
1088.desc: So easy, skillet and an egg.... fried egg.

1089.name: Hearty Breakfast
1089.desc:
Now let's combine all the things together and make a healthy and delicious breakfast.

Some cooked meat, potato cakes, toast, fried egg and a tasty juice provide you with a very nourishing meal.

You can use any cooked meat and any fruit juice here.

1090.name: Time to Dry Out the Nether
1090.desc:
Pumps can do more than pump oil and water. You can go to the nether and pump lava from the huge lava lakes too.

Once you reach HV, lava can be centrifuged for Silicon Dioxide, Magnesia, Quicklime, Gold, Sapphire, and Tantalite. You can get Silicon, Magnesium, Calcium, Gold, Aluminium, Manganese, Tantalum, and Oxygen out of them.

This is a good use for your LV pumps once you upgrade to MV pumps for oil. If you get into Thaumcraft or Blood Magic, there are ways to produce lava with magic unlimitedly and easily too.

[note]You'll want either ULV tanks and cells, or BC tanks and a dolly to transport your lava.[/note]

1091.name: Diamond Tanks... Ooo, Shiny
1091.desc: After you build a cutting machine you can make some diamond plates for your new 64,000L single block tank.

1092.name: New Source of Glowstone
1092.desc: Find some glowflowers in the Nether and plant them in the Overworld. You will never need to go to the Nether and harvest glowstone again.

1093.name: Did You Run Low On Gold or Redstone?
1093.desc:
Did you run low on redstone and/or gold? It's no problem with a glowstone flower farm. You can centrifuge glowstone dust to redstone and gold dust. Redstone is also a good source of chrome. You'll need tons of gold for HV, so it's a good idea to set this up before then.

[note]There are actually a couple plants that produce glowstone, pick the one you like![/note]

1094.name: Diamond Chests... Ooo, Shiny
1094.desc:
After you build a cutting machine you can make some diamond plates for your new, even bigger diamond chests.

[note]Just as with the previous chests you can also upgrade with a fitting upgrade item.[/note]

1095.name: Even More Compact Storage
1095.desc:
If you found enough gold, you can upgrade your chest to make it an even bigger inventory of 9x9.

[note]Do you have iron chests that are too small? You can also upgrade with a fitting upgrade item.[/note]

1096.name: Scribing Tool
1096.desc:
To unlock research you need a scribing tool and paper. To do the research, you need a scribing tool and the research note you made in the research table, so you should probably make two tools.

Note: Thaumcraft Research Tweaks has reworked the research table GUI. Aspects are now combined with drag & drop between aspects in the GUI. You can also right-click while dragging the aspect instead of dropping; the same works with the hexes. All the new features are described in your Thaumonomicon, make sure to check out the 'Research', 'Research Expertise', 'Research Mastery' and 'Research Duplication' pages.

1097.name: Bathing the Warp Effects Away
1097.desc:
Litmus paper will tell you how high your warp level is, but how can you get rid of the nasty warp effects? Bath salt will help you by prohibiting warp effects for a short period. Just throw the salt into a 1x1 water hole and jump inside. You will feel relieved a bit after bathing.

[note]Note: The more permanent warp you have, the shorter the duration.[/note]

1098.name: Bathing in a Spa. Automated Warp Removal
1098.desc: Bathing in a spa will automate the process of temporarily removing warp effects. Take the bath salt with you into the spa.

1099.name: Temp Warp Remover Soap
1099.desc:
This soap is very special. It will remove temporary warp and has a small chance to also remove some normal warp, if you are lucky. Taking a bath first increases your chances.

This can later be upgraded to remove your permanent warp.

[note]Hint: You need to chisel your tallow block for the recipe to work.[/note]

1100.name: Enchanting Table 1.0
1100.desc: Finally, with your new wand with 50 stored vis you're able to build your own enchantment table. The recipe can be found on the GTNH Tab in the Thaumonomicon.

1101.name: Division Sigil
1101.desc:
Find a sigil in a chest? Get one from the Wither? You've heard about a ritual to activate it.

You need an enchantment table, 8 redstone dust, a weapon, and an animal you want to sacrifice. Catching and holding it with a Golden Lasso would be the easiest way. Check in LV for the Golden Lasso quest if you haven't completed it yet.

Place your enchantment table on a grass field and surround it with redstone dust. At midnight, place the animal on or near the table and kill it. You can check with the sigil if it's time yet by right-clicking it on the enchantment table. Of course you also need to have your sigil on you.

Beware! Your actions will have consequences.

1102.name: Beacon
1102.desc: With your ascension to adept thaumaturge you are ready to fusion craft beacons. The beacon recipe is found on the GTNH Thaumcraft Page. Activate it in your base to gain various positive effects.

1103.name: Cursed Earth
1103.desc:
You can kill the powered-up monsters that spawn on the cursed earth, or just sleep and despawn them. Use a shovel with the silk touch enchantment to dig up the cursed earth blocks. You can use these blocks to make a mob spawner, or place one under a vanilla spawner to let it run even when you're not around.

When setting up a mob spawner in the Nether using Cursed Earth, poor design can cause huge TPS issues.

Make sure the Cursed Earth is not attached to anything from the Nether. For example, you can place it on top of marble, but the marble cannot touch anything in the nether (netherrack, brick, etc.), and must be floating in the air. Also, limit the Cursed Earth to 1 chunk max, with 2 block buffer to the chunk edge, including a wall.

1104.name: Silky Jewel
1104.desc:
Silky jewels add silk touch to your tinkers tool, making them useful for collecting GT ores intact. You can also use them for other purposes, like obtaining cursed earth or grass blocks if needed.

The recipe can be found on the GTNH page after researching the enchantment table.

1105.name: Stable Division Sigil
1105.desc:
The second ritual will not be as easy as the first one. After this ritual your sigil will be stable enough to make stable unstable ingots which will not explode, and will no longer take durability damage.

This ritual needs to be performed in The End dimension. The ritual is performed by placing a Beacon in the center of a flat area of at least 11x11, not necessarily End Stone. Then place a chest in each of the cardinal directions leaving 4 spaces empty between the chest and the Beacon. In each chest insert these specific contents:

North: iron ingot, gold ingot and the other things listed in the task.
South: The different Ore Blocks and the other things listed in the task.
East: The potions.
West: The Music disks.

A spiral pattern of Redstone and String is then laid out around the beacon. At any time, shift-right-clicking the Beacon with an Activated Division Sigil in hand will show the building progress of the ritual.

Finally, kill an Iron Golem while it stands within the ritual area to activate the process. If it works, there will be an explosion that destroys the beacon and chests, while every Enderman in the End vanishes. During the ritual, throngs of aggressive mobs imbued with Haste will begin spawning and attacking the Player who needs to kill 100 of them to complete the ritual. If the Player dies or leaves the dimension, the ritual will fail and will have to be started over. Once enough mobs have been killed, the first activated Sigil in the inventory will stabilize. The Pseudo-Inversion Sigil can be used an unlimited number of times, and dividing an Iron Ingot by Diamond will produce Mobius "Unstable-Stable" Ingot which does not explode, can be produced in any crafting interface and will stack up to 64.

1106.name: Blub Blub Blub.....
1106.desc:
The brewing stand is really important for brewing the various potions like night vision, healing, haste and jump. Toxic potions or splash potions can be created too. You'll also need it to make a brewery.

With your basic wand you can finally create one. The recipes can be found on the GTNH page in the Thaumonomicon.

1107.name: The Iron Guy
1107.desc:
Do you feel lonely? Need some extra protection in your base? Want to keep the pigmen off your back in the Nether? Craft some Iron Golems and the monster problem inside your base will be gone.

They can do good amounts of damage and aggro enemies like the pigmen away from you. They can even take care of that pesky Vampire Pigman.

1108.name: The Cool Guy
1108.desc: Feeling lonely? Located far away from a snow biome? Craft a Snow Golem, and perhaps challenge him to a snow ball fight.

1109.name: Ohhh, Crunchy
1109.desc: After many, many, hours of doing Thaumcraft research you've found a way to craft pure tears. Ichorium purifies the nether star which sucks all the warp away. Be careful, as this may have a deadly end.

1110.name: Purge All Your Warp
1110.desc:
Did you ever have a dream where you could purge all your warp with no bad side effects? This purification talisman will let your dreams come true. It gets rid of it a little at a time, so no effects!

Don't forget to wear it.

1111.name: A Chest for Bees
1111.desc: A chest for bees only. It can hold up to 125 different bees in one chest.

1112.name: Frames
1112.desc:
Untreated frames are the simplest frames. They do not work in bee houses, only in apiaries or alvearies. You will need them to craft bee houses.

They increase the bee's performance. Each untreated frame will double a bee's productivity.

1113.name: Impregnated Frames
1113.desc:
Impregnated Frames are even better than untreated Frames.
Each frame doubles the bee's productivity. It has a longer durability than the untreated frame, but a shorter one compared to the proven frame.

1114.name: Production Frames
1114.desc:
You can make proven frames or purchase them from a villager. If you can't find a beekeeper, there are a couple of different options to get one. Rescue a zombie villager, add doors to a village to get them to make kids, or perhaps use a cupid's bow and arrow...

About magic frames:
They're the core of magically endowed frames. They also have a special ability - no maximum production chance. Normal frames are ineffective to get above 16x production (4 frames) but with magic frames you can keep going - just watch out, above 16x you'll be working your bees so hard they have a chance to degenerate into ignoble ones. This can happen any bee tick, not just when they die. Frames to lower genetic decay will lower the chances of this happening.

Of course, with very long lifespans, high production genes, swarmers, and tons of frames, you may think this is worth it.

Note that normal products have a cap of 200% produced, and specialties 100%.

1115.name: Moron's Guide to Better Trees
1115.desc:
Forestry trees have a variety of attributes. Once you get a tree with a useful attribute, you can breed that attribute into other tree species. This way you can get an ultimate tree that combines all the attributes you want - including the type of fruit! You can also extract the genes with Gendustry if you can make the machines.

[warn]Please do your Forestry tree-breeding indoors or underground because butterflies cause lag.[/warn]

Keep in mind that for quests, you will need to breed the species "pure", not a hybrid. You should probably save the crossbreeding until after you get all the good ones.

For faster tree breeding, enclose your trees so that butterflies cannot escape, and breed bees with better traits for area and pollination speed. There's also frames too if you can afford it. The sieve may also help if you want to do it more exactly if you can make an alveary. You need either bees or butterflies, trees won't pollinate each other.

Trees have the following characteristics:
Saplings/Fertility: chance of getting a sapling from a destroyed leaf. Good for making into biomass or other uses. Balsa, Gooseberry and Sweetgum are what you want.

Allele     Drop Chance
Lowest   1%
Lower    2.5%
Low       3.5%
Average 5%
High      10%

[warn]Maturation/Matures[/warn]: the growth speed of a sapling into a tree. Important if you plan to cut it down right away for logs. Golden Raspberry is the fastest.

Allele    Time
Slowest  10
Slower   7
Slow      5
Average 4
Fast      3
Faster   2
Fastest  ?

Height: controls how high the tree grows. Giant Sequoia is the best, though it may be a little too large.

Allele    Value
Smallest  25%
Smaller   50%
Small      75%
Average 100%
Large    125%
Larger   150%
Largest  175%
Gigantic  BIG

Girth: the width of your tree. Wider trees have more leaves and logs, but will also need more saplings to begin growing. All saplings must be the same species and girth. The sapling in the NW corner determines the stats of the new tree, and is where bone meal must be applied to mature the tree. The Giant Sequoia is best here too.

Allele/Size
1x1
2x2
3x3
4x4

Yield: chances of getting a fruit from a destroyed leaf. Orchard Apple and Chili Pepper are the best here.

Allele     Drop Chance
Lowest   2.5%
Lower    5%
Low       10%
Average 20%
High       30%
Higher    ?%

Sappiness: the amount of biomass a sapling produces in the Forestry Fermenter. You can boost the Fermenter's output by using honey or fruit juice. Blue Mahoe and Red Maple are the best. NEI doesn't show recipes for analyzed saplings, and probably hybrids too, in the fermenter.

Allele  Forestry Biomass
Lowest   100mB
Lower    200mB
Low       300mB
Average 400mB
High       600mB

[warn]Fireproof[/warn]: applies to the wood. Only the Coast and Giant Sequoia have this.

Effect: not used in trees, so it's None for any.

Below is more information on some other parameters:

Growth: Sipiri trees have Tropical instead of Light, can only be bred in a Biome with temperature 90%-190% and humidity 90%-200%, and can only grow in 120%-190% temp and 90%-200% humidity. All other trees just need light to grow.

[note]Territory/Soil[/note]: all trees have Average, whatever that means. Probably something related to the below that never really got used.

Tolerates: if it can be planted on sand or not. Only Desert Acacia and Grandidier's Baobab have this. The trait is either Plains or Plains, Desert.

Supports: what kind of fruits it can theoretically have. Determined by Species. Only fruits from families listed under this trait can be produced by the tree, regardless of what appears under the tree's Fruits trait. There are a number of trees from the Extra Trees mod that support all 4 types, but none from Forestry itself.

Fruit: determines what fruit or nut it produces. Each fruit/nut provides different advantages:
Plum and Cherry Plum only produces 100L Fruit Juice, but have a 60% chance of producing Mulch in the Fluid Extractor or Squeezer.
Papaya produces 600L Fruit Juice, but only has a 10% chance to produce Mulch.
Coconuts produce 300L Seed Oil, but only have a 25% chance to produce Mulch.
Use a Forestry Multifarm so you don't have to break the leaf block to get the fruit/nut. Set it to Manual Orchard, and place everything first.

Family: Tells you what family of the fruit listed under the Fruits trait is: Pomes, Prunes, Nuts or Jungle.

1116.name: Breed some cultivated bees
1116.desc:
Common Princess and Forest Drone can be bred to cultivated bees.

Cultivated bees have the shortest lifespan - this means they make great princesses to breed with your newly bred drones. The queen will die quickly, quickly giving genetic material to work with. Make sure the traits you want are kept!

They are also a Fast production bee, which means lots of honey combs for powering your Beealyzer or Treealyzer.

Cultivated Bees produce Honey Combs every 0.9 minutes. Note that production times are an approximation based on the bee's base production speed and the product's rarity. They do not reflect actual production time.

Make sure your cultivated bees are pure with a Beealyzer or GT scanner.

The main bee breeding quests will require pure bees but you can use Ignoble or Pristine princesses.

1117.name: Breed some common bees
1117.desc:
To cross breed bees you need two different species of bees. One drone and one princess from different world-gen hives (Meadows and Forest as an example)

Bee Houses do not allow crossbreeding. You need an Apiary to crossbreed them to a new species. Certain frames will help this process. Shortening their lifespan means faster crosses. Increasing Mutation rate means getting mutations and thus rare bees quicker.

Common Bees produce Honey Combs every 2.2 minutes. Note that production times are an approximation based on the bee's base production speed and the product's rarity. They do not reflect actual production time.

Make sure your bees are pure by checking their genetics with a Beealyzer or GT scanner.

1118.name: Breed some noble bees
1118.desc:
Cross breeding common and cultivated bees can result in noble bees.

Noble bees produce dripping combs every 3.8 minutes and honeydew. Note that production times are an approximation based on the bee's base production speed and the product's rarity. They do not reflect actual production time.

You've heard of a drunk beekeeper saying he has perfected a method of creating proven frames using Honeydew and a little magic. It might be worth your time to try experimenting yourself.

1119.name: Dripping Combs
1119.desc:
Your noble bees produce some dripping combs.
A good source for honey, and if you have a magical mind, honeydew for proven frames.

1120.name: Breed some majestic bees
1120.desc:
Cross breeding noble and cultivated bees can result in majestic bees.

If you didn't get it from Wintry bees already, Majestic bees have a very important attribute - high fertility. They will leave 4 drones when they die. This means lots of genetic material for the next generation to work with.

Majestic bees produce dripping combs every 1.5 minutes. Note that production times are an approximation based on the bee's base production speed and the product's rarity. They do not reflect actual production time.

1121.name: Breed some imperial bees
1121.desc:
Cross breeding noble and majestic bees can result in imperial bees.

These bees produce a Regeneration buff in their active area. You can probably find a use for this in case of...accidents.

Imperial bees produce dripping combs every 3.8 minutes and royal jelly every 5.1 minutes. Note that production times are an approximation based on the bee's base production speed and the product's rarity. They do not reflect actual production time.

Royal jelly is needed in making scented paneling for an Alveary - the third tier bee housing.

You can now start working towards the Master Registry in the How to Be(e) tab.

1122.name: Royal Jelly
1122.desc: Royal jelly is a honey bee secretion that is used in the nutrition of larvae, as well as adult queens. It is only produced by the imperial bee. Its production can be increased with proven frames or any other kind of frames except for the soul frame. Royal jelly is also useful because it can craft ambrosia, which fills 8 hunger points (4 full food icons) and gives Regeneration I for 40 seconds. Royal jelly is used to craft scented paneling which are required in the creation of alveary blocks.

1123.name: Alvearies
1123.desc:
The alveary is an advanced variant of the apiary. The alveary allows for combs to be produced faster due to its natural speed increase to production (as well as the ability to add up to six frames via the Extra Bees frame housings) and due to its ability to be modified by other blocks.

It is possible to adjust the internal temperature of the alveary using the alveary heater or alveary fan in order to accommodate bees that require hotter or colder climates.

Certain bees will not produce their specialty items unless they are Jubilant. This requires matching their preferred Temperature/Humidity, without any genetic modifiers. Some bees also need a block of something beneath them as well.

It is also possible to use mutators to increase chances of mutation, alveary lighting and alveary rain shield to allow bees without the nocturnal and tolerant flyer traits to work during the night and rain, respectively, and the swarmer to use excess royal jelly to create princesses that last for a limited number of generations.

[note]If you're mutating bees that need a specific block in it, you need to place the required block under the middle bottom, and have the one directly above be a normal alveary block.[/note]

1124.name: Barrel Upgrades
1124.desc: Are your cobblestone or dirt barrels filled to the brim? No problem, upgrade them!

1125.name: Barrel Upgrades Tier 2
1125.desc: Are your upgraded cobblestone or dirt barrels full again? No problem, upgrade them some more!

1126.name: Obzinite
1126.desc:
It's upgrade time again. You could in principle make obzinite by mixing obsidian, steel and zinc in the smeltery. But to get a much better yield, you can show you are a GregTech hero and search NEI for an alternate recipe using dusts. Obzinite pickaxes are able to mine ardite in the Nether.

Leave the rest of your Obzinite as liquid in your smeltery, you'll want it to make the Tool Forge. You need 16 ingots in the smeltery to make 2 Large Plates.

1127.name: Reinforced X? Unbreakable
1127.desc:
With your steel tool you are able to mine obsidian. You can make the Reinforcement which gives a reinforced modifier which increases the durability of your tool. Reinforced level X makes a tool unbreakable.

PS: In many cases this is not worth it, since the tools don't degrade that fast, it doesn't cost much to repair, and you get better materials (with higher durability) to make them out of reasonably fast. Plus, by the time you got it to unbreakable, you'd be auto-mining everything with a miner anyway. Just use redstone, or maybe luck/silky. However, there are a few very nice use cases like lumber axes and tools for automation setups.

1276.name: Korn and Doppelkorn
1276.desc: Korn is german for wheat. That's why this special alcoholic brew is named like this. Doppelkorn means double-wheat.

1277.name: Sugarcanes
1277.desc: Sugarcanes are a source of sugar, and therefore alcohol. If you want some rum or sugar, you'll need these.

1278.name: Vodka
1278.desc: Vodka is Russian for "small water". It is a rather high proof alcoholic fluid.

1279.name: Firestone Ore/Dust
1279.desc:
Firestone ore is used to make some BC machines like the filler, and some other stuff. Unfortunately, it's in the nether...at the bottom of the lava ocean!

Here are some tips for finding it:

-Firestone only spawns in deep lava, at least 6 deep. It will only replace netherrack, so a fjord filled with soulsand won't have any. Can you think of ways to check the lava depth, and what's down there?
-Alternatively, if you keep your head at just the correct height above the lava and at the correct angle, you'll be able to see underneath it - surely there must be a hovering jetpack?
-...or just look for the biome when your cursor is over the JM map. Hell is the default one that has netherrack.

Firestone can be mined with the GT miners, if you place it within horizontal range. Firestone ore and products will cause random fires to start nearby, so get it into a backpack quickly!

1128.name: Lapis? Lucky!
1128.desc:
Maybe you were lucky and received the luck modifier on your pickaxe. If not, you may still have an unused modifier on your pick so you can add some lapis lazuli. Or you can perhaps add another modifier by adding a diamond and a gold block to your pick and then add some lapis.

You can put the lapis lazuli in more than one slot in the tool station, use blocks to raise the luck level faster, and even use lapis blocks in the crafting station with your tool for maximum luck gain.

1129.name: Redstone? Haste!
1129.desc: Mining and digging can be soooo slow. Get some redstone on your pick, shovel or axe to make it faster. You can use the Crafting Station to upgrade your tool 8 dust at a time. Later when you get a compressor you can use blocks to do 9 per block.

1130.name: Extra Modifier
1130.desc:
If you don't have an extra modifier on your tool you can easily add one. A gold block and a diamond are all you need.

You can add two more modifiers, but you'll need to read the tinkers book yourself to know what to use.

1131.name: Ardite and Cobalt
1131.desc:
If you have some time you can look for cobalt and ardite in the Nether.

You've heard rumors of a special wood only found in another land, a forest of Twilight, that can make tools strong enough to mine them.

Remember, you need an electric blast furnace to process these materials before you can make tools with them.

1132.name: Nether Quartz? Sharpness!
1132.desc:
Your sword is too dull? Get some nether quartz to sharpen it.

If you have lots of Certus quartz, 1 piece of Certus is equal to 24 Nether quartz.

1134.name: LV Advanced Steam Boiler
1134.desc:
The LV GT++ Steam Boiler is the next machine you can build to produce steam more efficiently than with the previous single block boilers. It won't use fuel unless it cools below 100 degrees and gains a higher efficiency with longer burning fuels.

[note]Fortunately, it won't explode if you let it run dry but it will waste fuel if there is no water present.[/note]

1136.name: Lossless LV Cables
1136.desc:
How about lossless 32 EU cables? Redstone alloy is a superconductor and will transfer your energy without any loss.

Redstone Alloy is a new alloy which requires an EBF. Mix redstone, silicon and coal by hand, or in the mixer for a better result.

[note]Remember not to mix it up with Red Alloy, that's the ULV one.[/note]

1137.name: Kinetic Power HV
1137.desc:
The Kinetic Generator will transform mechanical energy into electricity. The EU output depends on the KU input. It is most commonly used together with a Kinetic Wind Generator or Kinetic Water Generator. The Kinetic Steam Generator only has one tier, so you should probably use a Steam Turbine instead.
