When placing a kinetic generator, any time, the "input" side is the side facing you. So you will place the kinetic generator, then the wind/water/steam gen, and then place a rotor inside. Power can be drawn from any side of the kinetic generator, up to the rated amount per side.

WARNING: Breaking a kinetic generator improperly will not return the generator. Use a GT wrench, and right-click it. It should drop immediately. Try again if it just changed directions.

1138.name: Kinetic Wind Power HV
1138.desc:
The Kinetic Wind Generator generates energy from wind power which varies randomly, although certain factors can change this.

Unlike the Windmill, the Kinetic Wind Generator will require maintenance in the form of Rotors. These are needed to run the Kinetic Wind Generator.
Once a Rotor is placed inside the GUI, the block is given sufficient space, and the current wind level is adequate for the current rotor, the Kinetic Wind Generator will begin to generate Kinetic Energy.

This can be converted into EU by using a Kinetic Generator attached to the correct face (the black circle).

About Wind
First off, the wind strength of a particular area is affected by 3 factors: Height, Weather, and Chance. The higher up in the world you are, the higher the wind level. Below y64, there is never enough wind to be used, and KWG's will not function at all. Weather will also give boosts; Rainy weather will give a 20% boost to normal wind levels, and stormy weather will give a 50% boost. The final factor of chance makes the wind strength vary randomly, however there is a limited range of variation.

The easiest way to check the current wind level in an area is by using a Windmeter. Wind is strongest at around Y160, towards the build limit of Y255 there is very little wind, hence no point attempting to run Windmills that high. While inconvenient, the Wind Generator can run anywhere.

1139.name: Kinetic Water Power HV
1139.desc:
The Kinetic Water Generator allows Kinetic Units to be produced via the movement of water in river and ocean biomes. As with the Kinetic Wind Generator, it requires either Gearbox Rotor. Search NEI for appropriate options.

If the Kinetic Water Generator's Rotor isn't entirely submerged in water, it will alert you there is "no room for the rotor". If the Kinetic Water Generator isn't placed in a river, ocean or deep ocean biome, it will alert you it "must be placed in a river or ocean biome". Check the Game Mechanics spreadsheet on Discord for a list of biomes and their tags.

While inconvenient, the Water Generator runs more consistently.

[warn]You can use Witchery (for example) to convert biomes.[/warn]

1140.name: Rotor
1140.desc:
The Kinetic Wind and Water Generators need Gearbox Rotors to function. They can be added and removed with standard automation. Higher tier rotors will last longer. In addition, the iridium ones don't break. The IC2 ones might bug out for durability loss though. Energetic is probably the best for now, since it can give out a full amp, and more is wasted.

[note]You only need to make one rotor to complete this quest.[/note]

1141.name: Wind Meter
1141.desc: The Windmeter measures wind strength. It shows you on which height you can place your Wind Generator.

1142.name: HV Battery Buffers
1142.desc:
You'll probably want HV battery buffers as well.

[note]You only need to complete one task.[/note]

1143.name: HV Battery Hulls
1143.desc:
Better machines need better batteries. Let's make some HV battery hulls for new batteries.

It's up to you what kind of battery to make, but realistically you'll probably make Sodium or Lithium.

1144.name: HV Battery
1144.desc:
Better machines need some better batteries. Lithium is the best material to use when making batteries, at least for LV-HV.

[note]You only need to complete one task.[/note]

1145.name: Sending the Power Back
1145.desc:
Kinetic generator compatibility has been improved so that you no longer need transformers, you can connect directly with a cable. But why not use your transformer to send the power back to base more efficiently, it's a loss of about 97EU per HV amp just going from 160 straight down to 63 without it. If you transform up (after going through 1 block of cable to transform it to GT power), you only lose about 97EU per EV amp, which saves 4x the energy in regard to cable loss.

[warn]IC2 machines don't care about rain, but remember to cover your transformer.[/warn]

1146.name: GT6-Styled Pipes
1146.desc:
Wondering why new pipes don't always auto-connect? This was changed to make the pipes smarter. You can now use a wrench to connect only the pipes you want. You don't need a plate or a foil to prevent pipes from connecting anymore. The pipes will still autoconnect if you build one onto the other.

Pipes transferring anything hotter than a certain amount, like steam, will cause damage to a player touching them. You might want to cover hot pipes to prevent getting hurt. Also, if the heat level is higher than the pipe's heat capacity, it will melt.

Pressing RMB on a pipe in the center of the 'grid' will enable or disable a connection. It will have an X through it if the connection is enabled. You can do the same to any of the 4 corners to adjust the opposite side. You can similarly connect each of the 4 sides by clicking on the closest box in the grid.

Pipes have a free built-in shutter to reduce sloshing. Shift-RMB with a wrench on the side you want to prevent input.

Pipes attempt to transfer half of their capacity twice per second, so the full capacity per second in total.

Large or Huge fluid pipes can be a poor man's storage tank, holding large amounts on their own.

For machine outputs, including the boilers and Railcraft water tanks - the one made of Water Tank Siding - you will not need pumps. Other tanks will need pumps or Tinker's Construct faucets to move fluids.

You can put covers such as GT oak planks or plates on a pipe as well. Normally this prevents connections in that direction, but you can use a screwdriver to enable connections. This lets you make pipes that look nice going through walls. Use a crowbar to remove covers.

1147.name: GT6-Styled Wires and Cables
1147.desc:
They work basically the same as pipes, but you need wire cutters (or a soldering iron) instead of a wrench. Additionally, you take damage if they're not insulated (wire) but not if they are (cable).

Hint: If you click one wire or cable on the other it will auto-connect. Cables and pipes can be painted to make sure you do not cross your MV line with your EV line, or dump hot lava into your plastic water pipes. No boom today.

Ha ha just kidding there is always a boom today!

1148.name: Tier 2 (MV)
1148.desc:
The MV Machine Hull needs aluminium. You're also gonna need aluminium for every MV machine part, so keep that EBF truckin'.

Here are some tips on where to start:
- MV Electrolyzer makes getting aluminium easier.
- MV Extruder makes parts cheaper.
- MV Miner saves your time.

Have you read the quest Safety First for details about mixing tiers?
Sending MV to an LV machine causes it to explode.
Sending MV voltage over LV cables causes them to melt, which causes fire that will likely cause the machines to blow up.
Additionally, recipes done in a tier higher than the original one (not ULV, ULV counts as LV) are overclocked (OC'd). The OC'd recipes use 4x power but complete 2x faster.

1149.name: A New Alloy - Magnalium
1149.desc:
Wondering why you need magnalium now? It's not for GT machines but for a material update for your crossbow.

You already made some Aluminium dust before. Getting Aluminium will become easier once you get an EBF or MV electrolyzer.
If you can't find any aluminium, search for chests in the Twilight Forest or kill a few monsters. 

Combine Aluminium and Magnesium in your alloy smelter to get Magnalium.

1150.name: Magnalium Crossbow
1150.desc: It's time to craft a Crossbow Limb and the Crossbow Body to upgrade your old Wooden Crossbow.

1151.name: Wooden Crossbow
1151.desc:
Now you are able to make a better long range weapon - a crossbow. Use wood for the crossbow limb and the body and use obsidian (Reinforced III) for the tough binding. Normal strings for the bowstrings would be enough for now.

Assemble the bow yourself on your tool forge.

[warn]For upgrades:[/warn]
[note]Redstone - Speed on the draw
Lapis - Fortune on kills
Moss - Self-repair[/note]

NOTE: Some mobs require damage from specific tools. IF YOU ARE SHOOTING AT SOMETHING AND IT IS NOT GETTING HURT, *RUN*. And then figure out what weapon might be best to kill it.

1152.name: Fiery Bowstring
1152.desc: Why not upgrade your bowstring too? You got some from the last quest, but will need to find some more in the Nether by killing spiders. Craft some fiery bowstring with it.

1153.name: Ardite Crossbow Body
1153.desc:
To make your crossbow even better, craft an ardite crossbow body. You received a few ardite ingots as reward before but will need to find ore in the Nether and process it in an Electric Blast Furnace.

With redstone you can make your bow faster. This requires a free modifier slot on your crossbow.

Don't bother putting Nether Quartz on the crossbow, it only improves damage from whacking mobs over the head with it. Put the Nether Quartz on your bolts instead.

1154.name: Magnalium Crossbow Bolts
1154.desc:
Magnalium bolts are much better than your old wooden bolts.

You can use nether quartz on the bolts to make them even better. This requires a free modifier slot.

Experiment with NEI to find other good bolt materials.

To make these bolts, you must use a Fluid Solidifier instead of the Tinker's casting table. It's important that you get the molten iron out of the smeltery and into the Fluid Solidifier, the GT molten iron won't work.

The bolts should be in your inventory or in a bauble slot if you want to pick up the ones you fired.

1155.name: Basic Fluid Solidifier
1155.desc:
To get your molten fluids back to a solid state you need a fluid solidifier. This is handy for processing molten rubber directly from the chemical reactor. It's used for crossbow bolts as well.

Keep in mind that GT fluids are different from Tinker's Construct. You will mainly work with GT fluids going forward.

1156.name: Wooden Bolts
1156.desc:
Your crossbow need some ammo to shoot with. Let's craft some wooden bolts. For now, you can put either iron, obzinite, or steel on the tip of your bolts using the Smeltery. For higher tier bolts and tips you will need to pump metals into a Fluid Solidifier with the correct tool rod inserted.

[warn]For upgrades:[/warn]
Nether Quartz - More damage
Moss - Self-Repair (unlock in GTNH Thaumcraft tab)

[note]You can store these in a bauble slot to save on inventory space![/note]

1157.name: Rubber Soft Mallet
1157.desc:
Your wooden soft mallet breaks after a few uses. Now you can make a rubber mallet which is much more durable.

The best one is made of Steeleaf, followed by Ironwood, both easily attainable once you get access to the Twilight Forest.

1158.name: Ironwood
1158.desc:
Ironwood is much harder and durable than rubber. Ideal for a soft mallet. Collect some liveroot underground from some roots, macerate it and mix the powder with iron and gold to create a new magical material.

You can make some unique armor with this material.

1159.name: Steeleaf
1159.desc: If you locate a labyrinth you may find steeleaf in chests inside. This is the best material for a soft mallet for now, however a unique armor out of steeleaf can be made too.

1160.name: Empty Cells
1160.desc: With your plate bender you're able to make empty cells. The circuit needs to be configured to 12 otherwise you will make foils. Once you have an LV Extruder, you can make cells directly from ingots.

1161.name: Red Alloy Cables
1161.desc: Want some red alloy cables? Buy some!

1162.name: Tin Cables
1162.desc: Want some tin cables? Buy some!

1163.name: Copper Wires
1163.desc: Want some copper wires? Buy some!

1164.name: Universal Macerator
1164.desc: One of the most important machines in HV is the Universal Macerator. It has an additional output slot to get more materials when macerating ores. You want this, really, really, badly. If you get it, you can say goodbye to your Gallium worries!

1165.name: Rubber Ingots
1165.desc: Want some rubber ingots? Buy some!

1166.name: Quartzite Veins
1166.desc: The Nether is full of different kinds of quartz. Go and find a quartzite vein. They're located between Y 80-120. It might be hard to find one, because that area is usually air, you'll just have to keep looking.

1167.name: Lithium
1167.desc: Lithium can be washed out of Lepidolite Ore or centrifuged out of either Lepidolite Ore or Spodumene Ore. At MV you can electrolyze clay dust for it. Look for canyons to find hardened clay and macerate it for dust.

1168.name: Sodium
1168.desc: Sodium can be washed out of Glauconite Ore and Sand. It can also be electrolyzed out of salt.

1169.name: Bath Salt
1169.desc: Ugh, you feel so dirty. Go and take a bath.

1170.name: Bath Soap
1170.desc: Ugh, you feel so dirty. Get some soap and take a long hot bath to get rid of some of that nasty Warp.

1171.name: Damascus Steel Ingots
1171.desc: Damascus steel is very durable and useful for GT tools. Buy some!

1172.name: Cheaper Annealed Production
1172.desc: It's even faster and cheaper to make annealed copper in the arc furnace than in the electric blast furnace. Annealed copper is a very good material for cables in MV. It only loses 1 EU per block.

1173.name: Wrought Iron Ingots
1173.desc: Want some wrought iron ingots? Buy some!

1174.name: Steel Ingots
1174.desc: Want some steel ingots? Buy some!

1175.name: Aluminium Ingots
1175.desc: Want some aluminium ingots? Buy some!

1176.name: Stainless Steel Ingots
1176.desc: Want some stainless steel ingots? Buy some!

1177.name: Thaumium Cooking
1177.desc: Now it's time to 'cook' your first thaumium ingots. After first researching it, you need iron ingots and praecantatio essentia to make it.

1178.name: Food 2.0
1178.desc:
Forget everything you know about food and values. With your new canning machine you are able to fill tin cans with very nourishing food. This food can be consumed in milliseconds and makes you full and happy. And you can eat as much as you want without getting tired of the food.

Eating before you are starving restores saturation and conserves cans.

1179.name: EBF Info
1179.desc:
You can use a scanner to manually check how your EBF works, or you can make an information panel from nuclear control and always see the status. Make a GT sensor kit and use it on the EBF controller. Place the sensor card in your info panel. Enable it with a redstone signal.

The GregTech sensor card can monitor GregTech machines like the EBF, Turbines, and Pyrolyse ovens. This is handy for keeping an eye on maintenance issues for multiblocks.

If you want to put the panels far from the machines they are monitoring, you will need range upgrades.

[note]There's also a more advanced version.[/note]

1180.name: Passive Chunkloading
1180.desc:
Using the MV bender you can finally press dense plates. So let's craft a passive chunkloader. It will also load a 3x3 chunk area. You need ender pearls or coins to power the chunkloader.

Passive anchors will run for 1 hour off of an ender pearl, and a varying amount depending on the coin used.

Personal Anchors are loaded once the owner visits, and stay loaded while the player is logged in.

Passive Anchors are loaded once the owner visits, and stay loaded until the server resets.

[warn]World Anchors[/warn] are always loaded, even after a server reset.

1181.name: Minecart with Tank, Workbench or Furnace
1181.desc: Want to transport oil or other fluids, have a portable crafting table or a portable chest? Then you need to craft some minecarts with tanks, chests or crafting tables.

1182.name: Fluid Loader and Unloader
1182.desc:
If you plan to automate the oil transport from your oil field far away, you need a fluid loader and unloader. A locking track will stop the train as long as oil is loaded or unloaded.

[note]PS: You should probably use the Long Distance Fluid Pipeline once you can make it in LV unless you really love trains.[/note]

Full details on how to use these may require online research (GTNH changes the recipes, not normal Railcraft functionality), but here is a simple list of things to do:

1. Make a loop of track from your base to the oil well.
2. Place a locking track near the well, and put a fluid loader two blocks above it. The track should be in Locking Mode - use a crowbar to change it.
3. Use the tank car on the filter in the loader and set it to Immediate mode.
4. Connect the well to the loader as normal.
5. Put a fluid unloader under a locking track at your base, and set it the same as the loader.
6. Automate the locomotive similarly - a fluid loader above a steam locomotive set to manual and filtered to the locomotive.

Either you got it working, you need to do more research, you realized it moves 32 buckets a trip and decided to just walk, or you can go to Discord and ask why anyone would build a train.

Item loaders also exist, but the main use for them may be to refuel your locomotive.

1183.name: Locomotive 0.1 Alpha
1183.desc:
The furnace cart is a cheap locomotive. Click with coal or charcoal on the cart and it will start moving.

This is a curiosity, and probably you will use the steam locomotive from the next quest for an actual train.

1184.name: Steam Locomotive
1184.desc:
The steam locomotive is much better than the furnace cart. You can control the speed and the direction the locomotive will move. You need to put water and coal inside to make steam to power the locomotive.

You can use a water tank or other water source to refill this locomotive by placing a Fluid Loader above it at a stop. The loader can be placed either directly above the track, or a block higher to give you clearance when riding the train. (You can't actually ride a train unless you sit in a normal minecart.)

This locomotive runs on any fuel you can burn in a steam boiler, perhaps you will want to use compressed coal blocks or similar. You can use an Item Loader to refuel it.

Once you have stable electricity in MV, you will likely move to the Electric Locomotive.

1185.name: Electric Locomotive
1185.desc: Electric Locomotives are much better than steam ones. You need electric rail for it.

1186.name: Boost Your Speed
1186.desc: If you decide to not use a locomotive and drive with one or two carts only, you can use some booster tracks. Activate them with a lever or a redstone torch. You can also use them to slow down, especially when doing corners!

1187.name: The Transcontinental Railroad - High-Speed
1187.desc: Did you ever dream of driving in a railcart at high speeds? Well your dreams can come true with the high speed tracks. Be careful though; you need to lower the speed when your rails change direction or when the track ends.

1188.name: The Transcontinental Railroad - Creeper-Proof
1188.desc:
Railroads in the nether are dangerous. The reinforced tracks are blast proof. Hopefully you're blast proof too?

You can move 25% faster than with normal tracks.

NEI doesn't show the right recipe on the first page. Go through the recipes a bit to find the right one.

1189.name: The Transcontinental Railroad - Electric
1189.desc:
Electrical tracks are used together with your electric locomotive. You need to power the rails with energy.

NEI doesn't show the right recipe on the first page. Go through the recipes a bit to find the right one.

[note]Alternatively you can use the search bar to search for a specific name, in this case electric tracks![/note]

1190.name: Electric Feeder Unit
1190.desc: To get your tracks electrified you need an electric feeding unit, shunting wires and some support frames. You can use an MFE or a transformer to get GT energy into the feeder unit.

1191.name: Magic "Staffter"
1191.desc: It is also possible to create a bizarre hybrid of staff and scepter (sometimes called a "staffter"), by using a staff core instead of a wand core in the scepter recipe. The resulting device can neither hold a focus nor be placed in a crafting table, but it can still be used to activate constructs, or as a "vis battery" for Repair-enchanted equipment or Runic shielding.

1192.name: Aluminum Berries
1192.desc: And a few aluminum berries for cash.

1193.name: Gold Berries
1193.desc: And a few gold berries for cash.

1194.name: Iron Berries
1194.desc: And a few iron berries for cash.

1195.name: Copper Berries
1195.desc: And a few copper berries for cash.

1196.name: Tin Berries
1196.desc: And a few tin berries for cash.

1197.name: Fusion Reactor MKII - Bigger, Better, Fusor
1197.desc:
Now that you have some Europium, put it to use by making a MK II reactor.

This one requires the same block placement, but replaces the LuV casings with fusion machine casings and the superconducting coil blocks with fusion coil blocks. The energy hatches must now be at least ZPM too.

Each energy hatch has an energy capacity of 20M EU and draws up to 8192 EU/t. That means it can handle start costs up to 320M EU and power consumption up to 131072 EU/t. Furthermore, MK I recipes in a MK II reactor are granted a 2/2 perfect overclock which doubles the speed and power consumption.

1198.name: Upgrading the Reactor
1198.desc:
A MK II is nice, but why not make a MK III? This time you need UV+ energy hatches, as well as the Fusion Machine Casing MK II.

Each energy hatch has an energy capacity of 40M EU and draws up to 32768 EU/t. That means it can handle start costs up to 640M EU and power consumption up to 524288 EU/t.

The MK III reactor also grants a 2/2 perfect overclock for every machine tier above the minimum recipe tier.

1199.name: Making Americium
1199.desc:
To upgrade your reactor further and to make UV motors, you will need to get some americium. The recipe is pretty expensive, but once you made the next tier of reactor, you will unlock a simpler and cheaper one. However, to get there you will need a lot of americium already, so better make sure you are producing enough lutetium.

Let's start with just 48 ingots. Enough for a single UV motor.

1200.name: Making Power With Your Plasma
1200.desc:
Now that you have a reactor, it's time to utilize it to create power.

The simplest plasma you can make for power is Helium plasma, but it also provides a lot of power. Deuterium is renewable from water, and you can get Helium-3 from Endstone.

However, you will need a way to convert the plasma into EU. To do this you will need a large plasma turbine. This will also need a dynamo hatch that can handle the power you're outputting. For Helium plasma, you will want a LuV Buffered Dynamo or above.

1201.name: Turbine Time
1201.desc:
Your fancy new Large Plasma Turbine will need a rotor to go inside of it.

You can make it out of any GT material, but the best right now is NORMAL Draconium. [warn]Always test in Creative first![/warn]

When using turbines, be careful of flow rates. To find the optimal flow rate, you divide the optimal energy flow rate listed on the turbine, by the energy value for the plasma. You can also use the Large Turbine Calculator (there's a link on the main spreadsheet, which is linked to in discord), or scan the multiblock's controller while feeding it a minimal amount of plasma to see what the optimal flow is. Make sure to use a Fluid Regulator set to this flow rate for highest fuel efficiency, or go beyond it to get more EU/t at a cost to efficiency, with Plasma Turbines being the best at retaining efficiency beyond the optimal flow.

If you go beyond the optimal flow, you will get a higher EU/t value, but less fuel efficiency, and the maximum EU/t value depends on the turbine type and its Overflow Efficiency tier. For this type of turbine, tier 1 reaches the max at 250% of optimal flow to produce 158% of the EU/t, tier 2 reaches the max at 400% of optimal flow to produce 214% of the EU/t and tier 3 reaches the max at 550% of optimal flow to produce 301% of the EU/t. The closer you get to this maximum, the more fuel efficiency you're throwing away.

The choice is yours!

[note]For a lot of extra information on fusion, check out the new quest line called Powerful Nuclear Physics.[/note]

1202.name: I Put Fusion in Your Fusion so you Can Fusion While You Fusion
1202.desc:
To make Oganesson, you'll need to make Californium. To make that, you'll need Beryllium and Plutonium 239, all in the Fusion Reactor. After the two fusion chains, you'll have the best noble gas in the pack, which is used in several places.

In this tier, you can send it to Void Miners, to increase their ore production speed, or EBFs, to make them run faster, although the EBF option is too weak right now (changes coming soon). Later on, Oganesson will be used in other places, like the optical circuit line, or to produce Metastable Oganesson, which is needed in much larger quantities later on.

Oganesson replication is on the way out, and the fusion recipe has improved a lot, so there is no longer an incentive to replicate and your Fusion Reactor should keep running for Og.

1203.name: Time for Another Reactor
1203.desc:
While one reactor is nice and all, why not make another? This time you can use the MK3 americium recipe to make it easier.

Costing just 1 ingot of Plutonium, and 2 cells of hydrogen, this recipe is much easier and faster than the MK2 version. By making americium plasma, then putting it through a turbine, you will get americium in the liquid state. You will need at least 132 plates for the controller and the fusion machine casings.

Plus, free power!

1204.name: Crafting Time
1204.desc: It's time to get yourself a 3x3 crafting area. Use your flint and some wood to build one!

1205.name: Hoppers
1205.desc: Hoppers come in handy when you want to automate your machines. You can input items via hoppers directly into GT pipes and you don't have to use a conveyor module. However, the input rate is much slower, and the hopper must be beneath its source inventory.

1206.name: Light Fuel
1206.desc: Want some light fuel? Buy some!

1207.name: Heavy Fuel
1207.desc: Want some heavy fuel? Buy some!

1208.name: Cart Modules: Solar Power
1208.desc: Your cart needs power, obviously. A cart can be powered via coal, solar or lava. If you are able to make solar panels you can power your cart with the sun.

1209.name: Cart modules: Digging
1209.desc: Want to automate digging for ores? A drill head made out of iron is the cheapest alternative.

1210.name: Cart Modules: Thermal Engine
1210.desc: Your cart needs power, obviously. A cart can be powered via coal, solar or lava. Why not power your cart with lava?

1211.name: Cart Modules: More Storage
1211.desc: After you craft side chests you can add more different chests to the cart. Some sides are reserved for tools so no chest can be placed there.

1212.name: Starting Fusion
1212.desc:
Nuclear fusion is the next major step in your progression, and to accomplish it you will need a Fusion Reactor Mark I. You will unlock new materials across the next few tiers with fusion, making this a required multiblock. Europium is what you will need first to make it to ZPM.

At the same time, fusion can generate power for you, primarily via Helium Plasma in this first tier. The speed of the reactor can make for significant EU/t, but actually consuming this plasma fast enough at this early stage is difficult. You will have Large Plasma Turbines, which can run a buffered dynamo for 4 output amps. This might require a lot of spam compared to powergen setups you might be using, such as UCFE, vac nukes or other options. Later, you will unlock XL turbines and a fusion line for them. 

For your first reactor, you will need a controller, 1-16 fluid input hatches, 1-16 fluid output hatches, and 16 LuV+ energy hatches. Each energy hatch has an energy capacity of 10M EU and draws up to 2048 EU/t.

That means you can do any fusion recipe with a start cost of 160M EU or less, and takes 32768 EU/t or less. Using more fluid hatches means you need less fusion casings. While this doesn't matter as much for the mk1 reactor, this principle helps tremendously with the higher reactor tiers.

1213.name: Getting Europium
1213.desc: To make a mk2 reactor, ender quarry, T5 rocket, unlock ZPM, or other things, you will need europium. Once you make a t5 rocket you will be able to find this as part of a very rare ore. Until then you need to produce it in the reactor. But it's probably not worth it to look for the ore anyway.

1214.name: Clockwork Engine
1214.desc: Producing RF Power by hand is a good idea for making one or two recipes in the Forestry machines. The turrets can be charged with it too.

1215.name: Bio Gas Engine
1215.desc:
Producing RF power with lava is possible with the help of the biogas engine. You need water, fruit juice, liquid honey, milk, seed oil, or forestry biomass in the other slot. After a brief warmup time, the engine outputs up to 50 RF/t depending on the fluid used.

The possible fluids give the following power:

Water: 10 RF/t for 1k ticks
Fruit juice: 10 RF/t for 2.5k ticks
Liquid honey: 20 RF/t for 2.5k ticks
Milk: 10 RF/t for 10k ticks
Seed oil: 30 RF/t for 2.5k ticks
Biomass: 50 RF/t for 2.5k ticks

1216.name: Peat Fired Engine
1216.desc:
Peat fired engines use Peat or Bituminous Peat to run. Peat burns for 5000 ticks and produces 10 RF every tick, 1 peat makes 0.66 Ash, so you get 2 Ash for burning 3 peat. Bituminous Peat burns for 6000 ticks and produces 20 RF/tick.

Peat Farms can be set up by using a managed farm with tin electron tubes. It is possible with a manual farm too.

1217.name: Copper, Tin, Bronze Tubes
1217.desc:
Electron tubes are used to set farms to a special kind of crop and can be used in engines, alvearies and some crafting recipes.

Use a soldering iron to attach an electron tube to a circuit board.

1218.name: Iron, Golden, Diamantine Tubes
1218.desc:
Electron tubes are used to set farms to a special kind of crop and can be used in engines, alvearies and some crafting recipes.

Use a soldering iron to attach an electron tube to a circuit board.

1219.name: Rubberised, Obsidian, Lapis Tubes
1219.desc:
Electron tubes are used to set farms to a special kind of crop and can be used in engines, alvearies and some crafting recipes.

Use a soldering iron to attach an electron tube to a circuit board.

1220.name: Blazing, Ender, Emerald Tubes
1220.desc:
Electron tubes are used to set farms to a special kind of crop and can be used in engines, alvearies and some crafting recipes.

Use a soldering iron to attach an electron tube to a circuit board.

1221.name: Advanced Circuit Assembler
1221.desc:
EV circuits need an MV Circuit Assembler, so make one.

[note]Shortly after this, all circuit recipes will need a cleanroom, so get started on it![/note]

1222.name: Advanced Circuit Assembler II
1222.desc: With your workstation circuits you can craft an HV circuit assembler. This is needed for IV circuits.

1223.name: EV Circuit Assembler
1223.desc: An EV circuit assembler requires IV Circuits to craft. With this assembler you can make higher tier circuits.

1224.name: Epoxid
1224.desc:
Epoxid is needed for Advanced Circuits boards. You need Sodium Hydroxide, Epichlorohydrin and Bisphenol A to make it.

You can also use the molten form for some other things you might want to check out.

[note]Using a Large Chemical Reactor? You might be able to skip some steps, look around NEI to find out more.[/note]

1225.name: Mainframes, Your First IV Circuit
1225.desc: Mainframes are the first IV Circuits you can make. It's used for a few EV machines, and basically all IV machines and components.

1226.name: Glycerol
1226.desc:
Glycerol is a byproduct of making bio diesel, but it's really useful too. It's used to make epoxid in EV, and for making dynamite, I guess. Both TNT and iTNT are better though.

[note]PS: You don't actually need dynamite, and epoxid can be made another way, if you don't want to make bio diesel forever/at all.[/note]

1227.name: HCL
1227.desc:
More than just a byproduct of your Sodium Persulfate production. HCl becomes important for Epoxid in HV/EV tier. You can also use it to process bee combs.

You can also make it directly from Hydrogen and Chlorine.

1228.name: Epichlorohydrin
1228.desc: Epichlorohydrin is another product needed in your epoxid production. By now you should be experienced enough to figure out which path is best for your setup using NEI.

1229.name: Phenol
1229.desc:
The next circuit line requires Epoxid. To start, let's get some Phenol.

The easiest way is to get phenol out of heavy fuel. There are other ways, like getting it out of wood tar from a pyrolyse oven, isopropylbenzene and oxygen, or chlorobenzene and water.

Already automated Epoxid? Hold 128 sheets to skip this questline.

1230.name: Bisphenol A
1230.desc: The next step towards Epoxid is to get Bisphenol A. You will need to combine Phenol, Acetone and HCl in a chemical reactor.

1231.name: You're Gonna Hate This 1-4 in HV
1231.desc: Pumps, pistons, conveyor modules and robot arms are the most needed machine parts in HV. Go and craft one of each.

1528.name: Personal Chunkloading
1528.desc:
Let's craft a personal chunkloader. It will load a 3x3 chunk area. You need ender fragments, ender pearls, or coins to power the chunkloader.

Personal anchors can be powered with ender fragments for 1h, ender pearls for 4 hours, and a varying amount depending on the coin used.

Personal Anchors are loaded once the owner visits, and stays loaded while the player is logged in.

Passive Anchors are loaded once the owner visits, and stays loaded until the server resets.

World Anchors are always loaded, even after a server reset.

1529.name: Chunkloading
1529.desc:
With your HV assembler you can craft a world anchor. So let's make one. It will again load a 3x3 chunk area.

You need coins to power the world anchor, it will run a varying amount depending on the coin used.

Personal Anchors are loaded once the owner visits, and stay loaded while the player is logged in.

Passive Anchors are loaded once the owner visits, and stay loaded until the server resets.

[warn]World Anchors[/warn] are always loaded, even after a server reset.

[note]Check with server operators in case world anchors are disabled. On the official servers, world anchors are currently disabled to keep TPS high.[/note]

1530.name: Ceres Dungeon
1530.desc:
Go get the schematics for tier 4 rockets.

This will open the Planets Venus and Mercury and the Jupiter Moon Io.

1531.name: Tier 4 Control Computer
1531.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

1488.name: Dezil's Marshmallow
1488.desc:
In the last step you need to infuse your Marshmallow with some epic food in the thaumcraft infusion altar. Before you can do it you need to unlock the research.

Is this worth it? Is there any enemy worthy of using the buffs this gives on? That's up to you.

1489.name: Now You're Really Gonna Hate This 1-4 in LuV
1489.desc: Pumps, pistons, conveyor modules and robot arms are the most needed machine parts in LuV. Go and craft one of each with your new Assembly Line.

1490.name: Now You're Really Gonna Hate This 5 and 6 in LuV
1490.desc: Some machines need emitters and sensors, like the analyzer or the circuit assembler.

1491.name: HSS-G
1491.desc:
After you craft an IV assembler, upgrade your Electric Blast Furnace with new Coils out of HSS-G Ingots. This ingot becomes even more very important in LuV tier. But right now both of these are still far away. Just make a bit that you need for the next rocket.

To find a Molybdenum Vein, you probably want to make a high quality Prospector's Scanner with a good range.

1492.name: Trichlorosilane - continued
1492.desc:
Keep the hydrogen and continue in a second chemical reactor to obtain the purified Solar Grade Silicon. You get all the Hydrochloric Acid back!

Maybe you can automate this process?

1493.name: Tier 6 (LuV)
1493.desc: LuV-Level. You will need Rhodium-Plated Palladium for machines. Suggested planets and moons to visit: Jupiter's Moon Io, Venus and Mercury. Machine parts will now require an Assembly Line - Check Multiblock Goals for details.

1494.name: Stainless Taking Too Long?
1494.desc:
Time for a second EBF!

You can make ingots faster in 3 ways - better coils, higher tier energy hatches, or adding more furnaces. It's probably time to add a second EBF to your base.

Multiblock machines can share casings, mufflers, and input/output hatches/buses, but not maintenance hatches (you can share the auto ones though). You can share energy hatches, but only if you can supply enough power to all machines it's a part of.

Remember, pollution will be very high with two EBFs active at once! Watch out for high pollution effects. Use the best mufflers available and don't place the EBFs in the same chunk as farmland or other high pollution machines like Pyrolyse Ovens and Large Boilers. Maybe it is time to do something more active?

You can use whatever coils you have lying around, but you certainly want to start searching for Mica if you haven't found any yet.

1495.name: All Alone in the Night
1495.desc:
Thanks to our amazing devs, you no longer have to use a command block to reload your questbook between updates. This means you'll get all new quests automatically upon updating to a new version of GTNH! Isn't that convenient?

If, for whatever reason, you want to manually reload your questbook, you can do so by typing /bq_admin default load in the chat window. You will need to be an admin for it to work, though.

Here's another questbook in case you already lost the one you spawned with. Don't forget that it can also be viewed at any time by pressing the Tilde (~) key if you have not changed its keybind.

Some command blocks are still used for specific tasks, but they do not concern the average player since they are admin-only and thus are not craftable.

1496.name: How the Heck Do I Find Ores?
1496.desc:
GregTech ore generation is based on veins. Veins have a fixed height and a rectangular base.

There is a vein every 3 chunks in x and z direction. The veins typically extend into the neighboring chunks and have varying forms. The center point however is very regular. It is based on the chunk number. Look at upper left of the screen to find the chunk number. This info is displayed by InGame Info XML mod. For now, you need to see chunk info like "Chunk X: 3 Z: -2".

The 3,-2 is the chunk's address. The ore centers are located at chunk numbers of the form 3N+1 for both x and z. So, 4,7 is an ore center, as well as 10,16, -23,19, and so on. If you are in the right spot InGame Info will also say that "This is an ore chunk.".

Another useful tool is the NEI ore vein overlay which you activate by pressing F9 three times. It shows the 3x3 areas and marks the center blue.

The generation of Ore Chunks is not related to biomes.

For reading all the way, here's some iron.

1497.name: Digging Deeper
1497.desc:
Now that you know what an Ore Chunk is, dig all the way down to bedrock to find if there is a vein located in it.

Check out an ore in NEI to see where you can find a vein that contains it. Search for it and click on it to get this info, among other things.

There are many types of ores, even just for getting one material like Iron, so don't stick to only one ore.

You can also search small ores in NEI, but not all of them are used.

1499.name: Chemical Bath
1499.desc:
Wait, what? You want more byproducts from your ores? A cheaper way to make paper? Well, the chemical bath is perfect for you!

You'll have to provide liquids like mercury to extract different products like nickel, silver, copper, and platinum (only as PMP) from ores.

For paper you'll only need some water and wood pulp. Maybe you'll find more uses for it!

1500.name: Better Questing?
1500.desc:
Better Questing has a number of useful commands for the questbook (QB). You need to have cheats enabled to use them.

/bq_admin default load
Use this command to update your QB after you update the modpack. It reloads the quest database from the file. Your save file stores a version of the QB that can be different from the one in the file.

/bq_admin edit
Toggles editing quests. Pressing RMB on the quest will open a context menu to share it or copy its ID.

/bq_admin reset all <Player>
Resets all quests. Use this if you want to start over, or are editing the QB. You should specify the player as well if it is a server, otherwise you can skip that part.

/bq_admin reset <ID> <Player>
Resets a specific quest, defined by the quest ID. Sometimes it may be easier to go into Edit Mode > Designer Tool to specify what to reset.

/bq_admin complete <number> <Player>
Completes a specific quest, similarly to the above. You can still claim the rewards. Use this command if a quest is broken, and then report it on GitHub, but please check if it's already reported.

/bq_admin default save
Saves changes to the QB after editing it.

/bq_copyquests <PlayerWithQuestsCompleted> <NewPlayer>
If you want to sync the quests with someone in your party, then use this. They won't get any rewards for that though.

/bq_admin hardcore
Enables BQ3 Hardcore Mode. You only start with 3 lives, so best of luck. Additional life hearts are available in the Kill all the Things tab.

Aside from the consume-type quests, a quest counts for everyone in the party if one person finished it. However, if someone joined your party later, you need to get them up to speed first either through /bq_copyquests above, or manually. Otherwise, your quest completion won't count for them because the requirements for that quest are not fulfilled for them.

If your singleplayer world does not have cheats enabled, go to the ESC screen, open to LAN, then enable cheats. This makes pausing impossible until you reload afterwards. If you host a server, you can run the commands in the command line, without "/". You can also edit the level.dat file, and set 'allowCommands' to 1, but make sure to backup your world before doing that.

[warn]For quests to save properly, you must be online and logged into your Mojang account when starting the game. Sorry, it's a limitation of Better Questing.[/warn]

1501.name: Backup Your Stuff...
1501.desc:
The mod ServerUtilities (SU) is included in the pack to backup your world. You can adjust save path, frequency, and the number of backups to keep in its settings at '.minecraft\serverutilities\serverutilities.cfg'.

If you need to restore from a backup, you can find them in the backups folder in the instance directory.

Remember that the mods JourneyMap and Thaumcraft Node Tracker store information outside of what most solutions backup, so please backup your journeymap and tcnodetracker folders yourself.

The Thaumcraft data is a little buggy, losing scanned aspect data and Thaumonomicon data, so consider backing it up as well. The file is at 'saves\<worldname>\playerdata\<nameofplayer>.thaum'.

If you move to another drive or computer, don't forget to migrate all the backed up files, not just the world folder.

1502.name: Tesla Would be Proud
1502.desc: Tired of sticking your EnderIO items in the capacitor bank to charge it? Time to make a wireless charger.

1503.name: Nobody Wants an Explosion...
1503.desc:
...right? So when building multiblocks, make sure you keep in mind that electric machines don't like getting rain or snow on them. They'll start fires, and those fires have a chance of causing an explosion. This explosion will explode other machines, cascading until your whole base is a hole in the ground. This is one of the reason backups are a thing. Stay safe out there!

If worse comes to worst, there is logs/Explosion.log that tracks what caused an explosion. However, this file appears to reset on world load.

1504.name: Divine Rod of the Gods?
1504.desc:
The Ore Finder Wand reduces the tedium of locating needed veins. If you're not sure how to craft Magnetic Iron Rod for it, check its Shapeless Crafting tab in NEI.

Once you put an ore sample into the wand, it will tell you if this ore is nearby. When you get close to the target, you will hear the sound, and the wand will tilt down to the point of being upside-down if there's a lot of target ore nearby.

Use NEI to figure out which ore is the best for your search. If you don't have the needed ore, some basic ones can be purchased in the Coins tab.

Open the wand's inventory with Shift+RMB when looking at the ground. Put the sample ore into the slot.
The rod has a 60-block range both up and down, so it can miss a vein that is located very low or if you are above the sea level.

The rod appears to work based on the GT's Material system, so anything of the same material will work.
That includes crushed ores, dusts, ingots, gems, rods, you name it. For things that don't belong to a particular Material, the rod appears to search only for that exact block - for instance, you won't detect small ores with the usual ore block.

If the detected ore is a part of a vein, you will see a discovery message in the chat, and the vein will be automatically logged into the visual prospecting tab of your journey map.

The wand also works from inventory and hotbar, so you don't need to be actively holding it, but you get style points if you do.

The ore in the wand is stored with the player, not the wand. In other words, remove the ore if you want to give the wand to your teammate.

The Ore Finder Wand doesn't work in space! The vast cosmic energies of the sun interfere with its inner workings! Or the devs want you to use something more complicated than a stick. You choose.

1505.name: Not Enough Items - Now with Bookmarks and Recent History!
1505.desc:
More like too many items! Here are some tips for using NEI.

When you have your inventory open, you can type the search terms in the text bar at the bottom. Make sure your GUI size is set correctly, or you won't be able to see it. Double-click the bar to highlight any matching items in your inventory or inventories you're looking at. Handy for searching for a specific ore, as they tend to look the same!

Even better, you can use T to search nearby inventories, which will emit some particles.

We have also provided the special Y command to look for a machine in the world, in case you've forgotten where you placed that Advanced Circuit Assembler.

We now have an extra-fancy Bookmark feature! The default key is A, but can be changed in the NEI config window. Bookmarks will be on the left side of the inventory, and you can look up their recipes or uses as normal. To move a bookmark, drag it with Shift+LMB. You can even bookmark entire recipes with Shift+A or recipes with amounts using Ctrl+Shift+A.

Pressing O will hide NEI, useful for fancy screenshots.

1506.name: NEI is More Powerful Than You Imagine
1506.desc:
NEI has some really nice advanced features too.

Clicking LMB or pressing R on an item shows the recipes to create it. RMB or U show recipes that use the item. Backspace moves you back to the previously browsed recipe.

Hold Shift to stop the oredict cycling through items, and to show the oredict entries for the current item under your cursor.

Search using @ to find all items from that mod; for example, @thaumcraft will list all Thaumcraft items and blocks.

You can combine multiple search terms with a space. @thaumcraft table will search for anything with table in the name or description, in the mod Thaumcraft.

It has even more advanced features than mentioned here and many configuration options, you can look in the NEI option menu under Inventory to find a few. Here you can change more things about how the search works (like using regex), toggle collapsible items or the item zoom panel, or configure the history panel to your liking.

1507.name: Making a List, Checking it Twice
1507.desc:
As your base grows, you're going to have a dozen different long term goals each with many steps. Build a clipboard to keep track of what you need to do.

[note]Hint: You can put the clipboard on a wall using shift-right-click.[/note]

1508.name: A Better Crafting Table
1508.desc:
Tired of your stuff falling out of your crafting table every time you walk away? This fancy table from Tinker's Construct has slots to hold the items in your recipe.

You can also use it to upgrade tools with 8 modifiers at a time. Very handy for Redstone additional speed and Lapis looting!

When it's placed next to a vanilla chest, it will even show the chest's inventory in the crafting window. Single or double vanilla chests only, please.

Cut it again for a sleek, low profile version.

1509.name: Travelocity!
1509.desc: A staff of traveling can get you really moving around your base. Hold shift and right-click to teleport in that direction. If you've got travel anchors, just right-clicking will send you to them. The range is 16 blocks for normal TP, 128 to an anchor.

1510.name: Biomes Everywhere, But Not the One You Want?
1510.desc:
Tired of searching all over the map for that Bamboo Forest or Tropical Rainforest? Desperate for clay dust and need to find a Canyon or Mesa biome? Nature's compass will lead you to the biome you're looking for, if it's within its range.

The compass menu has the following functionality:
* When Sorted By: Name, you can filter the list by name or its parts. The search is case-insensitive.
* You can search by keys and their combinations. For instance, name: bamboo, climate: warm, bh: -1.8, tag: forest.
All available keys are:
name,
climate [hellish, hot, warm, normal, cold, icy],
baseheight | bh (float, can be negative),
heightvariation | hv (float),
rainfall | rain (float),
temperature | temp (float),
humidity [damp, normal, arid],
tags.

Some things to keep in mind:
* Biomes from other dimensions show up on the list, don't bother searching for those unless you're in that dimension.
* In general, the full list has biomes that are not generated by Realistic World Generator, like Sunflower Plains.
* Radius 10000 means it couldn't find the biome. In this case after the initial search, its menu will show only the biomes in the 10k radius. Use Shift+RMB to see the whole list again.
* You can try again at a different location - the range is 10k from your current position.
* Artificially created biomes count, so remove them if you want to find a natural one.
* There's lots of different 'versions' of biomes under the same category, so if you want a tropical one for tropical bees, there are actually a bunch of places you can get it.
* For those who don't mind the freeze when searching, the radius can be changed in a config. The HUD position can also be changed in the config.

1511.name: Humid and Warm?
1511.desc: Stop guessing what the humidity and temperature are for your biome. Use the habitat locator to get a reading on your current climate.

1512.name: Put the Drones in a Jar and Shake it Up
1512.desc:
Once you get good effects from your bees, instead of just trashing the excess drones, you can put them in collector's jars to get the effects anywhere in your base.

Healing? XP? Poison?

The collector's jars can even be piped or connected to a conveyor so that drones last a long time.

[note]PS: Using the regen effect of Imperial bees while doing Blood Magic may be useful.[/note]

1513.name: Death to Bees!
1513.desc: These frames will let you kill your bees faster. It's to breed the next generation sooner, right? Not because you don't like them?

1514.name: Keep Them Around
1514.desc: These frames will keep your bees buzzing for longer. If you care, that is.

1515.name: Are the Bees Buzzing, or Screaming?
1515.desc:
These frames cause mutations at an increased rate. They're super useful.

About the stats on frames you see when holding shift, you'll note that some have a different number in brackets next to them, this means that this is the maximum they can be stacked. This only applies to the Extra Bees ones.

The stats on the frames are also multiplicative instead of additive, so after 4 frames in an alveary, soul frames will max out. On the other hand, accelerated frames will take 9 to get there, but you can just keep on going.

If you pile up the multipliers, 5x from stimulator, 3x from mutator, and 4x1.8x frames, it'll get you to 157x, getting you those 1% bees in a single generation!

1516.name: Bees Buzzing Beyond the Dark
1516.desc:
If even the soul frame takes too long for your taste, the oblivion frame will destroy a bee in a single bee tick (27s). Be careful using more than one, there are rumors it might harm your princesses...

[note]You can find them in Stronghold libraries or make them from Dragon Eggs. You can also use a Thaumic Restorer to fix it, and most frames too.[/note]

1517.name: Need a Place For All Those Ores?
1517.desc: ...at last, the final chest, it will keep its inventory even after breaking! Item transportation will become much simpler with this!

1518.name: All the Crops You Could Want
1518.desc:
Tired of applying bone meal or fertilizer by hand? A sprinkler can take water and boost your crop growth rate. Bone meal will make it work even faster.

[note]Note that the sprinkler does not work with IC2 crops.[/note]

1519.name: Portable Tanks Too Small?
1519.desc:
How about large steel fluid cells instead? Each holds 8000L, and they can stack to 64. Unfortunately, you have to load and unload them using a machine's interface - like ULV tanks for example - but hey, 512 buckets in a single slot is pretty good!

You cannot use these cells directly in the world, like BC tanks, or to dump the fluid on the ground.

1520.name: Large Aluminium Fluid Cells
1520.desc: 512 buckets in a stack not cutting it? Aluminium cells can hold 32 buckets per cell, and stack to 64. 2,048 buckets in a single stack? That's half a supertank!

1521.name: Separating Gems From the Dust
1521.desc: The sifter runs sooo slow, you should make an HV sifter to get gems faster.

1522.name: Niobium-Titanium
1522.desc: Niobium-Titanium becomes more important in LuV Tier. It needs an EV Mixer to mix and an Electric Blast Furnace with TPV heating coils.

1523.name: Can't Find Those Ores?
1523.desc:
Having trouble locating some of the basic Ores? Trade in some coins for ore blocks to use in the Ore Finder Wand.

If you're clever, you can use one ore block to find a vein containing another ore block to use to find another vein. For example, if you choose Redstone, you can use that to find Cinnabar, which can be used to find Thaumcraft Aspect Shard Ores in the Twilight Forest. Experiment and find some combos!

1524.name: Better Bolts
1524.desc:
Now that you are in MV and have an EBF, you can make new bolts with vanadiumsteel-tipped magnalium. Your enemies will tremble at your might.

Check "fletching" under NEI for fletching options.

Good hunting!

1525.name: Best Bolts?
1525.desc:
That meteoric iron you found on the moon, when sent through the EBF, turns into this amazing meteoric steel, able to keep a fine edge. Put some on an Energetic Alloy tool rod to make an awesome bolt. Your next bolt you'll have to figure out yourself.

Use whatever fletching you want.

1526.name: Best Crossbow?
1526.desc:
Energetic alloy has proven to be an amazing material. Its light weight and high stiffness makes it perfect for a crossbow limb. It is slightly slower to draw but still has amazing stopping power with its very high bolt speed. Durability is much improved over a carbon limb.

The material would also make an excellent bolt body. Perhaps you will find a suitable tip to match?

1527.name: Caging a Demon - Wither Killing Chamber
1527.desc:
Ever wanted to trap a wither but the only option was non movable bedrock? Now that you've made dark steel you can get reinforced obsidian which is wither-proof. Useful for manual killing and even automation once you get a powered spawner supplying you with wither skeleton skulls.

[note]Hint: Unlike vanilla obsidian, reinforced obsidian CAN be moved by pistons which might come in handy.[/note]

1280.name: The industrial way
1280.desc: Making rum can be automated, but you need to follow this industrial way for it.

1282.name: Sugar: The Gathering
1282.desc: Stockpiling sugar might be a good idea if you have a sweet tooth. Or not?

1283.name: Honey for sugar.
1283.desc:
Since honey contains a lot of sugar, it might be a good idea to get some honey.

[note]Just having the fluid in cells or buckets in your inventory lets the quest detect and consume it.[/note]

1284.name: Wasps honey
1284.desc:
Giant wasps can be found in the Nether. Quick! Sneak into their hive and steal some honey!

[note]Just having the fluid in cells or buckets in your inventory lets the quest detect it.[/note]

1285.name: Diamond Spikes
1285.desc:
Diamond Spikes damages mobs (or you!) that touch any side of it other than the bottom. With 7 attack damage, the Spike does as much damage as a Diamond Sword. Mobs will not walk around the block, but will try to walk over it as if it were a regular solid block. Most explosions won't destroy the Spike because it has a very high blast resistance.

These can also be enchanted with all the enchantments a regular diamond sword would receive. This includes Looting and Sharpness, which function exactly as it does on a sword. Interestingly, mobs killed by the Diamond Spike will return experience as if killed by the player.

There are weaker versions, but who wants that? If you happen to want the last hit, you might want the wooden ones though.

1286.name: Primal Shrooms
1286.desc:
Primal shrooms allow you to refill wands easier before you can move nodes to your base.

Go and find some Vishrooms in a magical forest biome. Try using a Nature's Compass. Or buy some using coins if no biome is close to your location.

1 pumpkin and 4 tiny piles of stone dust are the perfect combo to make these.

Whenever they're fully grown, you can break them to get some vis orbs to drop, just like enemies do. You do need to be holding the wand to collect it though. For these to grow, they will need a certain light level.

1288.name: Hops, the Source of Beer
1288.desc: If you don't want pure alcohol, but beer, get some hops by crossbreeding IC2 crops.

1289.name: HV Bender
1289.desc:
An HV Bender is 4x faster than your LV Bender, but it also opens up an important recipe - Tin Cans!

With this bender you can turn Tin Casings into Tin Cans. Using the Tin Cans with food in a Canner will make Canned Food. This can stack up to 64, and will fully restore your shanks - no need to rotate food types anymore! You will eat as much as you need with one click. One drawback is you will no longer get the Well Fed buff with its slight health regen bonus, nor will you gain more hearts from eating new food. Also, these cans don't fit in the lunch bag or lunch box so you don't need those anymore.

1290.name: Imma Chargin' My...
1290.desc:
Charged certus quartz is one of the basic components for a lot of AE recipes. Just mix some certus quartz and redstone dust to get charged certus quartz dust.

Use the autoclave to make crystals. Or if you have lots of sodium, you can use the chemical reactor recipe. You can also make the charger to go directly from crystal to charged crystal, but it's not very fast. The charger can connect directly to a GT cable or a fluix cable from the bottom.

[note]Note that some recipes only have a chance of producing a charged crystal, which is true of many of these.[/note]

1291.name: Keeping Your Network Happy
1291.desc: Helps with fluctuating power. Energy cells store up to 2,000,000 AE. They do not accept power directly but are used to buffer some power in an already existing ME network. Energy Cells do not use a channel.

1292.name: Storing Your ME Data
1292.desc:
A block designed to do one thing, store storage cells. This block holds 10 storage cells so you can tightly pack your storage into a very small space. ME drives show their contents and the storage cell status on the front of the drive face.

Red - Indicates the storage cell is full.
Orange - Indicates the storage cell cannot hold any more types, but it can store more items.
Green - Indicates the cell can hold more types, or more items.
Black - Indicates there is no channel, or power is offline.

It's important to note, that without an ME Network this block does nothing. It's only useful when combined with a way to input, and output items, and requires 2 AE/t power to function, and additional power for each storage cell stored inside it.

About Priority: Many devices in AE2 have a priority setting, this determines what gets items first. This is useful in some situations, but not so much in others. Higher number = higher priority.

1294.name: Speeding Up Growth
1294.desc: Used to accelerate the process of purifying crystals, which allows you to create pure certus quartz crystal, pure nether quartz crystal and pure fluix crystal. Must be powered by an ME Network via the sides with the +, and consumes a steady 8 ae/t while plugged in. Crystal Seeds must be in an adjacent water block to be affected - the seeds will shimmer more rapidly when in the presence of a powered crystal growth accelerator. These do not use a channel.

1295.name: Perfecting Crystal Growth
1295.desc: The crystal growth chamber is used for fast crystal growth, and it is possible to increase its speed by installing acceleration cards in an upgrade slot. It works more like a normal device, with the crystals growing in its internal inventory. Unlike the more primitive versions, it doesn't use power when not in use. It also doesn't use a channel.

1296.name: Purifying the Impure
1296.desc:
The purified versions of Nether, Certus and Fluix crystals can be grown from seeds made in the assembler from crystal dust and sand.

They can be grown in-world for free by dropping them in water, or at a much faster rate in a GT Autoclave.

[note]Distilled water reduces the recipe time in the Autoclave by half.[/note]

1297.name: Dragon Essence
1297.desc: Need some Dragon essence? Buy some!

1298.name: Wireless Setup
1298.desc:
Allows you to configure which users, and what permissions the users have with the ME System. By existing it enforces permissions on the usage of the system.

The security system does not prevent destructive tampering, removing cables / machines or breaking of drives. If you need to protect your system from physical vandalism you will need another form of physical security. This block provides network level security.

The player who places the ME security terminal has full control over the network and cannot exclude themselves any rights. By adding a blank biometric card you define a default behavior for every player who has no biometric card registered.

In addition to security on the software layer, you can link up your wireless terminal with the network and access it wirelessly.

If you have one on your main network, your subnets may also need one to have access to your main network, but this is not always the case depending on how you set it up.

The ME Wireless Access Point wireless access via a wireless terminal. Range and power usage is determined based on the number of wireless boosters installed into the ME wireless access point. A network can have any number of ME wireless access points with any number of wireless boosters in each one, allowing you to optimize power usage and range by altering your setup.

1299.name: Dense Boys
1299.desc: Giving your network even more stability. Dense energy cells store AE energy up to 16 million units. They do not accept power directly but are used to buffer power in an already existing ME Network.

1300.name: Wireless Applied Energistics Terminal
1300.desc:
After you link up the wireless terminal in the ME security terminal, it grants portable access to the ME network. Put it into a charger to recharge it.

Holds 1.6m AE in its battery and drains 1 AE/t for each block you are away from the nearest ME wireless access point.

Charge your Wireless Terminals (and the successors) with an EIO Wireless Charger if you don't want to wait a century for them to charge.

1301.name: Annihilation and Formation
1301.desc:
Formation Core - a component which can convert energy back into matter.
Annihilation Core - a component which can convert matter into energy.
Gates all network I/O.

1302.name: Interfacing with Machines
1302.desc:
The ME Interface is the only component which can be used as a part, or as a Block. Crafting an ME interface in either form by itself produces the other form. The thin form is useful if you want to provide several different interfaces in a single block of physical space, but each will need its own channel. The block form lets multiple other blocks connect to a single ME interface, using only one channel for the interface. The ME Interface acts as an in between when working with pipes, tubes, networks, or machines from other mods.

[note]If you're using a resource pack and the interface's GUI doesn't show up properly, you'll need to delete the assets\appliedenergistics2\textures\guis\interface.png file in your resourcepack zip file.[/note]

1303.name: Interfacing with Inventories
1303.desc:
The ME Storage Bus, when attached to another inventory block in the world lets you access that inventory via networked functions. This allows you to use chests, barrels, or other types of item storage in your networks.

The storage via the ME Storage Bus is bidirectional, it can both insert, or extract items from the inventory block it is attached to as long as the ME storage bus has its required channel.

The UI allows you to control which items are selected as storable items, this selection has no effect on what items can be extracted once they are in the storage.

The storage bus will function with nearly any inventory block, including ME Interfaces, EFR barrels, JABBA Barrels, Storage Drawers, etc. They can also be used to route items passively into Buildcraft pipes.

If you place a storage bus on an ME Interface the storage bus will be able to interact with the full contents of the target network, unless that interface is configured to store items inside itself, in which case it will see those stored items.

1305.name: For Fluid Recipes
1305.desc: Placing your fluid crafting patterns in a Dual Interface. That will allow AE2 to push the fluids and items both. Note. Fluids are being sent first, then items.

1306.name: Extracting Items From the Network
1306.desc: The ME export bus extracts items from the ME network's networked storage and places them into the inventory it faces. You must configure which items it will insert, leaving the configuration blank will result in nothing. The ME export bus will try to export any of the items on its list skipping over those it cannot fit into the destination. This is the functional opposite of the ME import bus.

1307.name: Dragon Essence II
1307.desc: Need some Dragon essence? Buy some!

1308.name: Inserting Items Into the Network
1308.desc:
Pulls items from the inventory it is pointed at and places them into the ME network's networked storage. You can specify which items it will pull out via the UI, else it tries to pull out any item in the adjacent inventory. The ME import bus will attempt to import any possible options, even if 1 or more of the configured items cannot be stored. This is the functional opposite of the ME export bus.

When your machines are very fast, it's better to have them output directly into an ME interface instead, since that works instantly.

1310.name: Something to Put Your Components in
1310.desc:
An empty storage container, you can insert the vanilla AE2 storage cell parts into it to create a usable storage cell.

You can also craft it with the storage components when making them to skip combining them.

1311.name: AE Tier 1 Item Storage
1311.desc:
The main processor for 1K and 4K cells.

Storing items directly in AE cells is very performant and usually the best option.

1312.name: Automating Crafting
1312.desc: Is a component of the crafting CPU multiblock. This particular block provides the CPU with no additional features, but can be used as a "filler" block and as a crafting ingredient for the crafting storage, monitor and co-processing units.

1313.name: 1k Item Storage Cell
1313.desc:
Lowest tier storage cell, which can contain 1,024 bytes of storage. 1,024 bytes of storage can hold 127 stacks of a single item, or 65 stacks, while holding 63 different items. The 1k storage cell uses 8 bytes of data to store a single type.

When placed inside a drive or chest will consume 0.5 AE/t. It must be in an ME drive or ME chest to be usable.

You can remove the 1k ME storage component by fully emptying the storage cell, and sneak-clicking it in your hand.

You can format it in the cell workbench.

1314.name: 1k Component
1314.desc: Smallest storage component, used to make 1k ME storage cells, or can be upgraded to make 4k ME storage components. Can be recovered from a crafted 1k ME storage cell by fully emptying the storage cell, and shift-clicking it in your hand.

1315.name: 1k Crafting Storage
1315.desc: Component of the crafting CPU which provides 1024 bytes of storage for crafting. The Crafting Storage blocks are the only necessary part. Each crafting CPU multiblock allows for a separate crafting job (this is different from sub-tasks of the same job). The more items in the recipe and sub-recipes, the more crafting storage you'll need. Having one big and a couple small might be a good idea, but remember it will try to use the best one available first when you order a craft. They are also used when a crafting card requests a craft, so you'll need several separate ones if you intend to use those a lot. A crafting CPU multiblock uses only one channel, regardless of its size. It must be square to form.

1316.name: 4k Item Storage Cell
1316.desc:
Second tier storage cell, which can contain 4,096 bytes of storage. 4,096 bytes of storage can hold 508 stacks of a single item, or 260 stacks, while holding 63 different items. The 4k storage cell uses 32 bytes of data to store a single type.

When placed inside a drive or chest will consume 1.0 ae/t.

1317.name: 4k Component
1317.desc: Second smallest storage component, used to make 4k ME storage cells, or can be upgraded to make 16k ME storage components. Can be recovered from a crafted 4k ME storage cell by fully emptying the storage cell, and shift-clicking it in your hand.

1318.name: 4k Crafting Storage
1318.desc: Component of the crafting CPU which provides 4,096 bytes of storage for crafting.

1319.name: AE Tier 2 Item Storage
1319.desc: The main processor for 16k and 64k cells.

1320.name: Advanced Compressor
1320.desc: The MV compressor is twice as fast as the LV one but uses 4x the power.

1321.name: Advanced Macerator
1321.desc: The MV macerator is twice as fast as the LV one but uses 4x the power.

1322.name: Advanced Centrifuge
1322.desc: The MV centrifuge is twice as fast as the LV one but uses 4x the power.

1323.name: Biomass
1323.desc:
Chose the IC2 or the gregtech way to make biomass. Water and Bio Chaff is needed.

You can also use the pyrolyze oven to make biomass in a more efficient way if you make it.

Note that forestry has its own biomass as well. It can also be made into fermented biomass like this kind. Choose which kind you want to make later on yourself.

1324.name: Advanced Fluid Extractor
1324.desc:
The MV fluid extractor is twice as fast as the LV one but uses 4x the power.

To make bio diesel you need seed oil or fish oil. Seeds give seed oil and fish produce fish oil when placed in a fluid extractor.

1325.name: Dagger of Sacrifice
1325.desc: Time to get more blood. Mobs are a good source for more blood. Transform your knife of sacrifice in your tier 2 altar into a dagger of sacrifice.

1326.name: Co-Processing
1326.desc:
Component of the crafting CPU which provides additional item delivery from the CPU to the ME Interface for crafting.

This can be used to make more assemblers active in parallel for the job, and thus increase overall crafting speed. These only help if your setup has steps properly separated, so the system can run multiple tasks in parallel, or even split the same pattern across multiple interfaces.

1327.name: Recipe Patterns
1327.desc: Save patterns onto these to do autocrafting. They go into molecular assemblers and ME interfaces.

1328.name: Pattern Terminal
1328.desc:
A specialized version of the ME crafting terminal designed to encode blank patterns into encoded patterns.

Lets you browse the contents of your network like other terminals, but also contains an area for designing patterns. There are two modes for pattern encoding. Crafting patterns, and processing patterns. Processing patterns are designed for use with machines that do not use standard crafting recipes - such as furnaces, or other machines. To select between modes, click the button to the right of the interface: when it shows a standard crafting table, it will create crafting patterns, and when it shows a furnace, it will create processing patterns.

For crafting patterns ("Crafts..."), you specify the input crafting materials on a standard 3x3 crafting grid, and the output materials are determined automatically.

For processing patterns ("Creates..."), you specify the input materials and output materials, including quantity, by placing stacks of items in the interface. If a processing operation is not guaranteed to succeed (such as secondary products from some machines), it will not work correctly as a processing pattern.

When designing crafting patterns you can click the output to extract a crafted item as long as you have the materials required to craft the item.

[warn]Be cautious about accidentally enabling oredict substitution, it's rarely used and you will keep it disabled 99% of the time.[/warn]

1329.name: ME in Your Cleanroom
1329.desc:
Wireless Connectors are a close range Quantum Network Bridge alternative.

Connections are point-to-point - you can't connect more than 2 blocks together. Each block will use 10 + distance * ln(distance^2 + 3) AE/t. Does not work across dimensions. Power needs to be provided on only one side. Up to 32 channels can be transferred through the connection. Can connect directly to dense cables. The Wireless Connector itself does not use any channels.

[note]Also, these are more TPS friendly than large chains of dense cables![/note]

1330.name: Ore Dictionary Output
1330.desc:
The ME ore dictionary export card will attempt to push the configured item/fluid [From the same ore dictionary] from the ME network into a machine or other input inventory. To configure an item/liquid, type in the item's name [e.g. if you want to export all ores put "ore*"]. The rate of export is very fast.

[note]PS: Use crushed* & !crushedPurified* & !crushedCentrifuged* to get any crushed ore, but not ore that has been washed, then crushed (to get purified) or run through the thermal centrifuge.[/note]

The card can be used in more devices, and causes less lag, but works in a similar way. Put it inside a bus and set the filter using the T looking button that appears. The standalone bus is no longer craftable.

1331.name: To Link P2P Buses
1331.desc:
A small item that can be used to store, copy, and paste settings. Shift-right-click on a configurable object to save the settings onto the memory card, then right-click on any other block of the same type to paste the settings. Shift-right-click on a non-configurable object (or the air) to clear. They are also used to link the input P2P tunnel to the corresponding output P2P tunnels. They can also link Vis Relays to compatible devices.

If you're doing more advanced operations in a large network of P2Ps, it's recommended you use the advanced memory card. It has a helpful UI that allows you to visualize and set names, inputs, and outputs of P2P tunnels. 

[note]Remember P2Ps can only have 1 input! The only exception being fluid P2Ps whose input can function as an output and whose outputs can function as an input.[/note]

1332.name: Compressed Solar LV
1332.desc: The power of the sun x10. This solar panel works like 10 regular ones, but costs only 8. How is this possible? Magic! NOTE: They give out GT EU. NO TRANSFORMER NEEDED!

1333.name: Essentia Generator: Potentia
1333.desc:
Aren't you tired of voiding all that useless potentia essentia? Well here's the solution:
A generator that consumes potentia and outputs GT EU NO TRANSFORMER NEEDED!

1334.name: Essentia Generator: Auram
1334.desc: This Generator is quite tricky. It requires Auram Essentia, that isn't that common, but its output is nice.

1335.name: Infused Solars
1335.desc: Compressed Solar Panels can be infused with Aspects for certain additional features. Check them out and choose one of them.

1336.name: Double Compressed Solar
1336.desc: 100EU/t for 64x1EU/t Solars? Sounds like a good deal. Get one of these. They can be Infused as well.

1337.name: Even More Infused
1337.desc: You can infuse double or triple compressed solars as well.

1338.name: Triple Compressed Solar
1338.desc: Triple Compressed Solars. Yes, that's right. They are a bit more expensive than their lower counterparts, 512 Solars in total. But they give out 1,000 GT EU per tick and have no need to be cleaned. How so? Magic.

1339.name: Essentia Generator: Ignis
1339.desc: So, you got rid of the Potentia, maybe you don't want Ignis, but Potentia? Get this one to be able to burn Ignis.

1340.name: Essentia Generator: Arbor
1340.desc: When using magical plants to get Praecantatio Essentia, you'll end up with a couple dozen of Arbor Essentia jars. But no worries! This generator runs on Arbor! Awesome!

1341.name: Essentia Generator: Aer
1341.desc: Sugarcanes to power...? This generator runs on Aer Essentia, it might not be as powerful as its counterparts, but its fuel is cheap.

1342.name: Essentia Generator: Lucrum
1342.desc: This highly-warped, high-power Essentia generator is a lifesaver, when you run out of energy. Just toss some gold into your Arcane Furnace and burn the Lucrum you get.

1343.name: Works like in anime!
1343.desc: You want to fly. Badly. So go and ductape wings!

1344.name: Angel Wings
1344.desc: Since you have access to better materials than cardboard now, you wanted to make wings. Angel wings.

1345.name: Secret-Angel-Assasin
1345.desc: Infusing your Wings with nano technology seems to be the next step, to further improve them and combine magic and technology.

1346.name: Dropped in Asgard, Fallen to Earth
1346.desc: You've heard rumors of a mighty weapon, dropped in Asgard and fallen to earth. Could this be the mystical Item?

1347.name: Repaired it!
1347.desc: Sadly enough, the hammer was broken. But you have the skills and the knowledge to repair it, don't you?

1348.name: Super-Mjolnir
1348.desc: Additional tweaks to your Mjolnir have changed it. Now you can summon some Guardian Snowmen to help you out!

1349.name: Electrogoggles
1349.desc: As you wander with your Goggles of Revealing you noticed that they tend to break easy. So craft yourself some electric ones that just need to be charged again!

1350.name: Nano Goggles
1350.desc: The #1 product for spies and wanna-be-spies. Nano-Goggles. Don't you want one of these too? But they are sold out? Craft some!

1351.name: Quantum Goggles
1351.desc: As you continue to progress, you notice that your black goggles don't match your nice white and shiny armor. You decide to get a pair of white ones.

1352.name: Goggles > 9000
1352.desc: This special pair of goggles recharges itself while you wander in sunlight. How convenient.

1353.name: Sparking Nitor...?
