1353.desc: YELLOW! What an ugly color! I want PURPLE Nitor! Get me some and you'll receive something nice in exchange.

1354.name: Rechargeable Scribing Tools
1354.desc: Are you tired of getting all the Ink for your scribing and you don't want to use your blood either? Get this Hi-Tech Scribing Device which will make your life much easier! Treat it like an HV battery - charge in any HV machine's battery slot, or in an HV battery buffer.

1355.name: Angel Wings - Ultra Force!
1355.desc: From ore to ingot, from nano to quantum, as you progress, your technology level increases, so should your wings as well.

1356.name: Angel Wings - Combine!
1356.desc:
The Angel Wings aren't very protective on their own. Crafting the Infused Quantum Chestplate allows you to combine with the Angel Wings to have a functional and protective combination!

[note]This is still not nearly as good as the other option, the GraviChestPlate.[/note]

1357.name: Electric Hoe-ing
1357.desc: While the Hoe of Growth breaks easily, we have a fix for you! Get this one and simply recharge it when it's empty.

1358.name: EU->VIS
1358.desc: This device allows you to charge your wands using EU, but it costs 10kEU per Vis restored.

1359.name: Press and Hold the B Button to Block!
1359.desc:
The Wand Focus: Shield creates a shield around you which blocks any incoming damage and removes potion effects. Sneak away if you're fighting an enemy too strong for you!

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1360.name: Reactor Shielding 2.0
1360.desc: Shield Blocks - explosion proof - single use. Get a couple of these to shield your reactor!

1361.name: What the Heck...?
1361.desc: You found a yellow-ish glowing ball. It might be useful later on.

1362.name: The One Ring
1362.desc:
So you found it. The One Ring, forged by a mighty Dark Wizard. A strange feeling tells you to put it on. The Ring seems to whisper to you as well...

[warn]Yeah, maybe don't do that. Unless you like tons of warp and withers spawning on your ass, blowing up your base.[/warn]

The ring may make you invisible and invincible, but not only does it collect warp itself, it piles it on you as well. After an hour the amount SIGNIFICANTLY increases so try not to wear it that long unless you have a pure tear handy. You can also expect other effects, and the ring to not want to come off either.

1366.name: Ocean Bees
1366.desc: Want some Ocean Bees? Since some servers don't generate ocean biomes, buy some!

1367.name: Upgrade Your Forestry Backpack
1367.desc:
With your woven silk you can upgrade your Forestry backpacks. Each Pack has 45 slots.

You need a carpenter, your old backpack and a bit of seed oil.

If the recipe isn't working, put the bag in a crafting grid to reset its NBT data. After removing anything in it of course.

1368.name: Stuck in the Twilight Forest?
1368.desc:
If you're in the Twilight Forest and your portal gets broken, you're kinda stuck. Every 24h you're able to exchange some materials to get a new portal crystal to escape.

Also consider making an extra yourself and sticking it in a chest on the TF side, just in case.

1369.name: Where'd I Put That Amber?
1369.desc: Every Thaumcraft Infused Stone vein has a bit of amber ore in it. I'm sure you have a bunch by now.

1370.name: Light It Up!
1370.desc:
The arcane lamp creates additional light sources which acts like a Torch in a sphere with a radius of 16 blocks centered on it. This will include caves or other areas behind walls. That might be useful...

When attached to the base of an arcane bore, the arcane lamp gains an additional ability: It will light up the tunnel that the bore digs.

1371.name: LOX and Bagels
1371.desc:
The LOX canister is an alternate solution to refilling your oxygen tanks.

To make liquid oxygen you need to put an oxygen cell into a vacuum freezer to get a cell of liquid oxygen. Transfer this liquid oxygen into a tank, and then into a LOX canister.

To refill your oxygen tanks just put the tank and the LOX canister in the oxygen compressor to transfer oxygen, or use an oxygen collector.

Each LOX canister can fill 4 Heavy Oxygen Tanks, 6 Medium Oxygen Tanks, or 12 Light Oxygen Tanks.

So much Greggy!

1372.name: I Don't Want to Set the World on Fire
1372.desc:
Wand Focus: Fire is a basic wand focus that is able to spew a short-range cone of fire that deals damage and sets mobs ablaze.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Alchemist's Fire (even levels): Hit mobs burn for longer and lights blocks on fire
Fireball (level 3): The focus now shoots a large, explosive ball of fire which sets the ground on fire if paired with Alchemist's Fire
Fire Beam (level 3): Concentrates the cone of fire into a beam that does more damage and shoots farther

1373.name: Diggy Diggy Hole
1373.desc:
The Wand Focus: Excavation emits a green beam of light that will mine blocks of any hardness (except for unbreakable blocks like Bedrock) from a distance.

This focus is also required to operate the Arcane Bore. Upgrading foci to be used in the Bore with Enlarge is highly recommended, as it increases the radius by 1 per level.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases block breaking speed every level
Treasure: Acts like vanilla Fortune, but does not apply to GT ores
Enlarge (even levels): Breaks an extra adjacent block per level
Dowsing (level 3): May drop Thaumcraft Native Ores from ores, but does not apply to GT ores
Silk Touch (level 5): Acts like vanilla Silk Touch

1374.name: Bzzap!
1374.desc:
The Wand Focus: Shock will shoot a bolt of lightning. It homes in slightly to the nearest mob/player from where it is aimed. It is even fast enough to hit Endermen before they can teleport away!

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Chain Lightning (level 3): The lightning bolt now arcs to two nearby creatures. Great for dealing with bats from warp!
Earth Shock (level 3): The focus now shoots a ball of lightning that leaves the ground electrified
Enlarge (requires Chain Lightning): The lightning bolt arcs to two additional targets per level. Show those bats who's boss!

1375.name: Trade Offer
1375.desc:
Wand Focus: Equal Trade is able to switch out blocks in the world with blocks in the player's inventory. Shift-right-click on a block to attune the focus to the block. Once attuned to a block, left-click on a single block to replace it with the substitute block. Right-click will transform a contiguous 5x5x5 area of the same block that has air on at least one side. This can go through thin walls or into caves, so be careful. Very useful for upgrading EBF coils or for swapping other blocks that are slow to break like Obsidian! It cannot be used to break or place blocks with Tile Entities like chests.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Enlarge: Expands the maximum range by 1 block in each direction per level
Treasure (level 3): Works like vanilla Fortune
Architect (level 3): Allows the focus's working area to be modified with the Misc Wand Toggle keybind. Sneaking with the keybind changes which axes are modified. This upgrade also changes the working area to a plane, so be sure to test this upgrade out before committing to it and applying further upgrades
Silk Touch (level 5): Works like vanilla Silk Touch

1376.name: Snowball Fight!
1376.desc:
The Wand Focus: Frost will launch a ball of ice that can bounce off of blocks and mobs.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Alchemist's Frost (odd levels): Applies Slowness to hit mobs. Higher levels increase the potency of the effect
Scattershot (level 3): The focus now shoots a volley of shards that break on impact. The normal damage immunity timer is ignored, so multiple shards can hit the same mob at once and all will deal damage. Potency increases the number of shards instead of the damage
Ice Boulder (level 3): The focus now shoots a larger, bouncier, and deadlier ball of ice

1377.name: Magical Base Defense
1377.desc:
The Wand Focus: Warding can be used to make blocks invincible with right-click. Warding may be removed by right-clicking it again. It is not able to ward transparent blocks like Glass or non-full blocks like slabs, but there is a block known as Warded Glass available with the Warded Arcana research if you want a protected transparent block. It is also unable to ward blocks with Tile Entities like chests. Take note that only the player who warded the block can unward it.

Holding a wand with this focus will show an outline on nearby blocks you have warded. They look normal otherwise. Warded Blocks are [note]completely indestructible[/note] and will produce particle effects similar to runic shielding when a player tries to break them. Blocks will lose any special properties when warded: sand or gravel will not fall, grass will not grow, and packed ice will no longer be slippery. Warded blocks cannot be affected by any outside influence such as redstone or pistons. The only way to break the warded block is via the owner right-clicking with a wand with this focus.

[note]Special Note: Warded blocks emit light. They can also cause quite a bit of lag if used at scale.[/note]

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
§
Architect (level 2): Allows the focus's working area to be modified with the Misc Wand Toggle keybind. Sneaking with the keybind changes which axes are modified. This upgrade increases the maximum area to 7x7x7
Enlarge (Requires Architect): Expands the maximum range by 1 block in each direction per level

1378.name: Keep it Away from Bags of Holding
1378.desc:
The Wand Focus: Portable Hole will create a temporary 3x3x32 hole centered on the targeted block. Some blocks cannot be passed through, such as Bedrock or blocks warded by the Wand Focus: Warding.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Enlarge: Extends the hole by 8 blocks per level
Extend: The hole will stay open 3 seconds longer for every level

1379.name: Cart With Chunkloader
1379.desc:
When transporting oil from long distances, a train is going to need a chunk loader. Craft one, and I'll give you a second one for free.

[note]This quest might not be completable in MP?[/note]

1380.name: Plague Inc.
1380.desc:
The Wand Focus: Pech's Curse is a rare wand focus which cannot be crafted by players. It can only be obtained from a Pech Mage -- either by bartering valuable items or by killing it for its wand to drop. Pech Mages are rare and the drop chance is quite low, so bartering is recommended. [warn]If attacked, the Pech will use this focus against you![/warn]

This focus fires a small ball of energy with a bright bluish-green trail. This missile is affected by gravity, so the shots need to be "arced" to hit distant targets. When the shot hits a mob, it does one heart of damage and randomly inflicts one of several effects on the target: Poison, Weakness, or Slowness.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases potency of the applied effect. Being able to apply Weakness III to mobs might be useful for Blood Magic...
Extend (even levels): Extends the duration of the applied effect
Nightshade (level 5): Applies all three effects at once

1381.name: It's Mesmerizing...
1381.desc:
The Wand Focus: Primal will hurl an orb of primal energy with right-click. The orb explodes on impact, and it has a small chance to cause other effects. It sometimes creates taint when it lands, and it very rarely creates a node if it destroys the block it impacts. Its base vis cost constantly fluctuates. [note]Maybe studying it and its projectiles will give you a better understanding of vis.[/note]

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Seeker (level 3): The orb homes in on the closest target, but loses some of its special effects like creating taint or nodes

1382.name: Pimp Your Wand Focus
1382.desc:
The Focal Manipulator requires both XP levels and centi-vis in order to upgrade foci. Each focus can be upgraded a total of five times, with the level cost increasing by 8 and the vis cost doubling for each upgrade.

Most foci accept Frugal (lowers the vis cost to cast by 10% per level) and Potency (increases damage dealt or improves the focus in another way if it doesn't deal damage) at every level. Foci with an area of effect (like Wand Focus: Equal Trade) accept Enlarge, which usually expands the max area by one block in each direction. Some foci have special upgrades that can only be applied at certain levels (often level 3 or 5), some of which have prerequisite upgrades. See the Focus on Wand Foci & EMT tab for more information about focal upgrades and their effects.

If you regret your past decisions, you can remove upgrades in the Focal Manipulator if you have the Focal Disenchanting research. It will even refund some of the XP!

1383.name: Advanced Node Stabilizer
1383.desc: The advanced node stabilizer acts like a normal node stabilizer but with key differences. It will still stop the node it affects from being drained but the node can still drain from other, lesser nodes. It also has a higher chance to improve fading and unstable nodes. The downside is that any nodes it affects will have their recharge rates effectively reduced to nothing. They will recharge at an extremely reduced rate.

1532.name: Tier 4 Heavy Duty Plates
1532.desc: Rockets require heavy duty plates for the main hull. Let's craft 13 tier 4 heavy duty plates out of tier 3 plates and compressed ice plates.

1533.name: Callisto Trip
1533.desc:
Callisto Ice is a material you need for Tier 4 Rockets. Look for it at Y 40-60. You can also find platinum series and radioactive ores here.

[note]You can also find Mytryl and Ledox here as small ores.[/note]

1534.name: Europa Trip
1534.desc:
Ledox is a material you need for Tier 4 Rockets which can be found on Europa. Look for it at Y 55-65. You can also find Chrome, Molybdenum, Manganese, Tungstate and Rubracium, among others.

[note]You can also get Ledox from small ores on Callisto.[/note]

1535.name: Rocket Tier 4
1535.desc:
After weeks of hard work and a thousand stacks of iTNT your fourth rocket is finally ready.

You need to use Dense Hydrazine Rocket Fuel.

[note]In case you forgot to complete any, holding the rocket in your hand will unlock all pre-requisite quests in addition to this one.[/note]

1384.name: Pocket Furnace
1384.desc:
Wand Focus: Efreet's Flame shoots a beam similar to the Wand Focus: Excavation that smelts blocks like a Furnace.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1385.name: Pocket Dolly
1385.desc:
Wand Focus: Dislocation is a very useful focus that allows its user to take and place nearly any block, even Tile Entities and one that usually can't be moved, like Mob Spawners, planted IC2 Crops, and more.

[warn]Please use responsibly, and don't move things you shouldn't, like door halves, or some multiblock parts, unless you like the idea of world corruption destroying your world. That said, if you're in SP and make a backup, do whatever you want, just know the risks.[/warn]

It uses:
5 Ordo + 5 Perditio + 1 Terra to take any mundane block.
25 Ordo + 25 Perditio + 5 Terra to take any block with a tile entity (such as a Chest).
100 Ordo + 100 Perditio + 20 Terra to take a Mob Spawner.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1386.name: Medic!
1386.desc:
The Wand Focus: Mending is a wand focus that will slowly mend the caster's wounds by expending Vis. It heals half a heart at a time. It's an invaluable tool in a Blood Mage's arsenal.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases healing by half a heart per level.

1387.name: Fly High!
1387.desc:
The Wand Focus: Uprising propels its caster forward. It works well to gain height to glide for long distances or to scale tall obstacles. Fall damage is only reset when cast, so be careful when using it!

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1388.name: Projectile Protection V
1388.desc:
The Wand Focus: Distortion protects the caster from "ordinary" projectiles such as arrows, snowballs, and potions while right-click is held.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Enlarge (even levels): Expands the projectile deflection range by 1 block in each direction per level

1389.name: Ender Backpack
1389.desc:
The Wand Focus: Ender Rift opens your vanilla Personal Ender Chest when used. It can also be attuned to an Ender Chest from Ender Storage with shift-right-click. Shift-right-click a block of obsidian to set it back to your Personal Ender Chest.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1390.name: It Can't Bend Spoons
1390.desc:
The Wand Focus: Telekinesis is a wand focus that can move nearby dropped items. It either moves them where you are pointing with the wand or toward you while sneaking.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1391.name: Knowledge is Power
1391.desc:
The Wand Focus: Experience Drain will drain the player's experience at a rate of 15 XP per tick to restore vis at a rather fast rate.

[note]This focus cannot be upgraded in the Focal Manipulator[/note]

1392.name: Wand Focus Shadow Beam
1392.desc:
The Wand Focus: Shadowbeam shoots a beam of dark energy that is reflected by any block and damages everything it passes through. The beam travels about 40 blocks and does about 100 damage.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level

1393.name: Weaponized Blight
1393.desc:
The Wand Focus: Tainted Storm will emit a stream of tainted bubbles which damage anything in a short distance and inflict Poison for 5 seconds. The focus may also debuff the caster with Flux Flu III for 50 seconds.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt and increases the number of bubbles every level
Tainturgy (level 3): The caster will no longer get Flux Flu. This adds 0.6 Ordo to the casting cost
Enlarge (level 4): The tainted bubbles fan out across a wider area. This adds 0.6 Aer and Aqua to the casting cost
Corrosive (level 5): The tainted bubbles inflict the Wither effect instead of Poison. This adds 0.6 Perditio and Ignis to the casting cost

1394.name: Tainted Nuclear Detonation
1394.desc:
The Wand Focus: Tainted Shockwave will release a powerful shockwave around the caster which travels 10 blocks away, damaging all mobs in the radius and launching them into the air. After using this focus, the caster will be unable to use any other foci for 6 seconds.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Enlarge: Increases the radius by 2 blocks per level. This also adds 2.5 Perditio, Terra, and Aqua to the casting cost per level

1395.name: Heat-Seeking Vis-sile
1395.desc:
The Wand Focus: Vis Shard can be used to cast a homing projectile which deals 35 damage. This projectile can bounce off of solid blocks. If the target is not hit in 10 seconds, the projectile disappears.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Persistent (level 5): The shard will seek another target if its current one dies or gets out of range

1396.name: A Taste of Their Own Medicine
1396.desc:
The Wand Focus: Dark Matter allows its wielder to shoot the same projectiles as Eldritch Guardians. There is a chance for the caster to gain sticky warp for each projectile cast.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Corrosive (level 3): The orbs now inflict the Wither effect upon their targets
Sanity (level 5): The caster will not gain warp from using this focus

1397.name: So Take Me Back in Time...
1397.desc:
The Wand Focus: Time can be used to move time forward by 12 hours. Using this focus at noon will set the time to midnight, while using it at dusk will make it dawn. This focus will also make the player unable to use any other foci for 30 seconds.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1398.name: Can You Feel the Sunshine?
1398.desc:
The Wand Focus: Meteorology can be used to start or stop rain at will. The perfect thing for Water Infused Solar Panels!

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1399.name: A Battlemage's Best Friend
1399.desc:
The Wand Focus: Mage's Mace can be equipped by any wand and will boost its attack damage to 30 hearts. Equipping it to a staff will deal double damage.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrades in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level
Potency: Increases damage dealt every level
Bloodlust (level 5): Slain mobs may drop Crystal Blood like when killed with a Crystal Dagger

1400.name: Walking is Too Hard...
1400.desc:
The Wand Focus: Blink is a very powerful focus that allows its user to teleport anywhere in sight, as long as the destination is less than 50 blocks away. There is no teleport cooldown, so it can be used to travel very quickly in the right conditions.

[note]This focus accepts the following upgrade in the Focal Manipulator:[/note]
Frugal: Lowers vis cost to cast by 10% per level

1401.name: Infuse Your Focus
1401.desc: Try to add Potency to your Shock Focus. At Level 3 you can add Chain Lightning or Shock Wave.

1402.name: Oriharukon Screws
1402.desc: Enderium screws are nice, but they seem to break if too much vis channels through them. Get some Oriharukon screws.

1403.name: HV Steam Turbine
1403.desc:
Using Steam as your single power source in HV is pretty difficult. Your fuel automation will have to be very fast, your pipes will have to be very big and the final efficiency of HV Steam Turbines is 66%, much lower than the other generators' 85%.

This is best used with an efficient furnace, like the GT++ Advanced Furnaces, to power something specific like a farm. Powering your entire automation in your path to an EV, using only these, is a nightmare. If you want more efficiency out of your Steam, consider building a Large Steam Turbine.

1404.name: MV Superconductors (128 EU/t)
1404.desc:
How about lossless 128 EU Wires? Pentacadmiummagnesiumhexaoxide is a superconductor wire and will transfer your energy without any loss. Every Voltage going forward has a superconductor wire with zero loss.

Mix Cadmium, Magnesium and Oxygen in an MV mixer. Next, the base dust needs an EBF with Kanthal Coils to be smelted. A vacuum freezer is also required to cool it down. Make some MV Superconductor Base Wires and use an Assembler to combine these wires with Helium, Stainless Steel pipes and MV pumps to get your 128 EU/t superconductor wires.

[note]Whether these are worth it to save a little power or not is up to you of course. Also note that although they're wires, you aren't hurt by coming into contact with them.[/note]

1405.name: HV Superconductors (512 EU/t)
1405.desc:
How about lossless 512 EU Wires? Titaniumonabariumdecacoppereikosaoxide is a superconductor wire and will transfer your energy without any loss.

You'll need Nichrome Coils and an HV Assembler.

[note]Whether these are worth it to save a little power or not is up to you of course.[/note]

1406.name: EV Assembler
1406.desc: An EV assembler is required to make lapotron crystals, coils, and other stuff.

1407.name: EV Laser Engraver
1407.desc: An EV precision laser is required to make quantum crystals.

1408.name: EV Superconductors (2048 EU/t)
1408.desc:
How about lossless 2048 EU Wires? Uraniumtriplatinide is a superconductor wire and will transfer your energy without any loss.

You'll need TPV Coils.

[note]Whether these are worth it to save a little power or not is up to you of course.[/note]

1409.name: IV Superconductors (8192 EU/t)
1409.desc:
How about lossless 8192 EU Wires? Vanadiumtriindinide is a superconductor wire and will transfer your energy without any loss.

From now on, SCs are not optional, you need to make them to craft stuff.

1410.name: IV Mixer
1410.desc:
An IV mixer is required for example to mix HSS-S dust or to make IV superconductors.

You can also check out the Mass Processing tab and make a multiblock mixer out of this one.

1411.name: Some Rare High Tier Metals
1411.desc:
Platinum, Palladium, Iridium and Osmium can be extracted with a chemical reaction from Chalcopyrite and Pentlandite using sulfuric acid. You don't have the technology to process them properly right now but later on you need large amounts of them.

Note that this is not the proper method to get rare metals. This means none of them are pure, and cannot be utilized without the platline method, aside from EBF-ing platinum. Proper Platinum processing is EV, but you'll have to wait until IV to use the rest. Collecting a lot of it now (especially using this method) might not be a great idea depending on how long that will be.

[note]A Chemical Bath also extracts Platinum from Nickel Ore if you use Mercury. Time to head back to the Twilight Forest![/note]

1412.name: Assembly Line
1412.desc:
The Assembly Line (or 'Assline') is an important machine for high tier components in LuV tier and above for making Motors, Pistons, Robot Arms, Pumps and many more.

The multiblock can be 5-16 blocks long. The minimum length required depends on the number of components and fluids required by the recipe it's crafting. A length of 11 blocks will fit 80 percent of all recipes in GTNH.

The bottom layer must have on one outer side all the Input Hatches for fluids (4), in the bottom middle layer Input Buses for items (10), and on the end 1 Output Bus. Also on the bottom outer layer must be one Maintenance Hatch. The rest is Solid Steel Machine Casings.

The second layer on the outer sides left and right are Reinforced Glass and in the middle center Assembly Line Casing.

The third layer has on the left and right Grate Machine Casings and in the middle Assembler Machine Casings (Note the name change!). The Assembly Line control block and Data Access Hatch are on the opposite edge from the Input Hatches - not at the end with the Output Bus.

The fourth layer is a single line of Solid Steel Machine Casings in the middle (11), with two Energy Hatches for power. Once you have it built, you can replace the two IV energy hatches with 1 LuV energy hatch.

Automating the assline is a difficult challenge! You can use advanced tools like SFM or OpenComputers, but maybe you can figure out how to do it with EnderIO or just AE2.

For more details on using the Assembly Line, check out the wiki page [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Assembly_Line[/url]

[note]BTW the input buses are ULV to help with your automating, if you don't want to automate it, you can use higher tier ones, unlike the CAL.[/note]

[note]You can have up to 16 input buses. If you use the full 16 you should put the output bus to the side.[/note]

[note]When using buses of a higher tier than ULV it will look for items starting at the top left slot, down to the bottom right slot.[/note]

1413.name: You're Gonna Hate This 1-4 in IV
1413.desc: Pumps, pistons, conveyor modules and robot arms are the most needed machine parts in IV. Go and craft one of each.

1414.name: You're Gonna Hate This 5 and 6 in IV
1414.desc: Some machines need emitters and sensors, like the analyzer or the circuit assembler.

1415.name: Pure Uranium Ores
1415.desc: If you want pure uranium ores for your nuke, you'll need to visit Deimos or Phobos to fetch them. They're at Y 20-30.

1416.name: Mallard Rust Smelly
1416.desc:
Or is it Alloy Blast Smelter? Well, that makes more sense. This multiblock creates the higher tier alloys needed for more advanced multiblocks and your soldering needs. It directly smelts ingredients into liquids. You can even melt down metals like Tungsten faster too! Until you get better coils for your EBF at least.

[note]Most of the multiblocks you can now make are optional, but quite powerful upgrades to your current singleblock setups. Not only do they provide more speed, they also have a bit of advanced functionality: you can also electrolyze materials without using cells or use a single multiblock for many different extruder shapes. They also act as cleanrooms where needed.[/note]

1417.name: Cough Cough - Something Needs to Be Done About the Smell
1417.desc:
Birds falling out of the sky, fish dead and floating belly up in the rivers, acid rain melting the ground...Time to start picking up the trash and cleaning up the air. Small black steel turbines should be fine for now. You'll need two amps of steady power to keep the scrubber working.

The detector's setting can be changed by clicking on the +/- texture with a screwdriver. Also, in general, better (and larger) turbines work better at cleaning up pollution. The code is too complex to fully explain here, but that's basically it. More EU turbine = more pollution cleaning turbine.

[warn]This quest covers one method of combating pollution - there are others, including not making as much to begin with.[/warn]

1418.name: EU packets flowing everywhere...
1418.desc: ...in my dreams. What if instead of having battery packs on every generator, we could centralize all the generation in one location? Put it away from the well traveled parts of the base? Out in the country somewhere? Let the animals deal with the pollution. Anyways, let's start working on a Power Sub-Station to store EU and deliver it to our base.

1419.name: Who Cares About a Little Cancer...
1419.desc:
...when it means you don't have to strip naked to recharge your nanosuit?
The Wireless Charger will keep your armor and tools charged up without having to put them into a battery buffer.

It will charge armor and tools at the tier and below.

You didn't want kids anyways...

1420.name: Shake That Booty...
1420.desc:
...out of the purified ores. Anyways, a Large Sifter will increase your sifting speed significantly. What will you do with all these gems??

[note]You need one more casing if you don't want to use 2 energy hatches.[/note]

1421.name: Hulk Smash!
1421.desc: Mass destruction! Or at least, lots and lots of crushed ores. Build a maceration stack to keep up with all the ores you are getting. In IV you can upgrade the Multi to be much better with an upgrade chip and better casings.

1422.name: The rocks in the washer...
1422.desc: ...go round and round, all the live long day. A large ore washing plant gets your rocks nice and clean. You can switch between Ore Washer and Chemical Bath modes with a screwdriver.

1423.name: Dancing in circles
1423.desc:
Spinning around, getting hot - sounds like a dance. Or a thermal centrifuge. Build a large thermal refinery to get the most out of your ores.

You can use Noise Hazard Signs instead of Thermal Processing Casings, as long as you have at least 8 Thermal Processing Casings.

The muffler goes on the bottom layer.

1424.name: Find Ilmenite
1424.desc: You actually get more Ilmenite from the Bauxite Vein. But maybe you are here for the Chromite?

1425.name: Calculation Processors
1425.desc: Want some calculation processors? Buy some!

1426.name: Find Some Meteoric Iron
1426.desc:
To reach tier two planets like Mars or its moons, Phobos and Deimos you need a better rocket. Your next goal is Tier two rocket plates which need Meteoric Iron.

You will find small meteoric iron ore under the surface. Use an HV miner or multiblock miner to get extra fortune on small ores. Small meteoric iron is only from Y 50-70, so try to find tall hills.

You can also just mine the fallen meteors on the surface. Use your ears to listen to their impacts.

1427.name: Tier 2 Heavy Duty Plates
1427.desc:
Rockets require heavy duty plates for the main hull. Let's craft 11 tier 2 heavy duty plates out of tier 1 plates and meteoric iron plates.

You'll need to make a couple of tungstensteel ingots to make these plates. You can process end stone or end stone dust, or macerate moon blocks to get some.

1428.name: Tier 2 Control Computer
1428.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

1429.name: Rocket Tier 2
1429.desc:
After hours of hard work and tons of stacks of iTNT your second rocket is finally ready. But before you can go to Mars you need to craft a few more things.

[note]In case you forgot to complete any, holding the rocket in your hand will unlock all pre-requisite quests in addition to this one.[/note]

1430.name: Recycle Your Rocket and Save the Environment
1430.desc:
Why not recycle your old Tier 1 rocket to get components for your new tier 2 rocket? Put your old rocket in an arc furnace or plasma arc furnace to get back lots of materials.

Make sure you don't need it anymore first!

1431.name: Booster
1431.desc: The distance between Mars and Earth is much larger than to the Moon, so the rocket needs boosters to reach it.

1432.name: Mars Lander
1432.desc:
The Mars lander is required for a soft landing. Put the Moon lander on a Parachute and surround it with airbags - that will do the trick. You hope.

Hint: Sometimes you can't loot or break the lander. Try to retrieve the contents with a hopper underneath. To prevent this, make sure to wait until you come to a full and complete stop, like when at a stop sign. Everyone does that, right?

1433.name: Moon Dungeon
1433.desc:
While looking around for meteoric iron you might find a moon dungeon. The entrance looks like a deep hole. Go and kill the Boss Monster. It will drop the key for the chest in the next room with the schematics for tier 2 rockets.

Kill the Boss Monster again in a different dungeon if you want an extra tier 2 schematic to exchange for a moon buggy schematic.

Place the schematic in the NASA Workbench and unlock it on the last tab. This will unlock it for your character, so you can move the workbench if you want.

[note]If you've made the chip in an older version, you can turn it back into a schematic in a PLE.[/note]

[note]All dungeons work the same, just with a different boss. Also, sometimes the boss room or chest room doesn't generate, you'll need to find another dungeon.[/note]

1434.name: Moon Patrol
1434.desc: Moving faster on the Moon or other planets surface will be fun. I'm sure it will be totally safe.

1435.name: Moon Buggy Schematic
1435.desc:
The moon buggy needs a schematic. Place it in the NASA workbench and unlock it on the last tab.

[note]If you've made the chip in an older version, you can turn it back into a schematic in a PLE.[/note]

1436.name: Fueling Your Buggy
1436.desc: The moon buggy needs fuel to work. Build a 3x3 fueling pad, connect it with a fuel loader, and place the buggy on top.

1437.name: Bigger Oxygen Tanks
1437.desc: The Medium oxygen tank is an oxygen storage tank that can hold up to 2000 units of air.

1438.name: ME Glass Cables
1438.desc: Want some ME glass cables? Buy some!

1439.name: Permanent Oxygen Systems
1439.desc:
You decided to have a longer stay on the moon and look for a permanent oxygen setup to refill your outdoor gear and building a small Base.

Don't forget to bring repair stuff for your tools and armor! Dream 2018!

1440.name: Oxygen Bubble Distributor
1440.desc: The Oxygen Bubble Distributor takes oxygen from the Oxygen Collector, and makes an oxygen bubble providing an area to safely breathe in without needing Oxygen Gear. The bubble is a sphere surrounding the distributor and will go through walls (or any block). Alternatively, you can use an Oxygen Sealer (which needs compressed Desh Plates) to contain oxygen inside a room.

1441.name: Space Torches
1441.desc:
Normal torches won't work in space, only in the area within the oxygen bubble.

One solution is glowstone torches. You can also use other mods' lights and lamps. You'll probably want 3-5 stacks for mining veins, unless you just use a singleblock or multiblock miner.

Another option is to use nightvision and a magnum torch or GregTech monster repellator to keep mobs away. These do not work on the official servers, instead claim and unclaim chunks.

1442.name: Compressed Steel Armor
1442.desc: The compressed steel armor has more durability than the steel armor.

1443.name: Compressed Desh Armor
1443.desc: The compressed desh armor has more durability than the compressed steel armor.

1444.name: Compressed Titan Armor
1444.desc: The compressed titan armor has more durability than the compressed steel armor.

1445.name: Moon Day and Night time
1445.desc:
The Moon's day-night cycle is 2h 40min long, equaling 8 Overworld days and it is linked to the Moon phases seen on the Overworld. The daytime on the moon lasts from a full moon to the next new moon (4 Overworld days, equaling 1h 20min) and, logically, the nighttime lasts from the new moon to the next full moon.

Since the 1st Overworld night is full moon, you can safely land within the 1st 4 Overworld days, and then the next 4 Overworld days are more dangerous since hostile mobs will be able to spawn in the dark, and so on. If you miscount, the moon phases can tell you when it is daytime or nighttime on the moon. Full moon is the start of the moon day, new (or dark) moon is the start of the moon night.

During the nighttime, the environment light levels get very low, allowing hostile mobs to spawn on the surface.

1446.name: Mars Dungeon
1446.desc:
The Mars dungeon works the same as last time, so go find it!

If you need the schematics for the astro miner or the cargo rocket, kill another Boss Monster and exchange the schematics.
Before you journey home make sure you fulfill the quest requirements in the EV tab.

1447.name: Astro Miner Schematic
1447.desc: The astro miner needs a schematic.

1448.name: Tier 3 Control Computer
1448.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

1449.name: Cargo Rocket Schematic
1449.desc:
The Cargo Rocket has the capability to transport solid items between dimensions, and is a perfect way to transport items between dimensions without relying on ender chests.

This rocket can be loaded with 2000L of Rocket Fuel. The safe fuel level is 80 percent.

You can fill up the right slots with chests to increase the storage space to 36 and 54 slots.

1450.name: Astro Miner
1450.desc:
The Astro Miner is an automatic mining vehicle which moves around, makes tunnels, mines up everything it finds, and brings it all back to a base station where it docks. It is not player-rideable, so don't try and climb inside it!

It's not very fast or efficient, and it seems to discard half or more of the ore it attempts to collect. Only consider using it if you want the huge amount of planet blocks it picks up.[/note]

1451.name: Astro Miner Base
1451.desc: To construct the dock, place 8 Astro Miner Base blocks in a cube, 2 blocks high, 2 blocks wide and 2 blocks deep. When the last of the base blocks is placed, it should automatically convert into an Astro Miner Base.

1452.name: Launch Controller
1452.desc:
The Launch Controller has been modified in GTNH to provide additional functionality. If you launch from a Launch Pad with the Launch Controller set up, the countdown will be greatly reduced, and you will teleport directly to the receiving Launch Pad. It will use half the rocket's fuel up though. This is very useful on laggy servers and can act as an early teleport option if you don't like the magical solutions. You will need to set up a Rocket Launch Pad with Launch Controller and possibly a Fuel Loader on either side.

If you don't to use it for yourself, you can also try to use it for the Cargo Rocket. To use those at full capacity it is necessary to equip a Rocket Launch Pad, to send items, with:

Fuel Loader
Launch Controller
Cargo Loader
And the receiving side with:

Fuel Loader
Launch Controller
Cargo Unloader

It is also possible to use Buildcraft pipes to transfer items into the Cargo loaders. Next step is to set up the Frequencies in both Launch Controllers (i.e. Set: A to target B, B to target A). Have a look at Launch Controller to set up the automated launch. The first time you have to right-click the Cargo rocket and press launch at the top right corner.

1453.name: Tier 3 Heavy Duty Plates
1453.desc: Rockets require heavy duty plates for the main hull. Let's craft 38 tier 3 heavy duty plates out of tier 2 plates and desh plates.

1454.name: Rocket Tier 3
1454.desc:
After days of hard work and a hundred tons of stacks of iTNT your third rocket is finally ready.

You need to use Dense Hydrazine Rocket Fuel or better to fuel it.

[note]In case you forgot to complete any, holding the rocket in your hand will unlock all pre-requisite quests in addition to this one.[/note]

1455.name: Fluix Crystals
1455.desc: Want some fluix crystals? Buy some!

1456.name: Tier 3 Lander
1456.desc: The Tier 3, or Deep Space lander is required for a soft landing.

1457.name: Heavy Rocket Nose Cone
1457.desc: Every rocket needs a nose cone. Tier 3 rocket needs a heavy nose cone.

1458.name: Heavy Rocket Engine
1458.desc: Every rocket needs a rocket engine. A heavy one is needed in the tier 3 and 4 rockets.

1459.name: Heavy Rocket Fins
1459.desc: Every rocket needs a few rocket fins. Tier 3 rockets need heavy ones.

1460.name: Medium Fuel Canister
1460.desc: Every rocket needs fuel storage canisters. For the tier 3 rocket you need to craft two small and two medium canisters.

1461.name: Just Tell Me What to Scan Already!
1461.desc:
Don't want to guess at what has what aspect? Just want to get to work? Then scan this stuff. The first set should be scanned in order. If any of them don't work, try scanning something else first (especially the third set).

Watering Can
Torch
Coal
Grass Block
Trapdoor
Chest
Glass Block
Bleached Bone Block
Chicken
Soul Sand
Paper
Rotten Flesh
Wheat
Flint
Obsidian

Ash Cloud
Any Dye
Wool
Spider Eye
Snowball
Milk Bucket
Vanilla Hoe
Vanilla Pickaxe
Arrow
Vanilla Armor
Quicksilver
Fence Gate
Iron Nugget
Gold
Zombie Brain
Ender Pearl
Slimeball
Tainted Anything. Try overfilling your cauldron to create flux goo to make the slimes (not in your base)
Ethereal Essence (any Aspect) from wisps OR Terra Wart

Obzinite Ingot
Platinum Metallic Powder
Fine Copper Wire
Magnetite Dust
Thorium Dust
A Pig
Netherrack
Gold Armor
Bed
Lead (not the metal)
Ghast Tear
Clock
Crystal Matrix Ingot

The last 5 aspects might only be obtainable by combining aspects or by creating a node that has them. You can combine:

Cognito + Ordo
Cognito + Vitium
Vacuos + Motus
Lux + Primordium
Humanus + Iter

Note that the above list is for aspects, you may need to scan things like the Nether Portal to unlock certain researches.

1462.name: Thermal Cloth
1462.desc:
Thermal Padding is a set of garments made out of an advanced insulating material. The garments are able to keep a player from freezing to death in the cold environments of Outer Space. They are also able to keep the player from suffering heat exhaustion in an overly hot environment.

The temperature indicator on the Life Support HUD will show if the player is becoming too hot or too cold - as the temperature approaches dangerous levels, an "Invalid Thermal Protection" warning will be shown. On Mars and the Asteroids, simply wearing a full set of Thermal Padding should be enough to fix this.

[note]You'll also need some Silicone Rubber.[/note]

1463.name: Sleeping in Space
1463.desc:
The Cryogenic Chamber is used to sleep on other planets and skip long nighttimes. It can be used at any time, even during the daytime, and in any dimension, including the Overworld.

Using the chamber during the daytime will skip the time to the next sunrise, except on the Asteroids dimension where the Sun is always locked on West.

Once the player has used the Cryogenic Chamber, there is a 300 seconds cool-down time before he can use the chamber again. Breaking and placing back the chamber or trying to use different chamber will not reset the cool-down time, unless by dying.

The chamber does not require energy to work.

1464.name: Arc Lamp
1464.desc:
The Arc Lamp is a very bright lamp. It is directional - meaning the light it casts is brighter in the direction it is facing. Its facing can be rotated using a standard Wrench.

Including the Arc Lamp the brightness of blocks which are close to light sources is:

Redstone Torch: 3-6
Torch: 9-12
Block of Glowstone: 10-13
Arc Lamp: 13-14

The Arc Lamp can light up a fairly large area - for example a whole room - at light level 14: it is almost as bright as full sunlight.

The Arc Lamp also has the interesting property of actively repelling mobs. Not only does its light prevent mobs from spawning nearby, but it is so bright that if a mob comes close to the Arc Lamp the mob will turn around and walk away again. This mob-repellant effect is confirmed to work on: Zombies, Skeletons, Creepers, Evolved Zombies, Evolved Skeletons, Evolved Creepers. It may also work on other types of mobs (confirmation required). But it has no effect on Spiders (they are not afraid of sunlight either) or boss mobs.

1465.name: Airlocks
1465.desc:
An airlock is an airtight door used to provide a passage between enclosed rooms and between an enclosed room and outer space. It is made from Air Lock Frames and one Air Lock Controller.

The airlock will keep air inside the room while it is closed. But when the airlock is open, the room is no longer sealed and oxygen gear must be worn until the airlock is closed. In environments with extreme temperatures, Thermal Padding must also be worn.

1466.name: Temperature Control
1466.desc:
The Ambient Thermal Controller is an item for use in the Oxygen Sealer to provide thermal protection in sealed spaces.

An Ambient Thermal Controller is useful in the dimensions too cold or too hot for players to survive without Thermal Padding.

It can be placed in the 3rd slot next to the energy and oxygen slots in a working Oxygen Sealer. Once the Ambient Thermal Controller is in the sealer, it will heat or cool the output oxygen according to the local environment, and then the player can safely take the thermal padding off in the sealed room. Though there's absolutely no reason to do so.

1467.name: Oxygen Sealer
1467.desc:
The Oxygen Sealer is a machine that fills a room with oxygen (although the oxygen is generally not visible) up to around 800-1000 blocks (the amount stacks with multiple sealers within close proximity) allowing the user to breathe without Oxygen Gear. Unlike the Oxygen Bubble Distributor, the Oxygen Sealer confines the oxygen to the room it is in. The sealer must be supplied with power and sufficient oxygen. See Oxygen Collector for supplying oxygen.

The sealer will only work in a room that is completely sealed off from outer space. This can be achieved with either a completely closed off room, or with an Air Lock. The sealer must have nothing above it to block its vents. It is designed to check for a proper seal about every 5 seconds, so be sure to give it time to seal.

Each sealer requires at least 320 air points per second to work continuously or it will fail.

1468.name: Large Oxygen Tanks
1468.desc: The Heavy oxygen tank is an oxygen storage tank that can hold up to 4000 units of air.

1469.name: Bacterial Sludge
1469.desc:
Bacterial Sludge is a fluid block that generates naturally on Mars.

It generates in the giant underground caves in Mars in small amounts.

Collect it to produce mutagen for bees mutation in gendustry.

1470.name: Cavernous Vine
1470.desc:
Cavernous Vine is a poisonous plant that grows naturally in the Mars underground. It grows from the ceiling down to the floor in the huge caves flooded with Bacterial Sludge, emits light and can trap mobs in it.

Any mob that touches this plant will be poisoned, dragged up and lose health down to 1 health point (half a heart) while trapped in this plant. Mobs that are immune to poison, like Zombies and Skeletons, will not lose health.

They grow from the bottom face of a solid block down to 1 block away from the floor.

Cavernous vines can be broken by hand without taking damage or being poisoned but they will drop nothing. They can be obtained as an item using shears. Even picks with Silk Touch enchant will not obtain anything.

Once obtained, they can be replanted elsewhere and regrow, like cactus.

[note]That said, they're still useless.[/note]

1471.name: Slimeling
1471.desc:
The Slimeling is a utility mob that spawns from Slimeling Eggs found on the surface of Mars. It shares some features from vanilla wolves and mules: it can be tamed and attacks mobs that threaten its owners, and can be equipped with a special chest to transport items.

As an allied mob, Slimelings will actively help and follow the player-owner and will attempt to protect them. They can also have a neutral behavior, that is, they can attack if provoked or hurt by non-owners.

Items that you can feed Slimelings with:

Boat
Cooked fish
Gunpowder
Gold ingot
Redstone repeater
Wooden door

Slimelings can be equipped by their owners with a Slimeling Inventory Bag to transport items.

To complete this quest get any one of the eggs - note you may need a Heavy Duty Pickaxe.

1472.name: Grapple
1472.desc:
The Grapple is a grappling hook and rope which can be thrown by a player, and then used to swing the player in the direction it was thrown. Useful for reaching places that are otherwise too high to jump to, or traveling around the Asteroids. [note](Or just, you know, fly.)[/note]

Right-click to aim the Grapple at a block you want to reach, similar to aiming a Bow. Release the right-click - similar to firing an arrow from a bow - to grapple yourself in that direction.

For best results, aim the Grapple at a point 1 or 2 blocks higher than the point you want to reach.

If there is no safe platform to land at that point, you can fall back down and you will take fall damage, so it takes some skill to use the Grapple safely.

Take care using the Grapple near a Nether Portal, End Portal or at a low height (less than Y30) on a Space Station. There can be strange consequences if it crosses into another dimension.

1473.name: Nice and Comfortable - in Space!
1473.desc:
These thermal clothes will keep you nice and warm even in the coldest depths of outer space. Tier 3+ planets pretty much all require these.

[note]You'll also need some PBI.[/note]

1474.name: Want to Join a Band?
1474.desc:
Or at least play with drums? If you have extra Meteoric Iron after building your rocket and making your thermal padding, turn it into Meteoric Steel and use it to make some drums. These drums can hold 256 buckets even after breaking.

And did you know you can use them as cells? Try loading or unloading a supertank!

1475.name: Cows Are Supposed to Moo!
1475.desc:
What the...? You noticed that sometimes when you kill something, even a cow or chicken, it explodes! What madness!

To stop it, use a proper GregTech Knife or Butchery Knife to kill them. Craft a flint GregTech knife with a stick and a piece of flint.

To see its recipe, see how a generic knife is made - NEI should show you many pages. The recipe you need should be on the last page. You can scroll pages with your mouse wheel.

[note]Alternatively you can use the search bar to search for a specific tool material, in this case flint![/note]

As a reward, here's an iron knife to use once the flint one wears out.

1476.name: All the Finest Cuts of Meat
1476.desc:
This knife is not going to work for slaughtering the hundreds of animals you will need for food and materials. Make yourself a GregTech Butchery Knife. It stops the explosions, and kills the cows much faster, not to mention has Looting on it. Later when you get Stainless Steel, you can make a new one that will kill cows in one hit.

Note that the GT Tool damage is based on tinkers mining level.

1477.name: You're Gonna Hate This in MV Too #6
1477.desc: The sixth item you'll need a lot of. For the scanner and the seismic prospector.

1478.name: You're Gonna Hate This in MV Too #5
1478.desc: The fifth item you'll need a lot of. For the microwave, the scanner, the precision laser engraver, etc.

1479.name: Rubber Rings
1479.desc: Want some rubber rings? Buy some!

1480.name: Radon Decay
1480.desc:
Radon is needed for EV Emitters and Sensors.

You can get Radium-226 from sifting Thorium, Pitchblende, Uraninite, or Uranium 238. It needs 4500 seconds, or 75 mins, to decay, and it will debuff you until it decays unless you have complete hazmat protection. It's a good idea to make a Lead-Lined Box and let the Radium decay there, instead.

Thorium can be obtained from the Nether, whereas the other 3 ores are locked after the Tier 2 Rocket. However, Thorium gives a much smaller chance of getting Radium, so you'll need a good amount of it to continue.

After it decays, you can electrolyze Radon out of it and progress into the next circuit line. However, you will not be able to do the Radon loop here, that can only be done with Plutonium 239.

[note]You only need to do one task.[/note]

1481.name: What Was That? I Can't Hear You Over the Engine!
1481.desc:
The Extreme Combustion Engine is an upgrade to the Large Combustion Engine, the IV version that needs Tungstensteel instead of Titanium. The structure is essentially the same, and it's almost a 1:1 ratio between the two materials, so this is a good generator when you've accumulated several stacks of Tungstensteel.

The caveat is that it only accepts High Octane Gasoline. This is the last and most powerful type of combustion fluid made from Oil, and it has the longest processing line of them all. As a reward, it has an excellent 2.5M EU per bucket, and you can burn it here alongside some Liquid Oxygen for almost 4 IV amps per tick.

The Liquid Oxygen is very important, because it greatly boosts the speed of the multi and the fuel efficiency as well. It needs 40 L/s, so you will need more than one Vacuum Freezer to supply it continuously: for an ECE that is always running, you will need a double Vacuum Freezer, 1 EV and 1 HV. You can run fewer freezers if you overclock them, if the ECE isn't working all the time or if you upgrade to a Cryogenic Freezer,

1482.name: Portable Prospecting Tool
1482.desc:
Now that you have some tech, you've discovered a way to prospect for ores.

Unfortunately, the tech to make it repairable or rechargeable doesn't work, so you'll have to make a new one each time it breaks. It does work better at higher tiers and lasts longer with better materials.

It doesn't make a convenient book like the Seismic Prospector, but you can look at the results of scanning on the map, thanks to Visual Prospecting.

It can be hard to choose a good material for the Basic Prospector. Here are some good choices for you in their respective tiers, the size searched, and the average amount of veins they can scan:

LV Vanadiumsteel 3x3 - 50
MV Vanadiumsteel 3x3 - 213
HV Vibrant Alloy 5x5 - 299
EV Shadow Metal 7x7 - 1080
IV Oriharukon 7x7 - 2649

As you can see, the prospectors become better and better with each tier, but it might also be worth looking around for better materials.

Good luck.

1483.name: Really OP Food
1483.desc:
You've heard about Dezil's Marshmallow, a really OP food which can be found from time to time in chests. What about I show you a way to cook this stuff yourself?

After you've mastered the flowing spirit in the dream dimension (witchery), put a bit of flour, sugar, salis mundus and draconium dust in your IV Mixer.
The recipe starts when you give flowing spirit to it and gives you malformed slush, a very stinky and disgusting piece of...something.

Be careful, you'll get blindness II when holding it in your hand.

1484.name: Uncooked Slush
1484.desc:
Next step is to form this slush into the right shape. Use a Marshmallow form and press it with your iv forming press. Remember to sterilize it after using it.

Be careful, you'll get blindness and hunger II holding it.

1485.name: Brittle But Hits Like a Sledgehammer...
1485.desc: A Carbon crossbow limb is 1/3 the durability of the magnalium limb, but hits a lot harder and shoots a little faster. But you'll want to carry around a tinker table and extra ingots to repair it constantly, and watch that you don't break it in the middle of combat!

1486.name: Glowing Marshmallow
1486.desc:
Next step is heating up your Marshmallow in an Electric Blast Furnace at 4500K with some radon gas and making it glow.

Now you'll get a Paralysis II effect in addition to the others.

1487.name: Cooling Your Marshmallow
1487.desc:
In the next step you cool your Glowing Marshmallow in an HV Vacuum Freezer to make it more stable.

You still get Weakness II and Nausea II.

1784.name: Traveller's Wings Upgrades
1784.desc:
Your wings can be equipped with a few modifications such as feather falling and double jump. Place the wings in the center of a Crafting Station.

Feather Fall allows you to glide down slowly in air.
Double Jump means you jump while in the air to jump again.

Hint:
Autorepair can be added to the wings as well.
Every Armor part has 3 Slots for modification.

1785.name: Traveller's Gloves Upgrades
1785.desc:
Putting redstone on my weapon makes it faster. So why don't I put it on my hands directly!
This will increase your mining speed.

Hint:
Every Armor part has 3 Slots for modification, aside from gloves, which have 5.

1786.name: Traveller's Shoes Upgrades
1786.desc:
Your shoes can be equipped with a few modifications like water walk, double jump, lead boots or slimy shoes. Place the shoes in the middle of a Crafting Station.

Water Walk allows you to walk over water.
Double Jump allows you to jump while in the air to jump again.
Lead Boots allows you to walk under water, prevents you from swimming, and may cause excessive Blub. Bring along potions of water breathing.
Slimy Shoes reduces fall damage.

Hint:
Autorepair can be added to the shoes as well.
Every Armor part has 3 Slots for modification.

1787.name: Data Access Hatches
1787.desc:
So, if you want to do more than one recipe in your Assline, you want a Data Access Hatch. There are 3+1 versions.

Data Access Hatch: It holds 4 data sticks, so 4 recipes.

Advanced Data Access Hatch: It holds 16 data sticks. Ironically, you can make this right away.

Automatable Data Access Hatch: It's UV, but it allows I/O of the sticks. Skip it.

The Data Bank Multi: Can be connected to multiple Asslines, and can contain more than 1 DAH. This is the best way, when you can do it. Look in ZPM for the quest.

[note]You only need to make one.[/note]

1744.name: Chemical Lesson, Part Two
1744.desc: More advanced recipes need an MV chemical reactor. It also speeds up your existing recipes.

1745.name: Welcome to OpenComputers!
1745.desc: Hello there, my name is Teirdalin and today we shall embark on one of the most exciting and fun mods; OpenComputers! First things first though, you will need some patience to get through this mod, but it will certainly pay off in the end!

1746.name: Your First Microchip
1746.desc: Microchips will be used in most things you make, so if possible; I suggest bulk crafting quite a few of these.

1747.name: Your First Computer
1747.desc: Place the power converter you got earlier near a cable (you could also power it with RF if you wanted) and connect it with your wire cutters. Now place your computer case by the converter and by the magic of technology it shall fuel the computer case! Now take your hard drive, memory and graphics cards and place them inside, place the disk drive next to the computer and insert the operating system disk. Insert the BIOS we made into the case and turn it on.

1748.name: Arithmetic Logic Circuits
1748.desc:
Arithmetic Logic Circuits are used in numerous parts, typically in groups of 8-16, and occasionally up to 64.

So be sure to make plenty.

1749.name: All Your Card Are Belong To Us
1749.desc:
Card bases are just as you might think they are; they're the base part that each card you make will use to connect onto the motherboard.

I suggest making a handful of these for future cards.

1750.name: Short Term Memory
1750.desc:
Gotta get more RAM.

Memory, or RAM, is essentially a temporary storage of data on your computer that can be rapidly accessed via your programs.

Hence the name Rapid Access Memory.

1751.name: Woah, Rad Graphics!
1751.desc:
See the digital world.

Your graphics card allows the computer to draw text and other things to the screen.

The better graphics card you use the higher resolution, and better features can be drawn.

You also need a higher tier monitor to display colors or to get touchscreen compatibility, but that's something to worry about for another day.

1752.name: Control Units
1752.desc: So controlling! Strangely enough, this part takes an HV circuit to craft; so here's some to make your first computer parts in exchange for MV circuits in their place. Don't spend them all in one place!

1753.name: What Did the Data Say to the CPU?
1753.desc:
Cache me outside.

Your CPU will essentially be how fast your computer processes data.
A stronger CPU = A faster computer.

1754.name: Computer Case
1754.desc:
100 Percent Waterproof.

The computer base is the HUB of all your computer parts essentially. It takes two memory sticks, a BIOs rom, a CPU, and a hard drive at the very least to run your computer.

The power converter allows you to power it from your GregTech power grid, and the floppy drive will allow you to install your operating system onto the hard drive.

1755.name: My Little Scrench
1755.desc: A screwdriver and wrench in one. Not really a necessary tool, but has some purposes; I suggest referring to your computer manual.

1756.name: HDD
1756.desc:
Your hard drive is where all your programs and data is stored.

You're playing minecraft, you should already know this though. Unless you use an SSD I guess.

1757.name: Not Your Average Platter
1757.desc: We'll need these for our hard drives.

1758.name: BIOS
1758.desc:
The ROM disks are necessary to get your computer to start, they're also what you use to run a drone off from.

Your BIOS go onto these little disks, get a blank one and write the basic BIOS onto it.

You can do this later when you make more computers by flashing your existing BIOS onto new cards or combining a blank with the manual.

1759.name: Tier 1
1759.desc:
Congratulations, we have the parts to start making a computer!

Let's start with the basics here.

1760.name: Intelligence is the Ability to Adapt to Change
1760.desc: This block allows our computer to interact with GT machines and everything else. A must have!

1761.name: Hello World!
1761.desc:
First things first, let's install OpenOS to the computer so you don't always need disk in the drive. Type in 'install' and then type 'Y' when asked if you wish to install OpenOS.

When finished, remove the disk and toss it on your wall and reboot the computer.

Type 'df' to display all present file systems and mount points, you'll notice one named OpenOS; this is your hard drive's default name after installing the OS.

Let's change it with 'label -a OpenOS HDD', '-a' checks for names starting with what you type after it with that command. [note]<-- part of this is apparently wrong? Thanks for the non-ticket explaining what part, guys.[/note]

Now for fun, let's make your first program! How about something functional, make a with 'edit startup' this will bring you to an edit page for a new program called startup. Let's just have it do something simple, 'print("Hello World!")' Like that'll never get old.

Now press Ctrl+S to save, and Ctrl+W to get out of that screen. Back in your home root let's now type startup and you'll see it display "Hello World!" neat huh?

Let's now make startup actually work on startup. Type 'edit .shrc' .shrc is a mystic file that runs shell commands when booting your PC. So in .shrc type 'startup' save and reboot the computer. Now it should say "Hello World!" upon starting. Double neat right?

1762.name: I Love Pressing the F5 Key.
1762.desc:
It's so refreshing.

Place a keyboard facing a screen, or stuck to the back of a screen to make it able to interact with a computer.

Absolutely necessary to doing anything on your computer, but can safely be removed once you have an endlessly running program.

1763.name: Mounting Your HDD
1763.desc:
Alright, you have your computer, it says "Hello World!" When you start it, and you feel awesome and capable of world domination.

Now let's set up your HDD. Open .shrc again in the editor, and press ENTER to go down a line after the print, and type in 'cd ../' this will move your viewed directory that's being viewed to the absolute base directory.

Now go down another line and type in 'mount HDD C:' because why not, it's Classic; this mounts the file drive into the folder you're in. Go down another line and type cd 'C:/' save and reboot the computer.

You'll now start in C:/ nifty! Try typing 'mkdir programs' and now you have a dedicated directory to put your programs in for the sake of organization.
'cd programs' will then take you to programs, and 'cd ../' will take you back a directory; you can also do things like, let's say you have a folder called apples in C as well but you're in apples, type 'cd ../apples' and it'll take you to apples. '..' essentially just means previous directory and this can be stacked; works with copying files and moving them as well, very useful to know.

But right now we're going to do something functional, come over to the next quest and bring your adapter and cables.

1764.name: Basic Power Display
1764.desc:
For this part we will learn how to use our adapter, place it next to, let's say your EBFs battery buffer and connect it to the computer with cables.
Now let's type in lua while on the computer to open the LUA environment where we can test things without worrying about saving and trying our program.

Type in 'component.list()' this will display a table of all of .list from component. What we're looking for is gt_batterybuffer.
If you have too many nearby things it may not display and the way to search the entire list of components is too advanced for starters.

So now assign a variable to it; for example 'a = component.gt_batterybuffer'; now we can call 'a' to see what methods the batteryBuffer has.
What we'll be using in this example is the 'getBatteryCharge' method. So type  'a.getBatteryCharge(1)' this will call the method getBatteryCharge from the first inventory slot on the gt_batterybuffer in component.

So if we're using a 2x2 battery buffer; we can type something like, 'for i=1, 4 do print(a.getBatteryCharge(i)) end'
Poof you now see what all the batteries EU values. Now what can we do with this?

Try making a variable called 'batteryCharge = 0', now do 'for i=1, 4 do batteryCharge = batteryCharge + a.getBatteryCharge(i) end'

Now print(batteryCharge) will display the total charge. Here's a program example of this being used.

Place in your tape drive and type 'install', and follow the prompts to install it into C. Then edit it, test it, figure how it works.

I suppose that's enough for now; congrats! Experiment and learn, and don't forget to check google as much as possible!
This questline will be updated again in the future; but I figure this is a good ending point for now.

1766.name: Purchase Network Card
1766.desc: I'll sell you a Network Card for six Bitcoins.

1767.name: Purchase Tier 1 Wireless Network Card
1767.desc: I'll sell you a Tier 1 Wireless Network Card for 12 Bitcoins.

1768.name: Purchase Tier 1 Memory
1768.desc: I'll sell you a pair of Tier 1 Memory Chips for four Bitcoins.

1769.name: Purchase Tier 1 Graphics Card
1769.desc: I'll sell you a Tier 1 Graphics Card for four Bitcoins.

1771.name: Purchase Cables
1771.desc: I'll sell you 16 cables for two bitcoins.

1772.name: Purchase Tier 1 Screen
1772.desc: I'll sell you a Tier 1 Screen for three Bitcoins.

1773.name: Purchase Tier 1 Hard Drive
1773.desc: I'll sell you a Tier 1 Hard Drive for eight Bitcoins.

1774.name: Purchase Floppy Disk
1774.desc: I'll sell you a Floppy Disk for a Bitcoin.

1775.name: Purchase Keyboard
1775.desc: I'll sell you a Keyboard for two Bitcoins.

1776.name: Purchase Tier 1 Microchip
1776.desc: I'll sell you a Tier 1 Microchip for two Bitcoins.

1777.name: Coin Conversion
1777.desc: Sell 32 of those Dogecoins for a Bitcoin.

1778.name: Warp Warnings
1778.desc:
Thaumcraft offers considerable power from its devices and tools. But power comes with a price.... That price is represented by Warp: A corruption of the character's mind and soul, inflicting progressively more dire effects upon them.

While the effects of warp can range from annoying to deadly, accumulating enough of it can also grant you access to greater power... at the cost of increasing madness, and attention from dark powers. At least, others may call it madness... but is it truly insanity, when the voices in your head grant useful knowledge?

Warp Theory adds a bunch of new Warp Effects to the Player. Be very careful because you don't want to spawn a Wither in your base.

[note]If you're still early on in your thaumcraft journey, Warp shouldn't be something that scares you away from progressing further. You'll only stumble upon dangerous warp effects once you've done a lot of research. If you still wanna play it safe, make sure to avoid research that's labeled with "Forbidden Knowledge" [/note]

5 warp: Message
10 warp: Fake Explosion
15 warp: Coin, Pumpkin
20 warp: Lay Eggs, Spawn Phantoms
25 warp: Fake Creeper, Fake Rain
30 warp: GregTech Fake Sounds, Spawn Bats
35 warp: Blood, Fake Enderman
40 warp: Spawn Friendly Creeper
45 warp: Jump Boost, Nausea
50 warp: Insomnia, Vis Drain, Strange Noise
55 warp: Thunderstorm
60 warp: Spawn Fire Bats
65 warp: Blindness, Poison
70 warp: Livestock Rain
75 warp: Spawn Doppelganger
80 warp: Spawn Endermen, Wither Potion
85 warp: Wind
90 warp: Ender Pearl
95 warp: Blaze Fireball
100 warp: Litmus Paper, Junk, Call from the Deep
110 warp: Obsidian
120 warp: Lightning
125 warp: Inventory Swap
130 warp: Random Teleport
140 warp: Acceleration
150 warp: Fake Wither, Inventory Scramble
160 warp: Countdown Bomb
170 warp: Mushrooms
175 warp: Snow
180 warp: Decay
190 warp: Swamp
200 warp: Spawn Wither

1779.name: Step Assistant
1779.desc:
The Piston Boots are a bouncy pair of shoes for adventurers.

It functions as a piece of armor for the foot slot that gives a fun jump boost! They let the wearer jump up to THREE blocks in height and increase run speed. They also grant a Step-Up enchantment to allow the wearer to step up a single block without jumping while sprinting. You can repair them with leather using an anvil.

Basically, make them ASAP.

1780.name: Olive Saplings
1780.desc: Olive trees can't be found in the overworld, so buy one with coins.

1781.name: Traveller's Gear
1781.desc:
The goggles, wings, shoes, vest, belt and boots itself are not really special.

You can zoom with the googles, have an extra inventory hotbar with the belt, step-up assist with the shoes, jump boost with the wings and faster swimming ability with the vest.

You get protection like with normal armor too.

Later on you can upgrade your items to become more powerful ones.

1782.name: Traveller's Goggles Upgrades
1782.desc:
Nightvision is very useful. If you combine your Goggles with a night vision potion (3:00, make sure to make 3 at a time), a golden carrot, and a flint and steel inside the Tinkers Crafting Station you get Night Vision Goggles. Place the Goggles in the Center of the Crafting Station.

Hint: Autorepair can be added to the goggles as well.

Every Armor has 3 slots for modification.

1783.name: Traveller's Vest Upgrades
1783.desc:
Your vest can be equipped with a few modifications like perfect dodge and stealth.
Perfect dodge mean you're harder to hit and can be stacked up to 3 times.
Stealth turns you invisible while sneaking. Place the vest in the center of a Tinker's Crafting Station.

Hint:
Autorepair can be added to the vest as well.
Every Armor part has 3 Slots for modification.

1536.name: I Ain't Afraid of no Ghost!
1536.desc:
I've found a use for the Ectoplasm you can collect from spirits of the dead. It makes a very useful glass that you can walk on, but mobs cannot. However, they think they can!

So you can use this to create a moat around your base that drops mobs to the death in various ways, or just to shuttle them off somewhere else. Separate babies from mothers? Use it in a mob farm? Capture pigmen walking into the Overworld from your Nether portal? So many possibilities!

1537.name: Black and Decker
1537.desc:
Tired of having to replace tools all the time because they constantly break? Now that Steel is available, it's time to make some electric versions of the Screwdriver and Wrench! Charge them up like Batteries, and they'll last much longer than the non-electric versions of these tools.

You can use Lithium Batteries, or if you're on a budget, Cadmium or Sodium Batteries instead. This does reduce their energy capacity though. Later on, Vibrant Alloy will make excellent tools with great durability. There are, however, a multitude of other great materials that you may have access to, so take a look around in NEI and decide for yourself.

There are also MV and HV versions, though they require that you can process Aluminium or Stainless Steel, respectively.

1538.name: Ceres Trip
1538.desc:
You can find the same ores as Callisto minus callisto ice here, but the real goal is the dungeon and the small ores: Naquadah and Quantium.

Naquadah is used for efficient Silicon Wafer, as well as highly advanced components you'll need tons of at ZPM.

Quantium is used for T5 Rocket and item pipe that has the best throughput in GT.

[note]Make sure to bring your MBM to maximize ore yield.[/note]

1539.name: Quantum Star
1539.desc: Both the IV emitter and sensor need a Quantum Star to craft them. Hope you automated Radon production.

1540.name: Magnesium Source
1540.desc:
Magnesite, the source of Magnesium can be found in the Twilight Forest at Y 10-40. Fullers Earth can be found on the Overworld at Y 50-70. Marble dust is a great source of Magnesium too.
Other options in LV are Pyrope, Olivine, or Ashes.

You only need one of the three to complete the quest.

1541.name: Analyze Your Crops
1541.desc:
The cropnalyzer is a powered tool used to analyze IC2 crop seedbags. Charge it by placing it in an MV machine; it is recommended to also place an MV battery in it to keep it fully charged. To fully analyze a seed bag, you will need to put it through the cropnalyzer four times. If the crop is already planted, it is better to analyze it with the portable scanner instead of breaking it just to scan it - you have a chance of losing the seed every time you break it.

The cropnalyzer also has a feature called the IC2 Crop Calculator, where you can find the mix of outputs of breeding together any crops. To access the calculator, hit the IC2 side inventory key while holding the cropnalyzer. You can select any crops available and discover what plants have decent percentages of a result. And no, you can't do the reverse, search for the crops.zip on discord for that (it doesn't have micadia though).

You can choose up to four crops to breed together. Then click "process" and the bottom row will show the possible crossbreeding outputs, and their weights. You can use the + and - gui buttons to scroll through the row(s) of crops. You can also use a button in the GUI to sort the rows by tier or weight. You can invert the sorting by hitting the sorting button while holding shift.

When trying to get a specific crop, you generally want to maximize its percentage. Do this by breeding 2-4 of the same plant together (more is faster). There is no advantage to crossing plant types, it just reduces the % chance of getting what you want, contrary to what you might think.

Use the crops guide at [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/IC2_Crops_List[/url] for more details.

A large field of stickreeds has a good chance at making lots of valuable crosses. This is because it's decently high tier (they randomly mutate to tiers nearby mostly), and it's really fast, so you can get it to a high stat fast, which you should probably do first. Mutations are random, you can get transformium from stickreed, so keep that in mind. You do tend to get similar crops, so if you want metals, get the metal crops for example. Go to the wiki or spreadsheet (not up to date) to see the attributes that govern similarity.

1542.name: Crop Automation
1542.desc:
The Crop Manager is a LV machine that will monitor crops in a 11x5x11 area and automatically apply fertilizer, Weed-EX, and Water as needed. Water can be pumped in directly or use IC2 Universal water cells, normal cells aren't returned when they're used.

The Crop Manager is NOT rainproof, but you can cover it with a water source block just fine!

Tilled dirt still requires a nearby water block to be hydrated as the Manager will only water the crop, not the soil. Try putting a lily pad on top so it isn't annoying to walk on, or using Garden Soil instead.

1543.name: Weed Ex
1543.desc:
Weed-EX is used to prevent the growth of Weeds on empty Crop blocks at the cost of some weed-EX. Damage to the crop's quality is illusory, and not true. We have people that have looked at the code, we assure you. Unless it counts as a weed (venomilia Gr 8+, any other crop Gr 24+), then it loses maybe 1 stat. Maybe. And you can just increase it again too. And it might not take effect even then if you don't apply too much, like a crop matron won't.

Usage:
While holding Weed-Ex, right-click on a Crop to apply it. It can also be placed in a Crop Manager to automatically apply it to your crops.

1544.name: Fish Oil
1544.desc:
Got Fish?

[note]There are rumors of a weird crop in the shape of a fish...

Otherwise look under Fishing Farming Cooking tab for fish catching options.[/note]

1545.name: Time to Drill For Ore!
1545.desc:
