When you use the seismic prospector, you'll need a scanner and a printer to create the books full of tasty ores and delicious fluids.

Use ink sacs in a fluid extractor to get squid ink. Then move it using pipes or cells.

1793.name: Turning Dirt into proper Soil
1793.desc:
With an Assembler and some Seed Oil, you can turn Dirt and Sand into Garden Soil. This optimal Soil does not require nearby water, and Crops won't break when you fall on or run near it.

However, unlike watered Farmland, it doesn't contribute to the humidity stat or speed up IC2 crops, so it's not useful for min-maxing.

The same goes for Enchanted Earth, the Lamp of Growth, Sprinklers, etc.

1794.name: Crop Stats
1794.desc:
With the portable scanner, you can get the crop's stats and environmental info while it is still in the ground by right-clicking it. It also stays scanned after getting the seed. Look for the portable scanner quest after the EBF in Multiblock Goals. About those stats:

Growth:
How fast it grows, with higher = faster. Specifically, it increases the growth points your crop gets per growth tick. If it gets to 24+, your crop acts like a weed, so most people say to stay at 23 or less. However, it will only weed other crops under certain conditions.

1) Is in a cardinal direction on the same Y level. 2) Will only affect dirt/grass, or cropsticks or crops 3) If there's a crop there that is also a weed, if it's at a maturity level > 1, it won't affect it 4) If it affects the crop, it'll be replaced with a normal weed

Gain:
Influences chances of how many drops you get, and whether you get any at all. 31 is the best for sure.

Resistance:
This stat is responsible for a couple of crops' properties: chances for getting a seed while punching crop, resistance of your crop to trampling (jumping on it), decreases chance of crop dying due to lack of environmental stats, and resistance of crop to weed spread. It also reduces mutation chance as it goes up (apparently). Aim for around 10 for balance.

1795.name: Metals From the Sky
1795.desc:
You can even create metals not found on Earth using crops! You'll need the proper metal block beneath the crop in order for them to fully mature.

BTW, higher tier crops have a lower chance of dropping seeds, so consider breeding a second one before you try breaking them, OK?

1796.name: Better Gallium and Arsenic Sources
1796.desc:
There are a couple of ways to increase your Gallium.

1. Find TiCo zinc gravel ore and process it to get gallium.

2. Breed Zinc Bees and react the Gallium Combs with Hydrochloric Acid. The bee is not too deep in the breeding progression and the processing only requires LV.

3. Use IC2 crops under the Farming tab to get Galvania leaves, to 4x your zinc and sphalerite crushed ores to purified ores.

4. Use the HV macerator on the centrifuged ores to get a 10 percent chance of a whole Gallium dust.

5. Use plentiful Bauxite ores from the Moon.

6. Kill Redcaps/Redcap Sappers in Twilight Forest. Also Kobolds drop Arsenic.

7. Duplicate nugget with Thaumcraft. This requires Taboo researches, so you might not want to do this.

For Arsenic, Cobaltite ore in the Twilight Forest is a great source for more information go to the quest "Cobalt and Arsenic from Cobaltite".

Cobaltite can also be multiplied 4x by the IC2 crop Nickelback.

1797.name: New Threads
1797.desc:
Tired of killing spiders for string? Grow some flax for an easy non-biting source of string.

You can also directly place Cotton seeds on cropsticks to grow cotton that way. With some Corium in the next quest, you can get easy tanned leather.

1798.name: The Ground is Crying

1799.name: Blazes Too Hard to Kill?
1799.desc:
Then grow some blazereed to save yourself the trouble.

Tearstalks should easily yield blazereed.

1800.name: A Girl's Best Friend
1800.desc: Need to impress a lady? How about growing diamonds on reeds?!?

1801.name: Why Does My Plant Bawk Like a Chicken?

1802.name: Vegan, But Still Want to Eat Meat?
1802.desc: Instead of killing animals, just mutate some crazy GMOs into making animal products for you. Science!

1803.name: Magic Metal Berry
1803.desc: ...is obviously the name of my cover band. But also a great crop for Thaumium or Void metals. Requires a block of iron or thaumium to get thaumium, and void metal block to get void metal. Like other ore berries, it only grows in dim light. Any oreberry should have a decent chance of yielding this.

1804.name: Other Magic Flowers
1804.desc: Other magical flowers are possible such as shimmerleaf, mandragora, wolf's bane, and more.

1805.name: Stonelillies
1805.desc:
Stone lillies allow you to get dusts without having to look for granites, basalt, or marble. A great renewable way to get calcite, fluorine, or bulk aluminium (and a tiny bit of uranium).

These require the original block directly beneath them to grow properly.

There is also a Grey stonelilly for stone dust (who needs more of that??) and a Yellow stonelilly for sand or Endstone dust. Handy for the clever chemist!

1806.name: Growing some Glowstone
1806.desc: There are a couple of different glowstone crops - glowflower, glowing earth coral, and glowshrooms. Nether wart is a great source of these.

1807.name: Leaves of Steel
1807.desc: Well, steel leaves anyways. Instead of grinding Twilight Forest for steel leaves, grow a crop in peace.

1808.name: Plants That Make Their Own Fertilizer?
1808.desc:
Sign me up! This plant will create fertilizer ingredients for you.

The best plants to cross to get Fertilia are zomplant, venomilia, trollplant, or spidernip. Watch out for venomilia though, not only is it poisonous, it also acts like a weed at growth 8+!

Use the IC2 fertilizer and apatite this drops to make forestry fertilizer if using the multifarm.

[note]BTW this is the rarest one, with a 0.76% chance even for zomplant, so expect it to take approximately 3 eternities.[/note]

1809.name: Renewable Oil
1809.desc: Oil from crops? Makes as much sense as oil from bees!

1810.name: Boost Your Speed v2
1810.desc: High speed transition and booster tracks get your carts moving at really ridiculous speeds. Do not use normal track without several transition tracks or else your cart will explode. Do not try turning at high speed or, you guessed it, your cart will explode.

1811.name: Even LARGER Tanks
1811.desc: 4000 buckets not enough storage? Stockpiling diesel for a lifetime? Try a Super Tank 2.

1812.name: Teflon Don
1812.desc:
To make PTFE, aka Teflon, you only need a few steps.

The first one is hydrofluoric acid. If you haven't reached the moon yet, the best sources for fluorine might be cryolite. Other options are treating Sand or Sandstone in a Chemical Plant for some Fluorite Ore, and centrifuging stone dust or black granite dust for biotite. You can also get small amounts from Lepidolite in salt veins and Mica. Mix it with some hydrogen to get hydrofluoric acid.

If you have reached the moon, you can also process Bastnasite ore.

Already automated PTFE? Hold 128 sheets to skip this questline.

1813.name: Chloroform
1813.desc:
The next step is to mix methane and chlorine to make chloroform. Make sure you have your circuit set correctly.

Now is probably a good time to look at stockpiling chlorine. Look for salt ores, or lapis veins. You can also make salt water with ghast tears. Better get started with IC2 crops, you'll need a lot of chlorine later!

[note]Lucky for you though, in this line we can recover all of our Chlorine.[/note]

1814.name: Polytetrafluoroethylene
1814.desc:
Molten plastics smell great, don't they? Mix your Hydrofluoric Acid and Chloroform to make Tetrafluoroethylene. React the TFE with oxygen to make molten PTFE.

[note]To get the 125 sheets without any leftovers or anything missing, follow the amounts of the optional quest and use the recipe that uses 1000L of Tetrafluoroethylene and 7000L of Oxygen. [/note]

1815.name: How Much is Too Much Fluid?
1815.desc: There's no limit to how much fluid you might want to store. Make yourself some super tank III's for all your bulk storage needs.

1816.name: Calcite Getting you Down?
1816.desc: Having a hard time finding that Lapis vein for some calcite? I'll trade you some for your hard-earned coins and some stone.

1817.name: Electrum Flux Coil Upgrade
1817.desc: To process more advanced materials like Cosmic Neutronium, Tritanium and Awakened Draconium you need more than 9000K. Upgrade your EBF to 9900K.

1819.name: Silicon Dioxide
1819.desc:
Get yourself some Silicon Dioxide! Smelting it in your EBF with some carbon is the best way to obtain raw silicon ingots. It is used it a few places but most importantly you can macerate it back down to raw silicon dust. You can also turn the silicon dioxide into raw silicon dust via chemical processes using aluminium or magnesium.

You can get silicon dioxide easily by centrifuging glass dust or electrolyzing sand.

If you have raw silicon dust but want silicon dioxide for some reason, you can use an arc furnace or an autoclave.

1820.name: Lapotronic Energy Storage Unit
1820.desc:
The L.E.S.U. is one of the oldest machines still available in GregTech. Its origins are in Minecraft 1.3.X but has been redone to fit into this modpack. You can add a nearly infinite number of LESU casings to it and you'll have a huge Energy Storage. You might also live inside your Battery.

[note]PS: It sucks, don't make it. Maybe somebody will rework or buff it some day.[/note]

1821.name: Cable Diodes
1821.desc: Sometimes you just need a Diode, either to prevent burning up cables when splitting off a main line, or on a Thermos Server's chunk borders. Make one 4A Diode and get a 2A one for free!

1822.name: Manual Transformer
1822.desc:
Annoyed by building chains of transformers? Don't want an Explosion Risk at your base? Want to use these fancy 64A Hatches and Dynamos from TecTech to convert your power? Make a Manual Dynamo and convert it all!

[note]Also needs a muffler, energy hatch, and dynamo. Not guaranteed to work, wishing you all the best though.[/note]

1824.name: Rock Cutter LV
1824.desc: The Rock Cutter is an unbreakable and Silk-touched Mining Device. It will enable you to mine blocks without damaging them, like glass or stone, while it is charged. Uh, Woo?

1825.name: L.E.S.U. Upgrade - LV Tier
1825.desc: Adding this many Cells anywhere to your LESU will upgrade it from ULV to LV.

1826.name: L.E.S.U. Upgrade - MV Tier
1826.desc: Adding an additional amount of Cells will not only cause your storage capacity to rise, but also will enable MV Voltage.

1827.name: L.E.S.U. Upgrade - HV Tier
1827.desc: The HV Tier upgrade first needs over a Stack of Casings. More Casings, more Energy, more Capacity.

1828.name: L.E.S.U. Upgrade - EV Tier
1828.desc: Close to 8 Stacks of LESU Casings, this upgrade enables EV Voltage on your LESU. It's easy until now.

1829.name: L.E.S.U. Upgrade - IV Tier
1829.desc:
24 Stacks of LESU Casings. These do fit in your Inventory, but crafting them is another problem.

[note]If you don't want to clear out your inventory, just check the box when you have enough.[/note]

1830.name: L.E.S.U. Upgrade - LuV Tier
1830.desc:
To reach the highest Voltage Tier the LESU can offer, you need to add an additional amount of LESU Casings. 8193 Casings in total (all upgrades below and these additional) enable the LuV Input Voltage.

[note]I mean you could do that, but maybe just check the box once your autocrafting is done.[/note]

1831.name: Circle Shapes Give Me Comfort
1831.desc:
To build a Windmill you will need a Dispenser, but that item is locked behind the next Tier. Go and find one in a Witchery Circle. They look like small versions of Stonehenge.

Look for the main quest in Multiblock Goals.

1832.name: Grinding With Wind
1832.desc:
The Windmill is a grinder that uses the force of wind to macerate Items and Blocks. You will only get one crushed Ore from it sometimes. However it does give a bonus when you try to macerate other things. That bonus depends on the item inserted, for example Netherrack has a chance to give two instead of one dust and Wheat will always give one and a quarter flour.

The dispensers replace a clay block in the walls. Put anything you like in the interior - a bed, stairs, torches, etc.

Normal multiblock rules apply - do not cross chunk boundaries with this multiblock.

If this is your first GregTech multiblock, as with all the rest you will need to whack the controller with a soft mallet to activate it.

If you have troubles forming the multiblock, try breaking and replacing the controller block.

The controller can be fed items via hoppers etc. to allow for larger batches. Just don't allow the dispenser to fill up or it will void outputs.

The rotor blades will run if the controller is disabled or has no recipe. Remove the blades when not in use or else they'll be used up.

Speed and output will be affected by wind speed, recipe, and rotor.

Macerates 16 items at a time.

High wind speeds will decrease output.

The durability will decrease according to the wind speed.

1833.name: Awakened Draconium Coil Upgrade
1833.desc:
To process the most advanced materials like Infinity Catalyst or Infinity you need more than 9900K. Upgrade your EBF to 10800K.

[note]With the heat adjustment included, you can get up to 11601K if you OC with UHV hatches. Not that you need it or anything.[/note]

1834.name: Mushroom Biomes? Why Are There So Many?
1834.desc:
The GTNH pack is made for the Realistic World Generator (RWG).

If your setup uses the vanilla biomes worldgen, you will get huge Mushroom biomes over the map. If you have this problem, please generate a new world with the RWG.

If you met this problem on a server, put "level-type=rwg" into the server.properties file and generate a new world.

1835.name: Tantalum Ore
1835.desc:
Tantalum is used in the GT (optional) and Ender IO Capacitors, and is also a source for niobium. You can find Tantalite at Y 20-30 on the Overworld and at Y 20-30 in the Nether.

Keep in mind you need an HV + Kanthal EBF and an MV Chemical Bath to get Tantalum Ingots.

1836.name: Kitchenware 2.0
1836.desc:
GregTech tools like the knives get used up when crafting foods. Pams Tool like the Cutting Board, Juicer or the Bakeware can last forever.

After you reach stainless steel in HV you can make these tools that last forever.

1837.name: Ardite and Cobalt Mining
1837.desc: Ironwood is a much harder and durable material than you imagined at first. Making Tools and Armor gives them unique features. With a pickaxe, you can mine ores up to Cobalt level, meaning you can harvest Ardite and Cobalt without an EBF.

1838.name: Low on Sticks?
1838.desc: If you stay in the Twilight Forest for more than a few minutes you'll find Canopy Trees. This big tree generates a lot of logs for your Coke ovens and some roots when planted which give sticks. Be careful that no building or chest right beside or above the tree otherwise the tree replaces it. You might find a use for this, to sinfully get above a ceiling you really shouldn't.

1839.name: "Oh, Magic" Armor
1839.desc: Ironwood Armor is a magic armor with some special enchants. The Helm gives aqua affinity, the shoes feather falling, the plate and the legs extra protection.

1840.name: Maintenance Free At Last!
1840.desc: You'll never ever need to fix maintenance problems on any multi that has this, nor will it cost parts. Well, except to make it.

1841.name: Moron's Manual for Massacring Monsters
1841.desc:
If you haven't noticed, this world is a true hellscape full of Special Mobs and Infernals around every corner. Unlike in vanilla, a player has to actually stay aware and pay attention to what they are fighting. Here are some tips.

If an infernal has Vengeance, don't shoot it with your 56-damage crossbow!

A high health infernal with regeneration is hard to kill. Even worse if it is a witch that can also heal herself. In those cases, run like hell, or bring a soul vial to capture it and throw it into Mount Doom.

Watch out for zombies with fishing poles - they can pull you to them. And zombies with a bow and arrow - they can shoot you back!

1842.name: Creeping Dread
1842.desc:
Creepers can make you have a baaaad time.

Drowning Creeper - when it explodes, it surrounds you in a ball of cobble filled with water. Not only do you have to escape, but the cobble is infested with silverfish! And those silverfish are all infernal! The best way to deal with it is to first escape up or down. If you have to clear the cobble ball, first clear the water. Surrounding the cobble with a dirt moat will keep the silverfish inside and unable to hide. Put down punjis, quicksand, or lava, and/or spectral glass. Make a wall with a 1x1 window and use that to break the cobbles - fishing silverfish is especially dangerous.

Ender Creeper - Randomly teleports you a short distance - possibly deep underground.

1843.name: Caught in the Web
1843.desc:
Some spider have nasty modifiers:

Witch spiders reflect missile damage back at you. If you see a red X on their back, draw your melee weapon and prepare for close combat...

Mother spiders will spawn a lot of baby spiders on death, some of which may be infernal.

A flying spider can grab the oblivious flier.

Ghost spiders are partially invisible and can pass through thin walls.

1844.name: Porkchops With Attitude
1844.desc: The only zombie pigman to watch out for is the Vampire Pigman. He hardly takes any damage unless you use a wooden sword as a stake, or have a magical sword.

1845.name: Flaming idiots
1845.desc:
Blazes are all infernals, and all nasty. Avoid like a plague. Get awesome weapons, awesome armor, and prepare for a fight.

The alternative is to figure out a way to destroy them with technology.

The alternative to the alternative is to figure out a different way to get blaze powder and blaze rods.

1846.name: Filling the World With Fluids...
1846.desc: Need to make a lake? Look no further than the Flood Gate. It will fill the world with any fluid you provide it, up to the level below the gate. This can get kinda laggy in a large area, so keep that in mind.

1847.name: Portable Hole? How About Portable Chunk!
1847.desc: With further research, I've discovered a key made of spectral iron can open a portal into a pocket dimension. It's only one chunk wide, but perfect for storing stuff I might need while exploring faraway planets, or as a quiet refuge after a difficult battle. Using it as a teleportation hub might also be an idea...

1848.name: Armor of the Dead
1848.desc:
I've found another use for Ectoplasm - making Spectral Iron. An armor made from it is better than diamond and highly enchantable.

It also will drop healing orbs that will heal the nearest player when picked up.

Unfortunately, a side effect is that you'll become partially transparent. Side effect, I mean bonus feature!

1849.name: Tell Me When We Reach 20
1849.desc:
Jumping all the time is exhausting and makes you really hungry.
To reduce your food depletion, you might want to use stairs instead.

1850.name: All We Are is Dust in the Wind
1850.desc: Looking for an alternative to flowing water for your mobfarm? Use a fan to blow mobs or items where you want. Apply a redstone signal to activate.

1851.name: Stick a Finger in the Air
1851.desc:
But you really do care. Trying to find a good place to locate a windmill? You could just build one and see, or try this fancy windsock.

Paper rotors are great in low winds.
Wool rotors are cheap.
Leather rotors are durable.

Don't leave the rotors in the windmill when you aren't using it, it will use up durability.

1852.name: Around and Around She Goes...
1852.desc: ...But she'll never stop because you'll always have something to grind. You can use a hopper to keep the windmill full of items to process. Just remember to retrieve them from the dispensers.

1853.name: Moron's Manual for Effective EBFing
1853.desc:
Welcome to the latest Moron's Manual, Effective EBFing! This manual covers common problems encountered when using the GregTech 1000 Electric Blast Furnace.

Setup
When setting up your EBF, make sure to place it a few chunks away from things pollution affects, like farmland. Pollution is nobody's friend!

During setup, make sure the center of the EBF is hollow. Sometimes things can sneak in there without you noticing, like a Thaumcraft light orb!

Make sure your muffler hatch is placed on a middle block. The EBF can't work if it is not spreading those delightful odors!

When placing your power hatches, make sure to minimize the distance between any generators and the EBF. Especially at LV, those few blocks make a big difference! Every amp of energy traveling over a cable loses a little bit - and you'll need as much as you can get! Your best setup is to put two generators right next to each hatch, with a 2A cable. If you're poor, you could use 2 battery buffers with 2 batteries each directly attached to each hatch. Make sure to top off the buffers' generators. If you make 9 slot battery buffers, you can fill them up over time to get longer running time until you get stable power.

Output hatches and buses will automatically push into tanks and inventories placed next to them such as chests or BC tanks. They will also void if they're full, so keep an eye on them!

When building multiple EBFs, the GregTech 1000 Electric Blast Furnace can share walls. This can save you on input and output hatches too. Not recommended for energy and maintenance hatches!

1854.name: Just Like Mom Used to Make
1854.desc:
Jelly sandwiches are a great food source with decent saturation and are easy to make. Almost any fruit can be converted to jelly with sugar and a saucepan. There are a few different nut butters available. Peanuts can be found in Ground Gardens in forest, mountain, or taiga style biomes. Cashews are trees located in jungles or swamps. Chestnuts can be found in warmer forests. Pistachios can be found in jungles. Many of these saplings are also available for trade from villagers.

To keep all your recipes together, you can use either a Kitchen multiblock if you want to see all recipes available to you, or a Forestry Worktable if you're fine with the limit of nine recipes.

Some sandwiches have better saturation than others, so be sure to check before buying that sapling!

1855.name: I Like Big Sandwiches
1855.desc:
Now that you have dough, there are several options for large sandwiches. They offer great saturation restoration and are easy to make.

The veggie and cheese options aren't quite as good and need more work to produce.

There are also normal-sized varieties of these sandwiches if you need more diet choices.

1856.name: Staying Cool in the Heat
1856.desc: If you happen to be near a snowy biome, you can harvest the snow to make delicious smoothies. They are more satiating than eating the berries by themselves.

1857.name: No Chicken Escapes!
1857.desc: Do you like pies? I sure do! There are many varieties of pies available for you. The chicken pot pie is one of the easiest to make. Just a chicken, potato, carrot, and some dough. The fruit pies are a little simpler, so they don't quite restore as much saturation, but are great for filling tummies while you wait for the best foods to wear off.

1858.name: Magnetic Personality
1858.desc: If you haven't received one in a lootbag already, you can craft a nice magnet with a vibrant crystal. It doesn't work great on servers if you're moving around too fast, but it makes harvesting crops a lot easier.

1863.name: Throwing Out the Trash
1863.desc:
Already have barrels full of cobblestone? The voiding drop filter can be set up to automatically void any item on pickup. Cobble, dirt, arrows, saplings, etc. It's handy for when you don't have much room for junk.

To use, right-click the item filter you get as a reward. Put the things you want voided in the item filter list by clicking in there with them. Esc, then right-click the voiding drop filter, and put the item filter inside.

1864.name: No Self-Healing Tools Here...Yet
1864.desc:
In this pack you have to first gain expertise in Thaumcraft to craft the Tinker's Construct Ball of Moss. Go and become a dangerous wizard, and you'll gain the power needed to craft the Ball of Moss and make your tools heal themselves. Of course, you need to get to the Twilight Forest and get some aluminium ingots before you can begin. The Ball of Moss is in the GTNH tab of the Thaumonomicon, and requires infusion. There are many other great things in the Thaumonomicon, but be wary of warp.

Have a beer to prepare for your long journey.

1865.name: Enderman Smelterman
1865.desc:
Need enderpearls? Getting them by killing endermen can be dangerous. Instead of doing that, put an enderman into a smeltery with hot fluids and use a gem cast to get guaranteed pearls.

The higher the hit points, the longer they'll last, so the more pearls you get. If you can find a regenerating enderman, or an enderman spawner, you'll have all the pearls you would ever want. Later on with EnderIO you can even create your own powered spawner to make endermen on-demand. Or just place it in the End and move the mobs inside.

Use a nametag on a regenerating enderman to make sure he doesn't despawn. You'll want to keep him from seeing the sky or else he'll teleport away.

If you haven't found a regen enderman yet, you can buy enderman spawn eggs to put into your smeltery with coins. Maybe you'll get lucky and get a regenerating one.

1866.name: Options For Finding Zinc
1866.desc:
It can be hard to find Zinc Gravel Ore on old servers or unlucky world seeds.

The alternatives are:
- Buy the Zinc Gravel Ore in the Coins, Coins, Coins tab,
- Search for Small Zinc Ore in the overworld,
- Use the recipe with XP buckets in a crafting table.

Once you reach LV, you can get zinc from processing various ores like antimony, sphalerite, tetrahedrite and tin.
Check NEI for more options.

1867.name: MV Motor
1867.desc: Most machines in MV need a motor as well. If you haven't built a polarizer already, go craft one. MV motors need magnetic steel rods and aluminium rods, 2x cupronickel wires and copper cables.

1868.name: Ultra Low Power IC
1868.desc: MV Energy hatches need ultra low power ICs. Go and craft some.

1869.name: The Church of Benzene's Holy Altar
1869.desc:
The MV Gas Turbine pairs excellently with Benzene, now that you can actually make it. Look at the "Bio for the Masses" tab for more details on how to go about it, or just check NEI. Benzene is renewable from trees, and it's a powerful source of EU that can keep you going for several tiers as long as you upgrade it with the new multiblocks you unlock over time.

It's a good idea to switch off Steam, since you need big pipes to carry that around and the fuel efficiency on Steam Turbines is lower. Instead, make a Pyrolyse Oven or an Advanced Coke Oven to make Charcoal faster, extract Benzene from it and run it through a few of these Gas Turbines.

1870.name: Diesel Power
1870.desc:
The MV Combustion Generator burns Light Fuel faster than the LV version, but you can also automate Diesel and burn that instead, since it's even more powerful. It is arguably the easiest and most efficient way to generate lots of power for automation at this stage, and it'll last you a couple more tiers if you upgrade it as you go.

Additionally, Diesel can be used as fuel for the Copterpack, a good jetpack that makes it much easier to move around, inside and outside of your base. It's heavily recommended that you make one, if you have Diesel.

[note]This will also unlock new quests in the Armors and Jetpacks tab.[/note]

1871.name: LV Field Generator
1871.desc: Field Generators are powerful devices. They're generally used in super tanks, super chests, in UU-Matter production and use, or in teleporters. Also, anything someone wanted to make harder to make.

1872.name: Vinyl Chloride
1872.desc: Vinyl Chloride is the base product for making PVC for advanced pipes. These Pipes are needed in LuV machines or the Hermetic Casing IIs for Super Tank IIs.

1873.name: Polyvinyl Chloride
1873.desc:
Polyvinyl Chloride is PVC which can be used for the PVC Pipes. You need Vinyl Chloride and Oxygen to make it. You'll also want it for SMD capacitors later.

Do the recipe twice if you want to clean out your reactor, or three times if used the eight second recipe.

1874.name: PVC Sheets
1874.desc: PVC Sheets make SMDs (make foils), plastic circuit boards, and really good item pipes.

1875.name: A Soul-ution For Warp
1875.desc:
The Soul Brazier from Thaumic Exploration will store your permanent warp.

As long as it has Ignis centi-vis and Mortuus essentia fed from underneath it will stay active, even while you are in other dimensions. Turning it off or running out of power will dump all that warp back into your body, so watch out.

Containing warp does have a bit of a downside - taint. You'll need to figure out a way to stop that.

1876.name: Amplifying Your UUM Production
1876.desc: To get UU-Amplifier out of your scrap you need an Amplifabricator. Both types of scrap are equivalent.

1877.name: UUMatter
1877.desc:
Now you can actually make the UUM with a Mass Fabricator. Well, technically you could make it without UUA, but that's way more expensive. To make sure it only runs with UUA, put a programmed circuit (any value) in the machine's slot. UUMatter can be used for some very specific things, such as replicating some materials (check NEI, mostly/all periodic table), used with crops to create an ore, some specific high-level recipes need it, and...you can also sit in it to get regen.

The energy usage and overclocking of the Mass Fabricator is different from the usual GT way. To have fixed EU to UUM ratios for all tiers, the machine does perfect 2/2 overclocks. It also starts with a very high amperage in LV. The following chart should make it more clear:

Tier  Amps       EU/t         Time
LV     8A         256          160
MV     4A         512           80
HV     2A        1024           40
EV     1A        2048           20
IV     0.5A      4096           10
etc.

Put in power (and hopefully UUA), and out comes UUM.

For now, just stockpile the UU matter. You need data orbs to use the replicator, and that's explained next tier.

[note]You only need to complete one of the tasks.[/note]

1878.name: Data Orbs
1878.desc: Data Orbs are high tier data sticks to replicate materials or used as data storages.

1879.name: Create New Materials Out of Pure Energy
1879.desc:
Using the scanner, you can scan certain items (check the replicator's NEI recipes) to store the information of the item to a data orb so you can make copies of them with the replicator. This process uses a varying amount of UUM and time, depending on the item. Scanning also takes forever.

Some items you might want to replicate include:
Indium
Platline metals like Iridium and Osmium
Neon, Krypton, Xenon from liquid air distilling
Oganesson

For some things (Oganesson), this might be considered the only reasonable way of getting a decent amount.

1880.name: MV Field Generator
1880.desc: Field Generators are powerful but expensive devices.

1881.name: Clay for Pennies
1881.desc: Hard to find that clay? Have a stack for some coins.

1882.name: Tired of Grinding Reeds?
1882.desc: I can trade you some chad for coins.

1883.name: Glassy Eyed
1883.desc: ...from all the mortars you have to use to make glass dust? Have some for a low, low price.

1884.name: Shhh, Don't Tell 0lafe...
1884.desc: But here is a nice quest to get cheap fish catchers!

1885.name: Wood for Sheep?
1885.desc: No, thanks! But how about some crop sticks for coins and a log?

1886.name: Manure for Fertilizer
1886.desc: I mean, what else are you going to do with this junk?

1887.name: Electric Furnace
1887.desc: It's, like, faster than a normal one I guess? But uses power.

1888.name: Repairing Your Hazmat Suit
1888.desc:
Be careful when wearing your hazmat suit all the time. The durability is not very high. Even just falling a few blocks will damage the boots quickly.

You can repair your suit long before you can craft a new one in late LV Age, using some rubber sheets and a glue bucket.

1889.name: Healing Axe 2.0
1889.desc: What, you lost the axe? Well you can craft your own if you dip into Thaumcraft a bit, but if you hate magic, here's another.

1890.name: IV Assembler
1890.desc: An IV assembler is required to make new coils, or enriched Sunnarium for HV Solar Panels.

1891.name: Critical Materials
1891.desc:
For more tungsten and tungstensteel, you'll want scheelite and tungstate. But the most important is desh, found only as small ore elsewhere, and used for the next rocket. Check Y 5-40.

[note]Since there's a good chance you have the MBM by now, you can do the crushed ore task instead of the ore one. This holds for other, similar quests.[/note]

1893.name: Fluid Regulator
1893.desc:
Turbines require precise amounts of fluids or else they will [warn]explode[/warn]. Use the fluid regulator on the pipe attached to the input bus to control the rate of fluids into the turbine. To raise the fluid limit, look at the right side of the regulator and:

1L - Right-click with an empty hand
16L - Right-click with a screwdriver
256L - Right-click with a screwdriver while sneaking

To lower the fluid limit, do the same while looking at the left side.

Use the Large Turbine Calculator and the Turbine stats sheet on the spreadsheet to find what to set it to (after deciding on a fuel). At GTNH, we do the math for you! For this at least.

[note]If you shift-right-click on it you can use the interface which is more precise.[/note]

1894.name: Gas Turbine
1894.desc:
A Large Gas Turbine is more fuel-efficient than singleblock turbines, and can have lower pollution output with upgraded mufflers.

You will also need a Muffler and Dynamo Hatch to complete the multiblock. Don't forget to use a Fluid Regulator, to control the flow rate into the multi! It can usually be an LV Fluid Regulator, since the optimal flow is much lower here than in Steam Turbines.

Each turbine material has a specific fuel efficiency (the higher, the more EU you get from the same amount of fuel), and the multi only reaches this efficiency if the fuel flow into it matches the optimal flow.

If you go beyond the optimal flow, you will get a higher EU/t value, but less fuel efficiency, and the maximum EU/t value depends on the turbine type and its Overflow Efficiency tier. For this type of turbine, tier 1 reaches the max at 150% of optimal flow to produce 112% of the EU/t, tier 2 reaches the max at 300% of optimal flow to produce 180% of the EU/t and tier 3 reaches the max at 450% of optimal flow to produce 252% of the EU/t. The closer you get to this maximum, the more fuel efficiency you're throwing away.

For the best turbine materials, see the spreadsheet and Large Turbine Calculator.

1895.name: Manganese Ore
1895.desc: Can't find Manganese ore because it's not generated early game and you need to grow your Pyrolusium crop? Buy some Ores.

2044.name: Mega is Betta
2044.desc:
For someone who needs to blast a truly ridiculous amount of stuff, this is the machine for you. This monster machine can handle up to 256 parallel recipes at a time.

The glass tier limits the energy hatch tier that you can use. You can even use multiamp hatches from TecTech! If you want to use laser hatches however you need at least UV glass.

2045.name: Frostbite!
2045.desc: This mega vac freezer can handle up to 256 items.

2046.name: A Better Fluid Pipe
2046.desc: Now that you have lead you can convert your bronze pipes to potin pipes with higher capacities.

2047.name: Circuit Programming
2047.desc:
Some machines have a very, very large number of recipes. To avoid conflicts, many recipes have programmed circuits to ensure that the correct recipe is used. These programmed circuits are not consumed.

In this modpack, all singleblock GT machines now come with an icon in the GUI on the bottom-right that represents a ghost circuit. Click it or scroll to choose the number you need. If you do that, you don't have to insert a physical circuit. This is going to be your main option for all single block machines. This option is now also available for input buses of multiblocks, which you will use much later.

There are a few places throughout the modpack where you still need the physical circuits. You can turn an LV circuit into a programmed circuit in the crafting grid and then program it in various ways:

1) Find a correct crafting recipe with a screwdriver in NEI and use that.

2) Right-click while holding the programmed circuit to open an easy menu. You still need to at least have the screwdriver in your inventory though or it won't work. Eventually you can also make an electric screwdriver.

3) Use the BartWorks Circuit Programmer. It's only available in MV, but it does not use any durability and runs on EU.

4) Use the GT++ Circuit Programmer block. It isn't portable and only available in MV, but it allows for automation.

The tasks in this quest are just the showcase of what programming circuits there are. You can just click the checkmark once you understand how the programmed circuits work.

1896.name: Standard Nuclear Fuel II: Naquadah
1896.desc:
These are...not worth it. Go do something else with your enriched naquadah, like putting it in a naq gen if you want power. They work exactly the same as uranium other than materials cost/output.

[note]PS: You can get Nq+ from both centrifuging Nq dust or processing the ore.[/note]

[note]And why are they Naquadah rods when they're made of Enriched Naquadah?[/note]

1897.name: Tricky Nuclear Fuel II: Naquadria
1897.desc:
They're so not worth it I'm just going to give you back the materials. Like naquadah, they're the same as MOX other than materials.

[note]PS: You can get Nq* from both centrifuging Nq or Nq+ dust, processing the ores, or thermal centrifuging the depleted Nq+ rods.[/note]

1898.name: MV Advanced Steam Boiler
1898.desc: The MV GT++ Steam Boiler is the next machine you can make to produce steam efficiently. It regulates its fuel more efficiently if it ends up being full.

1899.name: Double the Fun
1899.desc:
Need even more power? Why not combine rods? Produces more power (NOT double, it varies per rod type) and more heat (again NOT double) but only uses up one slot. You need more complex designs to handle them, but you were ready for that, right?

So how about doubling your doubling to double your double (4x)? Quad rods put out even more power and heat, expect all the other rods to work similarly (for the most part).

1900.name: Unfortunately, There's No Depleted Uranium Bullets
1900.desc: After your rods have finished their cycle they turn into depleted rods. You can then thermal centrifuge them to get some materials back. Depending on the rod, you can get different stuff, see NEI or the wiki for details.

1901.name: HV Advanced Steam Boiler
1901.desc: The HV GT++ Steam Boiler is the next machine you can make to produce steam efficiently. It regulates its fuel more efficiently if it ends up being full.

1902.name: No Boom Tomorrow
1902.desc:
With a nuke, you can cause some real catastrophic damage to your base. You can either place it 20 chunks away, or surround it with blast proof materials.

Fused Quartz is no good now, forget about it OK? Instead, use the GTNH approved Plated Reinforced Stone series! Or one of the other options I guess, like Warded Glass. You may need multiple layers depending on your reactor, try it out in creative. Remember you must close in the corners - blasts can slip through the cracks. To get power out, do right angle turns on the wires as blasts go in a straight line.

[note]There are 6 of them, use NEI.[/note]

[note]Okay, okay, you don't have to forget Fused Quartz, I'll give you some. Maybe you can stop rain with it.[/note]

1903.name: HV Energy Hatch
1903.desc: The HV energy hatch is crucial to a faster EBF that can do higher tier recipes. You will probably want to make at least two.

1904.name: Helium is Not Just for Balloons
1904.desc:
Higher tier superconducting wires and energy hatches will require helium. Compressing air and liquefying it requires 1802 compressed air cells to get 1 He cell. It's probably better to get it from end stone dust or monazite from the Moon. Another option is to have a look at your oil processing and see how you can get Helium from there.

[note]PS: If you've progressed far enough in the TF, you can also chip off parts of the snow queen's lair. She won't mind, right?[/note]

1905.name: Large Heat Exchanger - Fermat's Last
1905.desc:
The Large Heat Exchanger is a powergen multi that accepts a non-direct fuel, and doesn't output EU. What is it for, then? To heat up water with hot liquids, that's what!

Effectively, it's a Steam Boiler that uses fluids instead of solids, but it's more complicated. The three sources of energy are Lava, Hot Coolant, and Hot Solar Salt. The salt is the most powerful, but hardest to get. Lava can be automated from Everburn Urns or a myriad of other options, Hot Coolant usually comes from Fluid Nukes, and Hot Solar Salt is produced in the Solar Tower.

For Lava, the LHE outputs 160 L of Steam for every 1 L of Lava, up to 1000 L/s of Lava input (4000 EU/t at 100% turbine efficiency). For Hot Coolant, the LHE outputs 400 L of Steam for every 1 L of Hot Coolant, up to 800 L/s of input (8000 EU/t at 100% turbine efficiency). For Hot Solar Salt, the LHE outputs 2000 L of Steam for every 1 L of Hot Solar Salt, up to 160 L/s of input (8000 EU/t at 100% turbine efficiency).

If you go above these input rates, the output becomes Superheated Steam, which needs to pass through a Large HP Steam Turbine before the regular Large Steam Turbine. The maximum input rate here is twice of what was before and the conversion rates are now 1:80 for Lava, 1:200 for Hot Coolant, and 1:1000 for Hot Solar Salt. The energy output at 100% turbine efficiency is 12000 EU/t for Lava, 24000 EU/t for Hot Coolant, and 24000 EU/t for Hot Solar Salt.

Very powerful, but complicated, and needs turbines!

For more information, visit [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Large_Heat_Exchanger[/url] or [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Fluid_Reactor[/url].

1906.name: Recycle for the Environment
1906.desc:
If you have too much wood, saplings, netherrack or other garbage, you can recycle it and get scrap [note](this does not have a 100% chance)[/note]. Scrap can be used to make UU-Amplifier to make UU-Matter in 1/4 the time, saving you a significant amount of energy.

Certain items such as cobblestone, stone, and tiny/small dusts are too easy to make and don't give any scrap. But otherwise any items can be recycled to get scrap.

1907.name: One in a Million
1907.desc: Or 4? Need to store ludicrous amounts of cobble? Stone dust? Saplings? Super chests are the thing to use. A Super Chest I can store 4 million items, worry free. You will need to take items out manually 64 at a time, or use conveyors etc. to extract them.

1908.name: Lucifer's Little Helper
1908.desc:
With an alloy smelter and some netherrack dust you can make Nether furnaces, which are extremely efficient cooking machines. Very handy for someone needing a lot of stuff furnaced in a jiffy.

In comparison to the Blast Furnace, the only downside to crafting Nether Furnaces is that they cannot be used as a component for the Electric Blast Furnace (EBF) craft later on. Blast Furnaces can. Then again, Nether dust is practically free, so what's the loss? You can always use them to bake stacks of bread!

1909.name: How Much is Too Much Cobblestone?
1909.desc: A super chest III is just ridiculous. Who would possibly need to store 16 million items? You, that's who.

1910.name: Better Tools
1910.desc: Now that you have an MV extruder you can make the next level of Tinker Tools - Vanadiumsteel head parts with Damascus Steel rods.

1911.name: Craftsman Used to Mean Quality
1911.desc: Now that you have an EBF you can make vanadiumsteel GT tools. With 192k durability they'll last you a long time.

1912.name: 3 Plus 4, Carry the 2...
1912.desc:
When doing infusions, it's always good to know how close you are in stability. The Arcane Abacus will tell you at a quick glance how stable your infusion altar is, and any asymmetries present. 

For now, you can have one in exchange for a few types of common infusion stabilization blocks- but if you need to replace it, it will cost you some circuits!

1913.name: Gut-Enough For You? Bet You Thought Newspapers Were Dead!
1913.desc:
When used together, the Typesetting Table and Printing Press allow you to duplicate enchanted books. You'll need a lot of XP, so either build a mob grinder or get farming. A Fortune III book, for example, requires 40 levels to convert into an enchanted plate.

To use the Typesetting Table, place a print press chase in the top slot and an enchanted book in the bottom left slot. Sneak right-click the enchanted book to convert it into an enchanted plate if you have enough XP.

To use the Printing Press, place an enchanted plate in the center of the machine, ink sacs on the top metal plate, and books on the bottom left side of the counter. It immediately starts processing without any power or redstone signal.

Wearing Reading Glasses, Tinted Glasses, or a Monocle will display more information about these blocks when looking at them.

Each enchanted plate can make three enchanted books before breaking, but the trick is to make two books and then repair it in a Thaumic Restorer for unlimited uses.

[note]You'll need some Thaumium ingots. You can get into Thaumcraft, find some in a chest, or try breeding the Magic Metal Berry IC2 crop.[/note]

1914.name: Do You Feel Unlucky?
1914.desc:
Well not anymore! With some emeralds and a blank book, I'll give you a Fortune I book you can use in your printing press. Once you've duplicated it, you can combine the 2 Fortune I books in an anvil to get a Fortune II. Duplicate the Fortune II books to get a Fortune III!

With a Fortune III book and a tier-specific item you can remove the trash item rewards from your loot bags. More good stuff!

Soulbound is another good enchantment, it will keep an enchanted item in your inventory in case of death.

1915.name: Mud, Can Also be Used For Facials
1915.desc: You may have found plenty of mud piles around the wetlands and thought of them as mere annoyances when jumping in the water or for that armor. Waste not! With your new extractor, you can pull out the clay hidden in the mud!

1916.name: Where's That Handyman When You Need Him?
1916.desc:
A Needs Maintenance cover will send out a redstone signal when the controller it's attached to requires maintenance. This is handy for the cleanroom you'll need to build in HV, since maintenance issues reduces cleanliness level and will cause voided materials.

For the cleanroom you can place this on the top or bottom. For other machines you might want to place it on the side so you can have access to the front face. In that case, you can use a soldering iron and fine solder to have it emit a strong redstone signal that will penetrate through a block next to the cover.

1917.name: BEEEP BEEEP BEEEP
1917.desc:
Knowing that your cleanroom needs maintenance is pretty important. Instead of just turning on a light, why not use a howler alarm? It'll notify you up to 128 blocks away.

[warn]Warning: May cause uncontrollable urine leakage when activated.[/warn]

1918.name: Too Much Fluid??
1918.desc: Impossible! If you really think you have so much fluid that you want to just get rid of it, make a fluid trashcan. You can route as much excess creosote or whatever into it that you want.

1919.name: Too Much Junk??
1919.desc: Impossible! But if you really want to get rid of excess items, make yourself a trashcan. If you want to be really fancy, you can use a hopper with a lever next to it to make sure you only throw away items you really want. I mean, I've never thrown away a tool accidentally, nope, never me.

1920.name: You Can't Tell Them Apart Either?
1920.desc:
Many plants look the same. Someday you'll be able to use a machine to tell them apart+more, but for now you can make a simple Plant Lens to at least identify species.

After using it you can tell the following:
Species
Crop tier
Whether it can grow (some crops have requirements)
Whether you can harvest it
Growth stage, and progress to the next

1921.name: Separating Molecules
1921.desc:
Many centrifuging recipes are slow. You better upgrade your machines.

There are many interesting things you can centrifuge outside your normal ore processing or Methane production. Glowstone, Redstone, Soul Sand, Lava, Ashes, Planet Dusts, Compressed Air, etc. Make sure to look around a bit.

1922.name: Transformers, Roll Out!
1922.desc: When you get to HV you'll want to start thinking about centralizing power generation and delivering power over longer distances. An HV to EV transformer will let you send power across your base without too much penalty.

1923.name: Not Enough Amps?
1923.desc: How about 4x more? A Hi-Amp transformer will transform 4A to 16A. Great for setting up a large power grid and locating polluting power generators further from your base.

1924.name: More Than 4 Amps? Crazy!
1924.desc: For the truly insane, there is a 16 to 64 A power amp transformer. Just ludicrous!

1925.name: What's in the Box?
1925.desc:
Tired of waiting around for hours with radium in your inventory, waiting for it to decay? With a Lead-Lined Box you can safely leave your radium in it and it'll decay while you do real work.

[note]Pipes work with it, but you can not use a WA.[/note]

Additionally, you can also choose to use the Decay Warehouse for this task; it's more efficient but comes at a much higher cost. (More info in Multiblock Goals tab)

1926.name: A Quicker Way to Get Around
1926.desc:
Are you ready for a longer trip? With your new lunch box, saddle, lead and of course a horse, we can start a small journey. Be sure to make paths over water, horses don't like going through deep water. If you get unseated in deep water, you can try getting on repeatedly and moving a small distance each time, this may be faster for small rivers. You can also try jumping over the deep parts if it isn't that big.

If you are playing single player or a local server, horses make a great way to get around. On non-local servers they can cause problems and desync if you have poor internet, or if the server doesn't have good TPS. Like all the official servers.

You can tie the lead to your horse and the fence to get it to stick around while you investigate something.

1927.name: Coal...From Withereed?
1927.desc:
Withereed is a source for coal, and more importantly, a good breeding stock for higher tiers.

You'll need coal blocks underneath it to fully mature.

1928.name: Son of Odin One-Eye
1928.desc: The God of Thunder crop lets you increase your Thorium ore from the Nether to 4x. It also will give you Uranium you can use for Reactoria crops.

1929.name: Just Cover Your Privates When You Walk By
1929.desc: Reactoria crops will drop Reactoria leaves which will 4x your Pitchblende ore.

1930.name: Ironwood is Better Than Normal Wood
1930.desc:
A necessary step for steeleafranks? Maybe not. But ironwood does make some great rollers for cooking, and a decent Sense blade.

Creeperweed has a decent chance of giving liveroots seeds.

1931.name: Vegan Leather?
1931.desc: If you want to save your cows and horses for milk and ...glue... you can use Corium to make leather instead.

1932.name: Gunpowder? More Like Dirtpowder!
1932.desc:
Creeperweed can supply your gunpowder needs without dealing with bothersome explosions. It also has quite a few attributes, making it a good source of random crops.

Corium can breed into creeperweed.

1933.name: Milk of the Earth-Mother
1933.desc: Milkwart means you can get rid of all your cows. Isn't that nice?

1934.name: Cotton and String
1934.desc: With some farmer coins I'll trade you cotton and string. Perfect for making tanned leather!

1935.name: A Better Chisel
1935.desc:
If you want to decorate your base, you're going to need a better chisel.

Normal: 256
Obsidian: 512
Diamond: 2560
Bedrockium: 32767

They obviously get harder to make the longer they last.

1936.name: The Right Tool for the Job
1936.desc: Tinker's Construct has their own chisel tool variant, for converting Tinker blocks into different types. Not a lot of uses, but if you check NEI you can see what you can do.

1937.name: Tinker's Blocks
1937.desc: Tinker's Construct offers a couple of useful blocks from the smeltery. Rough Brownstone takes Tin and Gravel and makes a block that lets you move a little faster. Cobblestone can be turned into Seared Stone, usable in the smeltery walls to make it larger without having to use clay.

1938.name: Some Handy Uses
1938.desc:
The TiCon chisel doesn't have a lot of uses, but here are a couple of interesting ones. Use NEI to see other options for more decorations. You can also slab these blocks if you want something monsters cannot spawn on.

Brownstone roads are slightly faster than rough brownstone.

1939.name: Chisel All the Things
1939.desc: If, for some reason, you need to chisel a large number of items, the autochisel is what you want.

1940.name: Chisel as Fast as You Can
1940.desc: The autochisel speed is pretty slow. Craft some upgrades to make it faster.

1941.name: My Ancient Enemy
1941.desc:
The one place Chisel is weak is in stairs. It would be nice to have more variety, but later I'll show you how to make any block into a custom stair.

You can also search NEI for "stairs" to find stairs from other mods.

1942.name: Basic Blocks
1942.desc: Chisel offers a wide variety of blocks. Searching NEI for "chisel" gives pages and pages. Here's a small sample.

1943.name: Fun Party Blocks
1943.desc: There's also some really funky blocks available.

1944.name: Concrete and GT Stones
1944.desc: Chisel can also transform Concrete and GT stones into new interesting blocks. Some of the GT stone blocks will even prevent mobs from spawning on top. Concrete will let you run faster.

1945.name: Basic Builder's Wand
1945.desc:
The only thing going for the stone builder's wand is that it's cheap. It doesn't offer much in special modes, and doesn't last long either. But you can make a lot!

To use, right-click on a block while holding more of that block in your inventory. An outline will show you what it will do.

1946.name: Low-Tier Wand
1946.desc: The Iron wand will last longer than the stone wand, and has a few modes.

1947.name: Mid-Tier Wand
1947.desc: The next tier up wand is the Diamond wand. Has all kinds of fun modes. Just be careful using it, would hate to place a bunch of blocks accidentally!

1949.name: Better Put On That Rubber Suit
1949.desc: Troll plant! What does it do? Troll you!

1950.name: Who the Hell is Bob?
1950.desc: Want emeralds for trading or other uses? These are the source.

1951.name: And I Dub Thee Sir Knight
1951.desc: Need an infinite source of knightmetal? It will be a hard plant to grow. Make sure you provide plenty of nutrients, clean air, fertilizer and water. Don't cry to me if it dies. Any oreberry is a good source crop, with Cobalt and Ardite best, then iron and the rest.

1952.name: Botany
1952.desc:
The Botany mod offers more colors for blocks and glass, and simple tile patterns or more complex patterns using a Tileworker. For now, we'll make an iron trowel.

This mod will make lots of butterflies, so make sure to do your gardening underground or under glass. The butterflies will spread and infect leaves around your area, making more butterflies, causing lag.

1953.name: Finding Flowers
1953.desc: To start, you'll need to find some flowers. Then take your trowel and use it on some dirt blocks to turn it into soil. Next place your flowers on the soil to turn them into Botany flowers. If you've reached MV tier, check out the How to Be(e) tab for info on Genetics.

1954.name: Analyzer
1954.desc:
The Analyzer is a machine that acts in an identical way to the Forestry Analyzer, in that it reveals the genome of bees, trees, butterflies and flowers. It can also be used to reveal the gene present on the DNA Sequence. Unlike the Analyzer, the Analyzer uses power (RF or EU) and DNA Dye to stain chromosomes and determine the genome directly. The analyzer takes 15 seconds to analyze a single individual.

Information from these quests was taken from Binnie's wiki at [url]https://binnie.mods.wiki/wiki/Tutorial/Genetics[/url]

1955.name: Powder Power
1955.desc:
Botany uses various powders to enhance soil properties.

Raise Ph - Ash Powder, Pulp Powder
Lower Ph - Mulch Powder, Sulphur Powder
Increase Nutrients - Fertilizer Powder, Compost Powder

1956.name: Killing Weeds and Growing Flowers
1956.desc:
Nobody likes weeds. Weedkiller will keep the weeds off your soil.

Bone meal can be applied to germling flowers to make them mature faster.

1957.name: Like Having a County Extension Office in Your Hand
1957.desc: The soil meter will tell you the soil stats - how dry, how many nutrients, etc. You can amend the soil with the various powders so that it matches the requirements for your flower.

1958.name: 80 New Colors to Play With
1958.desc:
When you plant a vanilla flower, it is already mature. Cut it with shears to get a germling you can replant to make more. Break the flower to get a mature flower. Turn the flower into pigment using a crafting table.

To get crosses, plant flowers next to empty soil blocks. Keep an eye out for weeds. Eventually a small germling will start growing.

Remember, do this underground or in an enclosure or your TPS will regret it.

1959.name: Glass of Many Colors
1959.desc: The pigments color an amazing variety of glass. Just one warning - unlike normal glass, this glass isn't fireproof.

1960.name: Ceramics
1960.desc: With some clay and pigments, you can make pigmented clay. Put the pigmented clay in a furnace to get basic ceramic blocks. These blocks are great if you want an area of nice even colors.

1961.name: Mortar and Tiles
1961.desc: To make ceramic tile patterns, you need to convert your blocks into tiles. First make mortar, and then use the mortar with your blocks in a 3 block + 1 mortar pattern in the crafting table to get ceramic tiles.

1962.name: Tile Patterns
1962.desc: Now that you have tiles, you can make simple tile patterns. A 2x2 of red/yellow and yellow/red makes a simple checkerboard. Using the checkerboard again in a 2x2 makes a checkerboard with smaller tiles. Use Binnie's Botany wiki page to find more patterns you can make with just tiles.

1963.name: Tileworker
1963.desc:
The tileworker can take the single-color ceramic blocks and turn them into pretty patterned blocks.

And contrary to the name, you use ceramic blocks here, not tiles.

1965.name: Glassworker
1965.desc: Want some custom glass shapes and configurations? A glassworker can provide some unique patterns to put in your windows. You'll need to make some glass fittings to hold the glass together.

1966.name: Mo' Hammers Mo' Problems
1966.desc:
Yes indeed, time for another type of hammer. The master carpentry hammer will allow you to rotate the blocks from the various workers.

Sneak to rotate the blocks vertically.

Why is the lower tier hammer the master hammer? Who knows??

1967.name: Panels to Cover Your...Everything
1967.desc: The panelworker will create wood flooring pieces for you to use.

1968.name: Fancy Woods
1968.desc: With the woodworker, you can create fancy looking types of wood blocks. You can use wood planks from Vanilla, Forestry, and Extra Trees.

1969.name: Look Mom, No Torches!
1969.desc:
Tired of a base using nothing but torches? This set of quests will go through the various options available to light up your beautiful base.

First up, Railcraft lanterns. These come in stone and metallic varieties. The stone varieties don't need much to craft. One plus/minus for these lanterns is that they are solid blocks - while you cannot walk through them, they also do not fall if the surrounding blocks are removed. Search "lantern" in NEI for more varieties. They are a bit annoying to craft now, but if you check NEI you can make them pretty easily with assemblers later.

1970.name: Second Saw
1970.desc: Forge Microblocks can be really handy to make custom pieces. There are many saw tiers available. If you make me an iron one, I'll give you a diamond one.

1971.name: Fancy Lanterns
1971.desc: Bibliocraft offers fancy looking lanterns. Make the candles from candleberries or beeswax.

1972.name: Even small Glowstone shines bright
1972.desc:
Now that you've been to the nether and gathered Glowstone, you can use that for light as well. You can color it to make the block color change (unfortunately 1.7.10 does not support colored lights) or you can use the Forge Microblocks saw to cut it up into tiny nooks to light up areas without taking up much space.

There are also various glowing plants you can find or breed, such as glowflowers.

1973.name: Basic ProjectRed Illumination
1973.desc: The basic ProjectRed Illumination lamps only need an LV mixer to make. The normal version needs a redstone signal to turn on. The inverted version is on unless a redstone signal is applied.

1974.name: Fancier ProjectRed Illumination
1974.desc: The fancier ProjectRed Illumination lamps, lanterns, and fixtures require the Forming Press and Assembler to create.

1975.name: ProjectRed Lights With a Purpose
1975.desc: ProjectRed offers a couple of special lights that can be used as buttons. The normal buttons are always lit up. The Feedback buttons are lit up when powered by any redstone signal.

1976.name: Flat Lamps
1976.desc: These simple lamps from Ztones are a nice, subdued looking lamp style. I like using them on ceilings for the "office" look.

1977.name: Glowstone & Chisel torches, space rated
1977.desc: When traveling off-world, you want the best in torches. Torches that will work in no-oxygen environments like the Moon. For that, you need glowstone torches. You can chisel these torches into a variety of torch types, also proven to work in deep space.

1978.name: Lighting Up a Large Area, Without Electricity
1978.desc: If you have a relatively large area you need to light up without using electricity, you can use these floodlights. They can take normal furnace fuels, including blocks and compressed blocks. They do require some electronic components to make, but can send out a beam a long ways, or various cone shapes a shorter distance. Use the wrench to change modes, and shift-right-click to invert.

1979.name: Lighting Up a Large Area, With Electricity
1979.desc:
Now that you have a vacuum freezer, you can liquefy compressed air cells and centrifuge them for rare gases. You'll need those to make the incandescent bulb for these lights. As long as they have EU, these lights can shoot a beam or do narrow or wide cones. Use the wrench to change modes, and shift-right-click to invert.

It's better to provide power to an Ender IO capacitor and then power these machines with RF, instead of powering directly with GT EU, because they don't work well with GT's EU amp system, and will draw a whole EU packet for a single RF.

1980.name: EnderIO Lights
1980.desc: EnderIO also has its own lights. These lights have a radius of 8 blocks vs the normal 7.

1981.name: BetterIO?
1981.desc: The Powered and Wireless Powered lights from EnderIO provide light up to 10 blocks away vs the normal 7 for a torch. They will require centrifuging liquid air to get the bulbs.

1982.name: What's Behind Door #3?
1982.desc:
The Malisis Door Factory lets you go crazy with all kinds of ideas for custom doors.

A Player Sensor is a nice way of opening a door. Better than pushing a button or stepping on a plate!

Maybe you can find a use for this block mixer, it's kinda weird and doesn't work right.

There are some craftable Malisis items as well, check them out in NEI.

1983.name: Build it Bigger
1983.desc: You can build it! The Building Guide helps show you an outline of simple geometric shapes. Provide it with a redstone signal to activate.

1984.name: Faster, Better, Buildi-er
1984.desc: The Enhanced Building Guide offers more features than the standard model. Most importantly, you can right-click on any white part of the block with the item you want placed and the block will place in the world. It needs a redstone signal to activate.

1985.name: So Much Nicer Than Ladders
1985.desc:
Elevators are great for fast transportation vertically in your base. Leaving a tinted elevator will stop at the same color elevator. Use some Malisis doors for a nice "real" elevator effect.
The rotating elevator will turn the player as they go up or down to face a specific direction. Handy if your elevator shaft is on one side at a floor and a different side at another floor.

1986.name: More Like Ouch-i-tect
1986.desc:
The sawbench can turn most full cube blocks into amazing shapes of various kinds.

Right-click with the hammer to rotate 90 degrees. Shift-right-click with the hammer to change the orientation of the "bottom" face.

The Chisel is used to alter the structure of blocks in various ways. Some blocks automatically form connections with adjacent blocks. These connections can be disabled and re-enabled by right-clicking with a chisel near the side that you want to change.

Some blocks can have a secondary material applied to them. The secondary material can be removed by right-clicking on the center of the block with a chisel.

You can also right-click on a vanilla Glass, Glass Pane, Glowstone or Ice block with a chisel to harvest the block without breaking it.

1987.name: Upgrading Drawers
1987.desc: Drawer upgrades are different from barrel upgrades. First you need to make the upgrade template.

1988.name: Your Mom Says to Upgrade Your Drawers
1988.desc:
There are many tiers of upgrades for storage drawers. Make some iron and gold tier upgrades and I'll give you some of the special purpose upgrades to experiment with in your base. Check NEI for more options such as Obsidian, Emerald, and Diamond upgrades.

[warn]To remove upgrades, shift-right-click with an empty hand to access the drawer interface.[/warn]

1989.name: Machine Inventory Manager
1989.desc:
The core block of Stacy's...er Steve's Factory Manager. SFM is a high tier automation system. While AE2 can handle a lot of different crafting tasks, SFM can handle even more complex crafting. It's not actually needed for the Assembly Line. But still, this is one place where you can go that route.

Inventory Cables connect the MIM to inventories or tanks.

Right now until some Moron figures it out, your best bet is to experiment, ask for help on Discord, and search Google. The interface resembles most GUI based automation systems like LabView.

There have been TPS problems caused by poorly designed SFM setups. Make sure your outputs are properly emptied, and don't get filled.

Inventory cables are the "basic" cable of SFM. They allow the MIM to interact with an inventory, and act as a connector cable to other cables.

mehdi2344 thinks my jokes suck, but he's an idiot, so there.

Thanks to Nori Silverrage for writing up these quests!

[note]BTW, there's a Creative Supplier for creative testing. The Energistics Connector is disabled because it doesn't work correctly.[/note]

1990.name: Moving the Manager
1990.desc: Ever finished a wonderful program and then realized your Manager needs to move and you have to rewrite the whole thing? Ever need to copy and paste a section of code a bunch of times and been frustrated that you can't? Well we've got you covered with the Manager Duplicator!

1991.name: Naming Names
1991.desc:
The Factory Labeler allows you to assign unique names to anything that can be connected to a Manager - making it easy to identify when you are setting up commands.

Type in the text box to set a label or to searched stored labels - click any stored entry to select it, double-click to edit it. Closing the GUI will set the Labeler to whatever was in the text box at that time.

Left-click to set labels, right-click to open the gui. Setting a blank label will remove any existing labels.

[note]May or may not be buggy. Will lose the label in MP when relogging, but works fine in SP. It'll still work until you need to change it though. Have fun![/note]

1992.name: Wand Enhancements
1992.desc: Thaumic Machina offers Wand Augmentations. These useful modifications to your wand can boost their capacity, lower their vis usage, or do other interesting effects.

1993.name: Charge Buffer
1993.desc: A Charge Buffer augmentation to your wand allows it to hold 25 percent more vis.

1994.name: Vis Channels
1994.desc: This wand augment will lower the vis cost when using your wand by 10 percent. I'm not sure it works, but give it a try and report back, OK?

1995.name: What Are You, a Seamstress?
1995.desc: Enchanted Fabric is the key material to fancy robes and wizard's clothing.

1996.name: K-Mart Special
1996.desc: Every point of vis is critical, so make yourself some magical robes to help conserve how much you use. Later you can make even better robes. They don't offer much in protection, so remember to change before going to hunt!

1997.name: Boots Are Made For Walking
1997.desc:
And swimming. These boots of the traveller will make you a blur as you go around the world. You'll need to research infusion before you can make these.

There's also Electric versions with greater discounts on the GAWD tab. ...I swear I didn't put any thought into the acronym of the tab.

1998.name: I Can See Everything
1998.desc:
With your new wand, you can make Goggles of Revealing. You can now see nodes clearly, more so than with the thaumometer, but all the time! Additionally, the goggles give you a 5 percent vis discount.

Simple lens upgrades will grant you various seeing abilities as well.

Later you can make more advanced goggles and even integrate the goggles into advanced armors.

With TC Node Tracker, it makes remembering nodes trivial. The default key is I. You can select from the list of nodes you have scanned, and an in-world arrow will show you the way. You can also sort the list.

1999.name: Redstone Emitter
1999.desc: The Redstone Emitter sends redstone signals on each of its 6 faces. The redstone signal strength can be set to any value, add, subtract, and more.

2296.name: Platinum Processing, Step 4
2296.desc: Platinum Chloride? You promised Pure Platinum Metal at a much higher efficiency than just smelting it! No worries, just react the Platinum Chloride with Calcium and you're done. The calcium and chlorine can be recycled as well!

2297.name: Platinum Processing, Step 3
2297.desc:
Hooray! All my Platinum Metal is now a fluid.... Time to add the second chemical to recrystallize the dust.

You do different things with each of the results:

Reprecipitated Platinum: Go to step 4, the last step.
Platinum Salt: Go to the recycling step.
Palladium Enriched Ammonia: Keep it like the Platinum Residue until IV. Or process it now if you like, but you don't need it until later.
Others: Can be reused.

2298.name: Platinum Salt Recycling
2298.desc:
What, recycling? That's lame. I just wanna void this stuff! - is what you might think right now, but be aware that Platinum Salt still holds a high concentration of Platinum. We can recycle it to get even more Platinum out of it!

You need to sift it to get Refined Platinum Salt. You only have a 95% chance to get a dust.

2299.name: Platinum Processing, Step 2b
2299.desc: Now, start with dissolving all the PMP into the Aqua Regia. You will also gain some Platinum Residue Dust. This highly concentrated dust contains a lot of even Rarer Metals, like Rhodium, Ruthenium, Osmium, and Iridium. You will need this at IV and higher, so stockpile it!

2256.name: Brazil Nut
2256.desc: Brazil Nuts don't give you much in the way of important attributes. Breed them from Common Beech and Jungle.

2257.name: Coconut
2257.desc:
Coconut is King for seed oil production - 300L per nut. Breed them from Brazil Nut and Balsa.

Coconut and Seed Oil Get!

2258.name: Aromatic Lump
2258.desc: Aromatic Lumps are twice as effective as Royal Jelly in an Alveary Swarmer. You'll need Enchanting Drops from Arcane bees and Pollen Clusters from Industrious bees.

2259.name: Infinite Bats! Wait, What??
2259.desc:
Tired of the constant chirping from the thousands of bats drawn to your world-bending activities? Use this handy device to attract them to a central location where they can be...disposed. The Corporeal Attractor can be configured to attract or repel mobs, and the redstone strength applied divided by two determines the range.

You can use Soul Molds to filter the mobs the Attractor draws in or pushes away. This gives you some idea for automating...

2260.name: Better Boots with a little pizzazz
2260.desc:
Electric Boots of the Traveller grant you a 2% Vis Discount along with all the benefits of the regular Boots of the Traveller.

In addition, they come with a wide variety of new features thanks to our amazing magic devs:

- Configurable Jump Boost: You can choose between 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, or a 100% jump boost.

- Configurable Speed Boost: Similar to the jump boost, you can adjust the speed boost percentage to your preference.

- Flight Inertia: When toggled on, this feature cancels inertia while flying, allowing for precise movement even when traveling at high speeds.

- Omnidirectional Movement: This toggle lets you decide whether your speed boost applies in all directions or only while running straight ahead.

[note]None of these keybinds are set by default. The easiest way to configure them is to go into Options -> Controls, turn on the "Category" button, and type "Thaumic Boots." Then, assign keys for the features as needed.[/note]

[note]If you want to advance in the dark arts you can also make them voidwalker boots.[/note]

2261.name: Walk all over you
2261.desc:
Nanosuit Boots of the Traveller provide all the benefits of the Nanosuit, plus a massive speed boost, higher jump, and improved Vis Discount. These are the perfect boots for the master magical engineer.

[note]If you want to advance in the dark arts you can also make them voidwalker boots.[/note]

2262.name: Walking across the stars
2262.desc:
Quantum Boots of the Traveller, for when you really need to run as fast as the wind. Might cause you some problems standing still!

[note]If you want to advance in the dark arts you can also make them voidwalker boots.[/note]

2263.name: Industrial Grafter
2263.desc:
If you're tired of constantly replacing branch cutters, this Industrial Grafter is rechargeable.

[note]Or you could just make better ones.[/note]

2264.name: Do You Taste Metal?
2264.desc:
Metal oreberries are a great source of new seeds. You can breed some or buy some from the Coins shop. I need some Copper myself, so give me some berries please.

Metal oreberries require a block of that metal to fully mature, but you can harvest a reduced amount before then. Pretty much everything else that needs a block can't be harvested before maturity though. Look at the seedbag to see what it wants. You can also go to the wiki for the exact blocks. Usually, you can use an ore or metal block for those that have them.

2265.name: Deep Blue Sea of Sapphires
2265.desc: Sapphirum are pretty easy to get from Copper Oreberries. You'll need a block of Sapphire beneath them to get them to maturity. They are a good source of aluminium and oxygen in LV.

2266.name: Red Touch Yellow
2266.desc: Garnets are actually more valuable than Sapphires. They're an early source of Chrome and Manganese, which you'll need for HV machines. You can also centrifuge Pyrope, Almandine, and Spessartine Dust with Nitric Acid to obtain a variety of additional passive resources. To reach maturity, you will need a block of Garnet (either type) beneath this crop. You can get Garnets from the HV macerator and Spessartine Ore, or you can use an Implosion Compressor to turn Garnet Sands into crystals to make blocks.

2267.name: A Travel Companion
2267.desc:
Lonely? The Traveling Trunk is a simple companion, giving you some extra space. With upgrades (one per trunk) it can offer you some nice advantages. Like other golems, you can pick it up with the Golemancer's Bell. Its brain is pretty small though, so it can easily get stuck. You can order it to stay in one spot via the GUI. It will attempt to teleport to you, as long as the chunk is still loaded. It can also follow you into different dimensions.

They will heal slowly on their own, or faster if you right-click on them with any food, including rotten meat. Keep away from lava.

Golem upgrade effects:
Aer: Moves faster
Earth: 1 more row of item slots
Fire: Attacks hostile mobs, setting them on fire
Water: Nearly invulnerable. In SMP, can only be opened or picked up by player who placed it.
Entropy: Sucks in loose items nearby if it has room
Order: Can be picked up with its contents, and nested in other inventories. Don't be stupid about this.

2268.name: Who Needs Friends?
2268.desc: Luggage is better than a dumb old traveling trunk. It automatically picks up dropped items, saving you time while mining. It's near-invulnerable, including in lava. It will follow you like an eager puppy, to the ends of the earth - but you probably should pick her up before changing dimensions. A lightning strike can provide more room. Shift-right-clicking will turn it back into an item without losing its contents. Look for her in the GTNH tab of the Thaumonomicon.

2269.name: Magenta
2269.desc: Magenta bees are also Fastest production, but come with +- 3 Climate. Breed them from Pink and Blue.

2270.name: Pink
2270.desc: Pink princesses provide pink dye. They also have Fastest production and +-3 Humidity. Breed them from Red and White.

2271.name: White
2271.desc: White bees provide white dye. Breed them from Wintry and Diligent.

2272.name: Red
2272.desc: Dye bees obviously provide dyes, but some of them are also useful for their traits. There are many dye bees, these are just a sample. They can be useful for the hard to find dye colors from flowers. Breed Red from Common and Diligent.

2273.name: Blue
2273.desc: Blue princesses will provide a handy source of blue dye. Breed them from Forest and Diligent.

2274.name: Batty
2274.desc: Batty bees will spawn firebats. So prepare for battle. Their specialty is Gunpowder. Breed them from Skulking and Windy.

2275.name: Ghastly
2275.desc: Ghastly bees will spawn Ghasts, so beeee careful. Their specialty is Ghast Tears. They only have 1 fertility so you want to make sure you breed the drones with your master template princess. Breed them from Batty and Ethereal.

2276.name: Smouldering
2276.desc:
Smouldering drones will spawn blazes, and make Blaze Rods as their specialty. Breed the drones with your template princesses so you can prevent the harmful effects from destroying your base. Also, their Hellish temperature and Normal humidity requirements are interesting - use an Acclimatiser from Genetics to make the bee tolerant or other methods to adjust the biome. Must be bred in the Nether. Breed them from Ghastly and Hateful.

To get their Blaze Rod drop, however, you'll need to place them in a biome that matches their default requirements. Sorry.

2277.name: Refined
2277.desc: Ah, here it is. This bee has been known to power whole civilizations. Placed in a top tier bee housing like an Alveary or Industrial Apiary, with appropriate frames, it can produce oil faster than some wells. In addition, you will get small amounts of Diesel fuel as well! Breed them from Oily and Distilled.

2278.name: Distilled
