2686.desc: Mix Hydrazine and Methanol in a Titanium Chemical Plant to make Dense Hydrazine for your Tier 3 and 4 Rockets.

2687.name: Monomethylhydrazine
2687.desc:
For Tier 5 and 6 rockets you'll need to make CN3H7O3 (Purple) Rocket Fuel. 

To start, make some Monomethylhydrazine.

2688.name: CN3H7O3 (Purple) Rocket Fuel
2688.desc: Combine Monomethylhydrazine and Nitric Acid in a Chemical Plant. Make sure to upgrade your casings to Tungstensteel.

2690.name: 2-Ethylanthrahydroquinone
2690.desc:
To make this complicated sounding chemical, combine the similarly named 2-Ethylanthraquinone and Hydrogen along with an Orange Catalyst in the Chemical Plant.

[note]You need Palladium to make the Catalyst, so keep that in mind.[/note]

2691.name: 2-Ethylanthraquinone
2691.desc:
Does anyone else wonder why these chemicals have numbers in the name? No? Just me? Moving on, to make it, combine Phthalic Anhydride and Ethylbenzene in the Chemical Plant to make 2-Ethylanthraquinone.

BTW the other recipe that makes this is used later in the processing chain. You can loop the 2-Ethylanthraquinone so you don't have to make more if you don't want to scale up.

[note]You need Palladium for the next quest.[/note]

2692.name: How Do You Even Pronounce This?
2692.desc: To make Phthalic Anhydride, use a dehydrator.

2693.name: Phthalic Acid, The Cool Kids' Way
2693.desc:
Make Phthalic Acid out of Naphthalene and Lithium in a Chemical Reactor.

Ironically, you can't make this out of Naphtha.

2694.name: Naphthalene
2694.desc: To make Naphthalene, combine your Coal Tar Oil with Sulfuric Acid to get Sulfuric Coal Tar Oil, then distill it to get Naphthalene. Seems easy? It gets harder.

2695.name: Serious Upgrades
2695.desc:
So if you thought 'hey, the purple stuff wasn't that bad, why not make the green?', well, first of all, you may need professional help. Second, you're going to need to be in ZPM to make the casings you need, full stop. You need a ZPM ABS for the components, so come back once you've made a ZPM Energy Hatch.

You can probably figure most of it out on your own, but keep in mind that some of the materials need to be created in the ABS, solidified, macerated, and then used in another ABS recipe. The examples here are Hastelloy-N for Lafium Compound and, Rhenium for Laurenium. Rhenium itself is made with a nonstandard ABS recipe. You also need Dysprosium which requires either making Rare Earth (III) or going to the Toxic Everglades. You'll also need either Rare Earth (II) or Rare Earth (III) for Ytterbium unless, again, you visit the Everglades for it. [warn]The Everglades ore veins might be very hard to find, due to their rarity.[/warn] You could also get Ytterbium from Almandine Ore if you are willing to try GT++ Milling and Froth Flotation.

Have fun with that!

[note]You'll almost certainly need more than this, but the exact amount, as always, depends on your hatches and buses.[/note]

2696.name: Beyond the Limit
2696.desc:
To make living solder for your UEV components or bio-circuits, you will need a chem plant with Rugged Botmium Casings. These are not easy to make!

You can certainly figure out the rest by now, but the Advanced Nitinol may confuse you. You make it out of blocks of Nitinol 60 with a Quantum Anomaly in a Laser Engraver. This means you need to make a UV Cyclotron, and hit several low chances in a row, so good luck with that!

2697.name: Chlorine, Your New Best Friend
2697.desc:
Chlorine is a fluid that is used in significant quantities throughout the game, so you might as well get used to collecting it now.

You can electrolyze Salt, Rock Salt, Sodalite (finally a use!), or Salt Water for it. Since you'll need so much, you may wish to consider using your first Super Tank to hold it all.

2698.name: Io Trip
2698.desc:
Any ore veins not on Mercury are on this planet for this tier. The pyrolusite vein can have niobium extracted from it, and samarium can be acquired from mytryl and rubracium. Infused gold is also found here.

[note]Like Venus, you can collect firestone from small ore here.[/note]

2699.name: Quantum Mechanics is Hot
2699.desc:
The QC has 2-24 computer racks depending on the length of the machine. Each rack has four total slots for either circuits (to generate heat and computation) or vents (to dissipate heat). Although conceptually similar to a nuclear reactor, the location of the components inside does not matter at all. Every rack is also completely independent from every other rack which means it is not possible to have one rack with all circuits and another with all vents.

The computation of a rack is the sum of all the components' individual computation. For example, two APU (Tier 3) and two Cooling Cores have 240 + 240 + 0 + 0 = 480 base computation per second. However, computation changes significantly depending on the values for overclock and overvolt:

[note]Computation = Base * (1+overclock^2) / (1+(overclock-overvolt)^2)[/note]

The heat of a rack is a value between 0 and 10,000 and is reported in the interface of the QC or by using a Portable Scanner on the rack. Every component has a certain heat limit which if exceeded will void that component. Breaking or wrenching a rack while it is hot (>2000) will similarly void the components inside. The QC will explode if the heat exceeds 10,000 but no component has a heat limit greater than that, so don't worry too much about ruining your base.

[note]Heat α HeatConstant * overclock * overvolt^2[/note]

The power consumption of a rack is 1A ZPM regardless of the components inside. This is in addition to the 1A ZPM of the QC itself. However, these EU/t values are directly proportional to overclock and overvolt which should increase as you unlock better circuits. Be sure to turn on the QC before the research station and only when you need it.

[note]EU/t = 131,072 * overclock * overvolt * (racks + 1)[/note]

To get you started, craft 4x APU (Tier 3) and 4x Cooling Cores. Place two of each component in the two racks and the QC will safely produce 1,920 computation/s for just 393,215 EU/t (0.75A UV) which is more than enough for researching the Wetware Mainframe. If you would like to further increase the computation/s at the cost of power, adjust the overclock and overvolt values to 1.44 and 1.03, respectively. This will generate 2,526 computation/s at a cost of 583,217 EU/t (1.11A UV).

If the APUs are too bothersome to craft, try Crystal Mainframes instead but you will need different overclock and overvolt values to get the most out of them. That goes for every unique combination of circuits and vents.

2700.name: LSC = MC^2
2700.desc: This is the final upgrade to the LSC when it comes to storage. You can use either the Ultimate or Really Ultimate batteries to craft it, it costs about the same either way.

2701.name: Chaotic Energy
2701.desc: Finally, Infinite RF! ...But what can I even use that for?

2702.name: Tier 7 Control Computer
2702.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

2703.name: Extra Large Fuel Canisters
2703.desc: Every rocket needs fuel storage canisters. For the tier 7 rocket you need to craft two large and two extra large canisters.

2704.name: Booster Tier 3
2704.desc: The distance you need to travel is much larger now, so the rocket needs bigger boosters to reach it.

2705.name: Heavy Duty Rocket Engine Tier 4
2705.desc: Every rocket needs a rocket engine. A heavy one tier 3 is needed in the tier 7 and 8 rockets.

2706.name: Heavy Duty Rocket Fins Tier 4
2706.desc: Every rocket needs a few rocket fins. Tier 7 rockets need heavy ones Tier 4.

2707.name: Heavy Duty Nose Cone Tier 4
2707.desc: Every rocket needs a nose cone. Tier 7 rocket needs a heavy nose cone tier 4.

2708.name: Pluto Dungeon
2708.desc:
Go get the final schematic, for the tier 8 rocket. You can also find the draconic core schematic here!

This will open a lot of planets outside of our solar system such as Barnarda.

2709.name: Booster Tier 4
2709.desc: The distance to other solar systems is quite literally Astronomical, so you'll need even bigger boosters to reach them.

2710.name: Tier 8 Control Computer
2710.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

2711.name: Fluids for Copying Cultures
2711.desc:
Ok, so let's say you've made some kind of culture, but you want more of it? You only need one per Vat, but they're consumed when making DNA Samples, so you should probably learn how.

To start off, you'll need to get an eColi Culture.

Use that to make the eColi Bacteria Fluid. [note](I know the name is completely different, deal with it)[/note]

Centrifuge that to make Liquid DNA.

And combine it with Radon in the Mixer to make FluorecentdDNA.

eColi Bacteria Fluid can also be Centrifuged for Plasma Membranes (10% chance) and EnzymesSollution, so do that as well.

And then Centrifuge the EnzymesSollution again for Polymerase.

You should expect to need a significant amount of eColi Bacteria Fluid if you plan on copying a lot of cultures, or making the hard to make ones.

[note]To search for items related to copying cultures, search for 'culture', 'dna sample', or 'plasmid'. You can't do this for the Data Orbs, you have to check the uses of DNA Samples.[/note]

A bunch of the fluids have incorrect spelling, don't blame me for it.

2712.name: DNA Samples for Copying Cultures
2712.desc: To copy cultures, you'll need to make a DNA sample first. Many of these have low chances, but this one's 100%. I'm sure you can figure it out yourself, but keep in mind that the Modules are not consumed, and that you may wish to use one of the IC2 Crops for the Detergent materials.

2713.name: Data Orbs for Copying Cultures
2713.desc:
After getting your sample you need to encode the information to a Data Orb. Again, many times you'll have to do it repeatedly (the Data Orb is given back if it fails), but in this case it's 100%.

[note]Look at the uses of the eColi DNA Sample to see the recipe.[/note]

2714.name: Actually Copying Cultures
2714.desc:
To copy a culture, you'll need
1) A Sterilized Petri Dish
2) A Plasma Membrane (made from Stemcells, or gotten while making EnzymesSollution)
3) Stemcells
4) A Data Orb with the appropriate data on it
5) Liquid DNA
6) A UV Bio Lab
7) A Clonal Cellular Synthesis Module

You can change out the Data Orb for one with whatever culture you wish to copy. Keep in mind that it might be easier to make a second culture the normal way, and that there's generally an alternative recipe that uses the fluid it produces to make it with a higher chance.

PS: This recipe is the only one in NEI that uses this module, so you can find it if you look, but the number will change, so I can't specify which page.

2715.name: General Purpose Fermenting Bacteria
2715.desc:
So if you looked in NEI and tried to figure out how to make this, you were probably confused by the fact that you use the fluid it produces to make it. This is where NEI is lying to you. Like the previous recipe, NEI doesn't show it correctly (they should be around the same page in NEI, they both also appear to produce Sterilized Petri Dishes, (even if that's not true) and both use unique Modules).

In order to make this, you'll need to make the Rotten Flesh Bacteria culture, get the DNA Sample, and then get the Data Orb. It changes its name to beta-Lactamase, that's normal.

Then, do the same thing for Common Yeast (get the right Yeast!).

Then you need to use the recipe to combine the information from the Data Orbs and get the Common Yeast Plasmid from that.

Lastly, use the Rotten Flesh Bacteria culture, the Common Yeast Plasmid, Penicillin (make it by centrifuging Common Yeast Fluid), and Distilled Water. You want the Transformation Module for this. It's a 100% chance.

You'll want to use the normal recipe to make more cultures, since it also becomes beta-Lactamase once you turn it into a DNA Sample.

2716.name: Longbow of the Heavens
2716.desc: Weak compared to both weapons of its tier, and the weapons it's made of, this bow doesn't use arrows, never breaks, does 10 damage, and has an AoE of 10 more arrows that do 10 damage each fly down each time it strikes.

2717.name: Xenoxene Xenoxsis
2717.desc:
To make the next tier plastic, Radox, or the next tier of SMDs, you will need Xenoxene.

Getting the Xenoxene Xenoxsis culture is a bit tricky. The recipe is similar to the General Purpose one. Remember that all recipes in this chain are low probability recipes.

Your first step is to make the Barnardafis culture, then the DNA Sample, and then the Data Orb. If you didn't grab the saplings and dirt previously (to grow more trees for logs), head back to Barnarda C.

Use the same recipe as for the General Purpose one, but use the Data Orb for Barnardafis instead of Common Yeast. You should get the Barnardafis Plasmid.

Again, it's the same recipe for the next step, but with the TCetiEis Fucus Serratus culture and the Barnardafis Plasmid, this should produce the Xenoxene Xenoxsis culture. It's a 7.5% chance.

You should probably use the normal alternative recipe to make more cultures, since it's easy to fail copying it too.

You should be able to figure out yourself how to make Xenoxene once you have the culture.

[note]After many years, the transformation recipe now even has NEI support![/note]

2718.name: Does Anyone Even Still Use These?
2718.desc: If you do, they're now really good. I guess. They sound like it at least?

2719.name: For All the Artists Out There
2719.desc: These are the Creative components. Depending on what it is, there's also T2 and T3 components too.

2720.name: Too Many Leaves
2720.desc:
Want to clear out shrubbery but don't want to pull out leaves by hand? Need to find that last log hidden in the middle of a bunch of leaves?

Try an Iron Scythe! When used, it clears out a 5x5x5 area of leaves, crops, flowers, or grass. Excellent for tearing through Sacred Oak leaves or dense shrubbery in certain biomes. Later when you have a Cutting Machine you can upgrade to a Diamond Scythe with a 7x7x7 area of effect, or use Amethyst for 9x9x9 to FLEX.

The lower tiers break leaves instantly but don't seem to have the 3x3x3 range for some reason.

2721.name: Personally, I Prefer Pepsi™
2721.desc:
You may have noticed that you can make more than just Charcoal in a Coke Oven, you can also make Coke.

You want to save all of your Coke for making Steel in your Bricked Blast Furnace (BBF) later on. As for everything else, check NEI for the uses of each Coal variant, since there are many.

[note]Once you get to the Steam Age, you can compress your Sugar and Cactus Coke for higher efficiency just like regular Coal and Charcoal.[/note]

2722.name: Water, Fast
2722.desc:
The Remote IO Water Reservoir works like the Everfull Urn in that you can pull water out as fast as you need. Additionally, you can pull water from any side of the block.

You can further upgrade it into a reservoir hatch, which can be used as an input hatch directly. Producing massive amounts of distilled water with a DT has never been easier!

Maybe those massive amounts of water could be condensed into something useful...

2723.name: Super Large Oxygen Tanks
2723.desc: The Super Heavy oxygen tank is an oxygen storage tank that can hold up to 8000 units of air.

2724.name: Ultra Large Oxygen Tanks
2724.desc: The Ultra Heavy oxygen tank is an oxygen storage tank that can hold up to 16000 units of air.

2725.name: Barley Time
2725.desc: It's time to plant some barley to make some bread. If you can't find wheat, barley is a good alternative source to make flour.

2726.name: Ichor Core
2726.desc: You can now make the best wand core, so go ahead and do so.

2727.name: Ichor Caps
2727.desc: These tie with Shadowmetal, but they look nicer.

2728.name: Best Wand
2728.desc: This is the best non-endgame/creative wand you can make. There's also a Scepter version.

2729.name: Genetically Modifying Your Plants
2729.desc:
There are 3 machines crucial to doing this. You'll also need bucketloads of power, and the same (literally) of UUM.

All 3 can only work on crops up to a certain tier, determined by the tier of the machine you made and the crop you're working with. Basically, it's (crop tier+2)/2, rounded up. So an LV machine can work on T1 crops, but an MV one can work on T3.

[note]LV=T1, MV=T3, HV=T5, EV=T7, IV=T9, LuV=T11, ZPM=T13, UV=T15, UHV=T17.[/note]

First is the Crop Gene Extractor:
It takes an existing crop seedbag, an empty Data Orb (See EV quests), and 1A while running. Use a circuit to determine what stat to copy to the Data Orb. You'll probably want either 23-31-0, or 31-31-0 for max speed and output, since you won't be breeding from these. Remember to take proper precautions if going over 23, as they will attempt to weed everything around them. There's a significant number of crops, so you'll probably need a ton of Orbs too, unless you want to reuse them. [warn]Remember to keep a Data Orb or seedbag backup![/warn]

Second is the Crop Synthesizer:
It takes 4 Data Orbs, one for each of the types from the CGE, 1000L of UUM per crop tier/2 (this is not rounded), and 16A while running. It will create a new crop seedbag with the desired stats.

Lastly is the Crop Replicator:
It takes an existing crop seedbag, 1000L per crop tier of UUM, and 6A while running. After finishing, it gives you back two of the same seedbag, duplicating it. This takes twice as much UUM, but less power, it's up to you whether that's worth it.

2730.name: Lasers Solve Everything
2730.desc:
Laser hatches are a high-end way of sending lots of amps of power, so much in fact, that it starts at 256A. This goes 1024/4096/16384+ and continues from there. They're also so powerful that only a few machines support them outside of the ever wonderful TecTech: LSC, MBF, MVF, and PSS. Remember to paint the Laser Vacuum Pipes, the Source Hatch, and the Target Hatch. Also, Source = Output, Target = Input. Laser Vacuum Pipes do not split or bend, to turn your laser use [note]Active Transformer[/note]. Later on you can use Laser Vacuum Mirror to bend your Laser Vacuum Pipes, this will however not allow splitting.

[note]Laser hatches can have their operational amperage adjusted in their GUI.[/note]

[warn]Be careful with using very high amp hatches as every Laser Hatch has 1EU/s * max amps passive power loss. For example, a 256A Laser Source Hatch and 256A Laser Target Hatch will both lose 256 EU/s regardless of the amount of power flowing through.[/warn]

2731.name: Transform and...Stay Put Actually
2731.desc:
Active Transformers will transform power up or down at only a 0.004% power loss, making using several in a row obsolete, if you can afford it. Unfortunately, it's kinda expensive. On the other hand, you can use it to turn your lasers from your laser hatches, which is probably a better use.

It doesn't need a Maintenance Hatch, just the I/O Hatches.

2732.name: Take charge!
2732.desc:
The Lapotronic Supercapacitor is your best option for massive power storage.

It can have maintenance issues which increase its power loss by 20% more each, so try not to let that slide. Though at most it's still only 2.2%

For the first, smallest one, you'll need the following, plus an Energy Hatch and a Dynamo. Remember that you can get your Lapos back if you run the capacitor through an unpackager. Finally a reason to make one!

You can use Laser Hatches, that can output very high amperage. You need at least UV Glass to use them.

Your LSC can hold between 300 MEU and 25.38 GEU using only the EV capacitors. Remember that you need more than half the blocks to be non-empty capacitors for it to be valid.

2733.name: Lapotronic Capacitor (LuV)
2733.desc: It holds more than the previous tier, other than that there is no difference. Your LSC can hold between 54B and 810B EU with these.

2734.name: Lapotronic Capacitor (ZPM)
2734.desc:
It holds more than the previous tier, other than that there is no difference. Your LSC can hold between 540B and 8.1T EU with these.

[note]Requires EV-tier Glass.[/note]

2735.name: Lapotronic Capacitor (UV)
2735.desc:
It holds more than the previous tier, other than that there is no difference. Your LSC can hold between 5.4T and 81T EU with these.

[note]Requires IV-tier Glass.[/note]

2736.name: Hangover From All That Glue?
2736.desc: Surface-mount devices are more advanced circuits parts. They're needed for the EV tier+ circuit line and are cheaper to craft than the basic versions, if you have the right materials. You need an MV Assembler and some molten polyethylene. For now, resistors are probably the only ones you can make with any ease.

2737.name: More Food, More Hearts
2737.desc:
So, food is great, right? But what's even greater is eating tons of it! Too boring and pointless you say? Well, what if I told you, that you can get extra hearts just by expanding your palate. You get 1 point for every half shank of hunger from a food eaten, and after 50 points, you get 1 extra heart! Now you have a reason to make all those Pam's foods. Each food contributes its shank-value once. Eating something from a lunch box counts as if you took it out and ate it manually.

If you want to see what foods you've eaten, you can use the Food Journal, but it's clunky. Realistically, you want to use NEI to both see foods eaten and not eaten. You can search for 'not yet eaten' to see foods left, and 'assisted' to see foods eaten. You need to have started diminishing returns first to see this.

If it doesn't work, go to NEI options at bottom-left when the inventory is open, then click on Inventory, and change Search Mode to Regex. Then restart your client and try searching again.

There are also two commands:

/foodlist size
Tells you the overall progress, and the progress to the next heart.

/foodlist sync <PlayerName>
Sync your food progression with the server if it gets out of sync.

2738.name: Ironically, It's Not Used in AE2 Anymore
2738.desc:
Need some Sky Stone, but it's all picked over? Buy some!

[note]PS: The presses are craftable at EV.[/note]

2739.name: Fire is Cool
2739.desc:
Want some more of those fire jets or smoking blocks from TF? Buy some!

[note]You can also make them into redstone controlled versions too![/note]

2740.name: Dragon Warrior
2740.desc: Did your Dragon Heart drop into the void somehow? Or maybe you just want more? Buy one!

2741.name: T1 BM Slates
2741.desc: Finally, a use for those bloody coins! Buy some slates!

2742.name: T2 BM Slates
2742.desc: Finally, a use for those bloody coins! Buy some slates!

2743.name: T3 BM Slates
2743.desc: Finally, a use for those bloody coins! Buy some slates!

2744.name: NASA Workbench
2744.desc:
The NASA Workbench is used to craft your rockets and other related vehicles. Build it to start your journey into space.

[note]Hint: Check out the nether star recipe which uses null catalyst and salis mundus. Null catalyst can be duplicated by adding magma cream, blaze powder and a tiny pile of nether star dust.[/note]

[note]Also, if you're having trouble getting the wither skulls, you can make a Cleaver or the Skullfire Sword to help with that.[/note]

[warn]For ALL Rocket Quests: If you forget to complete a part quest before crafting the rocket, you can simply hold the completed rocket and those quests will autocomplete, no need to make duplicate parts.[/warn]

2745.name: A Backpack for Bees
2745.desc: It's exactly what it says. It's also incredibly useful.

2746.name: A Chest for...Butterflies? Really?
2746.desc: Store your completely useless butterflies in this.

2747.name: A Bag for Butterflies, yawn
2747.desc: I'm not sure this has any utility value since the butterflies are going to all be in chunkloaded areas, and thus near your base, but feel free to make it anyway.

2748.name: Only the Best: Tree Breeding
2748.desc: For those who love Forestry trees.

2749.name: Only the Worst: Trees Suck!
2749.desc: For those who don't like flowers or trees :(.

2750.name: Proteus Dungeon
2750.desc:
Go get the schematics for tier 7 rockets.

This will open the planet Pluto and the Kuiper Belt.

2751.name: Tier 6 Control Computer
2751.desc: Each rocket needs a Control Computer, appropriate to the tier of the rocket.

2752.name: Enceladus Dungeon
2752.desc:
Go get the schematic for tier 6 rockets.

This will open the Moons from Neptune.

2753.name: Short Guide - A Basic AE2 System
2753.desc:
So you've unlocked AE2, but don't know how to use it? Not to worry, that's what this questline is for. For a basic (and not completely useless) ME system, make the items on the right first. After you go through the quests to get them normally of course.

[note]You could technically make one with an ME Chest, but there's no advantage over using a regular chest.[/note]

Connect power to the Energy Acceptor, place the ME Drive next to it, place a Fluix Cable on top of the ME Drive connect the ME Terminal to the cable. Just put in the Storage cells, and you're done! Remember you need a Network Tool to see how much power it actually uses. Also, it lets you pick up stuff easier.

To save power, let's do a little extra work. Place the Pressure Plate two spaces in front of the ME Terminal, place redstone next to it and beside the Energy Acceptor. Remove the EA and place a Fluix Cable there, and then place the ME Toggle Bus between the Drive and where the Energy Acceptor will be. Then plunk that back down and re-power it. This will cause it to only connect (and thus use power) when you stand on the plate. This tiny network uses about 38 EU by itself, so you should understand that an ME system is not cheap either in materials or power.

You should make either 1k or 4k storage cells to put into your ME Drive.

2754.name: Short Guide - External Storage
2754.desc:
Drawers are great, right? Well, let's connect your ME system to a bank of them. Set up some drawers nearby with a controller attached. Then place an ME Storage Bus against the controller and set the priority to +1. This will prioritize I/O to the drawer controller. Lock your drawers before they fill with random stuff. Now you have access to the whole thing! Great for the results of mining, and anything else you have thousands of, while keeping your ME system relatively empty.

You can also connect to chests and other things if you want. You can use an Ender Chest to send stuff into your network, which will then send it to the drawers, but you'll have stuff entering and exiting constantly, so it'll mess up your view in the Terminal. You can use filtered view cells, or process them before they go into your ME network (or in a different one) and/or send them to the DC directly via another EC. It depends on what you want to do and what it is.

[warn]Using the drawer controller with large amounts of I/O will tank TPS! If you're on a server, do not use drawer cubes, or expect the possibility of it being deleted or you getting banned.[/warn]

2755.name: Short Guide - Autocrafting
2755.desc:
This is the main purpose of AE2, so let's get to it. Plunk down the Crafting Storage and Molecular Assembler, connect the Pattern Terminal to a cable, and attach the ME Interface to the MA (make sure it has power from another side). You could run it without a ME Interface, but you can only do one recipe then. Place a Blank Pattern in the Pattern Terminal, and add the recipe for a Crafting Table, then click the arrow to save it to the pattern. Put this in the ME Interface and you can now request a craft of it. (if the items are in the network, otherwise it'll fail obviously)

For processing patterns (machines, anything not regular 3x3 crafting) click the button on the right. You'll need to specify all I/O for it yourself. You can use this on an ME Interface on a furnace (for example), combined with an ME Import Bus to collect the results. If it's a machine that auto-ejects (like multis) you can use a ME Interface to collect the results much faster (and with less lag).

2756.name: Short Guide - P2P
2756.desc:
One of the more complicated parts of AE2, you'll need to understand this to really get anywhere with the mod. First, replace your EA with an ME Controller. Now you have a bunch more channels to work with. Place the P2P Bus against one of the sides and then connect some cables to it to a place a bit away. Make sure to send power to this cable with an EA or Quartz Fiber, but NOT a normal cable, this is now a separate network, and that would cancel that. Connect the second P2P Bus to the other end, and then place at least 1 Dense Cable coming off of it. Shift-right-click your 1st P2P Bus, then right-click the 2nd. They're now connected, and there are 2 channels being used on your subnetwork, while you have the full 32 from the side of your Controller to use. For each subnetwork that doesn't have an ME Controller, you can send 4 sides, or 128 channels. I'm sure you can understand how useful this is, right?

There's lots more to understand about AE2, but you should probably either experiment, read the quests, or watch a video for more than the basics.

2757.name: Cool Guys Don't Look at Explosions
2757.desc:
They automate them instead.

[note]This multi is IV, but uses platline mats and ZPM circuits.[/note]

2758.name: Let me fabricate a new universe...
2758.desc:
This multi is dedicated to producing UUM and Scrap. It is one of the hardest GT++ multis to craft, but you will be able to replicate many things if you get it working.

[note]This multi is traditionally UV but can technically be made in ZPM.[/note]

2759.name: I Want SOLIDS not LIQUIDS!
2759.desc:
For when your ABS isn't the right machine. Also uses coils.

Note that this is a multiblock version of the alloy smelter, not a solid version of the alloy blast smelter.

2760.name: What Even is This?
2760.desc:
In addition to being a dehydrator multi, it's also a Vacuum Furnace, for which no singleblock exists. It's supposed to be used with the IsaMill Grinding Machine multi and the Flotation Cell Regulator multi for the purposes of ore milling, an advanced method of ore processing that needs a lot of infrastructure, but which gives many new outputs for each ore it supports.

[note]This multi is LuV now![/note]

2761.name: Do I Really Need to Make This?
2761.desc:
Oh wait, yes, you actually need this now! That was supposed to be a rhetorical question!

The main thing you will probably get out of it is the Quantum Anomaly.

[note]This multi is LuV. But you don't need it until UHV.[/note]

2762.name: This is Not How You Get Into Space
2762.desc:
This produces power out of Rocket Fuels. To see how to make them, look at the Space Race tab. These are specifically GT++ Rocket Fuels, and automating them for powergen in this multi will also provide you with endless fuel for your space travels.

The math regarding the Rocketdyne is complicated. Looking at the controller's tooltip helps, but the main gist of it is like this: provide Carbon Dioxide and make sure it doesn't run out, choose the EU/t you want to get, calculate the amount of fuel you need for the fuel you will use, and then provide enough Liquid Hydrogen to boost the multi, according to the EU/t value.

You will always want to have the Rocketdyne boosted, for several reasons. Assuming you have at least a buffered IV Dynamo Hatch, outputting close to the maximum on that hatch will lower the fuel efficiency, but not if it's boosted. Additionally, this multi doesn't consume fuel continuously, but with intervals of 1 second between consuming and not consuming, while generating power all the same. Boosting this consumption results in huge fuel efficiency, combined with the high fuel values of the Rocket Fuels.

2764.name: Steamy Mincer
2764.desc:
This is an upgraded version of your trusty ol' Steam Macerator. As a 3x3 Multiblock, it might seem expensive if you're in the Steam Age. However, the processing time per ore is so low that you can sustain your beginner's ore processing system with it!

The main reason to switch from this is the HV Macerator, which unlocks a slot for additional byproducts gained from the macerating step of ore processing. The Steam Grinder, in comparison, will only return the first item shown in the recipe output. However, this will do just fine until HV!

The Steam Grinder can only process recipes up to LV Tier.

2765.name: TFFT
2765.desc:
The TFFT is very useful if you don't want to have a bunch of Super Tanks, it also has a modular structure so it can be up to x4.33 times bigger than numbers in this quest.

With the tier I storage field block, it consumes 27 EU/t but only has about 0.27x the storage capacity of a Super Tank I per fluid.

With the tier V storage field block it consumes 1134 EU/t and has about 0.54x the storage capacity of a QT III per fluid.

With the tier X storage field block it consumes 0EU/t (only tier X uses 0) and has about x3.3 capacity of T7 YOTTank (divide by 25 to get per 1 fluid)

You can use the multi hatch to access all the fluids at once with an ME Fluid Storage Bus or Fluid Ender Conduit.

[note]Remember to turn auto-voiding on if you need it.[/note]

2766.name: Deep Earth Heating Pump
2766.desc:
With the power of geothermal energy, the Deep Earth Heating Pump can heat up Coolant or boil Distilled Water to create an energy source from the Earth's core.

By using a Heat Exchanger, you can convert the Hot Coolant into steam and use it in your turbines.

Alternatively, you can make it output Superheated Steam directly, but only at 66% of the efficiency the hot coolant can achieve

2767.name: Get it Together Bees!
2767.desc: These frames help prevent genetic decay.

3064.name: Leach The Concentrate
3064.desc: Here you have two options: Either you can mix some water with your nitrated mixture for relatively low yield, or you can mix it with the cerium-containing mixture you get from ores for double the amount.

3065.name: A Sift, a Sift! My Kingdom for a Sift!
3065.desc: Now to filter the leached concentrate in order to get the cerium out.

3066.name: The Start of Cerium
3066.desc:
This line will guide you through obtaining elemental cerium.

First, react your cerium dioxide with ammonium chloride for some cerium chloride.

3067.name: Compounding With Oxalic Acid
3067.desc: Combining your cerium chloride with oxalic acid yields you cerium oxalate. This can then be heated (in your EBF(s), of course) to get another cerium oxide.

3024.name: Maxed Oil Cracker
3024.desc: How many oils have you cracked at this point? No matter the number, you can do it cheaper. Once you reach HSS-G coils, you will have achieved the maximum EU/t discount of 50% on your Oil Crackers, and enjoy the full array of benefits that they can provide you. Don't forget to overclock them if they're running slow, the energy cost is minimal at this point.

3025.name: Super Fuel At The Top
3025.desc:
I was kidding, there's one more step in Oil, but it isn't really a better fuel, it's just the Tungstensteel Boiler.

This multi operates in the same fashion as the Titanium one. It needs a Super Fuel, it produces Superheated Steam and it makes you build a bunch of Steam Turbines to actually get power out of the fuel it consumes. The output energy of a single one of these boilers, at 100% efficiency, is 16k EU/t, so you'll need many turbines per boiler. You can build them like that, if you wish to, but at this point the Loose mode on those turbines is really useful. The efficiency hit is big, yes, but the Tungstensteel Boiler is 300% as efficient as the Bronze one, and a single Magic Solid Super Fuel burns for 4,5M EU at 100% turbine efficiency. Look at the amount of CBD you need to make one super fuel, and then you can decide if you want to tackle this super finale.

Yes, it's the end, basically. There are a couple of things you can still do, but I'll give you this as an award for having pushed through essentially all the Oil content in the pack. It has been a long journey, and I hope that you found it worthwhile and enjoyable.

3026.name: Breaking the Mold
3026.desc:
Welcome, o' Baron of Oil. For you who has completed the 5 questlines of oil processing, a new chapter awaits you.

The following quests are not guidelines on how to work with oil. In reality, they are meant for tiers where you're not supposed to be using this kind of power anymore. However, if you do want to continue it, and push it as far as it can go, the following are a few challenges that you can take on, mostly for bragging rights.

All these challenges are for the tiers after IV. Some of them are for LuV, others need further progression. Good luck!

3027.name: Ultimate Distillation
3027.desc:
The Dangote Distillus is not enough for the goals of an oil baron. You might have heard about "mega" multiblocks, namely the Mega EBF, but did you know that a Distillation Tower equivalent exists?

It does the same thing as the Dangote, parallels, but it does 256 at a time, and is much, much bigger than anything you've built so far. My challenge to you is to build a max-height, full-sized Mega Distillation Tower, and come out of your efforts with the biggest multiblock that the pack has to offer. You will need more, much more casings than what the quest asks for, but your inventory is not big enough to hold the full amount that will need to build this monstrosity.

3028.name: Hyperheated Steam
3028.desc:
If there is someone who found themselves invested in the Large Tungstensteel Boilers, this one is for you. Through the pollution, the multiblock spamming and the chained processing lines you need to get here, you will find that this is a lot of heat for one boiler to handle. Why not make two quad boilers?

You might be thinking about the number of turbine multis you need to withstand this, but fret not! There are extra large turbines, for the extra large steam enthusiast. You will need twelve turbine items for each multi, and you best bring the best ones you can make! Loose mode is up to you, just go wild with your super fuel.

[warn]You will need an Assembly Line to craft the dynamo needed to complete this quest.[/warn]

3029.name: Universal Fuel Power
3029.desc:
This is the most powerful combustion engine that has ever been built. In fact, it's not only that, but also a Gas Turbine and a Rocket Fuel Generator!

The Universal Chemical Fuel Engine burns fuel with some additives, as did its predecessors, but it runs at a lower efficiency. The 150% value is a mathematical limit, you cannot reach it without supplying infinite fuel, but this engine compensates by running as fast as fuel is input into it, without the maximum EU/t limitation the others had. The only two things you have to make sure of are that you supply enough Combustion Promoter to keep efficiency up (I'll let you figure the best ratio on your own, but 1:4 promoter:fuel is decent), and that the dynamo can support the EU/t that the generator is outputting.

[warn]You will need an Assembly Line to craft the dynamo needed to complete this quest. You better burn enough HOG to make it worth![/warn]

3030.name: Expert MB-Construction
3030.desc:
Right-click the controller while sneaking will invoke an action called survival autoplace. This will attempt to build the multiblock with items from your inventory, provided the area this multi occupies is clear of obstacles. Again, like hologram spawning, multiblocks that accept multiple types of tiered components or has variable length, the number of MSHPs you hold in hand will decide which tier of blocks will be placed. It will place correct amount of hatches for you, but will usually orient the hatches towards you, not perpendicular to the surface. It will just place casings for you if you don't have any hatches in inventory, unless this spot requires the use of a hatch (e.g. the top center spot of EBF).

Survival autoplace can also take from your alternative inventories, or items that contain/link to an inventory. Currently, this includes:
- adventure backpacks, either worn or as an item in inventory
- AE2 portable cells
- ME networks connected via any AE2 wireless terminal with item access capabilities
- Forestry backpacks
- ender chest inventory, if you have the Ender Backpack from Backpacks mod. Note: ordinary backpacks from the Backpacks mod are NOT supported at the moment.

3031.name: You Are Not Prepared!!!
3031.desc:
It's time for something really big: A smeltery! There's a lot to craft, so better gather some resources... or you could go try complete this with the help of some unhappy NPC friends, but that would void your quest rewards.

[note]Note: This quest is mutually exclusive with the other smeltery quest.[/note]

3037.name: Space Pumping
3037.desc:
At some point you may have wondered: There are these enormous gas planets in the solar system - could there be a way to "harvest" them? Indeed there is. By constructing the "Planetary Gas Siphon" multiblock, you are able to pump from the infinite supply of gas found on Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. You just need to build a space station around a gas planet to get close enough to reach the gas without falling into it.

Different gases can be extracted by pumping at different depths which is controlled by a Programmed Circuit. You need to supply (64 * depth) Mining Pipes to reach the selected depth.

While operational, the multiblock needs d*4^(T+2) EU/t where T is the tier of the space station and d is the depth.

You can find an overview of what you can pump and where in the Tips and Tricks chapter.

3038.name: UHT Resistant Mesh
3038.desc:
*Slaps UHT Resistant Mesh* This bad boy can withstand so much heat!

You should probably automate this if you want to build and use the Dyson Swarm multiblock.

3039.name: Dyson Swarm Ground Unit
3039.desc:
The Dyson Swarm Ground Unit multiblock is basically a solar panel, you just insert Dyson Swarm Modules (how many depends on how much energy you want) and get energy out. There are some complications though:
- Each hour, n of m modules are destroyed according to this formula: n = m (2 * 0.066) / (exp(-0.00005 * (m - 1))+exp(0.00003 * cps)), where cps is computation per second,
- Needs 3.6M L/h of Gelid Cryotheum.
- Up to 10,000 modules can be inserted into a single Dyson Swarm.
- Dyson Swarm can only accept up to 100k computation.

[note]Note: TecTech's Multi-Amp Dynamo Hatches and Laser Source Hatches are supported.[/note]

The amount of each part on the right is only the minimum, depending on how many Dynamo Hatches, Input Buses, Input Hatches and Optical Reception Connectors you use, you may need more Energy Receiver Base Casings, Module Deployment Unit Base Casings or Control Center Base Casings.

[warn]This machine will explode if Dynamo buffer is full![/warn]

Fun fact: The maximum output is 337B EU/t (limited by 10,000 Modules and the 3.37x dimension bonus of Horus)

(If you don't want to calculate how many modules you need per hour, the number is 64 for max stats)

3040.name: Where Do I Build the Dyson Swarm?
3040.desc:
If you read the Dyson Swarm Ground Unit's tooltip carefully (which you should), you might have noticed, that it mentions a "dimension-dependent factor". The dimension you build the Dyson Swarm multiblock in can greatly affect the real power output.

Sorted by factor (descending):
337% - Horus
231% - Barnarda C
228% - Neper
228% - α Centauri Bb
211% - Mehen belt
198% - Maahes
198% - Seth
198% - Vega B
194% - Venus Space Stations
181% - Anubis
176% - Venus
161% - Mercury
141% - Barnarda E
134% - T Ceti E
126% - Barnarda F
112% - Ross128b, Ross128ba
110% - Earth Space Stations
100% - Overworld, Moon
[warn]89% - Mars Space Stations
81% - Mars, Phobos, Deimos
61% - Asteroids
60% - Ceres
48% - Jupiter Space Stations
44% - Europa, Io, Ganymede
36% - Saturn Space Stations
32% - Enceladus, Titan, Callisto
25% - Uranus Space Stations
23% - Oberon, Proteus, Miranda
20% - Neptune Space Stations
18% - Triton
16% - Pluto, Kuiper Belt
15% - Makemake, Haumea
1% - Garden, Mining and Void Worlds
0% - All other dimensions[/warn]

3041.name: Getting Muddy With Monazite
3041.desc:
Pretty soon you're going to need lanthanide elements. A lot of them.
They're not that easy to separate, however...

Craft up a Digester to unlock the questline in the IV tab.

Note: This multiblock will not function correctly without a muffler hatch

3042.name: Dissolution Tank
3042.desc:
Basically a mixing machine purposely made for dilution.

You'll need to take care in providing the inputs at the required ratios, else you'll get no output.

Anyway, this plays a large part in the lanthanide line so craft it up.

3043.name: Monazite
3043.desc:
Welcome to lanthanide processing!
It'll be a fun journey, whether you like it or not :P

First off get some monazite. This is only to get you started,
as you're going to need a ton to truly reap the rewards
of this line.

You'll then need to combine it with nitric acid in the Digester multiblock,
to get its mud form.
Yes, this is a very nitric acid hungry process!

[note]To skip all of the Monazite quests hold a stack of each of these in your hand:

- Zirconium Dust / Ingot
- Hafnia Ingot
- Cerium Dust / Ingot
- Samarium Dust / Ingot[/note]

3044.name: Watery Mud
3044.desc:
That mud on its own is far too concentrated, so you'll need to dilute it with water in the Dissolution Tank.

Fun fact, the saltpeter in the recipe was only added due to a problem with recipe search!

You also get some Zirconium and Hafnium from this. You'll probably need both later on, so definitely don't void any.

3045.name: Zirconia-Hafnia Blend
3045.desc:
This process is a little involved so I'll guide you through it. First, centrifuge the dust you got from the dissolution tank.

Because Hafnium and Zirconium are refractory metals, like Titanium and Zirconium,
they have very similar process to it and each other.

3046.name: Sifting the Goodness Out
3046.desc:
Yes, you're seeing right. A fluid sifting recipe! A lot of the materials here won't be good sources in any way, but they're literally better than nothing.

Monazite Sulfate isn't an actual compound btw, just a term for a collection of rare earth sulfates and other things.

I'll only ask for the primary output as who wants to worry about chances?

3047.name: Hafnium Line
3047.desc:
There is a key difference with hafnium here, however. Follow this mini line to hear more.

First off you'll need to react your hafnia with some hydrochloric acid.

And, like with zirconium, you'll need the solution rather than the dust.

3048.name: Zirconium Line
3048.desc:
To get started, react your zirconia with some hydrochloric acid.

The dust on its own is kinda useless, though... Make it into a solution instead.

3049.name: EBF Time
3049.desc: For the next step in the process, just EBF your solution with some magnesium.

3050.name: Zirconium Ingots
3050.desc:
Just EBF your zirconium tetrachloride with some magnesium and you're done!

Zirconium is used in many different multiblock machines such as the Industrial mixer and the Alloy blast smelter that you might have already built. It's also used in the creation of High Density rods for an extra boost to your nuclear reactors.

3051.name: We Ain't Running a Farm!
3051.desc:
...What's this low-purity hafnium stuff?

Well, because you of course want your hafnium to be of the highest quality, you're going to have to do a little more than just EBF it with a little magnesium.

Go ahead and react it with some Iodine. If you're not too sure where to get Iodine, have a look at Seaweed Ash. (hint: you'll need to look at small piles)

Note that both the liquid and dust forms of iodine are optional, so you can complete the quest with one. Or none!

3052.name: Finally, Actual Hafnium
3052.desc:
Now that wasn't so hard, was it?

Hafnium is used as a component in making the Sparge tower which is useful if you ventured into the Liquid Fluorine Thorium Fluid Nuclear reactor.

You also get half of your Iodine back with this. Also, keep the runoff. It might become useful later, especially if stuff's suggested wink wink nudge nudge

3053.name: Dilution, Yet Again
3053.desc:
Those sulfates aren't going to dilute themselves!

You get more fluid per piece of monazite, what's not to like?

3054.name: Now for Nitration
3054.desc:
For the next step you'll need ammonium nitrate (again with the nitric acid!) and your diluted monazite sulfate.
You'll need to do this in an LCR.

The resulting fluid simply vanishes into the void. Best not to worry about it.

3055.name: A Little Bit of Sifting
3055.desc: Now you can sift some of the radioactive impurities out.

3056.name: This Is Probably Not Worth It
3056.desc:
You can EBF that thorium-phosphate dust to continue that chain but the yield isn't too great in the end...

Still, if you have the resources, why not?

3057.name: Chemically Bathing!
3057.desc: Yep, this really is an all-rounder line. Again you'll need some ammonium nitrate. Just another reminder for those who are yet to make a dedicated nitric acid setup :P

3058.name: Thorium and Phosphate
3058.desc: Now you can thermal centrifuge the dust to get both thorium and phosphate out. Told you the yields weren't great...

3059.name: To Sift, or Not to Sift?
3059.desc:
The real question is, who came up with all these sifting recipes?

You can get some Uranium out of this so it's not all bad.

3060.name: Uranium Filtrate
3060.desc: You can process this further with some hydrofluoric acid.

3061.name: Uranium?
3061.desc: ...And sift it to get the uranium. You do get some 235 out of it so time not entirely wasted, hopefully?

3062.name: Dry It Real Good
3062.desc:
Your concentrate is far too damp! You'll need to dry it off in the EBF.

Thankfully the recipe is far from difficult.

3063.name: Nitration Time... Again
3063.desc: You'll need to add even more nitric acid to be able to deal with the dust further.

2819.name: Toss Your Magic Junk in Here LV
2819.desc: And receive power in return. Especially good with Nether Stars if you've got a bunch. Or practically any magic material item (not crafted stuff mostly).

2820.name: Toss Your Magic Junk in Here MV

2821.name: Toss Your Magic Junk in Here HV

2822.name: Absorbing Magic Energy to Produce EU HV

2823.name: Absorbing Magic Energy to Produce EU MV

2824.name: Absorbing Magic Energy to Produce EU LV
2824.desc: These produce power by absorbing magic energy from nodes, dragon eggs, creeper eggs, etc.

2825.name: Absorbing Magic Energy to Produce EU EV

2826.name: Solid-Oxide Fuel Cell Mk II
2826.desc:
It's the same as the previous, but more/better. It takes less time to heat up, but it chews through fuel pretty fast too. It still isn't as good as an LGT for power produced over time, but it can produce power faster. As long as you use it with the HP Steam Turbine.

It can produce 1A+ of LuV if you set it up right.

2827.name: Solid-Oxide Fuel Cell Mk I
2827.desc:
It uses Gas fuels to produce power and Steam at the same time, while also generating 0 pollution. If the other generators were being problematic, this is the best solution.

It produces 4 HV amps per tick at a nice fuel efficiency, while consuming 100 L of Oxygen per second. It will heat up over time until it reaches 100% efficiency, at which point it starts outputting Steam as well. You can then use Steam Turbines, singleblocks or multiblocks, to generate even more power.

With these two things combined, it's more powerful than the Large Gas Turbine that you can build at the start. Only when you unlock better materials for that, will it generate more power than the SOFC Mk I.

2828.name: It's 'Super' Heated Steam
2828.desc: So that means it produces twice as much power! You can get this steam through the LHE, the SOFC Mk II or the Titanium/Tungstensteel Boilers. It gets converted to normal steam as the output at a 1:1 ratio, so make sure to have a non-HP one to take advantage of that. In total, an HP Steam Turbine and a regular one combined produce 3x the EU with Superheated Steam that normal Steam would produce.

2829.name: More Advanced Reactors
2829.desc:
Fluid Nukes, Vac Nukes and Wrench Reactors (lol), are more advanced versions of the normal one.

Fluid means you build it differently (with the parts on the right, it's a 5x5 cube surrounding the normal one), with the goal of producing as much hot coolant (cycle in non-hot) as possible, to use that to produce power. It doesn't produce power on its own, but more overall. Send it to the LHE to get SH steam out of it, then go through an HP steam turbine, then a normal steam turbine.

Vac nukes remove heat by sending it to coolant cells instead of coolant. Any cell in a cardinal direction will receive the heat instead of the reactor. Use a vacuum freezer to restore the Helium/NaK cells' durability.

Wrench reactors are when you fill the reactor with fuel rods, turn it on, and wrench it to reset its heat state before it explodes. If you can automate it, you deserve a medal!

If you still don't know what a MOX one is, it produces more power the higher the temperature, so you try to keep it heat neutral, and as hot as possible. Of course, this can cause things nearby to, well, melt. You use MOX or Naquadria fuel cells work for this. You can also get more Plutonium out of MOX than Uranium.

A breeder reactor is when you set up a reactor to get an item out of it rather than power, like Lutetium from Thorium, Plutonium from MOX, etc. Look at the depleted x fuel rod list in NEI or on the wiki to see more.

[note]Since Fluid Nukes are liable to explode if you run out of coolant, make sure you set up a cutoff tied to the temperature or amount of coolant with redstone.[/note]

2830.name: IV Energy Hatch
2830.desc:
You'll need this to increase the power to your multis and progress.

You need some Vanadium-Gallium to make HPIC Wafers for it, and an IV Cutting Machine if you haven't made the multi.

[note]I hope you had fun with the platline, lol.[/note]

2831.name: Covering Up
2831.desc:
This quest goes over all of the covers, just in case you forgot or they weren't mentioned. If they have their own quest, look at it for more info. You can put them on basically any GT block, pipe, machine, tank, etc. Though that doesn't consider whether it will work right or be useful. Shift-right-click will open a GUI for many of them. For covers that you can adjust the amount of, clicking the left side with a screwdriver goes down, and right goes up. All covers block rain on the side they're attached to. Generally you can adjust covers with a screwdriver, whether they have a GUI or not. You can remove them with a crowbar if you forgot.

Fluid-Related Covers:
Pump: Transfers fluids at the speed specified on the tooltip.

Fluid Regulator: Regulates the rate that fluid can go through it, as well as pumping it. Can work x/s or x/t.

Steam Valve: Moves more, but is only for steam. Only goes to IV. Does not work on SH steam.

Overflow: Voids anything over the specified amount in the tank.

Fluid Detector: Will emit a redstone signal (RS) based on fluid fill rate of the tank it's on. By default, empty=15, full=0. Use inverted mode to swap. Useful on tanks and hatches.

Ender Fluid Link: Cover version of ender tanks to TP fluid around. Doesn't connect to ender tanks though.

Drain Module: Protects from rain on that side, and will fill the tank with water from rain if it can. Useless.

Fluid Filter: Filters fluid going through it. Click with a cell to set it.

Shutter: Blocks items/fluids when active. Use it with a machine controller and RS. Does not visually disconnect/block. You place it between the pipes, and put the MC on the same pipe.

Item-Related Covers:
Conveyor: Sends items. Use an item pipe in import mode or on a GT block/machine.

Robot Arm: Sends from a specific slot in the machine it's on (internal) to another specific slot in a block next to it (adjacent). Keep going through numbers until you get it right, it seems to change per machine. If the output slot is full, it won't output. There need to be a valid block with slots to send to or the settings won't save.

Item Detector: Will emit RS based on item fill rate of the block it's on. By default, empty=0, full=15. Detects by filled slots, so doesn't work right with the super/quantum chests. Probably better for buses.

Item Filter: This one is work-in-progress that is not yet finished. Feel free to contribute.

EU-Related Covers:
Energy Detector: Will emit RS based on EU fill rate of the batbuff/tank it's on. By default, empty=0, full=15. Make sure to set it to include batteries. Can also detect steam for some reason.

Power Pass Upgrade: Read the tooltip, then ask Bart how it works, he didn't bother telling anyone. He'll probably just tell you the code is the best documentation, just like the Tectech guys. May not actually be finished/work, either.

Tesla Coil/Tesla Coil Rich Edition: Use with the Tesla Tower to send EU wirelessly or something.

Other Covers:
Player Detector: Detects players. About 10 block range. [warn]Currently owner/other is inverted on non-pipes due to some bug or another.[/warn]

Activity Detector: Detects either recipe progress or idleness. 0%=0 RS, ~95%=14 by default. In idle mode, idleness is 15, and is 0 when running by default.

Machine Controller: Turns machine on/off based on RS. Disable machine will keep it off regardless of RS state. Safe Mode only works on MBs. If a MB runs out of power, it will normally turn off, but a MC will turn it back on, continuing to void stuff. This will set it to the disable machine state instead. You will also get a notification. [warn]Safe mode is currently broken?[/warn]

Needs Maintenance: Emits RS if it meets the condition specified. Rotor=turbine?

Redstone Transmitter/Receiver (In/Out): OK, these things are confusing, so listen closely. First, set a frequency, either by using the GUI, or using the original way: right-clicking it with an item. Empty hand = 0, other items = their item ID. Be careful not to reset it to 0 by accident. Honestly, just use the GUI. You can set it to private or not, it's the same kind of thing as EnderStorage, so you don't blare RS across the server. Channel state = RS level.

RT(O) sets the channel state based on the redstone level sent from outside to the specific side. You can TP an RS from this to the RR(O).
RT(I) sets the channel state based on redstone level that would be emitted from the machine itself. [warn]Always sends 15, so it's useless.[/warn]
RR(O) outputs redstone level based on channel state. You can TP RS from the RT(O) to here.
RR(I) controls whether a machine can work based on channel state, just like a machine controller cover does. If it has 0 RS, it stays off. At least 1, and it turns on. You send the signal from elsewhere with the RT(O).
RR(I) can also be used in conjunction with other covers whose states talk about conditional.

IC2 Heat vents: Speeds up machines a tiny amount? Also looks neat. TODO: someone please look at the code to see what it actually does.

Crafting Table: Adds a crafting table to the block you can use. Mostly cosmetic.

Planks/Plates/Foils: Blocks I/O on that side. Use a screwdriver to set allowed types of stuff through. Blocks everything by default. Set the output to another side first to avoid changing its settings too. You'll probably 'use' this by accident at least once.

Computer Monitor: Adds a visual look. Cosmetic-only cover.

Lens: Cosmetic-only cover.

You can also use AE2 facades as cosmetic covers.

2832.name: Time to Kill (Bighorn Sheep)
2832.desc: Oh no, not the poor bighorn sheep! Fluffy and red, I'm so sad in the Twilight Forest.

2833.name: Time to Kill (Wild Deer)
2833.desc: Christmas is canceled this year. So sad.

2834.name: Time to Kill (Wild Boar)
2834.desc: Delicious roasted wild boar. Mmm... very tasty.

2835.name: Time to Kill (Forest Bunny)
2835.desc: Oh, how cute this little rabbit is. Pow! .... oh it's dead?

2836.name: Time to Kill (Raven)
2836.desc: Abracadabra... Give me your black feathers.

2837.name: Time to Kill (Penguin)
2837.desc: Be careful not to slip on the ice and land on a penguin.

2838.name: Time to Kill (Squirrel)
2838.desc: Mmm... yummy squirrel on a stick!

2840.name: Time to Kill (Skeleton Druid)
2840.desc: The druid skeleton is always infernal so watch out. It can be found in little huts all over the Twilight Forest.

2841.name: Time to Kill (Kobold)
2841.desc: Kobold spawners can be found in Hollow Hills. It will spawn almost anywhere that is dark within the Twilight Forest dimension and tends to spawn in large groups.

2842.name: Time to Kill (Redcap)
2842.desc: The Redcap is a goblin carries an Iron Pickaxe, wears Iron Boots, and constantly giggles to itself. They are found lurking in Hollow Hills, as well as occasionally appearing in the Goblin Knight Stronghold.

2843.name: Time to Kill (Fire Beetle)
2843.desc: The Fire Beetle is found in Hollow Hills and Labyrinths. Fire Beetles will shoot a breath of fire at nearby players, dealing 2 damage every half second the player is exposed to the fire.

2844.name: Time to Kill (Slime Beetle)
2844.desc: The Slime Beetle can be found in Hollow Hills, and Labyrinths. Slime Beetles fire slimy projectiles at players that deal about 8 points of damage on attack. They have incredible accuracy, and move extremely quickly when the player attempts to approach one.

2845.name: Time to Kill (Swarm Spider)
2845.desc: The Swarm Spider can be found in large groups inside various landmarks, such as Hollow Hills, Leaf Dungeons and Hedge Mazes. Swarm Spiders can sometimes spawn with Skeletons riding them. They have 3 health points, making them weak to almost any attack. Swarm Spiders will attempt to attack but due to their low attack, they barely inflict damage on anything.

2846.name: Time to Kill (Pinch Beetle)
2846.desc: Pinch Beetles have 20 hearts worth of health and can hit for 4 hearts of damage when grabbed. They can be found in the largest Hollow Hills, Labyrinths, and in Dark Towers. Pinch Beetles charge at you and grab your body. When in this situation you want to quickly attack and kill them. They are very fast and can be extremely dangerous when near lava, a cliff, or other enemies. Pinch beetles can suffocate you if the ceiling is too low.

2847.name: Time to Kill (Wraith)
2847.desc: The Twilight Wraith is a floating spirit and are not initially aggressive towards the player but will become angry when approached. When spotted by a Wraith it can be difficult to lose it, as they are relentless in chasing the player and its ability to phase through blocks. They are found in large Hollow Hills.

2848.name: How the Eff Do I Charge This Sh*t?
2848.desc:
Given the number of people asking about this and the incompatibility it has with so many methods, here's a quest to tell you what will work.

[note]You only need to make one.[/note]

Also keep in mind that the energy storage ones can have EU put in, if you just wanna charge, unlike the solars (also the solar wind ones don't work for some reason). The higher tier ones charge faster (advanced solars just point at the sun to get 100% power during daylight instead of only at noonish, but hybrids are indeed faster. Also hybrids don't crash the game just by placing them anymore!)

2849.name: Faster Steam Transportation
2849.desc:
Do you think that steam fluid transport is far too slow? Your LV pumps got you down, doing only 640L/s? Then it's time to make the 32 times faster LV steam valves!

They only work with steam and are just as fast as the valve on a Railcraft tank (if you put it on the bottom side).

There are also MV, HV, EV, and IV versions.

2850.name: Long Distance Item Pipeline
2850.desc:
Wouldn't it be great if you could transport ores from a far away ore vein by pipes to your base?

This is not a problem anymore. Connect your setup on the start and end point with a Long Distance Item Pipeline block. In between use the associated pipe blocks - the total distance between endpoints must be more than 64 blocks apart (4 or more chunks). There can only be one start and end point per pipeline.

To actually connect it, you need to align the start LDIP block such that the arrows are facing inwards, and the opposite side of it has a LDIPP block. Put a conveyor on the side of the block that has the arrows and set to import. It will now attempt to import stuff to the endpoint LDIP. This will go as fast as you can put items into it.

Do the opposite on the far side, except you don't need a conveyor.

Now you only need to chunkload the start and end points and the items will be "teleported" to you.

Of course, later on you'll probably use ender chests to actually teleport the ore, especially from other dims.

This setup is lag-free in the pipe blocks, so it's useful in your base as well if you want to reduce lag.

2851.name: Time to Kill (Lava Monster)
2851.desc:
They are everywhere where there is lava and they shoot at you with fireballs.
From the air with a bow and arrow or crossbow they're easy targets.

2852.name: Time to Kill (Wisp)
2852.desc: They appear everywhere in the Nether, and there are wisp spawners as well. Since they are very fast and small, it gets difficult in long-range combat.

2853.name: Time to Kill (Nitro Creeper)
2853.desc:
They are highly explosive and make holes much larger than regular creepers.

They also cause a lot more damage. So be careful!

2854.name: Time to Kill (Imp)
2854.desc:
Imps are little orange creatures who run through the whole nether. They are peaceful and they won't attack you either.

Kill a few to get their leather and build fireproof armor.

2855.name: Essentia Transmission
2855.desc: What if... you could send essentia from a different location to your infusion matrix? You could finally get rid of all those jars around your altar.

2856.name: Advanced MB Construction
2856.desc:
The ability to rotate MBs and view a structure hologram has been sto-borrowed from TecTech, giving you greater freedom to build the way you want! (until we nerf wallsharing at least)

Unlike TT, you can use a normal wrench for this, just aim at the front center of a controller (or one of the other sides if you want to. You just need to aim for the part of the grid with the mark) and right-click or shift-right-click. The first rotates it clockwise, the latter flips it. If you want to change the facing of the controller itself, do that the normal way.

Use a Multiblock Structure Hologram Projector or MSHP to visualize the structure, pay attention to the bottom left for structure details. For multiblocks that accept multiple types of tiered components or have a variable size, the number of MSHPs you hold in your hand will decide which tier is shown in the hologram.

[note]If you don't get it, try placing a controller, wrenching it, and using the MSHP on it.[/note]

While it's now possible to build a sideways EBF, or even build one into the floor, not everything can be rotated. Exceptions include: LSC, Charcoal Pile Igniter, BBF, DT, Miners, Industrial Washing Plant, Algae Farm, Bacterial Vat, DEHP, the MVF can, but the MBF cannot.

[note]PS: We did get permission, that was just a little joke.[/note]

2857.name: Magic Mirror Glass?
2857.desc: This glass seems useful for several things, including, perhaps, teleportation of items or essentia?

2858.name: Machine Interface Fluid I/O
2858.desc:
Thanks to the tireless work of the devs, you can now use cells to I/O fluids directly from the interface of GT machines. Just click on the fluid while holding a cell or other fluid container item.

Points to consider:
1) Left-click to attempt filling/emptying all fluid containers held on cursor.
2) Right-click to attempt filling/emptying one fluid container held on cursor.
3) All fluid containers that can be used in a standard gt tank (e.g. super tanks or the X voltage tanks) are supported, including but not limited to: water bottles, vanilla buckets, ic2 (universal and normal) cells, volumetric flasks, large fluid cells, pressurized fluid tanks, etc.
4) Can fill/take less than one cell (1000L or 144L if molten) of fluid if the fluid container supports doing so.
5) It won't check if it's a valid fluid for the machine/recipe, keep that in mind.
6) Doesn't work with Crops++ alcohols, feel free to code compatibility for us.
7) EIO tanks need to be placed and picked up first before they can be used.

2859.name: Combustion Engine
2859.desc:
The combustion engine combusts things for power like a car engine does. You need water in it if it's over 100 degrees, or it will explode. Don't run out of water! You turn it on with a redstone signal.

Your options for fuel are:
Oil @30RF/t for a total of 150K
Ethanol @40RF/t for a total of 360K
Diesel @60RF/t for a total of 1,500K

[note]Make sure to use Oil, not raw, light, medium, or heavy oil.[/note]

2860.name: Kinesis Pipes
2860.desc: To transfer RF power more than 1 block, you'll want some kinesis pipes. Use a wooden one first to connect to the pipe network, then other types to transfer it. That said, they seem to be lossy somewhat, so conduits are better, if more expensive. Also, you need capacitors to store the RF anyway.

2861.name: Prison Gangs are Obsolete
2861.desc:
Want lots of cobble, stone, obsidian, or another type of rock? This is the machine for you!

Cobble: Water and lava on the side.
Stone: Water on the side, lava on top.
Obsidian: Lava on top or side, Water on side, and redstone in the machine.
Netherrack: Lava on top or side, Water on side, and glowstone in the machine.
Basalt: Lava on side, soul sand below, and one packed ice in the machine.
Cobbled Deepslate: Lava on side, soul sand below, and one magma block in the machine.

You of course need to power it. If you don't want to spend power on cobble, do it the ExU way with the WIU.

You need to toggle the ghost circuit to circuit 1 or 6 depending on the recipe, check NEI.

2862.name: Coast Sequoia
2862.desc:
You can breed a Coast Sequoia by combining Mundane Larch and Bull Pine.

[warn]Fireproof Wood Get! If you care.[/warn]

2863.name: Wenge
2863.desc: You can get Wenge by combining Balsa and Cocobolo.

2864.name: Grandidier's Baobab
2864.desc:
You can get Grandidier's Baobab by combining Wenge and Balsa.

Ability to grow trees on sand, Get! Like you care.

2865.name: Giant Sequoia
2865.desc:
You can get Giant Sequoia by combining Coast Sequoia and Grandidier's Baobab.

Both Height and Girth, Get!

[note]PS: If this doesn't work (NEI says yes, internet says no) you should also be able to trade a forestry villager for it.[/note]

2866.name: Common Alder
2866.desc: You can breed a Common Alder by combining Silver Birch and Common Beech.

2867.name: Aspen
2867.desc: You can breed an Aspen by combining Common Alder and Common Beech.

2868.name: Elderberry
2868.desc: You can breed an Elderberry by combining Common Alder and Aspen.

2869.name: Raspberry
2869.desc: You can breed Raspberry by combining Hill Cherry and Elderberry.

2870.name: Pomelo
2870.desc: You can breed a Pomelo by combining Hill Cherry and Lemon.

2871.name: Manderine
2871.desc: You can get Manderine by combining Pomelo and Hill Cherry.

2872.name: Orange
2872.desc: You can get Orange by combining Pomelo and Manderine.

2873.name: Golden Raspberry
2873.desc:
You can get Golden Raspberry by combining Raspberry and Orange.

[warn]Fastest Maturity Get![/warn]

[note]That probably took forever, huh? BTW, it probably would've been faster to make more multifarms or farming stations instead.[/note]

[note]Also, you still gotta create the ultimate hybrid, too.[/note]

2874.name: Loading the Chunks
2874.desc:
Minecraft works in chunks - the 16x16 columns of blocks from bedrock to sky. The chunks in a radius around you are simulated or, in other words, chunkloaded. If a chunk is not loaded, nothing happens in it - crops don't grow, machines don't work. Thus, you want to have your base chunkloaded when you're off exploring. Later on, you want your base chunkloaded so things don't spontaneously blow up, especially multi-block machines.

You can chunkload in a few ways:

1) The ServerUtilities mod. With default settings, you will not be able to chunkload. Feel free to change that in the config if you are in singleplayer (SP) or running your own server.

2) Use four types of Anchors from Railcraft, although they are not craftable until the Low Voltage (LV) tier. In SP, or a server you run, cheat in Admin Anchors to chunkload forever, for free. They're limited on servers to prevent TPS dropping from people chunkloading a base forever when they aren't even there. On the officials, you need to use Ender Fragments, Ender Pearls, or Coins to fuel them. Look at the tooltip on each for how long it will work, and in what chunkloader.

3) Use one of the blocks that chunkloads itself. If you do this on a server, expect the admins to possibly get very mad at you if it's not something you're allowed to do. Same for vanilla methods.

Don't use chicken chunks to get anchor-like blocks. It's buggy.

2876.name: Loot Games
2876.desc:
It looks like you've found a secret room hidden...not so deep within the earth. Pretty close to the surface, actually. You can find these all over the place, and have a chance at sweet sweet loot if you're good at simon says. It counts the highest level you completed, so you can fail the other rounds on purpose to go faster if you don't think you can do better. BTW, cheaters go to minecraft hell, where all the blocks have hardness -1.

If you fail with Minesweeper, it blows up. Be sure to make room for escape!

Make sure that no water or oil is flowing into the room because of worldgen, or it will kill you and destroy your grave because of 'cheating'. This is a bug.

2877.name: Applied Greg?
2877.desc:
ME Buses are combination of technology and...other technology. Don't give me that look, it is, OK? With these, you can now directly I/O items between GregTech multiblock and ME network. They have significantly less lag, so please start using them as soon as possible on servers.

You just pop off your GT bus + ME bus or ME interface combination, and replace both with the new bus and connect a ME cable directly to it. It uses 1 channel, same as the other options.

The output bus and hatch have limited output space. If you need more, you can upgrade them either by crafting them with storage components or putting a cell inside. A digital singularity cell will make the cache near infinite.

Note that the output bus must eject each item from its cache in order. If it cannot eject the topmost item, it will be stuck and cannot eject any other items.

If voiding is turned off, the multiblock will not void when the ME Outputs are full.

[note]For usage in some machines, i.e. the advanced assembly line, the configured item must be in the first slot in order for the multi to work.[/note]

2878.name: Palladium
2878.desc: Lastly, add Formic Acid to Reprecipitated Palladium to get pure Palladium, along with other stuff you can reuse.

2879.name: Salt Can't be Stored in Water?
2879.desc: Dissolve the Rhodium Salt in Water in a mixer to get Rhodium Salt Solution.

2880.name: Sodium Nitrate
2880.desc: You need Sodium Nitrate to process your Rhodium further.

2881.name: Heat it Up!
2881.desc: You can make Hot Tetroxide Solution in the fluid heater so get on with it!

2882.name: RF Node
2882.desc: The RF Node acts as an I/O point for RF. If you need that, I guess.

2883.name: Why, Bart, Why?
2883.desc:
Take your Hot Ruthenium Tetroxide Solution and distill it in a DT to get Ruthenium Tetroxide. Also, Salt and Water.

The amounts in this step are quite annoying unfortunately. You need to run it 5 times to get whole cell amounts.

2884.name: Cool it Back Down
2884.desc: You just need to run your Ruthenium Tetroxide through a fluid solidifier in this step to, well, solidify it.

2885.name: Can You Really See Out of Those?
2885.desc:
If you take a little dip into the dark side, you can make these goggles. They give the best discount until you can make infinity armor. And who even does that?

You'll need infusion and some warping research first.

If you're a scaredy-cat you can make the Electric goggles on the armor tab instead.

2886.name: It's Called the Thaumatorium, OK?
2886.desc: This device will allow automating the crucible. Check the research to the far right of the crucible for details.

2887.name: These Items Will Not 'Be Back'
2887.desc:
One of the more useful things you can do with the pearl is to make a better Alchemical Furnace. You can melt items into essentia way faster with this.

[note]The pearl is not used up, nor does it take durability damage from this craft.[/note]

2888.desc: If you somehow manage to connect to it, you can get a lot of aspect points, but can't become God. You don't disappear though, so it's probably a decent tradeoff.

2889.name: Tungstate Processing - Step 1
2889.desc:
First, let's turn our Tungstate into Scheelite. When you can get Scheelite ore, you can just skip this step.

Note that if you're processing Huebnerite or Ferberite from ross128b or 128ba, the first step is slightly different.

2890.name: Tungstate Processing - Step 2
2890.desc: Next up is Tungsten Trioxide. Note that you got back all of the Calcium Chloride. If you process Scheelite ore at all, you'll want to set up an electrolyzer.

2891.name: Magnets != Magic
2891.desc:
Tired of running after ore or other blocks? Use a magnet! It has a limited range, but who cares? Just remember to turn it on.

In HV you can craft it into the better one, if you still need a magnet by then.

2892.name: HSS-S Coil Upgrade
2892.desc:
Technically this coil tier can be skipped. But you need LuV to get the next one, so you should really get these coils for now.

To process more advanced materials like LuV Superconductor Base you need more than 5400K. Time to upgrade your EBF to 6300K.

2893.name: Trinium Coil Upgrade
2893.desc: To process more advanced materials like neutronium or fluxed electrum you need more than 8100K. Upgrade your EBF to 9001K. (OVER 9000!!!)

2894.name: Lapotronic Capacitor (IV)
2894.desc: It holds more than the previous tier, other than that there is no difference. Your LSC can hold between 5.4B and 81B EU with these.

2895.name: Cooking for Blockheads
2895.desc:
Say thanks to mitch for backporting this great mod. You can use it to help you craft new food items. All blocks connected to the kitchen floor (use it as walls too!) are considered part of the same multiblock. Countertops will search up two blocks for cabinets to include.

Items stored in storages like the cabinet (or your inventory) can be used in recipes. All storage spaces for items are the same, so don't bother making a ton of fridges unless you really want to. Supply water to the sink to get water for recipes.

You can store some cooking tools in the oven. You can store the rest on tool racks to auto-use them all in recipes. This includes stuff like the GT soft mallet if you can't make all the Pam's tools yet.

The oven has 3 input slots and 3 output slots, and can work on 9 items at once. It's basically a furnace. You can request items be cooked from the cooking table too.

The Cooking Table will automatically show you all recipes that can be created with the existing ingredients, and you can click on them to craft them. Make all the food, and get all the hearts!

You can use dye to color the kitchen, except for the oven.

Note that the implementation is not finished, so there are some problems. Upgrades like powering the oven with RF/EU are not in yet. And so on. But we finally have a toaster so you can make TOAST!

[note]There is no cow in a jar, it will not come back.[/note]

2897.name: Making a Rapier
2897.desc: Problems killing armored enemies? Just use a Rapier that ignores armor, but doesn't do a lot of damage.

2898.name: Crispy Rotten Flesh
2898.desc: Rotten Flesh is not really healthy. It doesn't satisfy your hunger. What if you try to dry?
