It absorbs and radiates energy without any effort, but it would be stupid to attempt making coils out of this without assistance. Wetware wasn't good enough anymore, and only the most advanced living circuit could withstand the erratic behavior Hypogen demonstrates.

Provides 12601K heat. Almost certainly, this will end up on your DTPF.

3219.name: Eternal Coil Upgrade
3219.desc:
Beyond spacetime, beyond reality. You have peered into what never existed and brought into a fluid, and then a solid. It has been stabilized, against all odds. When you look at it, you think that you could hold the entire universe in your hand.

In the past, you were able to combine almost all materials into one, through an Eternal Singularity, an infinitely dense sample of the universe. However, automating this at the scale of the DTPF proved ridiculous, which would have stopped your progress altogether if not for the discovery of the Energized Tesseract. To physically manifest spacetime itself, you first needed to manifest the fourth spatial dimension, and that is what the Tesseract is: a 4-dimensional cube.

Cubes! Almost everything around you is made out of cubes. You have only glimpsed at the reality of the 4-dimensional world, but you have successfully used it to condensate spacetime, which can be arbitrarily molded into the complete Eternal mixture, a process that unfortunately cannot be reversed. Needless to say, living circuits will not suffice to maintain an infinitely dense, infinitely stable, multi-dimensional material.

Currently, the final upgrade with 13501K. This really makes your DTPF shine!

3220.name: The Roots of Magic
3220.desc:
If you're familiar with Botania, you will notice that the overworld is noticeably void of magical flowers and mushrooms. Such pretty expressions of magic sadly require you to have leveled up on your magical skills. For now you will have to make do with what the world has to offer.

Look in deep caves in the Twilight Forest at low Y-levels for glimmering mushrooms. These are your starting ingredient and can be used instead of flower petals. Beware, unlike flowers you can't grow more just yet, so be careful what you use them for.

3221.name: Flower Grafting
3221.desc:
The first item you want to make is the Petal Apothecary. A mushroom is needed to anoint it and give it its restorative properties.

To operate it, fill it with a bucket of water to keep the plants hydrated. After that you can add the ingredients by dropping them on top.

Each craft requires some magical ingredients, but also one or more regular flowers from the world that can function as a stable base. Once you've added all the ingredients, finish the craft by adding a seed. Any vanilla seed will do.

3222.name: Pure Daisy
3222.desc:
The first magical flower you are able to make is the Pure Daisy. This flower can slowly enchant nearby stone and logs into basic Botania ingredients.

After placing the flower down, you can use put down stone and wood in the 8 tiles around it, and after a certain time they will be converted.

3223.name: Your First Mana Pool
3223.desc:
Now that you have livingrock, create your first mana pool. Chop some pieces into slabs with a saw to make the bottom, then stick them together with plates for the sides.

You lack the knowledge to make it effective, so sadly it won't be able to craft a lot as a result.

3224.name: Upgrading Your Mana Pool
3224.desc: Now that you have discovered the power of the magically enchanted crucible, you can use that technology to make a better mana pool.

3225.name: Pollination Buddies
3225.desc:
Some flowers are everywhere, and some are limited to specific biomes. If you don't want to walk long distances from home, you can call in some help.

Bees with the flower trait will slowly generate new flowers in its working area, including plenty that have been brought in from distant pastures.

3226.name: Mana Diamonds
3226.desc:
Mana diamonds are magically enhanced diamonds, and they require more or less energy depending on the quality of the gem you're trying to enchant.

A completely full diluted pool is barely able to make a mana diamond out of an exquisite diamond. Once you upgrade the pool you can use regular diamonds - be it at a higher mana cost.

3227.name: A Glimpse Into A Watery Future
3227.desc:
The next step is to make some catalysts. The Alchemical catalyst can use mana to change one item or block into a related item or block.

Put it under a filled mana pool, then try it out by making some prismarine.

The recipe will need to be researched in the Thaumonomicon.

3228.name: Botanic Infusion
3228.desc:
The infusion altar will allow you to make runes, which in turn gives you access to progressively more advanced flowers.

To operate the altar, drop the ingredients on top, whack it with a wand of the forest, and when it's done drop a livingrock block on top to complete the craft. You will additionally need a mana source, such as a mana spreader pointing at it.

3229.name: Terrasteel
3229.desc:
To get some advanced tools and equipment you will need some terrasteel. This is done using a dedicated machine.

You'll need to layout the blocks underneath the plate as described in the Lexica Botania. Then place a spark on top of the plate nearby a mana pool with a large stockpile of mana. Lastly you'll place a mana diamond, mana pearl, and mana steel onto the plate to begin the transformation.

3230.name: Alfheim
3230.desc: Legends speak of a portal to a different realm filled with green energy.

3231.name: Round One... FIGHT!
3231.desc: Put the 4 pylons down and shift-right-click the terrasteel ingot on the beacon to summon the Gaia Guardian.

3232.name: A Deal With Alfar Industries
3232.desc:
So the rumors about the elves are true. Well, were true. Some are still true. And what isn't true used to be true.

Turns out they have since been talking to a mysterious individual they call the Dream Master. Apparently that sparked an industrial revolution among the Alfar. Sadly, that means that they now insist on receiving the latest fashion in machinery to fuel their ever-expanding factories, in exchange for the elven materials that are now rapidly going out of fashion.

Someone needs to have a word with this guy...

3233.name: You Are Worthy
3233.desc: Go no further if you value your sanity.

3235.name: Power in the Ether
3235.desc:
These new hatches allow you to wirelessly store and receive energy from any location in any dimension. They can be placed in any existing multi block structure and will function as normal energy hatches. 

Additionally, the wireless covers can be used for singleblock machines such as a World Accelerator or a BioLab.

The wireless network can store a total of 2^(2^31) EU, far exceeding even a max tier LSC.

You can share a wireless network with others by adding them to your network with /gt global_energy_join user1 user2. This will make user1 use user2's energy when interacting with the global network. You can leave a network at any time by doing /gt global_energy_join your_name your_name.

You cannot connect wires to these blocks. These are also designed to be highly lag efficient, meaning EU is added at large intervals to prevent needless calculations from occurring. This will also save you some TPS lag from vast wire networks.

[note]If your calculator is giving up, 2^(2^31) is a number with 646,456,994 digits.[/note]

3236.name: My Eyes Hurt! #4
3236.desc: So you finally did it... but there's so much more to do. Onwards comrade!

3237.name: You Know the Drill pt.2
3237.desc: Hope you have loads of transcendent metal ingots at the ready. :)

3238.name: Space Bees
3238.desc:
Breed an industrious with a heroic bee to obtain a space bee. You need the temperature to be icy for that.

Just one small step for you, but a giant leap for beekind.

[note]This is the starting point for many space related bees. Maybe check out NEI to see what you can aim for.[/note]

3239.name: Tesseract
3239.desc:
Engineering beyond your comprehension. Destroying this strange higher dimensional object in a macerator is very easy though.

[note]You may proceed to UIV now. Best of luck.[/note]

3241.name: Round Two... FIGHT!
3241.desc:
Now craft a Gaia Spirit ingot with your new Gaia Spirit from killing the Gaia guardian.

Put the 4 pylons down and shift-right-click the Gaia Spirit ingot on the beacon to summon a harder version of Gaia Guardian.

3242.name: UMV Circuits
3242.desc:
Pico circuits, a direct upgrade from Optical Mainframes, these UMV circuits will be required for a lot of future crafts.

[note]You will need an UMV powered Assembly Line to get started.[/note]

[warn]Rumor has it these circuits may not be around for much longer...[/warn]

3244.name: UXV Circuits
3244.desc: The final circuit in the game: Quantum Circuit, these will be required for lots of UMV/UXV crafts.

3246.name: Runes
3246.desc: Craft some runes, because why not?

3247.name: Tool: Never complete
3247.desc: This quest should never be complete which is useful to hide templates and other things.

3248.name: Dragonblood
3248.desc:
For your first batch of Hypogen you will need some Dragonblood. Maybe you already have some lying around somewhere from killing the dragon a long time ago. You have 2 other options though. Bees and the DE Fusion Crafter.

For the bee path you have to go very deep into the breeding tree, but maybe you have already done that? Check the Bee Breeding tab either way.

In the DE Fusion Crafter you have 2 recipes. The better one uses an Infinity Egg from Witchery. Without any Witchery you can also just a normal dragon egg for the weaker recipe.

Once you have a bit of Hypogen you can then use that as a starting point to make more in your DTPF without any Dragonblood. I don't know why it works that way, complain to Colen.

However, you will still need more Dragonblood eventually for UMV superconductors.

[note]There is actually another way to get Dragonblood which is similar to the DE Fusion Crafter recipes but in the mixer instead. It's even slower and less resource efficient. In particular, the dragon egg does not survive the process. So only use that if you are absolutely trying to avoid magic and bees.[/note]

[note]Just a reminder: a fluid task just needs you to have the fluid in some container in your inventory. (cell, universal cell, large cell, volumetric cell, etc.)[/note]

3249.name: Wait, PBI wasn't the last plastic?
3249.desc:
Let's make a brand new plastic! The trees that you found on Barnada C have very interesting properties. Still, you can't just use the extracted radox substance raw. Some serious refining steps are in order!

You'll need it for a ton of stuff like UIV and UMV superconductors, UEV+ hatches, or parts of the Dyson Swarm.

Compared to the previous plastics, most of the steps required here are not chemical reactions. They are also very slow, so you might want to use a mega multiblock to help with that. This production line also underlines the necessity of large-scale plasma production. You will only need more going forward.

[note]Super Heavy Radox will be needed for Dimensionally Shifted Superfluid, so store some for later.[/note]

[note]The Heavy Radox you get as a byproduct can be used for MkV Naquadah Fuel.[/note]

3250.name: AE2 Fluid Crafting
3250.desc:
Fluid crafting directly integrated in your ME system. NO WAY!

AE2FC is a powerful add-on that was backported for GTNH.

Now, what do you need to get that juicy stuff working?
Every ME System needs a Fluid Discretizer. It allows for on demand crafting. While you can use AE2FC without Fluid Discretizer for moving fluids, you need a Discretizer for autocrafting.

Then you also need a Fluid Terminal or a Fluid Processing Terminal for the Assembly Line.

When creating patterns you will notice your fluids being placed as if they were items.
Your finished patterns will look blue-ish. Placing those in a Dual Interface will allow AE2 to push the fluids and items both. Note. Fluids are being sent first, then items.
You can switch the dual interface on the top right if you want it to keep fluids in stock.

If you use a blue pattern (fluid pattern) in a normal interface, AE2 will send the fluid as a fluid packet. This is just a representation of the fluid as an item. A fluid decoder can turn it back into a fluid and saves it in the fluid grid.

You can use an ingredient buffer (large or normal) so your items and fluids sent by a pattern can get stored internally in one block. The normal buffer can hold 9 items and 4 fluids whereas the large one can hold 27 items and 7 fluids. It is not needed, but you can use it to automate things with item and fluid conduits.

If you want to automate single blocks it's as easy as eating chocolate cake while on a diet:
Make the pattern, make an interface, boom- done! It will automatically insert the fluid in the fluid inventory of a single block machine.

Even automating more difficult things like the Assembly Line becomes much easier with these tools.

If you need more detailed information, visit the wiki:

[url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Applied_Energistics_2#Autocrafting_in_AE2[/url]

[note]The Extra Cells mod has been removed. Any old components and cells were converted to AE2 and AE2FC parts where possible.[/note]

3251.name: The Final Goal
3251.desc: Thanks to your help I have managed to create the ultimate energy source: An encased wormhole that drains energy from a synthesized pocket universe, to the point when it reaches heat death. I have no use for it, so you may have it. Thank you for making my dream come true!

3252.name: What Are All These Flowers Even For?
3252.desc:
If you've never used Botania before, you may be wondering about the purpose of all this effort. This isn't a simple question to answer, but one thing you can do that's quite useful right now is making yourself some manasteel.

Among its many uses, manasteel has one of the best, if not simply the best, durabilities of materials available at LV, so it's perfect for all of your crafting tools.

If you have a particular dislike of Tinker's Construct, manasteel is a great material to make a Gregtech pickaxe out of too.

3253.name: Moron's Manual For Mana Manufacture And Manipulation
3253.desc:
To use the power of magical flowers, you must understand how to generate and manipulate mana. This ethereal blue-ish substance is produced by generating flowers, when their requirements are met. Mana is not easily quantified, so you will have to do a lot of estimating if you want to use it efficiently.

Once you have chosen which flower you want to use to generate mana, you must plant it near a mana spreader. When the flower's conditions are fulfilled, it will begin to produce mana, and store it in the internal buffer of the nearest mana spreader. The mana spreader itself will automatically emit any mana it contains, provided it is aimed at a valid destination (such as a mana pool). You'll need a lot of these devices, so craft a pair for now.

Mana spreaders will aim towards you when you place them down. To change their direction, use a wand of the forest: while holding the wand, shift-right-click on the mana spreader, and then shift-right-click on the block you want it to aim at. This method of binding also works for other things, such as binding a flower to a specific mana spreader.

3254.name: Solar Power: Floral Edition
3254.desc:
The daybloom is an extremely simple generating flower. All you have to do is place it down, and it'll produce mana for free during the day. The downside of this arrangement is that dayblooms die after only an hour.

You can find Goldenrod flowers in Prairie biomes.

Make one, and I'll give you another.

3255.name: Hydrobotanic Energy
3255.desc:
Mana is blue. Water is also blue. There's a correlation there, and the hydroangea turns that correlation into causation. Place a water source block nearby, and the hydroangea will consume it to produce mana - until it dies after an hour.

This one can be a bit tricky to automate given the lack of naturally-regenerating source blocks.

3256.name: Dark Energy
3256.desc: Perhaps not the kind of "dark energy" you're familiar with, the nightshade produces mana at night. Like other passive generating flowers, it dies after an hour.

3257.name: Is This A Combustion Generator?
3257.desc: The endoflame burns combustible materials in order to produce mana. It can efficiently consume fuels with a burn time of up to 64000 per item (equivalent to four blocks of coal); any burn time beyond this will be wasted. Endoflames don't die like passive flowers do, and are simple to use, so it's a good idea to make several of them.

3258.name: A Botanist's Best Friend
3258.desc:
Functional flowers are a large part of Botania. Most of them have nuances to how they operate, and these are generally best explained in the Lexica Botania: a codex of all Botania knowledge. This questline will explain the basics, but be sure to keep a copy of the Lexica Botania on hand so you can easily reference the details.

Besides being a repository of useful information, the Lexica Botania has another function: if, while holding it, you look at any Botania block in the world, you can shift-right-click on that block to open up the relevant page.

Something else you'll want for functional flowers is a handful of redstone roots. Flowers which have a redstone root in their recipe have the ability to toggle whether they are active by applying a redstone signal. Make a few before you move on.

3259.name: Anti-Magnetism
3259.desc: If you've made a ring of magnetization, you may be getting tired of having to take it off around various Botania systems that require items to be on the ground in order to interact with flowers. The solegnolia is your solution to that. Simply place one down anywhere you don't want items to be automatically put in your inventory, and the ring of magnetization will be disabled while you're in the vicinity of the solegnolia. It doesn't even require mana!

3260.name: Violence Is Blue
3260.desc: The dreadthorne is similar to the bellethorne, but it only kills adult animals. This is helpful for farming livestock, though it's only one aspect of a successful farm.

3261.name: Roses Are Blood-Red
3261.desc: The bellethorne is a delightful little flower with an [warn]insatiable thirst for blood[/warn], though fortunately your blood doesn't count. When provided with a supply of mana, it will inflict damage on any living entities near it, with the exception of players. It's a good extra layer of defense to place at the entrances to your early-game bamboo fortress.

3262.name: Magic Blue Dust
3262.desc: You'll need mana powder to proceed. Make four to start out with.

3263.name: Better Generating Flowers
3263.desc: If you want anything better than an endoflame, you'll need a runic altar. Fortunately, most runes are easily crafted as soon as you can make the runic altar. Sinful runes, however, require titanium to make. For now, just make one of each of the elemental runes; that'll be enough to get you started.

3264.name: Shut Down Your Canning Machine
3264.desc: Some flowers eat bees. Some eat leaves. Some eat slimes. This one eats everything (everything you can eat, anyway). The gourmaryllis is happiest when provided with a good variety of delicious meals, so I hope the HV-tier diet of canned sludge hasn't made you forget how to cook.

3265.name: Experienced Botanist
3265.desc: The rosa arcana transmutes experience into mana. A player standing near it will have their experience drained for this purpose. This process is unfortunately not very practical to automate, as the experience can only be extracted directly from a player, and not from orbs on the ground or any method of storage. It might be wise to place it somewhere you spend a lot of time.

3266.name: Mmmmmm, Slimy!
3266.desc:
Have you ever seen a slime and thought "I'd love to eat that"? The narslimmus has.

It needs to be planted somewhere slimes can naturally appear, but if you can find a good place for a narslimmus it'll consume those slimes and give you a good amount of mana in return.

3267.name: Flower!!! Unlimited Flower!!!
3267.desc:
The jaded amaranthus causes mystical flowers to grow all around it. Their vibrant petals can be used for magic, so you no longer have to go foraging for mushrooms.

There is another way of getting these, but you'll have to figure that one out for yourself.

3268.name: Obligatory Lava Power
3268.desc: The thermalily consumes lava, but it's not quite as simple as just constantly replenishing a lava pool. There's a cooldown that you'll have to account for. Even so, it's a fairly easy flower to automate if you have a good lava source.

3269.name: Chickenization
3269.desc: The soarleander generates mana by consuming anything chicken-related, including entire chickens.

3270.name: According To All Known Laws Of Mana Generation...
3270.desc: ...there is no way a bee should be able to produce mana. The beegonia doesn't know this, and will gladly eat up all of your spare drones to help with filling your mana pools.

3271.name: Dreams of Gold
3271.desc: Just try to dream of gold.

3272.name: Ignore the Glint in Their Eyes
3272.desc:
Well this is a bad idea, but since when has that stopped you before, golems with brains, what could go wrong?

You only need to complete one task.

3273.name: PCB Factory
3273.desc:
All those boards made in a chemical reactor. It's time to stop that, there must be a more efficient way to make them. After a couple days of research I have found one, a factory designed to print out circuit boards.

The PCB Factory is able to use a wide range of plastics to create circuit boards ready to be used. The printing mechanism overheats when it tries to overclock currently, so I will have to find a way to make that work later. Boards with neurons on them will also take a bit before they can be made, but it won't be hard to achieve.

Trace size: the trace size represents the lines between components, which are needed to transmit the electrical signals on the circuit. One can make it smaller to slow down the machine, but use less resources, or make it bigger to make it faster, but use more resources.

Formulas:
- 10000/(TraceSize/100) Production
- RecipeDuration/TraceSize^2 Time

Using the GUI, the player can go to the configuration menu. There they can change the trace size or add new parts to the multiblock. One can also change where they are placed around the multiblock.

[note]Make sure to set the configuration you want before using the structure lib blueprint for autobuild.[/note]

3274.name: Bio Chamber
3274.desc:
As I got new technologies, I have seen the need to make a chamber where the circuit board can contain neurons for the wetware boards or the upcoming bioware boards.

The Bio Chamber is a simple upgrade, it doesn't require many resources. Don't know why I didn't make it earlier.

[note]It can use any glass.[/note]

3275.name: Liquid Cooling Tower
3275.desc:
I have started to hit the limit of the multiblock without overclocking it, so I decided to give it a liquid cooling tower. This will allow it to overclock like every other multi with 2x Speed and 4x EU/t.

I will need to provide some distilled water to its hatch to absorb any excess heat.

[note]The Liquid Cooling Tower allows the Multiblock to have 2/4 OC at the cost of 10L/s of distilled water when running. The input hatch is placed in the bottom center of the Liquid Cooling Tower.[/note]

3276.name: PCB Factory Tier 2
3276.desc:
With the Nano Forge built, we can upgrade our production of circuit boards for free, but the nanites need a container to hold them. The current casings aren't enough, we need to find an alternative casing.

After some research, infinity as a frame and enriched holmium as the protection layer seems to work well. It can be allowed to let through nanites to go into the printer base so that extra material can be directly removed instead of trying to go slower.

[note]Allows the player to use the tier 2 recipes with the use of a silver nanite in the input bus. This also unlocks parallels of the PCB Factory with said formula: Log2(x) parallels, where x is the amount of nanites in the input bus.[/note]

3277.name: Thermosink Radiator
3277.desc:
With the control of spacetime you have achieved and its 0K temperature it would be possible to make an even better cooling unit. A casing able to keep its room temperature very low and nanites which can transfer heat effectively because of their small bodies. This doesn't quite work and some coolant is still needed, but water freezes at those temperatures so super coolant will be our choice.

[note]The Thermosink Radiator allows for 4/4 OC making it possible to speed through recipes. It requires 10/s of super coolant in its hatch, it's in the same place as the Liquid Cooling Tower.[/note]

[warn]This upgrade is incompatible with its lower tier predecessor.[/warn]

3278.name: PCB Factory Tier 3
3278.desc:
Why use a small nozzle to print out the conductor on the board when one can just build it using nanites? Well the silver nanite isn't rigid enough for that so a new nanite will be needed... and so will new casings. Heard that celestial tungsten is a good candidate for a frame. Quantum can be used for the protection as these nanites will be forever flying around inside.

The structure will need to change as it needs to be a bit bigger and taller.

[note]Works the same as the Tier 2 in most cases, but allows the player to use even less resources by using a gold nanite.[/note]

3279.name: Nano Forge Tier 1
3279.desc:
There must be a way to make a nano scale robots to work in a machine. Carbon ones can be made in an assembly line, but very inefficiently. There must be a better way.

What about a Nano Forge, able to cut up blocks into nano-thin sheets and then creating the first nanite, which helps create thousands of them in mere seconds.

[note]The Nano Forge is a big multiblock requiring lots of power over the course of a couple minutes. It is able to use TT energy hatches (including lasers). The controller must have a carbon nanite inside to make tier 1 recipes.[/note]

[note]Despite what NEI might claim there are no minimum voltages for the Nano Forge recipes. If you want to do a recipe you just have to achieve the minimum EU/t required, no matter the voltage[/note].

-6117290316329622558:63121469939273035.name: Metastable Oganesson
-6117290316329622558:63121469939273035.desc: You thought you'd be done with Fusion?

-5821693953433775363:72930984370652814.name: Prismarine Recycling Pt. 2
-5821693953433775363:72930984370652814.desc:
You'll notice after processing your first Prismarine Precipitates you are left with a second Contaminated Prismarine Solution. You can also recycle this for added Prismarine Solution and Prismarine-Rich Nitrobenzene Solution. Again, technically optional, but highly recommended.

[warn]If you didn't setup the recycling for the Contaminated Hydrogen Peroxide then you'll want to now since this recycling actively produces it.[/warn]

-5881168837331971518:97779429653103480.name: Prototype Matter Manipulator
-5881168837331971518:97779429653103480.desc:
The Matter Manipulator is an extremely powerful tool that you can use during your entire playthrough. It is essentially a survival version of world-edit that is custom made for the GTNH player's convenience.

The Prototype Matter Manipulator is expectedly the least powerful, most rudimentary version. You can only use the Geometry Mode to create and manipulate shapes for use in building. You will not be able to remove blocks until you install an upgrade unlocked in EV.

For the most accurate and detailed information visit: [url]https://github.com/RecursivePineapple/MatterManipulator/blob/master/README.md[/url]

[note]The best way to learn about it is to use it! Experiment with the UI and become familiar with how it works.[/note]

[warn]Checkout the Building Better Bases tab for the complete Manipulator questline.[/warn]

-8682800239418763402:168326707788926946.name: Behold: The Netherite Line
-8682800239418763402:168326707788926946.desc:
You didn't seriously think you only needed to do the Naquadah line in LuV did you? Well if so; better brace yourself for the Netherite line!

Don't panic, it's a relatively straight-forward line so long as you can wrap your head around minor processing loops and percent based output. Fortunately you have a quest book to guide you through your initial setup.

Be warned- you will need at least Grade 1 Purified Water and Naquadah Coils to make it through this entire line.

This questline will start you off with the three primary things you must prepare in order to begin:

- Milled Netherrack
- Nether Air
- Ancient Debris Collection

For at least one and likely two of those you will need to journey into the nether, so make sure you're prepared equipment wise!

[warn]If you already created the line and do not wish to complete the individual quests, hold one of these three item sets in your hand after completing this quest: [/warn]

- 1 Dense Netherite Plate
- 64 Intensely Bonded Netherite Nanoparticles
- 16 Netherite Ingots

-5119728323472026576:215323840087215569.name: Excavation Upgrade
-5119728323472026576:215323840087215569.desc: Mining out large chunks of stone can be a huge pain when it comes to creating bases. Why not craft the excavator upgrade for your Prototype Matter Manipulator so that you have the ability to remove blocks quickly?

-9129674536551875363:231037260834686310.name: Infinity Boots
-9129674536551875363:231037260834686310.desc:
An armor set worthy of a God. These boots provide a permanent jump boost and increased running speed.

While wearing the complete set, you are completely unkillable.

-9173649568252084802:524055433773926135.name: Energium
-9173649568252084802:524055433773926135.desc:
Are you tired of making Energium Dust by mixing Ruby and Redstone Dust? This bee offers a way to acquire it directly - how convenient is that? To breed it, you'll need to place some Hot Coolant below the hive while inside a Boneyard found in the Nether.

Unlike its parents, this bee won't burn down your base—but it will set you on fire if you're not wearing your apiarist suit!

-4660293697819101110:301401750890827141.name: Let's Go Lava Surfing!
-4660293697819101110:301401750890827141.desc: The Thaumium Boat is an upgraded version of your Greatwood Boat. The experience you have gained from manufacturing boats made of magical materials allows you to create a boat that can go even faster. Additionally, the Thaumium coating allows it to sail not only in rivers but also lava streams! Take it with you on your vacation in the Nether and enjoy.

-6062908746963570458:313634712247812384.name: Something From Nothing, Part 3?
-6062908746963570458:313634712247812384.desc:
Suck up liquids like never before! This module replaces the infinite fluid drilling rig, but has the convenience of being inside your space elevator. Look at the NEI recipes to see which fluids you can pump with this.

[note]Will require MK-II motors on Space Elevator to function.[/note]

-7099730498725039554:333969105743202216.name: Starting Your AE2 Journey
-7099730498725039554:333969105743202216.desc:
Applied Energistics 2 is an integral part of GTNH gameplay. 

AE2 is the primary networking and logistics management mod in GTNH for Items, Fluids, and Crafting. You will use it starting from EV and onward for most automations you perform. Given the sheer density of this modpack there are a variety of unique tools and features added specifically to aid in your adventure. Most of these tools are covered in this quest tab.

There is a lot to learn, but don't feel overburdened! Your abilities will continue to grow the more time you spend with it. There are a wide variety of helpful resources to take advantage of in addition to this book when it comes to learning:

1. The Wiki: [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Applied_Energistics_2[/url]

- The Wiki has a lot of great starter information on what various systems do. They also have a lot of machine specific automation tutorials so be sure to check the Wiki if you're struggling or want a point of reference.

2. The Discord: [url]https://discord.gg/gtnh[/url]

- The Discord is full of experienced AE2ers', check out #ae2-help to find plenty of people who will take time to help you figure something out.

3. Video Tutorials: [url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LUYVmIAEC-I[/url]

- There are a lot of great GTNH content creators who make high quality video tutorials for this. Here is a link to AE2 Fundamentals by IX. There's an advanced version as well.

-5290015041678497890:365830182917000544.name: Raw Uranium Ore Exchange
-5290015041678497890:365830182917000544.desc: You can exchange your stack of Raw Uranium Ore for IC2 Uranium Ore.

-6377301388234701261:441814403394913197.name: Growing Auram
-6377301388234701261:441814403394913197.desc:
One Essentia you might have a hard time gathering is Auram. Maybe you've gotten some from Ethereal Essence, which is dropped by the flying balls of light that rarely show up in the Nether, or some from Nodes. But that is hardly a good supply. The Aurelia plant can resolve this.

When a full moon is visible overhead, Aurelia flowers will open up their petals presumably to feed off of the light. Significant for you is that they will occasionally shed these petals, which can be collected off the ground as a dense source of Auram Essentia.

You should make sure the ground near the flower is level and clear, so you'll be able to collect the petals later. The flower itself is simply planted on grass, not farmland, so no need to have a water source nearby either.

[note]Terra Wart is also a growable source of Auram Essentia. It has a quest in the Fishing, Farming, Cooking quest tab.[/note]

-5629821162319387641:847036700695940726.name: Shulker
-5629821162319387641:847036700695940726.desc: I would carry out this battle indoors if I were you...

-8670597739683462194:579420945032495629.name: Thauminite Helmet
-8670597739683462194:579420945032495629.desc:
If you improve your Thaumium to Thauminite, you can make this very stylish helmet! Also comes with a very useful 5% vis discount while equipped.

Maybe this new material is useful for wands too?

-6274099661929415720:679876563460966933.name: Getting Samarium in IV
-6274099661929415720:679876563460966933.desc:
Mix Samarium chloride dust with Salt. Electrolyze the result and the Samarium dust will be separated out.

Sodium and chlorine can also be fully recovered.

You also get some additional residue. It does not have any uses currently but might be useful in the future.

-4753516693701930610:699514068662504492.name: Advanced Wireless Setup Kit
-4753516693701930610:699514068662504492.desc:
The Advanced Wireless Setup Kit is a must have for heavy wireless users. Instead of being a 1:1 back and forth it allows you to enter a "queuing mode" and a "binding mode" where you can select multiple targets at one time to bind in a row.

When binding to connectors, the kit will bind connectors in the exact order that they were queued in. 

To clear a queue and reset the kit, just shapelessly craft it into itself in your inventory.

-7154632998014096877:1099540675571499978.name: Advanced Xeninol
-7154632998014096877:1099540675571499978.desc:
It's here that you might yourself with some useful stuff alongside power. Xenon, which has become very useful in the EBF thanks to noble gas changes in its recipes, and Advanced Nitinol? If you don't already have a Quantum Anomaly setup for it, you can use this. Who knows what'll come in the future?

These recipes are the same 0.144 ratio ones that you've seen before and, since they're just before the final step, they will bottleneck you on the path to compact reactor setups. The one to focus on is Advanced Nitinol, which is the second last recipe, and where you'll need 9.216, or 10 Mk3 reactors to sustain a Celestial Tungsten compact at the end. If you build a compact here, however, you can feed 6 compacts as long as everything that comes before this is fast enough.

-8962793288102717677:1125906221885047226.name: Matter Manipulator MKII
-8962793288102717677:1125906221885047226.desc:
The MKII is the penultimate upgrade in the Matter Manipulator line. This one being much more powerful, storing 10x the power, with a range of 128 Blocks, and the ability to place 256 Blocks/Second.

In addition to all of the MKI features you now unlock two powerful new modes:

- Copy / Paste: Copy a selection of blocks and paste it as many times as you'd like. Works best with GT & AE2.

- Move: Similar to Copy / Paste, but will physically relocate something already built.

Here is a short list of things that are copied / moved with the MM, the more complete list is on the Readme, but this should give you a general idea of how versatile it is.
GregTech:

- Pipe/Cable connections
- Machine color
- Machine front & output directions
- Basic machine settings (auto outputs, etc)
- Hatch settings (sorting, stack limiting, etc)
- Multi machine settings (void protection, batch mode, etc)
- Multi rotation
- Covers
- Strong redstone output
- Custom names
- Ghost circuits
- Bus/hatch item/fluid lock
- Modes (for machines that use the new mode system)
- Data stick data
- TecTech machine input parameters
- ME output bus/hatch capacity
AE2:

- Any coloring
- The orientation
- The configuration
- The upgrades
- Custom names
- Any stored cells (will also automatically configure cells & install their upgrades)
- Any patterns (will encode but not decode patterns)
- Priority Settings
- P2P Settings
- OreDict / Regular Filter

-4721119019196807237:934917285194189830.name: Runite Plasma - The Mk2 Conclusion
-4721119019196807237:934917285194189830.desc:
To complete this chain, you need to activate the Astral Titanium Plasma you have with a special material, also a GT++ one, called Titansteel. This is a considerable powersink, which is why the input amount is so low at 2L, and it forces you to dedicate an ABS at UV tier to feed one mk2 reactor running the Runite plasma recipe. If you decide to upscale this setup, the best option is to simply build a mega ABS, where you can also use EBF coils to save some EU, and in turn make the powergen chain more efficient.

Runite Plasma has a 350M fuel value, which leaves it free to run on Trinium XLs without penalty, whereas Tin Plasma has a 74.5% penalty on the same turbines due to having less than half the fuel value. If you want to upscale your plasma powergen to great numbers, this is an option, but the mk3 chain is probably the one to choose.

The same ratio of 0.144 to 1 reactors also applies here.

-5758781817576436666:948315306589243560.name: Crystal Shulker Box
-5758781817576436666:948315306589243560.desc:
An enhanced version of the Diamond Shulker that allows you to see the contents within.

Has 108 slots.

[note]Can be Dyed![/note]

-5796823047327111175:960069936746873824.name: White Dwarf Matter
-5796823047327111175:960069936746873824.desc:
Who knew that creating artificial solar systems would finally allow you to extract the core of a freshly dead star?

With your current crude casings, you can only accelerate the life of an artificial solar system to observe white dwarfs.

Perhaps this matter will help you push the process even further.

[note]This is one of the three EOH materials. Use it to upgrade your casings up to T4.[/note]

-7126666155324853443:1020064013494797697.name: Wireless Power!?
-7126666155324853443:1020064013494797697.desc:
Are you sick and tired of having cables all around your base? Are you tired of having to upgrade your cables constantly to handle different amperage amounts? Are you a fan of lightning bolts zapping around your base?

Then this multiblock is for you, the Tesla Tower comes with high amperage output and full TecTech energy hatch support with this powerful multiblock.

To get started you will need to fill the tower with capacitors matching the tier of the Primary Tesla Windings within the capacitor bank. The Tesla Winding determines the tier of power that the tower transfers power at and the amount of capacitors determines the maximum amperage that can be output.

A total of 16 capacitors can be placed into one capacitor bank so you might need many banks depending on your power needs.

Once the tower is constructed click open the controller to configure the maximum transfer distance of the tower to fit the needs of your base. The range setting is currently buggy which means that at least for now you can do so without needing any plasmas. This option is called “Tesla Towers Transfer Radius Setting”.

Now that your tower is all configured how do you get power out of it to your machines? Well I am glad you asked. To get power to the machines of the base you will need to use the Tesla Coil Covers. Place them on the tops of your machines, energy hatches and even transformers to get power flowing from the tower. Energy hatches are a bit picky however and the hatch likes to be facing upwards to receive power.

[warn]Warning! The Tesla Tower will output only one tier of amp such as EV, IV etc. When upgrading the Primary Tesla Windings, please make sure to check that all of your coil covers and transceivers in the base can handle the upgraded power. Failure to do so will result in a machine explosion.[/warn]

[warn]Failure to turn off the controller before adjustment can also cause an explosion.[/warn]

-8996825125550293613:1067327358665575267.name: Algae Pond
-8996825125550293613:1067327358665575267.desc:
Sustainability. It's not the name of the game... but it could be!

If you've ever wanted to generate many chemicals from a single source, you've probably looked at Oil or Benzene. However there's also the Algae Farm. It's just a pond of sorts, so it doesn't need power to run. Since it also doesn't really follow normal recipes, you won't find any NEI recipe page for the Algae. The multi just produces them on a regular schedule.

The tier of the multi is given by the casings on the bottom layer of the multi, which all have to match to decide the tier. Each tier from ULV (starting at 50s per batch) till IV (which is 25s per batch) reduces the time it takes to produce Algae by about 5 seconds, and then beyond that each tier will cut the speed in half. Randomly the production speed can either double or triple the time it takes as well, but this happens relatively rarely.

Each tier increase also adds to the amount of Algae you get in a single cycle, as well as the type of Algae produced. Each cycle gives out an exact amount as well as a bonus amount 10% of the time. The Algae Farm also accepts Compost, that you can make from the algae itself, to boost the multi's tier by one. This might be expensive at the start, but it's heavily recommended in most cases. You can get regular Algae and Green Algae starting at ULV, Brown Algae starts appearing at HV, Golden-Brown Algae at EV, and Red Algae at IV, though most of the cases where they start appearing they start doing so in very small quantities.

To find out more about the exact output quantities and timings, please see the wiki: [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Algae_Farm[/url]

The Algae can be used for various chemicals like Methane, Ammonia, Sulfuric Acid, Sodium Carbonate, Ethylene, Lithium Chloride, and even some power-generating fluids. You typically need a Chemical Plant for that. In IV tier you can also combine Green Algae with Glowstone to get Glowing Coral and make Sunnarium from that. If you're curious about all the processing chains, look them up on NEI: quests to cover them are coming up, possibly with more recipes that don't need the chemplant.

-7064938304558843391:1427206946398421598.name: Infinity Pants
-7064938304558843391:1427206946398421598.desc:
An armor set worthy of a God. These pants have no special effects on their own but are required to complete the full infinity set.

While wearing the complete set, you are completely unkillable.

-9020589751289166951:1429520268554815440.name: Ozonation
-9020589751289166951:1429520268554815440.desc:
Want even more speed on your wafer processing? Using Grade 2 Purified Water, made by ozonation, you can now reduce processing time by 50%. To run this machine, you will need to make large amounts of Ozone Gas by bombarding air with a strong laser. If you haven't already, you should definitely make the Hyper-Intensity Laser Engraver multiblock to speed up this process.

[note]This multiblock is very picky about where the ozone input hatch goes. If you can't figure out why it's not forming, chances are you forgot to place this hatch in the correct location. Use the hologram projector to see where it goes.[/note]

-8199803816494517045:1162181130482500484.name: Mass Processing in LuV and Beyond
-8199803816494517045:1162181130482500484.desc:
As you enter LuV and beyond, fewer mass processing multiblocks are available as you replace old setups with new systems and scale up the multiblocks you've already built - there are still some important upgrades in these higher tiers, though.

Definitely don't miss out on the Circuit Assembly Line or you'll be kicking yourself later.

Be a true GT engineer and finish out your collection!

-7429140403958989457:1169604861435527549.name: A Portable Barrel?
-7429140403958989457:1169604861435527549.desc:
As an upgrade to the barrels, EFR also adds the ever-familiar shulker box! The shulker box allows you to portably tote it's contents. You can find the research for them in the New Horizons tab of the Thaumonomicon.

These have seven additional tiers much like the barrels and chests that give you additional storage space. 

You can dye any tier by shapelessly crafting them with a dye, and undye them by shapelessly crafting them alone. They will retain their inventory. 

[note]There are many ways to obtain shulker shells:
1. Fighting Shulkers in the Twilight Forest Lich & Ghast Queen Towers.
2. Fighting Shulkers from the eggs in the Coins tab
3. Manually crafting the shells[/note]

-6671981679462427921:1218620776680214398.name: EV 4A Hatches
-6671981679462427921:1218620776680214398.desc:
To insert and extract many amps from your LSC, make these special hatches.

Some multiblocks can also accept multi-amp hatches.

-9054185358983654723:1224892504375445106.name: Mana Fountain
-9054185358983654723:1224892504375445106.desc:
The Mana Fountain allows for quick and seamless automation of Mana Pool recipes.

You can assemble the multiblock as specified in the Lexica Botania / NEI. After it's assembled, right clicking it using the Wand of the Forest will show you whether it's complete and the amount of internally stored mana.

You can send the controller mana directly by either using a spreader or a spark much like a Mana Pool. Technically you don't have to use the Mana Pool in the center of the multiblock for it to function, but it is placed conveniently so that you can (plus, it doesn't hurt to have an extra buffer).

Items are allowed to input from any side of the controller, but can only output from the bottom. For early automation, it's recommended to just use hoppers. Once you uinlock AE2 however you can simply place an interface underneath the controller with the appropriate patterns and it should push and pull items automatically.

[warn]You cannot craft Alchemy or Conjuration recipes using this multiblock, those are separate multiblocks that you will unlock with more advanced robot arm technology.[/warn]

[note]This structure can absolutely wall-share. It has the perfect design to be able to share 4 different Mana Fountains between the center pool.[/note]

-7141204399473530015:1293209622553709762.name: Troubleshooting...
-7141204399473530015:1293209622553709762.desc:
If you find your pipe has thick red corners, then it means something is wrong. Either the network is out of power or you formed a junction with an Unrouted Transport Pipe.

When the system gets more complicated, the chances go up that something will go wrong. Have fun troubleshooting! :)

-8825999907887304936:1463936312007937688.name: Better Chassis
-8825999907887304936:1463936312007937688.desc:
Now that storage is handled, you want to dive into the automation part?

A Mk1 Chassis can't hold enough modules for complex functions. We need a better one.

Chassis Mk2 can hold 2 modules. It should do for now.

-7336878164390242998:1493066294491564329.name: Pollen Collection Kit
-7336878164390242998:1493066294491564329.desc: The easy way to get some pollen, just right-click on leaves. The Pollen Collection Kits are one-time use though, so maybe make a few.

-7583790943377787116:1498473744964405337.name: steve@mothership:~$
-7583790943377787116:1498473744964405337.desc: This block can be used to configure the Mothership's name and icon, who is allowed to land on it and who is allowed to access the Navigation Console and the Thrusters.

-5996163823746127218:1550266253939000626.name: Requestin' a Recipe
-5996163823746127218:1550266253939000626.desc:
Most of the time you are requesting things because you need to craft something. So here is the Logistics Request Table.

This thing comes with a 3*3 crafting grid that you can load with a NEI recipe and request the recipe. There is also an inventory buffer that you can use to store items within.

-8687505330958936569:1595245673746680403.name: Jeremy Fragrance
-8687505330958936569:1595245673746680403.desc:
The peak of all Incenses.

Purpura will increase your Incense from 2400 to 3500.

-8495200811387783221:1603543618291385233.name: Sorting Incoming
-8495200811387783221:1603543618291385233.desc:
Now that storage spaces are organized and assigned, time to let the network sort the items for us.

There are 2 ways to insert items into the system

1. Use an extraction module to extract items into the system. It is slow, but everything that has a valid destination will be sent into the network.

2. Use a QuickSort module. It extracts a whole stack of items that has a valid destination. This module will ignore items that have no destinations other than the default route though...

It is your choice. Pick what you like.

-6004958964934582288:1988409501110781230.name: 4096k Essentia Storage Cell
-6004958964934582288:1988409501110781230.desc:
These hold 8,388,608 units of essentia.

And can hold 48 types.

-5355942527641797439:1763751996399305522.name: The Real Planet Eater
-5355942527641797439:1763751996399305522.desc:
It has 8 parallels and supports higher tiers of drones.

[note]You can obtain pure platinum group metals with this. It would be a nice boost if you haven't made the QFT for it yet.[/note]

-5277210864538635347:1810298291960562416.name: Infinite Water Cell
-5277210864538635347:1810298291960562416.desc:
The ME Fluid Infinity Water Storage provides 4.5P water directly to your AE system, That's the equivalent of 2.25 million reservoir hatches, all conveniently packaged into one drive. 
Might come in handy...

[note]Have fun making those fluid packets![/note]

-9020796615899025430:1930161895658832038.name: Helioflux Melting Core my Beloved
-9020796615899025430:1930161895658832038.desc:
As usage of the Forge continues, more data on graviton shards is gathered. It has been found that they can stabilize the outputs of the Forge, allowing for the direct extraction of molten material while it is still close to the heart of the star.

[note]The second module of the Godforge, Helioflux Melting Core allows processing EBF recipes by directly outputting molten metal when possible. This may not always be as efficient as other methods, but the time and scaling saved from Vacuum Freezer steps often more than makes up for this.[/note]

[note]Usage of this module requires the FDIM upgrade.[/note]

[warn]Helioflux Melting Core <3[/warn]

-5284198952195300317:1943682690209565285.name: Power Goggles
-5284198952195300317:1943682690209565285.desc:
Wanna see how your LSC is doing but don't want to run to it or your information panel every time? Get yourself a pair of Power Goggles to see your energy storage straight from the HUD!

By default the goggles are unlinked and will monitor the amount of energy in your wireless network.

To link the goggles to an LSC, Right+Click the controller with the goggles in your hand. Shift+Right Click air to unlink.

If you wish to move or modify the display you can do so by opening the power goggles config menu!

[warn]Make sure to check your keybinds! There's also a keybind for toggling the power chart!

Or, if you don't wanna deal with that, you can use the /powergoggles command[/warn]

-8063346062660766612:2266700169884617512.name: Dreams of Rainbow... Stargates?
-8063346062660766612:2266700169884617512.desc:
UEV energy hatches? Great! Time for stargate trophy number two.
Once again, to get your trophy you have to submit two stargates' worth of materials.

Trophy number two brings us to chapter two of our journey through GTNHs past, the early UEV era.

The year 2022 has just begun and the UEV tier, albeit still quite empty, has become more useful than before. UEV-UMV energy hatches now have proper recipes (although with UEV parts) and optical SMDs are a thing now, hooray.
There finally are better powergen options than regular plasma turbine spam, namely naq fuel and compact fusions + XL plasma turbine spam. Technically dyson too, but that is horribly bugged and its 'legit' power output reasonably can't exceed around 6 billion EU/t (around the same as a compact MK4 running helium plasma).
Some useful newly introduced multis are the PrAss, MCR & Mega Oil Cracker and overclocks past MAX voltage are possible on megas now.
Stargate has once again become a distant goal, as it now required exorbitant amounts or infinity catalyst and bio circuits, requiring to utilize the new tech to its fullest.

Two players, Phineasor & GDCloud were determined to be first to completing this goal, and after months of a close friendly race, the winner had been decided. 
GDCloud won the race and conquered this round of nerfs on the 27th of May, 2022.

P.S.: Once again, if you want to be faithful to the challenge, try completing it without these additions:
-PCB Factory, Space Elevator, Component Assemblyline, AAL, EEC, EIG, MABS
-Any bee related GT machines
-Any UEV or above multis (except weak dyson and naq refineries)
-LEG
-Sub 1-tick OC, Batch Mode, inbuilt Void Protection on multis
-Level Maintainer, AE Fluid Crafting, Stocking Buses/Hatches, Crafting Input Buses, ME Output Hatches

P.P.S.: Again the chaotic capacitor banks have been left out for the same reason as stated in the UV gate quest. Oh and the stargate parts used to be UXV recipes, but since UMV hatches are not craftable in UEV anymore, they have been downtiered to UIV to make them craftable in UEV.

-8256300157046271476:2021394659373695087.name: More ItemSinks in NEI
-8256300157046271476:2021394659373695087.desc:
There are more kinds of ItemSink modules in NEI. Feel free to look through them to find the ItemSink module that suits your needs.

How they sort items and assign inventory spaces are mostly explained by their item names.

-7975621830681627535:2044539321501762815.name: Comparing at a Distance
-7975621830681627535:2044539321501762815.desc:
Comparators are wonderfully useful devices, but their need to be directly next to whatever they are comparing is quite limiting. If you could do so remotely that would free you from one major constraint.

Remote Comparators allow you to do just that. Sneak-right-click with a crystalline eye to bind the block you want to watch, and place the eye in a remote comparator to start monitoring the block remotely.

Given the extra distance, though the range of the remote comparator is quite limited, you should expect the tiniest amount of signal lag compared to a direct comparator. You're doubtful that is ever going to be a limiting factor, though.

-8724430134020257656:2302674002717395266.name: Assembling in Space
-8724430134020257656:2302674002717395266.desc: This new Space Elevator module can be used to make lapotron orbs more easily, and to make the raw components for optical circuits.

-8441065433407616811:2142225562593673653.name: Get Your Priorities Straight
-8441065433407616811:2142225562593673653.desc:
You might find yourself setting up complicated systems involving many storage buses with differing priorities. To make it less tedious, grab a priority card!

This tool allows you to set many buses to the same priority with a single click... but better yet, it also features increment and decrement modes! Every time you click on a bus, the priority will be set to one higher/lower than the previous bus you clicked, so setting up large arrays of descending priorities is a breeze.

-6008922449644892285:2197724194545617330.name: I'll teach you... how to Praise the Sun!
-6008922449644892285:2197724194545617330.desc:
TL;DR: The casing amount in this quest is for the best Solar Tower, which generates around 2 LuV amps and is a major Stainless Steel, Titanium and HV circuit sink, amongst other things.

Have you ever laid eyes upon a solar panel? You might have built a few of them here. What do they all have in common, I ask you... they generate either Steam or EU directly from sunlight, and that is not efficient. There's something that everybody forgot!

Singleblock solar panels are best to insert anywhere that has sun, and provide constant power to a machine. However, EU storage and transport are easy here, so why not build something better? Instead of generating energy directly, send those warm beams of sunlight into a central structure with circles of Solar Reflectors, and bask in the eternal power of the biggest fusion reactor in the Solar System!

(If you end up making a bigger one, don't come arguing to me about this. Be quiet.)

The whole idea is that the energy of the Sun's rays is directed towards a fluid that can retain the energy, a Solar Salt that does it far better than water. The Solar Tower transfers heat to all the fluid inside it, but watch out! If there's too much cold Solar Salt inside, it will drain away all the heat and slow down the entire process.

In more detail, the Solar Tower has a heat value that ranges from 0 to 100000, and it only heats up Solar Salt at 30000 or above. The amount of heat gained per cycle depends on the number of Reflectors, but also the current heat value: this process is optimal at exactly half the maximum, so 50000 heat. To get a perfect tower setup, you have to slowly feed cold Solar Salt to maintain this heat value, taking away just enough heat to keep it there. I'll let you figure out what the flow rate is, given that it depends on how many Reflectors you have.

From there, generate Steam or SH Steam by heat-exchanging in the same way as Hot Coolant does it. If you want this setup to truly be optimal, do place it in your personal dimension for permanent daylight.

-6241120305394579605:2204384302128186994.name: The Basic Pipe
-6241120305394579605:2204384302128186994.desc:
Unrouted Transport Pipes by themselves aren't useful.

Time to use some of them to craft some Basic Logistics Pipes.

The Basic Logistics Pipes can connect to adjacent inventories and blocks. They also have item routing capabilities, so you can use them to form junctions.

But they also cost some diamonds...

-5888310449509338577:2315452112214576468.name: Raw Netherquartz Ore Exchange
-5888310449509338577:2315452112214576468.desc: You can exchange your stack of Raw Nether Quartz Ore for vanilla Nether Quartz Ore.

-4851697106472398149:2334390499388638957.name: Fabricating Solar Systems... in Parallel
-4851697106472398149:2334390499388638957.desc:
As you've noticed, UXV components are extremely expensive compared to the amount of Universium you can currently produce.

The Astral Array Fabricator allows you to run EOH recipes in parallel. You can review the math in the second page of the tooltip.

For a direct answer, you can use this spreadsheet: [url]https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/15j30mNnFIWBhwpJGuDrKTfiHBXNEEqyvB_r7ztFWlyQ[/url]

[warn]Be aware that the EOH will use Raw Stellar Plasma instead of Hydrogen and Helium if you use Astral Arrays.[/warn]

[note]Make sure you are generating enough power.[/note]

-8332322836118716550:2345791078736349427.name: Star Fuel and Dark Matter
-8332322836118716550:2345791078736349427.desc:
In your research on Project Godforge, you determine that you need a new type of fuel to create a neutron star. Naquadah Fuel possesses some of the properties needed, but isn't enough. Stabilizing this fuel with Exotic Catalyst, and drastically increasing the density of the fuel with other things leads you to something that may just work.

During this synthesis, you realize that this fuel could be used to make [note]Dark Matter[/note], something you previously had only seen on the Mehen Belt in the Amun-Ra System.

[note]Dark Matter Shards can be found in the Mehen Belt and mined manually, if you don't want to synthesize it.[/note]

-6553493705247837396:2454373213744809830.name: The Backbone of the System
-6553493705247837396:2454373213744809830.desc:
Welcome to Logistics Pipes! This questline is dedicated to this automation mod, which is placed in the MV tier to help manage your chests, crafting and even fluid crafting. It's quite similar to ProjectRed: Transportation, but with some differences, particularly in fluid crafting.

Here, you can connect chests and machines to a central location, but before you build that central location, you have to get some pipes for connection. The Unrouted Transport Pipe is the first pipe you will ever need in Logistics Pipes.

They only connect to other pipes and don't have any item routing capabilities, but you will need a ton of them.

Warning: Do NOT form junctions with the Unrouted Transport Pipes. Items going into an unrouted transport pipe junction will come out of a random exit of the junction.

-7261433663633078124:2472633037684950997.name: Clean Implosions?!
-7261433663633078124:2472633037684950997.desc:
Introducing the bigger (and better) Implosion Compressor, the Electric Implosion Compressor.

The Electric Implosion Compressor does not need any explosive and only needs electricity, isn't that very nice!

Furthermore it gains extra parallels 4^(Tier - 1) based on the containment block used in the middle.

Tier 1: Neutronium Block
Tier 2: Infinity Block
Tier 3: transcendent Metal Block
Tier 4: Spacetime Block
Tier 5: Universium Block

[note]While the structure preview shows holes in the middle, it requires the full 27 containment blocks to form, once formed the middle 5 blocks will disappear visually, but sometimes when breaking you will get them back (and sometimes you won't). [/note]

[note]The minimum allowed energy hatch tier is one tier below the recipe tier, so let's start with 2 UHV energy hatches for now.[/note]

-7251013088481099277:2511307823284504692.name: Extraction Point
-7251013088481099277:2511307823284504692.desc:
Nothing turns solids to liquids faster than the Large Fluid Extractor - not yet, anyways. You'll need to make both Heating and Solenoid Coils for this multiblock.

[note]This multiblock is technically EV, but it will demand a lot of tungstensteel.[/note]

-6370490362068994558:2530647701290828017.name: There Is Extra Stuff...
-6370490362068994558:2530647701290828017.desc:
Sometimes, a recipe has some extra outputs but the crafting module only has 1 output slot.

That is where the Crafting Byproduct Extraction Upgrade comes in. You can assign a byproduct of the recipe and this upgrade will make the module extract the byproduct as well. Now they won't clog the output buffer.

-5852276776698153963:2840123053886360506.name: Turbine Housing
-5852276776698153963:2840123053886360506.desc:
At UV you unlock the ability to automatically refill turbines with rotors.

XL turbines do it by default much earlier...

-8682720142309394438:2870944252283735484.name: Prismarine Solution
-8682720142309394438:2870944252283735484.desc:
After you've managed to grab some Prismarine, you must process it with Industrial Strength Hydrofluoric Acid and Hydrogen Peroxide in the Dissolution Tank. Using these ingredients you can make your very first batch of Prismarine Solution.

[warn]After you've created your first batch of Industrial Strength HF, it's highly recommended to check the Chemical Plant recipes for further creation of it![/warn]

-8716647126264214083:2673664767747114720.name: Handle Resources Like a Bo$$ #2
-8716647126264214083:2673664767747114720.desc:
A feat of human engineering at your disposal!

This machine allows you to filter 9 items, and will only output set batches. This allows you to automate processes such as electrolyzing that require different amounts of inputs that may clog the system otherwise.

[note]This will be particularly useful in MV once you unlock more electrolysis recipes![/note]

-7031660740925257993:2690447010269578534.name: Hellish Metal
-7031660740925257993:2690447010269578534.desc:
Regular Netherite Nanoparticles alone don't have enough density to compress into Netherite... they require a strong and somewhat magical binding agent. 

Hellish Metal should be the perfect candidate, you can either fabricate it in the Electrolyzer or Infuse it.

[note]The Infusion recipe is much cheaper, but can only be used by those who have been proactive in their magic infrastructure.[/note]

-9188443817784325036:2744480314605653117.name: Prismarine!? What Do I Need That For?
-9188443817784325036:2744480314605653117.desc:
Prismarine is an ancient material long rumored to have originated from structures found deep within the ocean. Learning how to create this is extremely important, and may lead to advancements in technology with further exploration into the substrate.

You can get Prismarine in a few different ways as shown resource-wise by the optional tasks:

1. Through Botania by transmutating Nether Quartz directly to Prismarine via an Alchemical Catalyst or the Fountain of Alchemy

2. Using Red Algae Biomass and Certus Quartz dust in an Autoclave

3. Using Red Algae Biomass and Prismatic Combs in an Autoclave.

Which method you choose is entirely up to you and dependent on what infrastructure you have setup thus-far. Either way you would be wise to make a robust setup as you will be needing a decent amount of Prismarine in the future.

[warn]Hold 16 Naquarite Insulator Foil to skip the Prismarine line Quests.[/warn]

-8415680337631262256:2760534389945944436.name: Haha, Spinny Ingot
-8415680337631262256:2760534389945944436.desc:
Transcendent Metal is the primary metal used in UIV components.

This metal is a bit different from the ones you have been processing so far. You need to macerate your tesseracts to get the dust and then blast it at such high temperatures that you'll need a coolant to help manage the heat.

Due to the impossible nature of the tesseract particles, you'll need a medium to enable them to form 3D shapes. Tungsten, for some reason, works very well for this purpose.

The ingot itself is also not very stable; therefore, a coating of titansteel must be applied during the cooling process.

[note]Consider passively generating it. This process is slow.[/note]

-7598566101277288092:2757769119339072576.name: Four fluids in the one Hatch!
-7598566101277288092:2757769119339072576.desc:
Now that you're in EV tier you now have access to the Quadruple input hatches. These hatches can hold four different fluids in the one hatch which saves valuable spots in your multiblocks.

Perfect to be placed in your EBFs.

-5141317039308655896:2764710852113484272.name: The BEST Soap!
-5141317039308655896:2764710852113484272.desc:
Finally, you can now reduce your permanent warp.

The chance to reduce your permanent warp is 10%, increased to 25% if you have the Warp Ward effect and further increased to 40% if you're standing in Purifying Liquid. 

[warn]This soap has some side effects. Make sure you have a way to remove your normal (and temporary) warp.[/warn]

-5984592636755449553:2771602242186397908.name: EV Field Generator
-5984592636755449553:2771602242186397908.desc:
With Field Generators you can make your first Quantum Chests or Quantum Tanks. But the first two should be for your T2 rocket.

[note]Note that the Assembler recipe is more resource efficient.[/note]

-6466077424942248474:2802294169218272610.name: Raw Redstone Ore Exchange
-6466077424942248474:2802294169218272610.desc: You can exchange your stack of Raw Redstone Ore for vanilla Redstone Ore.

-7526210071214077051:2820742743395027494.name: Taint Warnings
-7526210071214077051:2820742743395027494.desc:
Now that you have started using Vis in the form of Essentia, a warning about Taint.

Vis in the state of Essentia is unstable if not stored properly. Left alone too long, or emptied out of the Crucible, Essentia will break down. But not into Vis. Rather, it will turn into Flux.

Flux manifests as a liquid or a gas, but is, itself, only a nuisance. Thaumaturges exposed to the stuff can end up getting Flux Flue, a condition which makes it harder to channel Vis, but it is only temporary.

Larger concentrations of Flux will solidify into Thaumic Slimes, bizarre creatures that try to merge with each other to grow larger. They can be dangerous, but are no less susceptible to crossbow bolts than most things.

However, if too much flux is allowed to linger for too long, it will corrupt the land and solidify into tendrils. This is a manifestation of the blight called Taint. Fibrous Tendrils (as well as condensed blocks thereof) spread the Tainted Lands, feeding on the magic it twists. Nodes are especially vulnerable, becoming Tainted themselves if exposed to this twisted magic.

Destroying the tendrils stops the spread, but unless all are destroyed this is only temporary. A more effective solution is to destroy the Tainted Land itself, as outside this specific biome tendrils quickly starve and die off.

If you are exceedingly careless with your Crucible you can, in fact, create Tainted Lands. So you would be wise to try wasting as little Essentia as possible, and clean up any you do waste.

-6774427117513567879:3120992369410721658.name: Balance is Everything
-6774427117513567879:3120992369410721658.desc:
You already transcended dimensions, but as your power demands spike to absurd levels, maybe it's time to transcend matter!

Using the unbreakable stability of Proto-Halkonite, harnessing the power of [warn]Chaos[/warn] becomes a reality. The  Semi-Stable Antimatter Stabilization Sequencer introduces the concept of Semi-Stable Antimatter, created from Protomatter.

But it's a cruel mistress... fail to manage it, and antimatter could void itself into nothingness.

The Semi-Stable Antimatter is produced in 1 second cycles, the amount generated is based on the total of antimatter currently stocked in it's 16 Antimatter Hatches and consumes equal amounts of Proto Matter.

Each cycle, the base production of antimatter per hatch is:
(1/16)*(Antimatter)^(0.5 + C).
Then each hatch will pull a random number from a normal distribution of 0.2 + A and a variance of 1, multiplying the base production with it, meaning the total antimatter change may be negative.

This causes an imbalance between the hatches. To properly automate the antimatter production, it's vital to always re-balance the amount of antimatter in the hatches. At the end of each cycle, the lowest amount of antimatter present in hatches is recorded, and any hatch that contains more than the lowest amount loses half the difference!

Proper use of stabilization fluids can significantly reduce energy usage and boost production:

It consumes 10,000,000 + (Antimatter * 1000)^(1.5 - M) EU/t passively
Magnetic Stabilization Fluids can mitigate the passive instability and have its exponent reduced:
Molten Purified Tengam Exponent = 0.1
Tachyon-Rich Fluid Exponent = 0.2
Molten MagMatter = 0.3

For each cycle, the SSASS uses (Antimatter * 10000)^(1.5 - G) EU
By regulating the gravity containment of the forge, this exponent can also be reduced:
Molten Spacetime Exponent = 0.05
Spatially Enlarged Fluid Exponent = 0.10
Molten Eternity Exponent = 0.15

By containing the antimatter more compactly, it is possible to generate larger gains per cycle. Containment Stabilization fluids alter the exponent in the production formula:
Molten Shirabon Exponent = 0.05
Molten MHDCSM Exponent = 0.10

By inserting fluids with high energy potential, it's possible to alter antimatter's flow in chaos, making their randomness more favorable. Activation Stabilization Fluids alter the average of the gaussian distribution, lowering the risks of negative changes in antimatter and favoring better gains.
Depleted Naquadah Fuel Mk V average = 0.05
Depleted Naquadah Fuel Mk VI average = 0.10

-7479578175990039290:2909443853208275575.name: Nefarious Oil
-7479578175990039290:2909443853208275575.desc:
Using purified water, you can use an Oil Cracker to convert the Nefarious Gas from the previous step into Nefarious Oil.

[note]If you are proactive and setup grade 2 water production, you unlock a recipe that produces much more oil, with much less water, in much less time. [/note]

-8023095913103763378:2909552437528773373.name: Transcendent Plasma Mixer
-8023095913103763378:2909552437528773373.desc:
Definitely consider making one.

The Transcendent Plasma Mixer is a powerful asset for all your DTPF catalyst needs.

The base processing time is always 5 seconds. You can specify the number of parallels the machine can run in the GUI.

Each parallel additively increases the power requirement, so you don't lose any efficiency while increasing production!

[note]Most of your power will go here. Consider upgrading your naquadah fuel or creating the Dyson Swarm.[/note]

-5931115528477845082:2914673156710419619.name: Naquadah Based Fuel MkVI
-5931115528477845082:2914673156710419619.desc:
2 077 795 200 EU/t at 12s per liter. A truly immense amount of power, with a hefty cost. You will need a lot of infrastructure to run the refinery, but doing so will reward you with trillions of EU/t.

The astral titanium and celestial tungsten for alternate recipe can be sourced from laser engravers or the Plasma Fuel Line.

The tritanium can be sourced using fusion reactors, or by mining in the Deep Dark.

The shirabon will require an immense amount of fusion reactors and DTPFs to sustain.

After Eye of Harmony, condensed raw stellar plasma can be used to improve the yield for even more power.

You will also want to consider the Tachyon Rich Temporal Fluid to get the most out of the Mark VI.

-8207540231386597249:2940675302440979885.name: Hyper Acceleration!
-8207540231386597249:2940675302440979885.desc:
Hyper-Acceleration cards are a faster version of the basic acceleration cards. 

[note]Hyper-Acceleration cards can only be placed in:[/note]

ME IO Port (3)
ME Fluid Import Bus (4)
ME Fluid Export Bus (4)

In an IO Port, the Hyper-Acceleration Card will transfer items at a rate of: 

1x Cards: 4,096 items/tick
2x Cards: 32,768 items/tick
3x Cards: 262,144 items/tick

In ME Fluid Buses, the Hyper-Acceleration Card will transfer fluids at a rate of:

1x Cards: 4,096,000 mb/tick
2x Cards: 32,768,000 mb/tick
3x Cards: 262,144,000 mb/tick

-4884820751170895114:2941762189842727046.name: See You Next Time!
-4884820751170895114:2941762189842727046.desc:
Still want more? Consider doing another run! Maybe even with some restrictions if you want to spice it up a bit. Check the first quest chapter for some recommendations!

There's also roles to be earned for completing the pack two or even three times if you want an extra incentive.

[note]If you are going for 100% questbook completion, you can choose between this quest or 'The Factory Must Grow!', both are valid options.
Be wary that choosing one will lock you out of the other though.[/note]

-8876130790529141535:3024167477773291144.name: Obsidian Barrel
-8876130790529141535:3024167477773291144.desc: The Obsidian barrel holds as many items as the Diamond one, and is also immune to explosions.

-5155982425555565036:3049567061182073538.name: Tier 4 Coil
-5155982425555565036:3049567061182073538.desc:
This upgrade will allow you to create fuel even faster than before and with an alternative recipe for MKVI fuel!
...but do you really need it?

-7393385288067338253:3066500133807605848.name: XL Turbo Gas Turbine
-7393385288067338253:3066500133807605848.desc:
The ultimate gas inhaler in GTNH. Check the XL Steam Turbine quest above for an explanation of the mechanics shared across all turbines.

[warn]This turbine does not accept Benzene.[/warn] To use it with your Benzene setup, you have to upgrade it to Nitrobenzene. Further changes will be made to this turbine to favor more high-tier gases, instead of spamming Benzene, Methane or other trivial options.

-8023933740364275001:3208324850848318123.name: ...or are they?
-8023933740364275001:3208324850848318123.desc: These caps are even better than thaumium!

-8021583113396239664:3254611251340790707.name: Celestial Tungsten Plasma - The Mk3 Finale
-8021583113396239664:3254611251340790707.desc:
This is the end of the mk3 fusion chain, and the three chains altogether. After 14 fusion recipes on top of Helium Plasma, you hold in your tanks the elusive Celestial Tungsten Plasma, whose fuel value of 720M makes it immune to XL Plasma penalties all the way to spacetime!

Upscaling plasma powergen up to endgame, and the Dyson Swarm, is most likely reliant on either this or Americium Plasma, which is 1-step but not as powerful on a per-reactor basis. This can net you above 8 billion EU per tick, for every Mk3 compact that you have running Celestial Tungsten at 64 parallels, with Infinity turbines. Upscaling this is not so difficult, if you keep in mind which recipes will bottleneck you the most, as long as you have the resources for it.

There are no plasmas that build off Celestial Tungsten Plasma just yet, but it's possible that some might be added in the future, especially for GT++ materials such as Dragonblood. Congratulations on finishing this fusion chain! May the power be with you.

-6516225634758235386:3264031433280667752.name: Growing Glowstone the Magical Way
-6516225634758235386:3264031433280667752.desc: There are many plants that can provide you with Glowstone Dust. But the best one might just be Glieonia. This magical plant, once matured, can be harvested for two Glowstone Dust directly.

-5692094280341935131:3288415079686816580.name: Double the Radius = Double the Fun!
-5692094280341935131:3288415079686816580.desc:
[quest]Advanced MagLev Pylon II (HV)[/quest]

The HV Pylon has a 32 block radius (65x65x65 cube centered around the pylon)

Radius is halved if the pylon is unpowered.

-7887020035666230346:3370495124061636609.name: Advanced Neutronium Tech
-7887020035666230346:3370495124061636609.desc:
Now that you've unlocked the neutronium energy cell, you can combine it with other objects to make powerful upgrades to your AE capabilities.

Combining with an ME controller gives you a controller with infinite energy.

Combining with a cable anchor & quartz fiber gives you a replacement for the quartz fiber cable that has infinite energy.

Combining with an advanced card will give you an energy card that will allow you to upgrade your wireless terminal to have infinite energy, wherever you go.

[note]It is HIGHLY recommended you use these tools wherever possible on main-nets and sub-nets as they are huge TPS savers[/note]

-4661739323824919812:3400405695913021327.name: Extract what you want
-4661739323824919812:3400405695913021327.desc:
Directly dissolve and chlorinate of lanthanide ores in Digester to get the concentrated solution of the corresponding ore.
